title: Abobora d'Agua Guisada (Braised Water Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- one water squash
- milk
- salt
- one spoonful of wheat flour
- two spoonfuls of butter
- parsley
- onion

## Directions

1. Boil a peeled and chopped water squash in milk and salt
2. When cooked and the milk is almost dry, add one spoonful of wheat flour fried in two spoonfuls of butter, parsley and onion
3. Once it has boiled a few times, serve

title: Abobora d'Agua Refogada (Sautéed Water Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- one water squash
- fat
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onion leaves
- one spoonful of vinegar
- one spoonful of water

## Directions

1. Peel a water squash, remove seeds and chop very fine
2. Sauté in two spoonfuls of melted fat
3. Add salt, little pepper, parsley, onion leaves, one spoonful of vinegar and one spoonful of water
4. Let boil over moderate heat and serve

title: Abobora d'Agua Refogada com Ora-Pro-Nobis ou Quiabos (Sautéed Water Squash with Ora-Pro-Nobis or Okra)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of pork
- fat
- one water squash
- tomatoes
- salt
- parsley
- pepper
- one cup of meat broth or water
- a portion of ora-pro-nobis

## Directions

1. Cut a piece of pork into small pieces and fry in fat
2. Add one chopped water squash, tomatoes, salt, parsley, pepper and one cup of meat broth or water
3. Let boil for half an hour over moderate heat
4. Add a portion of ora-pro-nobis and once it has boiled again, serve

title: Abóbora d'água Guisada (Stewed Water Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- water squash, peeled and chopped
- milk
- salt
- wheat flour
- butter
- parsley
- onion

## Directions

1. Boil the peeled and chopped water squash in milk and salt
2. When cooked and the milk is nearly dried up, add a spoonful of wheat flour fried in two spoonfuls of butter, parsley, and onion
3. Boil several times and serve

title: Agrião Refogado (Sautéed Watercress)
body:
## Ingredients

- watercress leaves
- water
- salt
- pepper
- fat

## Directions

1. Pick the watercress leaves and after cleaning and chopping them, boil them in water for some minutes
2. Remove, drain them and sauté them in fat, with a little salt and pepper
3. Boil them afterwards over coals
4. Serve

title: Aipo Frito (Fried Celery)
body:
## Ingredients

- celery stalks
- water
- salt
- butter
- wheat flour
- beef meat broth
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the celery stalks as in the preceding article; give them one boil and then allow them to cool and drain
2. In another pan, place in a casserole a piece of butter with a little wheat flour; place over the fire, stirring constantly without allowing to brown
3. Pour a portion of beef meat broth, place the celery stalks, and allow them to cook
4. Remove the stalks and allow to drain
5. Pass them one by one through a liquid batter of wheat flour, and thus moistened they are fried in butter
6. Place on a serving dish, sprinkling a little sugar over top
7. Serve

title: Aipo à Hespanhola Reduzido (Reduced Spanish-Style Celery)
body:
## Ingredients

- celery stalks
- water
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- reduced Spanish sauce
- meat broth
- bread slices

## Directions

1. Take a portion of celery stalks, peel and wash them, using the tender leaves and give them one boil in plenty of water
2. Remove, allow to cool, squeeze to drain the water, and chop them as one chops herbs
3. Place in a casserole a little butter, and with it the chopped celery, with salt, pepper, and a little nutmeg
4. Pour over top a little reduced Spanish sauce and the same amount of meat broth and allow to reduce
5. Serve with slices of bread placed around the plate

title: Alcachofras (Artichokes)
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- water
- butter
- wheat flour
- white wine
- olive oil
- vinegar
- peppers

## Directions

1. Remove the lower leaves of the artichokes which are not eaten
2. Cut the tips of the leaves with scissors
3. Place them in a steamer and set this in a pot with water
4. Cook them by steam
5. When cooked, remove the interior and the choke it contains
6. Serve hot with a sauce of fried butter, wheat flour diluted with white wine, or cold with a sauce of olive oil, vinegar and peppers

title: Alcachofras Cozidas (Boiled Artichokes)
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- water
- butter
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Boil a quantity of artichokes in water
2. When cooked, remove the choke from the center
3. Pour melted butter with lemon juice over them and serve

title: Alcachofras Guisadas (Stewed Artichokes)
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- water
- salt
- chopped filling
- wheat flour
- butter
- wine

## Directions

1. Boil a quantity of artichokes in water and salt
2. Remove the choke from the center
3. Fill this space with chopped filling
4. Pour over them a sauce made of toasted wheat flour, butter, and two cups of wine
5. Let it boil a little and serve

title: Alcatra de Vitella com Arroz (Veal Rump with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal rump
- butter
- beef broth
- parsley
- celery root
- salt
- pepper
- rice
- fresh peas

## Directions

1. Fry a veal rump in cow butter until it is of good color.
2. Then add to it a bottle of beef broth, a little parsley, a celery root, salt and pepper.
3. After it has boiled for one hour, remove the meat, add to it half a pound of rice, and when this is cooked, add to the rump a bowl of fresh peas, and let it finish cooking.

title: Aletria Caseira (Homemade Aletria)
body:
## Ingredients

- wheat flour
- five eggs
- salt

## Directions

1. Make a dough with wheat flour, five eggs and a little salt
2. Beat well until it shows air bubbles when cut
3. Unroll the dough on a cloth and when well extended and thin, roll it up on itself
4. Cut the roll into very fine strips

title: Aletria Caseira (Homemade Vermicelli)
body:
## Ingredients

- wheat flour
- five eggs
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix wheat flour with five eggs and a little salt
2. Beat well until air bubbles form when cut
3. Unroll the dough onto a cloth and, when well stretched and thin, roll it up
4. Cut the roll into very fine strips, beginning from one of the ends
5. Once cut into thin threads, keep them for use, preparing as one would do with store-bought vermicelli

title: Alface Cozida (Cooked Lettuce)
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce leaves
- water
- salt
- hot fat
- parsley

## Directions

1. Take a portion of lettuce leaves, wash and scald them
2. Allow to drain and place them in a casserole with hot fat
3. Add salt, parsley, and half a bottle of water
4. When cooked, serve

title: Alface Guisada com Nata (Stewed Lettuce with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce
- water
- salt
- wheat flour
- butter
- milk

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of chopped lettuce in water and salt
2. After five minutes, remove and place in cold water
3. In another pan, mix half a spoonful of wheat flour with one spoonful of butter and two spoonfuls of full cream milk
4. Add the lettuce and when it has boiled a little over moderate fire, serve

title: Alface Picada (Chopped Lettuce)
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce
- water
- salt
- salt
- one onion
- fat or butter
- one cup of water
- salt
- salt
- one beaten egg

## Directions

1. Chop a portion of lettuce, boil it in water for five minutes
2. Remove it immediately, and place it in cold water
3. In another pan, fry a chopped onion in fat or butter
4. Add the drained lettuce, one cup of water, salt, salt, and one beaten egg
5. When it has boiled a little, serve

title: Alface Quisada (Stewed Lettuce)
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce
- water
- salt
- butter
- wheat flour
- one egg
- vinegar
- one cup of white wine
- one cup of water

## Directions

1. Take a portion of lettuce, chop and boil it in water for five minutes
2. Remove and drain it
3. Fry, in another pan, two spoonfuls of butter with half a spoonful of wheat flour and before this begins to brown, add one egg dissolved in one spoonful of vinegar, one cup of white wine, and another of water
4. Place the lettuce in this broth, boil it over coals
5. Serve

title: Alforges de Marmelos (Quince Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces
- wheat flour
- two tablespoons
- one egg yolk
- one tablespoon sugar
- one tablespoon butter
- hot water or milk

## Directions

1. Peel some quinces and cut them into slices
2. Remove the center that contains the seeds
3. Make a porridge-like mixture of two tablespoons of wheat flour, one egg yolk, one tablespoon of sugar, one of butter, and the necessary amount of hot water or milk
4. Pass the quince slices in this porridge and then fry them in fat
5. Dust with sugar and cinnamon and serve as a dessert

title: Almond White Gemada (Gemada Branca Amendoada)
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces of peeled almonds
- four ounces of sugar
- one bottle of milk
- one spoon of polvilho
- nutmeg, grated

## Directions

1. Pound four ounces of peeled almonds with four ounces of sugar
2. Once a homogeneous paste is formed, add one bottle of milk boiled until reduced to half
3. Add one spoon of polvilho and a little grated nutmeg
4. Beat everything
5. Boil, pour onto plates
6. Cover with sugar and cinnamon powder
7. Serve

title: Almondegas de Carne de Vacca (Beef Meatballs)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds beef
- half pound fresh bacon
- one onion
- one clove garlic
- salt
- parsley
- pepper
- four to six spoons wheat flour
- three eggs
- rendered fat

## Directions

1. Chop together two pounds of beef with half pound of fresh bacon, one onion, one clove of garlic, salt, parsley and fine pepper.
2. Add more four to six spoons of wheat flour and three eggs.
3. Knead everything together.
4. Form then balls the size of ordinary English potatoes, which are fried in rendered fat, this should be very hot at the moment the balls are put into it.
5. Serve with any of the sauces indicated for beef.

title: Almondegas de Carne Secca (Dried Meat Meatballs)
body:
## Ingredients

- dried beef from Rio Grande
- hot water
- salt cod
- palm fiber
- eggs
- onion
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- pork lard

## Directions

1. Soak the Rio Grande dried meat in hot water for twelve hours.
2. Remove and dry it well and chop it very finely.
3. Add to it an equal amount of salt cod.
4. For two pounds of this mixture, add one bowl of palm fiber, six eggs, one onion, parsley, all finely chopped, little salt and pepper.
5. Make a dough, and from it form balls, which are fried in pork lard, and served with corn porridge or bean paste.

title: Almondegas de Gallinha (Chicken Meatballs)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- quarter pound of lard
- little salt
- grated nutmeg
- three cardamom seeds, crushed
- one handful of bread crumb
- four egg yolks
- one spoonful of sugar

## Directions

1. Take the chicken and remove its meat
2. Chop the meat very finely with a quarter pound of lard, little salt, grated nutmeg, three crushed cardamom seeds, a handful of bread crumb, four egg yolks and one spoonful of sugar
3. Mix everything well and form small balls
4. Fry them in a little fat
5. Serve with fresh vegetables, squash shoots or cooked palm hearts

title: Almondegas de Gallinha Fervidas (Boiled Chicken Meatballs)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat of one chicken
- one pound of veal
- quarter pound of lard
- salt
- clove
- nutmeg
- four spoonfuls of wheat flour
- six egg yolks
- one spoonful of sugar

## Directions

1. Chop the meat of one chicken, one pound of veal, a quarter pound of lard
2. Add to them salt, clove, nutmeg, four spoonfuls of wheat flour, six egg yolks and one spoonful of sugar
3. Mix well and form balls
4. Cook them in boiling water
5. Remove them and serve with a cream sauce, the one already taught for the golden chicken meatballs

title: Almondegas de Glotão (Glutton Meatballs)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds finely chopped beef
- one dozen sardines
- one dozen potatoes
- wheat flour
- one egg
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- butter

## Directions

1. To two pounds of finely chopped meat add one dozen sardines and the same amount of potatoes, reducing everything to a paste.
2. To this paste also add two spoons of wheat flour, one egg, salt, pepper and chopped parsley.
3. Form balls which are fried in butter.

title: Almondegas de paca (Paca Meatballs)
body:
## Ingredients

- one paca, cooked in water and salt
- half a pound of bacon
- one onion
- a portion of carapicús (mushrooms)
- a portion of bread crumb softened in milk
- four egg yolks
- salt
- ground pepper
- one clove of garlic
- grated nutmeg
- wheat flour
- egg yolks
- breadcrumbs
- fat

## Directions

1. Cook one paca in water and salt, separating the meat from the bones
2. Chop it with half a pound of bacon, one onion, and a portion of carapicús (mushrooms), a portion of bread crumb softened in milk, four egg yolks, salt, ground pepper, one clove of garlic, and grated nutmeg
3. Mix everything well and form into balls
4. Wrap them in wheat flour and pass them through egg yolks to cover them with breadcrumbs
5. Fry them in plenty of fat
6. Serve with this same fat, or better with some spicy sauce and salads

title: Almôndegas de Carne de Carneiro (Mutton Meatballs)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter of bacon
- one onion, chopped
- one pound of mutton, chopped
- one pound of mutton, chopped
- breadcrumb softened with milk
- four egg yolks
- nutmeg
- salt
- parsley, chopped
- one spoonful of wheat flour

## Directions

1. Cut the bacon into pieces and fry it a little with a chopped onion and a pound of chopped mutton meat
2. Remove from the heat and add another pound of chopped mutton meat, breadcrumb softened with milk, four egg yolks, nutmeg, salt, chopped parsley and one spoonful of wheat flour
3. Form balls with this mixture and fry them in butter or lard

title: Anchovy Butter (Manteiga de Anchovas)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen anchovies
- water
- butter

## Directions

1. Take half a dozen anchovies, wash them well, and cook them with a little water; pass them through a sieve, and knead them with an equal amount of butter.

title: Anta Assada na Grelha (Tapir Grilled)
body:
## Ingredients

- tapir meat
- salt
- pepper
- chopped onion
- parsley
- butter
- salad or burrage leaves
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into slices the thickness of a finger, sprinkle with salt, pepper, chopped onion and parsley
2. Beat it well with bread, pass it in butter, and roast it on the grill; serve it with salad or with finely chopped burrage leaves with salt and vinegar

title: Anta Assada no Espeto (Tapir Roasted on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- tapir loin
- pork fat
- salt
- pepper
- garlic
- parsley
- onions
- pepper
- vinegar
- paper
- butter or fat
- one egg yolk

## Directions

1. Lard the tapir loin with pork fat seasoned in salt, pepper, garlic
2. Put it to marinate in salt, parsley, onions, pepper comazí, and vinegar, enough to moisten, for twelve hours in this seasoning
3. Thread it on the spit wrapping it in paper greased with butter or fat, and roast over a regular fire
4. When cooked, remove it and coat it with a beaten egg yolk, and give it a few more turns on the spit so the meat takes on good color; serve with salads

title: Anta Assada no Forno (Tapir Roasted in the Oven)
body:
## Ingredients

- a cut of tapir meat
- vinegar
- four to five tablespoons of brown sugar
- one tablespoon of salt
- pepper
- onion leaves
- cloves
- marjoram
- pork fat
- pieces of garlic
- six tablespoons of wheat flour
- two whole eggs
- one tablespoon of beef butter
- milk

## Directions

1. Put a cut of tapir meat to marinate in vinegar with four to five tablespoons of brown sugar, one tablespoon of salt, pepper, onion leaves, cloves and marjoram
2. After twenty-four hours, remove it and lard it with thick pieces of pork fat and small pieces of garlic
3. Wrap it in a coating made with six tablespoons of wheat flour, two whole eggs, one tablespoon of beef butter, and enough milk to form a dough, which is spread on a cloth and in which the cut of meat is wrapped
4. Put the cut of meat thus prepared in a pan, having placed at the bottom of it a slice of pork fat; roast it in the oven or over moderate heat, for which three hours are needed; serve with the coating

title: Anta Ensopada com Cará do Ar (Tapir in Broth with Air Yam)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of tapir meat
- fat
- one tablespoon of wheat flour
- one chopped onion
- salt
- two pepper corns
- half a dozen tomatoes
- one bowl of water
- one glass of sour orange juice
- air yams peeled and cut into slices

## Directions

1. Take a piece of tapir meat and braise it in fat with one tablespoon of wheat flour, one chopped onion, salt and two pepper corns and half a dozen tomatoes
2. When the meat is equally fried, for which it is turned several times, add one bowl of water, one glass of sour orange juice, and air yams peeled and cut into slices
3. Let it boil until the meat is cooked and the broth is reduced

title: Anta Estufada (Braised Tapir)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tapir loin
- lardoon slices of bacon
- one cup of wine
- one glass of vinegar
- two tablespoons of sugar
- two onions, cut
- one clove of garlic
- salt
- peppers
- two cloves

## Directions

1. Place the tapir loin in a pan lined with lardoon slices of bacon, with one cup of wine, one glass of vinegar, two tablespoons of sugar, two cut onions, one clove of garlic, salt, peppers, and two cloves
2. Leave the meat in these seasonings for six hours, turning from time to time
3. Seal the lid, place the pan over the fire, and boil for two to three hours
4. Serve with the strained sauce without fat

title: Anta Estufada (Stewed Tapir)
body:
## Ingredients

- tapir loin
- pork fat slices
- one cup of wine
- one glass of vinegar
- two tablespoons of sugar
- two cut onions
- one clove of garlic
- salt
- pepper
- two cloves

## Directions

1. Put a tapir loin in a pan lined with slices of pork fat, with one cup of wine, one glass of vinegar, two tablespoons of sugar, two cut onions, one clove of garlic, salt, pepper and two cloves
2. Leave the meat in these seasonings for six hours, turning from time to time
3. Seal the lid, put the pan on the fire and boil for two to three hours; serve with the strained sauce without fat

title: Anta Estufada com Caratinga (Braised Tapir with Caratinga)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tapir loin
- milk
- salt
- bacon
- salt
- pepper
- one pan lined with bacon
- one cup of milk cream
- one tablespoon of sugar
- one clove of garlic
- a little grated ginger
- one or two caratinga roots, peeled and cut into slices

## Directions

1. Boil a tapir loin for five to ten minutes in milk and salt
2. Remove it and let it cool
3. Lard it with bacon seasoned with salt and pepper
4. Place it in a pan lined with bacon and one cup of milk cream, salt, one tablespoon of sugar, one clove of garlic, a little grated ginger, and one or two peeled caratinga roots cut into slices
5. Cover the pan, seal it, and leave it to boil over an active fire for two to three hours
6. Serve with its sauce

title: Anta Estufada com Caratinga (Stewed Tapir with Caratinga)
body:
## Ingredients

- tapir loin
- milk
- salt
- pork fat
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Boil a tapir loin for five to ten minutes in milk and salt
2. Remove it and let it cool
3. Lard it with pork fat seasoned with salt and pepper

title: Anta Guisada com Quiabos (Stewed Tapir with Okra)
body:
## Ingredients

- a portion of tapir meat, cut into pieces
- water
- salt
- two tablespoons of fat
- one cup of water
- one tablespoon of cornstarch
- tomatoes
- a portion of okra, cut into slices
- peppers
- parsley
- onion leaves
- one lemon, peeled and cut into slices

## Directions

1. Cut a portion of tapir meat into pieces
2. Boil it in water and salt until cooked
3. Remove it and sauté with two tablespoons of fat, one cup of water, one tablespoon of cornstarch, tomatoes, a portion of okra cut into slices, peppers, parsley, onion leaves, and one peeled lemon cut into slices
4. Let it boil for another half hour
5. Serve with corn porridge made from mill cornmeal

title: Anta Guisada com Sapucaias (Stewed Tapir with Sapucaia Nuts)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of tapir meat
- salt
- water
- onion
- parsley
- pepper
- cloves
- ginger
- three tablespoons of fat
- two tablespoons of wheat flour
- one onion, minced
- one cup of strained broth
- another cup of milk cream
- a portion of sapucaia nuts, cut

## Directions

1. Take a piece of tapir meat and boil it with salt, water, onion, parsley, pepper, cloves, and ginger
2. When cooked, remove the meat and sauté in three tablespoons of fat with two tablespoons of wheat flour and one minced onion
3. Add one cup of strained broth and another cup of milk cream, and a portion of cut sapucaia nuts
4. Let it boil and serve

title: Anta Guisada à Campeira (Stewed Tapir Rustic Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- a portion of tapir meat
- milk serum
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- ginger
- pepper
- mustard seeds
- one canteen of milk
- one tablespoon of sugar
- two egg yolks
- one small teaspoon of cornstarch
- toasted bread slices

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of tapir meat in milk serum with salt, parsley, onion leaves, ginger, pepper, and whole mustard seeds
2. When cooked, remove from the fire
3. In another pan, boil one canteen of milk with one tablespoon of sugar, two egg yolks, and one small teaspoon of cornstarch
4. Place the meat in this cream
5. Let the whole boil for a short time
6. Serve over toasted bread slices

title: Anta Refogada com Palmitos (Tapir Braised with Palm Hearts)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of tapir meat
- water
- salt
- fat
- one cup of water
- one cup of white wine
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- one tablespoon of lemon juice
- one palm heart cut into pieces
- two egg yolks
- one glass of lemon juice

## Directions

1. Boil a piece of tapir meat for a few minutes in water and salt
2. Remove it and braise in a little fat, turning on all sides
3. Add one cup of water, one cup of white wine, salt, parsley, onion leaves, one tablespoon of lemon juice and one palm heart cut into pieces
4. Let it boil with moderate heat
5. Place the palm heart on the plate, pouring the meat as strained sauce over it, having thickened it with two beaten egg yolks and one glass of lemon juice; serve

title: Anú Assado ou Ensopado (Roasted or Stewed Black Ani)
body:
## Ingredients

- one black ani bird
- salt
- angú de fubá de milho

## Directions

1. After plucking the bird, wash it well to remove half of its smell
2. Rub it only with salt, without adding any other seasoning
3. In this state, roast it on a spit, or cook it in water with salt and fat, and nothing more
4. The broth should be drunk two to three times a day
5. The meat is eaten with angú de fubá de milho

title: Arara, Papagaio, etc. Cozidos com Arroz (Macaw, Parrot, etc. Cooked with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bird (arara, papagaio, etc.)
- three spoonfuls of fat
- one dish of selected and washed rice
- one bottle of water
- salt
- parsley
- one chopped onion
- two hot peppers (pimentas comaris)
- four to five tomatoes

## Directions

1. Sauté the bird cut into pieces in three spoonfuls of fat.
2. Remove and, when the fat is hot again, sauté one dish of selected and washed rice.
3. Stir and when well heated, add one bottle of water, salt, parsley, one chopped onion, two hot peppers, four to five tomatoes, and the bird's meat.
4. Let boil for one quarter of an hour.
5. Remove the pan from active heat and let the rice dry, placing the pan near the fire.
6. Serve.

title: Arara, Papagaio, etc. Guisados com Gerumbeba (Macaw, Parrot, etc. Braised with Gerumbeba)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bird (arara, papagaio, etc.)
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onion leaves
- marjoram
- one or two quinces, cut into quarters
- one spoonful of wheat flour
- two spoonfuls of butter
- one chopped onion
- a portion of gerumbeba fruits, peeled and cut into pieces

## Directions

1. Boil the bird with salt, pepper, parsley, onion leaves, marjoram, and one or two quinces cut into quarters.
2. When cooked, remove the bird and cut it into pieces.
3. In two spoonfuls of butter, brown one spoonful of wheat flour and one chopped onion, letting it boil while stirring until it becomes a dark amber color.
4. Add the meat and the cooking broth reduced to two cups of liquid, along with a portion of peeled and cut gerumbeba fruits.
5. Let boil for half an hour over coals and serve.

title: Arara, Papagaio, etc. Recheados e Assados no Espeto (Macaw, Parrot, etc. Stuffed and Roasted on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bird (arara, papagaio, etc.)
- the bird's liver
- half a quarter of bacon
- two cooked eggs
- one onion
- parsley
- salt
- hot peppers
- olives
- a handful of bread crusts, grated
- brandy
- thin strips of bacon

## Directions

1. Chop the bird's liver with half a quarter of bacon, two cooked eggs, one onion, and parsley.
2. Mix with salt, hot peppers, olives, and a handful of grated bread crusts moistened with brandy.
3. Stuff the bird with this mixture and sew the opening.
4. Lard the breast with thin strips of bacon.
5. Spit the bird, wrap it with thin slices of bacon, and roast over regular heat.
6. When cooked, remove the bacon layer and turn it a few more times until the roast has taken on a good color.
7. Serve with some compote.

title: Arara, Papagaio, etc., Assados no Forno (Macaw, Parrot, etc. Roasted in the Oven)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bird (arara, papagaio, etc.)
- the bird's giblets
- four cooked and chopped eggs
- salt
- pepper
- one chopped onion
- three spoonfuls of fat

## Directions

1. Stuff the bird with its giblets, four cooked and chopped eggs, salt, pepper, and one chopped onion.
2. Sew the opening and sauté the bird in three spoonfuls of fat, turning it to brown on all sides.
3. Cover the pan and let it finish roasting either in the oven or over coals.
4. When cooked, serve with some spicy sauce or salad.

title: Arara, Papagaio, etc., Guisados com Pinhões (Macaw, Parrot, etc. Braised with Pine Nuts)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bird (arara, papagaio, etc.)
- water
- salt
- a portion of pine nuts
- breadcrumbs
- one chopped onion
- two spoonfuls of butter

## Directions

1. Boil the bird in water and salt with a portion of pine nuts.
2. When cooked, remove the bird and separate the meat from the bones, cutting it into pieces.
3. Dust the meat with breadcrumbs and one chopped onion.
4. Peel and crush the pine nuts into a paste and fry in a casserole with two spoonfuls of butter.

title: Arroz Cozido com Leite (Rice Cooked with Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a plate of chosen and washed rice
- one bottle of milk
- little salt
- parsley
- cow's butter

## Directions

1. Cook half a plate of chosen and washed rice in one bottle of milk with little salt and parsley
2. When cooked and dried, pour into the plate
3. Serve with a little cow's butter on top

title: Arroz Doce (Sweet Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- one plate of chosen and washed rice
- four bottles of milk
- half pound of sugar
- salt
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Boil a plate of chosen and washed rice in four bottles of milk with half a pound of sugar, salt, and lemon peel
2. When the rice is cooked and almost dry, add a little flower water
3. Pour into dishes and dust with ground cinnamon
4. Serve as a dessert

title: Arroz Doce Dourado (Golden Sweet Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- half plate of rice
- two bottles of milk
- half pound of sugar
- salt
- lemon peel
- grated nutmeg
- six egg yolks
- one glass of white wine
- two tablespoons of butter
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Boil half a plate of rice with two bottles of milk, half a pound of sugar, salt, lemon peel, and grated nutmeg
2. When the rice is cooked and almost dry, add six beaten egg yolks mixed with one glass of white wine and two tablespoons of butter
3. Let it boil a little more
4. Place on dishes, dust with ground cinnamon and serve hot

title: Arroz Doce à Mineira (Sweet Rice Minas Gerais Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- one plate of well-chosen and washed rice
- water
- four bottles of milk
- one tablespoon of butter
- one dozen egg yolks
- ground cinnamon
- flower water

## Directions

1. Take a plate of well-chosen rice washed in cold water and put it to cook in water, letting it cook for a quarter hour
2. Then pour it into a coarse sieve to drain
3. Then put it in a pot with four bottles of milk and let it boil
4. When the milk begins to dry out, add one tablespoon of butter and one dozen egg yolks, passing the yolks through a sieve before adding so they do not curdle
5. Place on dishes, dust with ground cinnamon, and serve; one can also add a little flower water

title: Arroz Estufado com Queijo (Rice Braised with Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a plate of chosen and washed rice
- two spoonfuls of fat
- one bottle of milk
- salt
- onion leaves
- a piece of cheese cut into thin slices

## Directions

1. Choose and wash half a plate of rice
2. Sauté in two spoonfuls of fat, stir
3. Add one bottle of milk, salt, onion leaves, and a piece of cheese cut into thin slices
4. Cover and cook over moderate heat until the rice is well dried
5. Pour into the plate and serve

title: Arroz Refogado com Amendoim (Sautéed Rice with Peanuts)
body:
## Ingredients

- two spoonfuls of fat or cow's butter
- half a plate of chosen and washed rice
- half a pound of roasted and ground peanuts
- four spoonfuls of sugar
- one bottle of water
- little salt
- lemon peelings

## Directions

1. Fry two spoonfuls of fat or cow's butter until very hot
2. Add half a plate of chosen and washed rice
3. Stir and add half a pound of roasted and ground peanuts with four spoonfuls of sugar
4. Add one bottle of water, little salt, and lemon peelings
5. Boil until well dried
6. Serve

title: Arroz Refogado com Batatas Doces (Sautéed Rice with Sweet Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- fat
- salt
- parsley
- a little Indian pepper
- some peeled sweet potatoes
- one plate of chosen and washed rice

## Directions

1. Put in a pan water, fat, salt, parsley, a little Indian pepper, some peeled sweet potatoes, and one plate of chosen and washed rice
2. Let boil until cooked
3. After drying, serve

title: Arroz Refogado à Brasileira (Brazilian Sautéed Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- one plate of rice
- two spoonfuls of fat
- water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- one dozen tomatoes
- a pinch of Indian pepper

## Directions

1. Choose and wash one plate of rice
2. Put a pan over high heat with two spoonfuls of fat and fry a little
3. Add the rice inside and stir for five to eight minutes
4. Add one bottle of water, salt, parsley, onion leaves, one dozen tomatoes, and a pinch of Indian pepper
5. When dried, serve

title: Arroz Refogado à Chineza (Chinese Sautéed Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- one plate of rice
- water
- salt
- one spoonful of butter

## Directions

1. Choose one plate of rice and wash it
2. Put over heat with water, salt and one spoonful of butter
3. Let boil and dry well
4. Serve

title: Aráras, Papagaios, etc. Refogados (Macaws, Parrots, etc. Sautéed)
body:
## Ingredients

- one fat bird (arara, papagaio, etc.)
- butter
- one spoonful of wheat flour
- one well-chopped onion
- one cup of white wine
- one spoonful of vinegar
- one spoonful of sugar
- salt
- nutmeg
- cloves
- cinnamon
- two egg yolks
- one glass of cognac

## Directions

1. Take one fat bird and sauté it in butter.
2. Cut it into pieces.
3. In the same butter, brown one spoonful of wheat flour and one well-chopped onion.
4. Add the pieces and one cup of white wine, one spoonful of vinegar, one spoonful of sugar, salt, nutmeg, cloves, and cinnamon.
5. Let cook over low heat.
6. Near the end, mix two egg yolks with one glass of cognac and add to the pan.
7. Serve.

title: Aráras, Papagaios, etc. Refogados com Beringelas (Macaws, Parrots, etc. Sautéed with Eggplants)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bird (arara, papagaio, etc.)
- wine and garlic marinade
- salt
- hot pepper juice (pimenta comari)
- parsley
- chopped onion leaves
- cassava flour
- two spoonfuls of fat
- two cups of water
- one glass of local orange juice
- one dozen eggplants
- the bird's liver, chopped
- a little chopped bacon
- grated cheese
- bread crumb
- wine
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the bird into pieces and marinate in wine-and-garlic with salt, hot pepper juice, parsley, and chopped onion leaves for four to five hours.
2. Coat in cassava flour and sauté in two spoonfuls of fat.
3. Add two cups of water and one glass of local orange juice.
4. Prepare one dozen eggplants stuffed with a mixture made from the bird's liver, a little chopped bacon, grated cheese, and bread crumb moistened with wine and sugar.
5. Add the stuffed eggplants to the pan.
6. Let boil for half an hour near the fire and serve.

title: Aráras, Papagaios, Periquitos, etc. Assados no Espeto (Macaws, Parrots, Parakeets, etc. Roasted on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- one fat bird (arara, papagaio, periquito, etc.)
- salt
- hot pepper (pimenta comarí)
- milk cream
- two egg yolks
- one spoonful of fine cornmeal
- cinnamon
- one cup of milk

## Directions

1. Take one fat bird and rub it inside and outside with salt and hot pepper.
2. Spit it and roast it over moderate heat, basting it with milk cream.
3. When roasted, pour over it two egg yolks mashed with one spoonful of fine cornmeal, a little salt, and cinnamon, diluted in one cup of milk.
4. When the bird is cooked, serve with some salad or compote.

title: Assados de Carne de Carneiro em Gordura (Braised Mutton Roasts)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton loin
- thin slices of ham
- slices of bacon
- two onions
- two cloves
- marjoram
- one bay leaf
- salt
- pepper
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Take the mutton loin, cut it into equal slices the thickness of a finger and beat them with the back of a knife
2. Place these pieces in a pot, over thin slices of ham, and covered with slices of bacon
3. Add two onions, two cloves, a little marjoram, a bay leaf, salt, pepper and meat broth
4. Simmer with heat below and above until cooked
5. Remove the slices, reduce the sauce after removing the fat, strain it and serve with the prepared meat

title: Atum e Beijupirá (Tuna and Crevalle Jack in Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- postas (steaks) of tuna or crevalle jack
- red wine
- vinegar
- garlic clove
- bay leaves
- cloves
- nutmeg
- pepper
- salt
- sweet oil

## Directions

1. Soak the fish steaks in red wine, vinegar, a garlic clove, bay leaves, cloves, nutmeg, pepper and salt for three to four hours
2. Place the steaks in a pan with some of this marinade, a spoon of sweet oil
3. Set the pan over coals and cook for one hour
4. Serve with the hot sauce

title: Atum e Beijupirá Assados (Roasted Tuna and Crevalle Jack)
body:
## Ingredients

- postas (steaks) of tuna or crevalle jack, two fingers thick
- sweet oil
- garlic clove, crushed
- two onions, sliced
- bay leaves
- salt
- peppers
- cloves
- parsley
- two spoons of butter
- one spoon of wheat flour
- two cups of white wine
- lemon slices, peeled

## Directions

1. Soak the fish steaks in sweet oil with crushed garlic, sliced onions, bay leaves, salt, peppers, cloves and parsley for three to four hours, turning the fish occasionally
2. Remove and drain the fish
3. Roast on a grill over moderate fire
4. Melt two spoons of butter and mix with one spoon of wheat flour, salt and peppers
5. Add two cups of white wine and lemon slices
6. Bring to a boil and serve hot with the fish

title: Atum e Beijupirá em Salada (Tuna and Crevalle Jack Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish, cut into steaks
- salt
- peppers
- fat or sweet oil
- bay leaves
- cloves
- garlic clove
- sweet oil
- cebola picada (chopped onion)
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the fish into steaks and sprinkle with salt and peppers
2. Coat with fat or sweet oil and roast on a grill
3. Before fully cooked, place the pieces in a pan with bay leaves, salt, peppers, cloves and a garlic clove
4. Pour sweet oil over until the steaks are covered
5. Save for later use
6. To make the salad: remove some steaks, cut into small pieces, mix with sweet oil, chopped onion and vinegar
7. Serve
8. Alternatively, remove steaks from the pan, let drain, sprinkle with parsley, chopped onion and cassava flour, fry in butter and serve with orange juice on top

title: Atum e Beijupirá no Espeto (Tuna and Crevalle Jack on the Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- posta (steak) of tuna or crevalle jack
- thin strips of sea eels (conger or moray)
- melted butter
- vinegar
- sliced onion
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- bay leaves

## Directions

1. Lard a fish steak with thin strips of sea eels
2. Thread onto a spit and roast over moderate fire
3. Continuously baste with melted butter mixed with vinegar, sliced onion, parsley, salt, pepper, cloves and bay leaves
4. Serve with the sauce when the fish is cooked

title: Azas de Peru com Quiabos (Turkey Wings with Okra)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey wings
- turkey neck
- turkey legs
- a piece of minced salt pork
- water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- okras

## Directions

1. Take the turkey wings, neck, and legs and place in a pan with minced salt pork
2. Fry a little
3. Add enough water to cover the meat, along with salt, parsley, onion leaves, and a quantity of whole okras
4. Allow to finish cooking
5. Thicken the broth with three egg yolks beaten in half a chicara of lemon juice
6. Serve

title: Azas de Peru Ensopadas com Carapicús (Stewed Turkey Wings with Carapicu Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey wings
- turkey neck
- turkey legs
- butter
- one tablespoon of wheat flour
- half a bottle of water
- one chicara of wine
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- carapicu fish
- cornmeal mush

## Directions

1. Simmer the turkey wings, neck, and legs briefly
2. Remove the bones and fry in butter
3. Add one tablespoon of wheat flour
4. Add half a bottle of water, one chicara of wine, salt, pepper, chopped onion, and a quantity of carapicu fish
5. Simmer near the fire
6. Serve with cornmeal mush

title: Azas de Peru Fritas (Fried Turkey Wings)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey wings
- turkey neck
- water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- six eggs
- lard

## Directions

1. Take the turkey wings and neck and cook in water with salt, parsley, and onion leaves
2. When cooked, remove the bones and cut the meat into small pieces
3. Beat with six eggs
4. Fry in lard
5. When fried, serve

title: Azeda d'Europa e Labaça (European Sorrel and Labaca)
body:
## Ingredients

- sorrel leaves
- water
- salt
- butter or fat
- one clove of garlic
- hard-cooked eggs

## Directions

1. After removing the stems from the leaves, wash them in cold water
2. When a portion of water with salt, placed in a casserole, is boiling, throw the leaves inside and allow them to boil for five minutes
3. Remove the leaves with a skimming spoon, and place them on a board, where they are chopped with a knife, and in this state, sauté them in a casserole in which a small portion of butter or fat is melted, adding one well-pounded clove of garlic
4. Place on a serving dish, placing over top some hard-cooked eggs divided in half

title: Açorda de Roscas (Bread Soup with Rusks)
body:
## Ingredients

- one or more rusks
- sugar
- hot water
- butter (optional, for patients who can tolerate it)

## Directions

1. Break one or more rusks into pieces
2. Place them in a bowl with a few spoonfuls of sugar
3. Pour hot water over them
4. Cover with a plate
5. Let sit for a quarter hour before serving
6. If the patient can tolerate butter, add one spoonful to the bowl before pouring the water

title: Açorda de Roscas (Toast Bread Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one or more round loaves of bread
- some spoonfuls of sugar
- hot water
- butter (optional)

## Directions

1. Break one or more round loaves of bread into pieces and place them with some spoonfuls of sugar in a bowl
2. Pour hot water over them
3. Cover with a plate and serve after a quarter hour has passed
4. When the patient can use butter, also add one spoonful to the bowl before pouring the water

title: Bacalhão Fresco, Mero e Badejo (Fresh Cod, Grouper and Cobia)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh cod, grouper or cobia fish, cut into steaks
- salt
- water
- cooked potatoes
- melted butter
- parsley, chopped
- or cream sauce with butter
- parsley
- onion, finely chopped
- whole milk

## Directions

1. Soak the fish steaks in salt water for twelve hours
2. Bring a pan of salted water to a boil and add the fish steaks
3. Boil for ten minutes
4. Remove and serve with cooked potatoes and melted butter mixed with chopped parsley and a little of the cooking water
5. Alternatively, make a cream sauce by melting two spoons of fresh butter, adding a pinch of parsley, a finely chopped onion and a cup of whole milk, boil for some time and serve hot with the fish

title: Bacalhão Secco Assado (Roasted Dried Cod)
body:
## Ingredients

- dried cod, cooked
- two tablespoons butter
- wheat flour
- pepper
- onion leaves
- bitter orange slices
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Skin and bone the cooked cod and cut it into slices.
2. Melt two tablespoons of butter, add some wheat flour, pepper and onion leaves.
3. Place the cod in this sauce.
4. Put the pan on coals or in the oven and let it roast for half an hour, then serve.

title: Bacalhão Secco Assado no Forno (Dried Cod Roasted in the Oven)
body:
## Ingredients

- two spoons of fat
- parsley
- onions
- one lemon, peeled and sliced into rounds
- peppers
- cooked dried cod

## Directions

1. Melt two spoons of fat in a skillet
2. Mix with some parsley, onions, lemon sliced into rounds and some peppers
3. Add the cooked cod to this sauce
4. Place the skillet over coals or in the oven and roast for half an hour
5. Serve

title: Bacalhão Secco com Nata (Dried Cod with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- dried cod, soaked and cooked
- two tablespoons butter
- one tablespoon wheat flour
- one small teaspoon mustard flour
- pepper
- one cup milk
- breadcrumbs, grated
- grated cheese
- butter, melted

## Directions

1. Soak and cook the dried cod, then remove the bones and skin.
2. Melt two tablespoons of butter with one tablespoon of wheat flour, one small teaspoon of mustard flour and some pepper.
3. Add one cup of milk and place the cod in this sauce.
4. Let it boil a little; then pour everything into a dish.
5. Cover with a layer of grated breadcrumbs and another of grated cheese.
6. Drizzle some melted butter on top.
7. Place in the oven and let it brown to serve at the table.

title: Bacalhão Secco com Nata (Dried Cod with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- dried cod, soaked and cooked
- two spoons of butter
- one spoon of wheat flour
- one small spoon of mustard flour
- peppers
- one cup of milk
- bread crumbs
- grated cheese
- melted butter

## Directions

1. After soaking and cooking the cod, remove the bones and skin
2. Melt two spoons of butter with one spoon of wheat flour, one small spoon of mustard flour and some peppers
3. Add one cup of milk
4. Add the cooked cod to this sauce and simmer briefly
5. Pour into a dish, cover with a layer of bread crumbs and another of grated cheese
6. Pour melted butter on top
7. Place in the oven and let it brown

title: Bacalhão Secco Cozido (Boiled Dried Cod)
body:
## Ingredients

- dried cod
- milk
- parsley
- one tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Soak the dried cod in cold water for twenty-four hours.
2. Remove it and place it in a pot with sufficient milk to cover it.
3. Add parsley and one tablespoon of butter.
4. Let it cook for another half hour.
5. Serve with its reduced sauce, if it is still thin.

title: Bacalhão Secco Ensopado (Stewed Dried Cod)
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons fresh butter
- parsley
- onion leaves
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- half tablespoon wheat flour
- one bowl of water
- dried cod, washed
- one glass of bitter orange juice

## Directions

1. Melt two tablespoons of fresh butter.
2. Add some parsley, onion leaves, pepper, grated nutmeg, half tablespoon of wheat flour and one bowl of water.
3. Place the washed cod in this sauce.
4. Let it cook for half an hour.
5. Serve with this same sauce, after adding one glass of bitter orange juice to it.

title: Bacalhão Secco Frito com Ovos (Fried Dried Cod with Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound dried cod, finely chopped
- six eggs
- one tablespoon cassava flour
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onion leaves
- fat

## Directions

1. Chop half a pound of dried cod very finely and beat it with six eggs, one tablespoon of cassava flour, salt, pepper, parsley and chopped onion leaves.
2. Melt some fat in a frying pan.
3. Pour in the beaten mixture.
4. Fry on both sides until golden and serve.

title: Bacalhão Secco Guisado (Braised Dried Cod)
body:
## Ingredients

- dried cod, cooked
- two tablespoons fat
- tomatoes
- one tablespoon cassava flour
- pepper
- parsley
- one cup water
- bitter orange juice

## Directions

1. Cook a piece of dried cod and fry it in two tablespoons of fat with a portion of tomatoes, one tablespoon of cassava flour, pepper and parsley.
2. Add the cod at this time, then one cup of water and the juice of one bitter orange.
3. Let it boil a little and serve.

title: Bacalhão Secco Recheado (Stuffed Dried Cod)
body:
## Ingredients

- dried cod
- two tablespoons butter
- one tablespoon wheat flour
- a handful of carapicú or chopped mushrooms
- one onion
- parsley, chopped
- one cup milk
- two handfuls of bread crumb soaked in hot milk
- butter
- one egg, beaten
- breadcrumbs, grated

## Directions

1. Cook a dried cod and remove all the meat, leaving the backbone and tail intact.
2. Put two tablespoons of butter on the fire with one tablespoon of wheat flour, a handful of chopped carapicú or mushrooms, one onion and some chopped parsley.
3. Moisten everything with one cup of milk.
4. Let it boil a little so the carapicú is cooked and mix in the cod meat.
5. Soak two handfuls of bread crumbs in hot milk and knead to form a paste.
6. On a buttered plate, lay a layer of the kneaded bread, forming a fish shape.
7. Place the backbone on top, cover it with the meat and carapicú, and cover this with the remaining kneaded bread.
8. Brush with beaten egg using a feather and sprinkle generously with grated breadcrumbs.
9. Place in the oven and let it brown to serve.

title: Bacalhão Secco Refogado (Dried Cod Braised)
body:
## Ingredients

- two spoons of butter
- one spoon of cassava flour
- parsley
- onion, chopped
- one good cup of milk
- cooked dried cod, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Melt two spoons of butter and mix with one spoon of cassava flour, parsley, chopped onion and one good cup of milk
2. Boil briefly
3. Add the cooked dried cod that has been finely chopped
4. Simmer a bit more and serve

title: Bacalhão Secco Refogado com Palmitos (Dried Cod Braised with Heart of Palm)
body:
## Ingredients

- dried cod
- water
- parsley
- celery root
- peppers
- onion leaves
- fat
- cassava flour
- heart of palm, chopped
- water

## Directions

1. Soak the cod for twelve hours in cold water
2. Wash in hot water
3. Place on fire with cold water, parsley, celery root, peppers and onion leaves
4. When cooked, remove the bones
5. Braise in two spoons of fat, sprinkle with cassava flour
6. Add chopped heart of palm and half a bottle of water
7. Simmer for another half hour and serve

title: Bacalhão Secco à Cebolada (Dried Cod with Onion Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one dozen onions, sliced into rounds
- two spoons of fat
- peppers
- one cup of vinegar
- cooked dried cod

## Directions

1. Fry the sliced onions in two spoons of fat
2. Sprinkle with peppers and when browned add one cup of vinegar
3. Boil briefly and pour this sauce over the cooked cod on the plate
4. Serve hot

title: Bacalhão, Mero e Badejo Ensopados (Stewed Cod, Grouper and Cobia)
body:
## Ingredients

- whole fish
- water
- salt
- butter
- parsley, chopped
- ground pepper
- grated nutmeg
- one bottle of wine
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Soak the whole fish in salt water for two hours
2. Remove and place in a pan lined with butter and chopped parsley
3. Sprinkle with ground pepper and grated nutmeg
4. Pour a bottle of wine over the top
5. Cover the pan and place in the oven or over coals with fire below and above
6. Let boil for half an hour
7. Moisten with lemon juice before serving

title: Bacalhão, Mero, etc. Ensopados à Bahiana (Cod, Grouper, etc. Stewed Bahian Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish, cut into large steaks
- water
- salt
- pepper
- one onion
- ginger
- two spoons of fat
- one dozen tomatoes
- pimentas (peppers), chopped

## Directions

1. Cut the fish into large steaks and boil for half an hour with water, salt, pepper, one onion and ginger
2. Remove the fish
3. Melt two spoons of fat with a dozen tomatoes and chopped peppers
4. Add the strained and reduced broth
5. Serve the fish with this sauce

title: Badejete, Corocoroca de Escabeche (Small Fish in Escabeche)
body:
## Ingredients

- small fish
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaves
- nutmeg
- hot vinegar
- sweet olive oil

## Directions

1. Wash a portion of fish without removing the scales.
2. Salt them well.
3. Place them in a pot with pepper, bay leaves and nutmeg.
4. Pour hot vinegar on top, enough to cover them, mixed with a quarter part sweet olive oil.
5. After fifteen days, they can be eaten simply or made into a salad.

title: Badejete, Corocoroca Guisados (Stewed Small Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons butter
- one tablespoon wheat flour
- one cup milk
- bay leaves
- pepper
- small fish
- melted butter
- breadcrumbs, grated

## Directions

1. Melt two tablespoons of butter.
2. Mix in one tablespoon of wheat flour, one cup of milk, bay leaves and little pepper.
3. Place the fish in this sauce and boil them.
4. When cooked, remove them, let them cool and pass them through melted butter.
5. Cover them with a good layer of grated bread.
6. Place on a plate between coals, grill them and serve.

title: Badejete, Corocoroca, Assados na Grelha (Grilled Small Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- small fish
- salt
- ground pepper
- melted butter
- grated breadcrumbs
- parsley, chopped

## Directions

1. Take a portion of fish, scale them, clean them and wash them, then dry them with a towel.
2. Sprinkle with salt and ground pepper.
3. Brush with melted butter.
4. Roll them in grated breadcrumbs.
5. Grill them and serve with chopped parsley and melted butter poured on top.

title: Badejete, Corocoroca, Refogados (Sautéed Small Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- small fish
- salt
- pepper
- two tablespoons butter
- half tablespoon wheat flour
- parsley, chopped
- onion, chopped
- two cups red wine

## Directions

1. Clean, scale and open the fish lengthwise.
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
3. Put two tablespoons of butter in a casserole with half tablespoon of wheat flour, chopped parsley and chopped onion.
4. Place the fish in this casserole with two cups of red wine.
5. Boil everything for a quarter hour over a lively fire.
6. Place the fish on a plate, strain the broth and serve.

title: Bagres Fritos (Fried Catfish)
body:
## Ingredients

- two dozen catfish
- salt
- lemon juice
- bitter peppers
- fat for frying
- juice of one sour orange

## Directions

1. Clean two dozen catfish, rub them with salt, lemon juice, and bitter peppers
2. Fry in fat
3. When fried and golden, serve, squeezing sour orange juice over the top

title: Bagres Refogados (Sautéed Catfish)
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two dozen catfish
- salt
- wheat flour
- fat for frying
- one cup of white wine
- a little chopped parsley
- one onion
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Take one or two dozen catfish, open them, remove the entrails, wash well and rub with salt
2. Roll them in wheat flour and fry in fat
3. Once fried on both sides, add one cup of white wine, a little chopped parsley, and one cut onion
4. Let them boil for a quarter of an hour
5. Mix the broth with two egg yolks and serve

title: Bananas Assadas com Cascas (Baked Bananas in Their Skins)
body:
## Ingredients

- bananas
- cornmeal
- lard
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take a quantity of bananas and thrust them into hot coals
2. Remove and peel them
3. Wrap them in cornmeal
4. Fry them in lard and serve with sugar sprinkled on top

title: Bananas Fritas (Fried Bananas)
body:
## Ingredients

- bananas
- water
- butter or lard
- sugar
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Boil a quantity of bananas in water with their skins
2. Peel them, split them in half and fry them in butter or lard
3. Sprinkle with sugar and grated cheese and serve
4. Note: if the bananas are very ripe, they do not need to be boiled; peel them and fry them directly

title: Bananas Guisadas (Stewed Bananas)
body:
## Ingredients

- bananas
- lard
- cornmeal
- egg yolk
- milk or cream

## Directions

1. Open a quantity of bananas lengthwise
2. Fry them in a small amount of lard
3. Add cornmeal, a beaten egg yolk, and a cup of rich milk or cream
4. Let it boil a little and serve

title: Barbo e Crumatá Assados na Grelha (Barb and Crumatá Fish Grilled)
body:
## Ingredients

- barb and crumatá fish
- salt
- pepper
- chopped parsley
- cow butter
- breadcrumbs
- one onion
- cow butter for frying

## Directions

1. Open the fish along the entire length, keeping it secure on one side with the other by the skin of the back
2. Brush with salt, pepper, and chopped parsley
3. Cover with a layer of cow butter and grill over a very moderate fire
4. Place on a plate and serve with breadcrumbs and one onion chopped and fried in cow butter

title: Barbo e Crumatá Ensopados com Chuchús (Barb and Crumatá Fish Stewed with Chuchús)
body:
## Ingredients

- barb and crumatá fish
- water
- salt
- parsley
- pepper
- one cup of vinegar
- two tablespoons fat
- one onion
- fish broth
- two egg yolks
- small portion of peeled and cut chuchús

## Directions

1. Scale and clean the fish, boil in water, salt, parsley, pepper and one cup of vinegar
2. When cooked, remove the fish
3. Fry two tablespoons of fat with one onion, add the fish broth
4. Mix two beaten egg yolks, boil and add a small portion of peeled and cut chuchús
5. Cook and serve around the fish

title: Barbo e Crumatá Estufados (Braised Barb and Crumatá Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- barb and crumatá fish
- red wine
- one onion
- bay leaves
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- two tablespoons butter
- two tablespoons wheat flour

## Directions

1. Scale and wash the fish
2. Boil in red wine with one onion, bay leaves, salt, pepper and parsley
3. When cooked, place the fish on a plate
4. Add to the broth two tablespoons of butter mixed with two tablespoons of wheat flour
5. Let it boil a little longer, pour the sauce over the fish, and serve hot

title: Barbo e Crumatá Refogados (Sautéed Barb and Crumatá Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- one barb and crumatá fish
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- equal parts water and wine in sufficient quantity to cover the fish
- salt
- pepper
- onions
- cloves
- parsley
- thyme

## Directions

1. Clean and wash one fish, but without removing the scales
2. Rub with salt, pepper and vinegar
3. Put in a pan equal parts of water and wine in sufficient quantity to cover the fish
4. Add a little salt, pepper, onions, cloves, parsley and thyme
5. When this broth is boiling, add the fish cut in strips
6. Once cooked, scale it, removing the skin with the scales
7. Serve with pirão made from the broth

title: Barbo e Crumatá Refogados com Ora-pro-nobis (Barb and Crumatá Fish Sautéed with Ora-pro-nobis)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon butter or fat
- one chopped onion
- one tablespoon cassava flour
- some tomatoes
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- one cup of red wine
- one bowl of water
- cleaned and scaled fish
- portion of ora-pro-nobis

## Directions

1. Melt one tablespoon of butter or fat
2. Add one chopped onion, one tablespoon of cassava flour, some tomatoes, parsley, salt, and pepper
3. Add one cup of red wine and one bowl of water
4. When boiling, add the cleaned and scaled fish
5. Once cooked, remove the fish and add a portion of ora-pro-nobis and serve

title: Batata Doce Ensopado (Stewed Sweet Potato)
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet potatoes
- butter
- wheat flour
- white wine
- meat broth
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Peel a portion of sweet potatoes and cut into pieces
2. Boil briefly in salted water
3. Drain and let drip dry
4. Melt a spoonful of butter with a spoonful of wheat flour, a cup of white wine and another of meat broth, a little salt, and nutmeg
5. Place the potatoes in this sauce and let cook over moderate heat
6. When cooked and the broth reduced, serve

title: Batatas Assadas (Roasted Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- lard or butter

## Directions

1. Take a portion of potatoes cooked in their skins
2. Peel and place in a frying pan with a little lard or butter
3. Let fry and turn so they do not burn
4. When of a good color, serve

title: Batatas Doces Fritas (Fried Sweet Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet potatoes
- butter or lard

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of sweet potatoes
2. When half cooked, peel and cut into slices
3. Fry in butter or lard until they turn a good color
4. Serve

title: Batatas Ensopadas em Molho Branco (Potatoes in White Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoon of butter
- one spoon of wheat flour
- two cups of milk
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Melt one spoon of butter
2. Mix one spoon of wheat flour
3. Add two cups of milk, salt and pepper
4. Put the small potatoes in this sauce
5. Let it boil a little and serve

title: Batatas Fritas à Moda (Fried Potatoes in Fashion)
body:
## Ingredients

- some small potatoes
- wheat flour
- salt
- fat or butter

## Directions

1. Cut the small potatoes into thin slices
2. Coat them in wheat flour and salt
3. Fry them in fat or butter
4. When well toasted, serve

title: Batatas Fritas à Moda (Fried Potatoes in the Fashion)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- wheat flour
- salt
- lard or butter

## Directions

1. Cut some potatoes into thin slices
2. Coat in wheat flour and salt
3. Fry in lard or butter
4. When well toasted, serve

title: Batatinhas Cozidas (Boiled Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook the potatoes in water or steam
2. Place in a pot with boiling water
3. When cooked, serve
4. Potatoes can also be cooked peeled

title: Batatinhas em Gemada (Potatoes in Egg Yolk Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- some small potatoes
- one spoon of butter
- one spoon of wheat flour
- salt
- pepper
- one cup of milk
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Peel some small potatoes and boil them in water and salt
2. Cut them into slices
3. On the other hand, melt one spoon of butter with one spoon of wheat flour, salt and pepper
4. Add one cup of milk beaten with two egg yolks to the sauce
5. When it has boiled, serve

title: Batatinhas em Pirão (Potatoes as Mash)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- milk
- salt
- onion

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of peeled potatoes until well cooked
2. Drain the water and mash well until forming a homogeneous paste
3. Mix with a few spoonfuls of milk and salt
4. Place on a plate and pour a chopped onion on top
5. Serve

title: Batatinhas em Pirão Dobrado (Potatoes in Double Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen large potatoes
- water
- salt
- roasted peanuts equal in weight to the potatoes
- grated cheese
- one spoon of sugar

## Directions

1. Peel a dozen large potatoes and boil them in water and salt
2. When cooked, mash them and reduce to a homogeneous pulp
3. Mix this with equal weight of roasted peanuts, grated cheese and one spoon of sugar
4. Put everything on a plate greased with butter and place over coals, covering at the same time with a lid full of coals, and serve

title: Batatinhas Empasteladas (Potato Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- milk
- butter
- egg yolks
- sugar
- wheat flour
- butter for frying

## Directions

1. Take a portion of potatoes, boil, peel, and mash
2. Mix with each pound of mass two spoonfuls of milk, one spoonful of butter, four egg yolks, one spoonful of sugar, and two of wheat flour
3. Knead everything well and form into balls
4. Fry in cow butter and serve

title: Batatinhas Ensopadas (Potatoes in Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- salt
- cinnamon
- nutmeg
- onion

## Directions

1. Peel a portion of potatoes and cook in salted water
2. Cut into slices and arrange on a plate with salt, cinnamon, and nutmeg, and chopped onion
3. Let boil a little more and serve

title: Batatinhas Ensopadas com Toucinho (Potatoes with Bacon)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of fresh bacon cut into small pieces
- one spoon of wheat flour
- salt
- nutmeg
- one bottle of water
- some peeled raw potatoes cut into pieces

## Directions

1. Fry a quarter of fresh bacon cut into small pieces
2. When fried, add one spoon of wheat flour, salt, nutmeg, one bottle of water and some peeled raw potatoes cut into pieces
3. Let it boil and serve

title: Batatinhas Fritas (Fried Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- lard
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel a portion of potatoes and cut into small pieces
2. Fry in lard
3. When well toasted, serve with a little salt

title: Batatinhas Reduzidas em Pirão (Potatoes Reduced to Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- some peeled potatoes
- water
- salt
- cow butter
- pepper
- nutmeg
- one cup of fatty milk

## Directions

1. Cut some peeled potatoes and boil them in water and salt
2. When cooked, drain them, mash them, and put the pulp in a pan with cow butter, salt, pepper, nutmeg and one cup of fatty milk
3. Boil over moderate heat until dry
4. Add one cup of milk to the mass again, boil until dry, repeating this operation five or six times
5. Put on a plate, forming a pyramid, put in the oven to take color, and serve

title: Batatinhas Refogadas (Sautéed Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- lard or fat
- onion
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- wheat flour
- water

## Directions

1. Take a portion of potatoes, peel and cut into pieces
2. Fry in lard with a chopped onion, a little parsley, salt, pepper, and a spoonful of wheat flour
3. Stir and add a bottle of water
4. Let boil and reduce
5. Serve

title: Batatinhas à Parmesã (Potatoes Parmesan)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- butter
- cheese, grated
- sugar

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of potatoes, peel and cut into pieces
2. Butter a casserole well and place the potatoes cut into slices in alternating layers with layers of grated cheese
3. Let bake in the oven
4. Place on a plate, carefully removing with a knife so they come out whole
5. If desired, sprinkle with sugar and serve

title: Beefsteaks (Beef Steaks)
body:
## Ingredients

- sirloin slices
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- parsley

## Directions

1. Take some slices of sirloin the thickness of a finger.
2. Beat this meat so it becomes flatter; then trim the skins around it.
3. Dip it for some minutes in hot butter with salt and pepper.
4. Take it from there to a skillet, which should be over fire, turning the slices after a few minutes.
5. Serve these steaks with the same butter and chopped parsley.

title: Beefsteaks com Batatas e Molho à Chefe de Cozinha (Beef Steaks with Potatoes and Chef's Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef loin
- twelve equal pieces
- butter
- salt
- potatoes
- chef sauce

## Directions

1. Cut a beef loin into twelve equal pieces.
2. Beat these pieces with bread, trimming afterwards.
3. Put a quarter pound of butter to melt with a little salt; coat in it the beefsteaks and place them to roast over moderate fire.
4. When they are quite firm to the touch, remove from fire, place potatoes around the platter and pour over them a chef sauce.

title: Beefsteaks Estufados à Ingleza (Braised Beef Steaks English Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- medium-thick beefsteak
- butter
- wheat flour
- broth
- herbs
- onion
- carrots
- salt
- pepper
- rice flour
- paprika powder

## Directions

1. Fry well on both sides a medium-thick beefsteak.
2. Remove it from the skillet and put in the butter one spoon of wheat flour.
3. Stir until it becomes a beautiful golden color and pour on it a little broth, enough to completely cover the steak, placed again in the skillet.
4. When this sauce begins to boil, add to it a bunch of aromatic herbs, one onion and chopped carrots.
5. Season with salt and pepper and let the meat boil gently for two to three hours.
6. Half an hour before serving, add one spoon of rice flour, a little paprika powder and two small heads of onion chopped and fried separately in butter.

title: Beefsteaks Fritos (Fried Beef Steaks)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- salt
- ground pepper
- wheat flour
- butter
- meat broth
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cut the beef into very fine and small slices and sprinkle with salt and ground pepper and wrap in wheat flour, then fry on both sides in butter which should already be hot on the fire.
2. Remove ten minutes later and fry in the rest of the butter one spoon of wheat flour until it acquires golden color.
3. Add some cups of broth, some mushrooms, salt, seasonings as desired, and finally put in the beefsteaks.
4. Let them boil for half an hour and serve.

title: Beefsteaks ou Bifes Allemães (German-style Beef Steaks)
body:
## Ingredients

- sirloin slices
- butter
- salt
- potatoes
- onion

## Directions

1. Cut a beef sirloin into slices the thickness of two fingers.
2. Beat these slices on a block with a heat-shield until they are quite tender.
3. Then melt a little butter in a skillet over coals.
4. When melted, put into it the beef slices dusted with a little salt and immediately place the skillet over lively fire, turning the slices from time to time when they are fried on one side.
5. At the same time, fry separately in a separate skillet peeled and sliced potatoes with a chopped onion in very little butter until well toasted, which are placed around the beef on the same plate.

title: Beringelas Fritas (Fried Eggplants)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggplants
- cheese
- breadcrumbs
- cooked egg yolks
- butter
- cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cook some eggplants
2. Split them lengthwise so the pieces do not separate
3. Remove the pulp which is mashed with cheese, breadcrumbs, cooked egg yolks, butter, cinnamon and a little sugar
4. Fill the eggplants with this mixture
5. Tie the two halves and fry them in butter
6. Place them on a plate and serve sprinkled with sugar and cinnamon

title: Beringelas Refogadas (Sautéed Eggplants)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggplants
- water
- salt
- wheat flour
- butter
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the eggplants in two parts
2. Boil them in water and salt
3. Drain them and coat them in wheat flour
4. Place them in a casserole with butter and vinegar
5. Put them in the oven and when browned, serve

title: Beringelas Refogadas e Recheadas (Sautéed and Stuffed Eggplants)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggplants
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- egg yolk
- bread
- meat broth
- eggs

## Directions

1. Cook the eggplants
2. Cut them in two parts and remove the pulp
3. Mash this pulp with butter, breadcrumbs, an egg yolk and fill the eggplants with this mixture
4. At the same time, place some slices of bread in a casserole moistened with a cup of meat broth and two beaten eggs
5. Place them over the eggplants
6. Boil for about half an hour and serve

title: Beterraba Refogada (Braised Beets)
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two beets
- water
- one spoon of fat
- two cups of wine
- two egg yolks
- one spoon of sugar
- salt
- garlic

## Directions

1. Boil one or two beets in water
2. Peel and cut into pieces
3. On the other hand, melt one spoon of fat and add the beets
4. Add two cups of wine in which two egg yolks and one spoon of sugar have been dissolved
5. Season with salt and garlic, and having boiled for half an hour, serve

title: Beterrabas em Quibebe (Beets in Quibebe)
body:
## Ingredients

- some beets
- two spoons of butter
- one spoon of wheat flour
- one cup of wine
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Boil some beets and after peeling, grate them
2. On the other hand, melt two spoons of butter with one of wheat flour, one cup of wine, salt, pepper, nutmeg
3. When boiled, add the pulp of the beets
4. Let it dry over moderate heat, stirring, and serve

title: Bifes de Anta (Tapir Steaks)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tapir loin
- salt
- pepper
- a portion of cooked caras, peeled and cut into fine slices
- one onion
- minced parsley
- little butter

## Directions

1. Take a tapir loin and cut it into slices the thickness of two fingers
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper
3. Beat them and layer them one on top of the other in a casserole with little butter, a portion of cooked caras peeled and cut into fine slices, one onion, and minced parsley
4. Let it fry and serve

title: Bifes de Fígado de Porco (Pork Liver Steaks)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork liver
- salt
- garlic
- onion
- parsley
- pepper from India
- wine
- fat

## Directions

1. Cut the liver into slices the thickness of a finger
2. Place these in a vessel with salt, garlic, onion cut into pieces, parsley, pepper from India, and half a glass of wine
3. After soaking in this seasoning for two hours, place them in a pan with some very hot fat
4. Layer the slices one on top of the other
5. Cover the pan hermetically, putting a little cornmeal mush on the edges
6. Let them cook for half an hour over gentle heat

title: Bifes de Fígado de Porco (Pork Liver Steaks)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork liver
- salt
- garlic
- onion
- parsley
- Indian pepper
- half a glass of wine
- lard

## Directions

1. Cut the liver into slices the thickness of a finger and place them in a vessel with salt, garlic, onion cut into pieces, parsley, Indian pepper, and half a glass of wine
2. Leave them in this marinade for two hours
3. Place the slices in a hot pan with a little lard, stacking them one upon another
4. Cover the pan hermetically, putting a little cornmeal mush around the edges
5. Allow them to cook for half an hour over low heat
6. Serve the steaks with the sauce on a bed of cooked fern fronds or prepared carapicú

title: Bifes de Lombo de Porco (Pork Loin Steaks)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pork loins
- butter
- salt
- ground Indian pepper
- meat extract
- Spanish sauce
- meat broth
- pepper sauce
- bread
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut the loins in the manner of small steaks and round them
2. Place them in a frying pan with melted butter, salt, and ground Indian pepper
3. Shortly before serving, sauté the steaks, turn them, and place on a plate
4. In the same frying pan, add a little meat extract, two spoonfuls of Spanish sauce, two of meat broth, and the same amount of pepper sauce
5. Reduce everything together
6. Finally place slices of bread spread with butter and add lemon juice
7. Sauté again with the sauce and serve

title: Bifes de Veado (Venison Steaks)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison meat
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onion
- fat

## Directions

1. Cut the venison meat into slices the thickness of a finger
2. Beat them well and wrap them in salt, pepper, finely chopped parsley and onion
3. Fry some fat in a saucepan in another place and put the steaks in this hot fat
4. Fry them on both sides to take on a beautiful color
5. Serve with side dishes or fried carapicús

title: Bifsteack de Porco (Pork Steaks)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat
- salt
- parsley
- garlic
- Indian pepper
- onions
- tomatoes
- vinegar
- pork lard
- mangarito (a type of vegetable)

## Directions

1. Cut the pork meat into thin slices, beat them slightly, and trim the edges
2. Place them in a vessel with salt, parsley, garlic, Indian pepper, cut onions, tomatoes, and a little vinegar
3. Sauté them in a large frying pan in which a little pork lard has been melted beforehand
4. When placing the slices in the pan, arrange them in layers and cover
5. Once cooked, add some cooked and peeled mangaritos
6. After a few minutes, place on a serving dish and serve

title: Bofes de Vacca (Beef Lungs)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef lungs
- water
- salt
- parsley leaves
- vinegar
- sugar
- nutmeg
- wheat flour
- butter
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Wash and cut the lungs into small pieces
2. Put to cook in water, salt, parsley leaves, vinegar, sugar and nutmeg
3. Half an hour before serving, add a little wheat flour fried in butter and the juice of one lemon
4. The sauce should be abundant

title: Bofes de Vitella com Carapicú (Veal Lungs with Carapicú)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal lungs
- butter
- tapioca flour
- beef broth
- red wine
- cloves
- salt
- pepper
- carapicú
- hot water
- lemon or sour orange juice
- sugar

## Directions

1. After the lungs are well washed, chop them finely and put them in the pot with melted butter.
2. Fry them a little, taking care to stir them.
3. Add to them one spoon of tapioca flour, one cup of broth and another of red wine, cloves, little salt and pepper, and let them cook over coals.
4. Add to them more a portion of carapicú well washed, chosen and scalded in hot water.
5. Let everything finish cooking, and serve, adding to it at this time the juice of a lemon or sour orange and half a spoon of sugar.
6. Eaten with corn porridge.

title: Bofes de Vitella em Polpa (Calf Lungs in Pulp)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf lungs
- water
- salt
- vinegar
- bread crusts, grated
- sugar
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Wash and drain the lungs
2. Cut them very finely
3. Boil in water, salt and a little vinegar
4. When cooked, add grated bread crusts, a spoonful of sugar, and some lemon slices
5. Serve with cornmeal porridge (angú de fubá de moinho)

title: Bofes de Vitella em Polpa (Veal Lungs in Pulp)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal lungs
- water
- salt
- vinegar
- grated bread
- sugar
- lemon

## Directions

1. Wash and let the lungs drain, chop them finely, boil them in water, salt and a little vinegar.
2. When cooked, add to them grated bread crumbs, one spoon of sugar, some slices of lemon, and serve with corn porridge.

title: Bolinhos de Abóbora (Squash Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- squash
- water
- salt
- four eggs per pound of pulp
- four tablespoons of sugar per pound
- three tablespoons of wheat flour per pound
- fennel
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Peel and cut a squash into pieces and cook in water with salt
2. When cooked, pass through a sieve and mash each pound of this pulp with four eggs, four tablespoons of sugar and three tablespoons of wheat flour, fennel and cinnamon to taste
3. Form small balls with this mixture, fry them in fat and dust with sugar and cinnamon
4. Serve as a dessert

title: Bolinhos de Frango (Chicken Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat of one chicken roasted on a spit
- half a pound of ham
- half a pound of veal meat
- a quarter of bacon
- one onion
- one spoon of wheat flour
- four egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- ground clove
- one cup of wine
- three egg yolks
- one cup of milk
- one spoon of sugar
- a little nutmeg

## Directions

1. Take the meat of one chicken roasted on a spit, half a pound of ham, half a pound of veal meat, a quarter of bacon, and one onion.
2. Mince everything well, mix with one spoon of wheat flour, four egg yolks, salt, pepper and ground clove.
3. Make small dumplings and fry them in butter.
4. When almost cooked, add one cup of wine, three egg yolks dissolved in one cup of milk, one spoon of sugar and a little nutmeg.
5. Let it come to a boil and serve.

title: Bolinhos de Manteiga (Butter Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread the size of two closed hands
- hot milk
- three tablespoons of wheat flour
- four egg yolks
- two egg whites
- salt
- half tablespoon of sugar
- one tablespoon of butter

## Directions

1. Soak bread the size of two closed hands in hot milk
2. When softened, remove it and knead with three tablespoons of wheat flour, four egg yolks and two egg whites, little salt, half a tablespoon of sugar and one tablespoon of butter
3. Form small balls with this mixture, boil them in water that is already boiling
4. Serve with meat broth or egg sauce

title: Bolinhos Doces (Sweet Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- one dozen egg yolks
- one pound of sugar
- six tablespoons of raw grated potatoes
- six tablespoons of grated bread
- salt
- grated nutmeg
- fennel
- sugar and cinnamon for dusting

## Directions

1. Beat one dozen egg yolks with one pound of sugar
2. Add six tablespoons of raw grated potatoes and six tablespoons of grated bread, little salt, grated nutmeg, and fennel
3. Knead everything well and form small balls
4. Bake in the oven on banana leaves and dust with sugar and cinnamon
5. Serve as a dessert

title: Bolos com Recheio (Ravioli Pasta Pockets)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight ounces of wheat flour
- three eggs
- salt
- grated nutmeg
- ground cinnamon
- beef or chicken meat
- fat
- pepper
- bay leaf
- tomatoes
- grated cheese
- onion
- butter

## Directions

1. Knead eight ounces of wheat flour with three eggs, little salt, grated nutmeg and ground cinnamon
2. When of good consistency, unroll the dough on a cloth and stretch it until it is as thin as a thread
3. Cut into three-inch square pieces and place one small spoonful of filling in the middle of each square
4. For the filling: finely mince a portion of beef or chicken meat and sauté in hot fat; add salt, pepper, bay leaf and tomatoes
5. Fold the edges and seal so the minced meat stays inside
6. Cut the edges with a pastry wheel and finally boil in a sufficiently large pot for two to three hours
7. When cooked, remove with a skimmer, let drain well, and serve covered with grated cheese and an onion minced and fried in butter

title: Bolos de Carne (Meat Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound of roasted meat
- one onion
- four to five eggs
- wheat flour
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Mince a quarter pound of roasted meat with one onion
2. Knead with wheat flour, four to five eggs and little nutmeg
3. Take small portions of this mixture and boil them gradually in a pan that is on the fire with hot water
4. When the balls are cooked, serve with sauce or melted butter

title: Bolos de Fermento (Yeast Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of wheat flour
- bread yeast
- milk

## Directions

1. Knead the evening before one pound of wheat flour with a little bread yeast and the necessary milk to form a regular dough
2. Place in a warm location and the next day, remove from it some balls the size of a hen's egg and place them in a very large pan that is on the fire with boiling water
3. Let them cook until they float
4. Remove with a skimmer and after draining, serve dusted with grated cheese and melted butter

title: Bolos de Gallinha Fritos (Fried Chicken Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- half a pound of lard
- salt
- peppers
- parsley
- onion leaves
- one garlic clove
- one spoonful of vinegar
- one bottle of water
- two handfuls of bread crumb
- twelve egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil one chicken with half a pound of lard, salt, peppers, parsley, onion leaves, one garlic clove, one spoonful of vinegar and one bottle of water
2. When cooked, remove the bones and chop the meat together with the lard
3. Mix in two handfuls of bread crumb and twelve egg yolks, mixing everything well
4. Place this mixture by spoonfuls to fry in hot fat and serve

title: Bolos de Leite Coalhado (Curdled Milk Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- ten tablespoons of fine corn meal
- two tablespoons of tapioca starch
- three eggs
- curdled milk

## Directions

1. Knead ten tablespoons of fine corn meal, two tablespoons of tapioca starch, three eggs, and curdled milk as much as needed to form a dough of good consistency
2. Form balls the size of an egg, cook in boiling water
3. Serve with sesame seeds or a similar topping

title: Bolos de paca com môlho de sapucaias (Paca Cakes with Sapucaia Nut Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- paca meat separated from bones
- half a pound of bacon
- salt
- peppers
- parsley
- onions
- garlic
- grated ginger
- some cassava flour
- two eggs
- hot fat
- wheat flour
- sugar
- hot water
- selected raisins
- sapucaia nuts

## Directions
