1. Separate the meat from the bones, chop it with half a pound of bacon, salt, peppers, parsley, onions, garlic, grated ginger, a little cassava flour and two eggs
2. Form this mixture into cakes, which are passed in hot fat
3. Remove the cakes, and in the fat add one tablespoon of wheat flour and one of sugar, heating them to brown
4. At this time add two cups of hot water, a portion of selected raisins and the same amount of sapucaia nuts cut up
5. Boil a little, and put the cakes in this sauce
6. Let it boil for one more hour, and serve

title: Bolos de Pão (Bread Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread the size of two closed hands
- milk
- four tablespoons of wheat flour
- four eggs
- half tablespoon of sugar
- salt
- nutmeg
- fat

## Directions

1. Soften in milk a piece of bread the size of two closed hands and knead with four tablespoons of wheat flour, four eggs, half a tablespoon of sugar, a little salt and nutmeg
2. Remove small portions with a spoon and place in boiling water
3. When they float, remove with a skimmer, cut into slices, fry in fat, and serve

title: Bolos de Queijo (Cheese Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- ten tablespoons of wheat flour
- ten tablespoons of grated cheese
- half dozen eggs
- salt
- milk if necessary

## Directions

1. Knead ten tablespoons of wheat flour, the same amount of grated cheese, half a dozen eggs, a little salt, and if necessary a little milk for the dough to be of good consistency
2. Roll to form pastries from which cut pieces one inch long
3. Place in a large pan that has boiling water and a little salt
4. When they float, remove and serve with sesame seeds or some fatty sauce

title: Bolos de Queijo (Cheese Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- ten spoonfuls of wheat flour
- ten spoonfuls of grated cheese
- half a dozen eggs
- a little salt
- a little milk if necessary for the dough to have good consistency

## Directions

1. Knead ten spoonfuls of wheat flour, the same amount of grated cheese, half a dozen eggs, a little salt, and if necessary a little milk for the dough to have good consistency
2. Roll out to form pastes from which pieces of one inch in length are cut
3. Place them in a large pot that has boiling water and a little salt
4. When they float to the surface, remove them and serve with fried foods or some rich sauce

title: Braised Mutton Feet (Pés de carneiro estufados)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton feet
- water
- salt
- pepper
- garlic clove
- onion
- bacon
- carapicu
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil the feet in water, salt, pepper, one garlic clove and one onion cut in pieces until almost cooked
2. Remove the bones and strain the broth through a sieve
3. Return the feet to this broth, whole, with a good piece of bacon, a handful of carapicu and a little vinegar
4. Let boil until the broth is almost dry
5. Place two spoonfuls of the same broth on a platter, cover with dried crackers in powder, placing the mutton feet on top
6. Bake in the oven for half an hour, and serve with pea or lentil puree

title: Braised Mutton Feet (Pés de carneiro refogados)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight mutton feet, cleaned and scalded
- salt
- one spoonful of vinegar
- small piece of bacon
- cabbage

## Directions

1. Place the cleaned and scalded feet in a pan with salt, one spoonful of vinegar and a small piece of bacon
2. Let cook on moderate heat with cabbage cut in four pieces
3. When cooked, separate the broth and reduce to two cups
4. Thicken with two spoonfuls of fine cornmeal and two egg yolks
5. Place the cabbage on a platter around the feet, pour the sauce over them, and serve

title: Braised Mutton Tongue (Lingua de carneiro refogado)
body:
## Ingredients

- one mutton tongue, skin removed, cut in long slices
- butter
- two spoons of wheat flour
- four glasses of white wine
- two egg yolks
- one handful of raisins
- one dozen peeled and chopped almonds
- one spoon of sugar
- salt
- nutmeg
- thyme

## Directions

1. Turn the slices in hot butter and add two spoons of wheat flour, letting them brown.
2. When the tongue is dark colored, add four glasses of white wine in which two egg yolks have been dissolved.
3. Add a handful of raisins, a dozen peeled and chopped almonds, one spoon of sugar, a little salt, nutmeg and thyme.
4. Cook over coals.
5. Serve with vegetables.

title: Braised Turtle or Terrapin (Cágado ou Tartaruga Refogados)
body:
## Ingredients

- turtle or terrapin
- fubá mimoso or wheat flour
- butter
- one onion, chopped
- parsley
- one cup of water or wine
- one cup of vinegar
- salt
- peppers

## Directions

1. Cut off the head and feet of the turtle
2. Boil for five minutes
3. Remove the shells and cut the meat into strips
4. Roll them in fubá mimoso or wheat flour
5. Braise in a little butter with chopped onion and parsley
6. Add one cup of water or wine and one cup of vinegar, salt, and peppers
7. Let boil for a quarter of an hour
8. Serve

title: Brotos d'Espargos Cozidos (Boiled Asparagus Shoots)
body:
## Ingredients

- brotos de espargos (asparagus shoots)
- agua (water)
- sal (salt)
- manteiga (butter)
- pão ralado (bread crumbs)

## Directions

1. Tie the shoots in small bundles and boil them in water and salt
2. Having boiled, remove them and serve them with melted butter fried with bread crumbs

title: Brotos d'Espargos Cozidos (Boiled Asparagus Sprouts)
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus sprouts
- water
- salt
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Tie the sprouts in small bundles
2. Place them to boil in salted water
3. Once boiled, remove and serve with melted butter fried with breadcrumbs

title: Bucho de Vacca com Molho Encorpado com Gemas de Ovos (Beef Tripe with Egg Yolk Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tripe, prepared as in recipe 94
- butter
- mushrooms, sliced
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- three egg yolks
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare the beef tripe as described in recipe 94
2. When cooked, cut into round slices the size of a forty-reis coin
3. Place these pieces on the fire with butter, sliced mushrooms, salt, pepper and nutmeg
4. After they have boiled for a little while, add three egg yolks and a little lemon juice
5. Serve the tripe on a platter with slices of bread

title: Bucho de Vacca com Mostarda (Beef Tripe with Mustard)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tripe, cleaned
- water
- salt
- fat
- dozen small onions
- pepper
- broth
- mustard powder

## Directions

1. With the tripe well cleaned, cut and cooked for two hours in salted water
2. Place on the fire to fry in two spoonfuls of fat together with a dozen small onions, salt, and pepper
3. Add two or three cups of broth
4. Let boil over coals and at serving time, add one spoonful of mustard powder

title: Bucho de Vacca à Francesa (Beef Tripe French Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- half quarter of butter
- dozen small onions
- wheat flour
- half bottle of white wine
- beef tripe, cooked
- seasonings

## Directions

1. Fry in half a quarter of butter a dozen small onions until golden
2. Dust with wheat flour
3. Pour half a bottle of white wine and the cooked tripe cut with necessary seasonings
4. Let boil for half an hour over moderate heat and serve

title: Bucho de Vacca à Milaneza (Beef Tripe Milan Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tripe
- bread slices
- butter
- Flemish cheese
- bread marrow

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook the beef tripe in the same manner as tongues, but without onions
2. When the tripe is well cooked and well reduced, place in a deep platter with a layer of bread slices fried in butter, a layer of beef tripe, a layer of Flemish cheese, and so on
3. Put a little bread marrow on the last layer
4. Let all cook moderately

title: Cabeça de Porco (Pork Head)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork head
- fresh pork meat
- herbs
- nutmeg
- clove
- pepper
- salt
- onions
- chives
- salt pork
- carrots
- bay leaves
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Stuff a pork head with pieces of fresh pork meat after carefully removing all the bones
2. Season with aromatic herbs, nutmeg, clove, pepper, salt, onions, and chopped chives
3. Place everything in a vessel and leave for eight to ten days
4. Remove the head from the vessel and let it drain
5. Sew up the openings where the bones were removed and tie with string so it maintains its shape during cooking
6. Wrap it in white cloth tied at both ends
7. Place it in a frying pan with the head bones, salt pork, carrots, onions, six to eight bay leaves, sprigs of chives, clove, a little salt, and some pieces of pork or other meat
8. Moisten everything with meat broth
9. Cook over low heat for ten hours
10. Remove the head and squeeze it gently to release the liquid it contains, being careful not to lose its shape
11. Trim any protruding points and serve cold

title: Cabeça de Porco do Matto Recheada (Stuffed Wild Pig Head)
body:
## Ingredients

- one wild pig head
- water
- salt
- bell peppers
- bay leaves
- juniper berries
- marjoram
- thyme
- ginger
- meat from the same pig
- one pound bacon
- pepper
- parsley
- onions
- cloves
- bread crusts soaked in milk
- bacon for lining
- one cup wine
- one tablespoon lemon juice

## Directions

1. Boil the wild pig head in water with salt, bell peppers, bay leaves, juniper berries, marjoram, thyme, and ginger.
2. When well cooked, remove all the bones.
3. Make a forcemeat with meat from the same pig, one pound of bacon, pepper, parsley, salt, onions, cloves, and bread crusts moistened with milk.
4. Stuff the head with this filling.
5. Place it in a baking dish lined with bacon and add one cup of wine and one tablespoon of lemon juice.
6. Roast in the oven until it has a good color and serve.

title: Cabeça de Porco à Francesa (Pork Head French Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork head cut to mid-shoulder
- pork meat
- pork tongue
- salt
- saltpeter
- thyme
- bay leaf
- garlic
- clove
- sage
- Indian pepper
- juniper berries
- coriander
- pork ears
- ham slices
- pickled cucumbers
- pork sausage meat
- lard

## Directions

1. Cut the pork head to mid-shoulder and pass it through the flame to remove any remaining bristles
2. Wash it well and scrape with the back of a knife to clean it, removing the bones carefully without damaging the skin
3. Fill the head with meat cut into strips and the tongue, having first spread them with salt and saltpeter
4. Place it in a large earthenware or stone vessel with thyme, bay leaf, garlic, clove, sage, broken Indian pepper, juniper berries, and coriander
5. Cover the terrine with white cloth and place another vessel on top to seal it as well as possible
6. Leave it to cure in salt for eight days, then allow it to drain
7. Garnish it with pork ears, tongues, thin slices of ham, pickled cucumbers, all cut into thin strips that will have remained soaking with the head
8. Alternatively, garnish the pork head with sausage meat, slices of ham, and shavings of hard lard

title: Cabeça de Vitella com Carurú (Calf Head with Caruru)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf head
- water
- salt
- caruru
- cornstarch
- butter
- parsley
- pepper
- onion leaves
- beef broth
- lemon

## Directions

1. Cook a calf head in water and salt
2. Remove the bones and cut the meat into small pieces
3. Take a handful of finely chopped caruru and fry in a saucepan where a sauce has been made with cornstarch fried in butter, parsley, salt, pepper, onion leaves, beef broth, and lemon juice
4. When cooked, serve with cornmeal porridge (angú de fubá de moinho)

title: Cabeça de Vitella com Môlho Pardo (Calf Head with Brown Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf head
- calf feet
- calf heart
- water
- salt
- parsley
- onion
- herbs for seasoning
- toasted flour
- raisins
- bread crusts
- vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Place the head, feet and heart to cook in water with salt, parsley, an onion, and a bundle of herbs
2. When all is cooked, remove the bones from the feet and head, leaving the meat and the nasal bone so the head retains its shape
3. Take the tongue, remove its skin, cut into rounds
4. Do the same with the heart
5. Place the prepared head on the plate, with the feet and tongue rounds, and heart rounds around it
6. Serve with sauce made from the broth, with toasted flour, some raisins, bread crusts, vinegar and sugar

title: Cabeça de Vitella Corada (Roasted Calf Head)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf head
- bacon slices
- spicy sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook the head as explained for boneless calf head
2. Remove from heat, let drain and place in a deep pan over some bacon slices
3. Go to the oven where it is left to roast for one hour
4. Serve with a spicy sauce

title: Cabeça de Vitella Cozida (Boiled Calf Head)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf head
- cold water
- wheat flour
- butter
- salt
- onion leaves
- parsley
- beef broth or water
- lemon

## Directions

1. Clean and depilate the head thoroughly with hot water
2. Place in cold water and let soak for one hour
3. Place on a board and opening from the underside, remove all bones without damaging the outer skin, until reaching the eye sockets and the lower part of the skull
4. Remove likewise the bones of the jaws, nose, and those forming the throat
5. Work to give the muscles their original shape
6. Rub with lemon, wrap in cloth, tie with string
7. Cook in a sauce made with a spoonful of wheat flour, another of butter, salt, onion leaves, parsley, eight cups of beef broth or water, and slices of two lemons
8. After cooking for three hours, remove the cloth and string, and serve thus

title: Cabeça de Vitella Desossada (Boneless Calf Head)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf head
- calf brains
- calf tongue
- calf or chicken meat
- wheat flour
- eggs
- raisins
- beef broth
- white wine
- carrots
- herbs for seasoning
- pepper
- cloves
- lemon

## Directions

1. Clean the head well and open from the underside and remove the bones, except for the upper part of the skull, without damaging the outer skin
2. Cut the brains, eyes, and tongue with some calf or chicken meat and make a mass of these offals with three spoonfuls of wheat flour, three eggs and some raisins
3. Fill the head with this mass, giving it its original shape, sewing the openings with string
4. Cook in beef broth to which half a bottle of white wine, carrots, a bundle of herbs for seasoning, pepper, and two cloves are added
5. When cooked, remove and let the broth thicken by adding a spoonful of wheat flour or cornstarch and the juice of a lemon
6. Remove the string from the head, place on a platter and serve when the sauce has been poured over it

title: Café com Leite (Coffee with Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- ground coffee

## Directions

1. Heat a portion of milk until boiling
2. While boiling, add the ground coffee
3. Stir and strain through a cloth
4. Serve
5. Alternatively, serve strained coffee in one pot and milk in another, allowing each guest to mix according to their taste

title: Café à Turca (Turkish-Style Coffee)
body:
## Ingredients

- ground coffee
- water
- cold water

## Directions

1. Heat the ground coffee with water to a boil
2. After it has boiled, add a few drops of cold water, which will precipitate the grounds
3. The liquid will then be clear as if it had been strained

title: Caldo com Ovos (Egg Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight eggs
- ten spoonfuls of meat broth
- a little nutmeg
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix eight eggs in ten spoonfuls of meat broth, a little nutmeg and salt
2. Place this mixture in an earthenware soup tureen inside a pot with boiling water until it hardens
3. Remove small portions of this mass with a small spoon
4. Place them in hot broth when serving at table

title: Caldo de Azedinhas (Sorrel Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoonful of fat
- four spoonfuls of wheat flour
- a portion of sorrel leaves
- meat broth
- a little sugar
- nutmeg
- toasted bread

## Directions

1. Fry one spoonful of fat until it stops crackling
2. Add four spoonfuls of wheat flour and a portion of washed sorrel leaves
3. Mix and stir with beef broth
4. After adding a little sugar and nutmeg, pour over toasted bread in a tureen

title: Caldo de Azedinhas (Sorrel Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoonful of fat
- four spoonfuls of wheat flour
- a portion of sorrel leaves, washed
- beef stock
- a little sugar
- nutmeg
- toasted bread

## Directions

1. Fry the fat until it stops crackling
2. Add four spoonfuls of wheat flour
3. Add a portion of washed sorrel leaves
4. Stir and mix with beef stock
5. Add a little sugar and nutmeg
6. Pour over toasted bread in a tureen

title: Caldo de Franga para Doente (Chicken Broth for the Sick)
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter of a chicken (not very fat)
- water
- salt
- a parsley leaf
- a slice of bread

## Directions

1. Cook the quarter of a chicken in a bottle of water with little salt and a parsley leaf
2. After one hour, add a slice of bread
3. Remove from the fire and serve to the sick person

title: Caldo de Gallinha (Chicken Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- salt and seasonings
- one spoonful of arrowroot
- two cups of cold water
- two egg yolks
- four spoonfuls of vinegar or juice of lime

## Directions

1. Cook the meat of a chicken as when making soup
2. After adding seasonings to thicken the broth, add one spoonful of arrowroot dissolved in two cups of cold water
3. Add two egg yolks dissolved in four spoonfuls of vinegar or lime juice

title: Caldo de Mocotó (Veal Knuckle Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- one veal knuckle weighing at least two pounds
- four onions
- a handful of watercress
- three bottles of water
- salt
- pepper from India

## Directions

1. Split the veal knuckle lengthwise through bones and meat into four pieces
2. Sauté with four onions cut into rounds and a handful of chopped watercress
3. When ready, add three bottles of water and boil for two hours, replacing water as it evaporates
4. Season with salt and pepper from India
5. Separately, pound three old pigeons and twenty-five live crabs
6. Toast the mixture in a pot with a little butter
7. When the mixture sticks to the bottom of the pot, gradually add the strained veal knuckle broth until it is used up
8. Boil for an hour
9. Strain everything and take one cup in the morning and another in the afternoon

title: Caldo de Vinho Repentino (Sudden Wine Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- white or red wine
- water
- sugar
- butter
- cinnamon
- nutmeg
- dried rusks ground
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil equal parts of white or red wine and water
2. Add a little sugar, butter, cinnamon, nutmeg and ground dried rusks
3. Finally add beaten egg yolks

title: Caldo de Água (Water Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- some slices of well-toasted bread
- some roots and parsley leaves
- hot water
- a little butter
- some egg yolks
- one cup of cold water

## Directions

1. Place some slices of well-toasted bread, some roots and parsley leaves in a pot
2. Pour hot water and a little butter over them and let boil until the bread dissolves
3. Strain this broth and add some egg yolks beaten in one cup of cold water

title: Caldo Reconfortativo (Restorative Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- six large onions
- three carrot roots
- a handful of parsley
- fresh butter
- six ounces of candy sugar
- twenty grains of amber crushed with a piece of bread
- three bottles of water
- one old rooster
- two pounds of beef
- salt
- pepper from India

## Directions

1. Chop the onions, carrot roots, and parsley
2. Sauté them in a pot with a piece of fresh butter
3. When the mixture is ready, add six ounces of candy sugar, twenty grains of crushed amber with a piece of bread, and three bottles of water
4. Boil for an hour, adding water as needed to maintain three bottles of water
5. Separately, kill, pluck, and clean an old rooster, then pound it with its meat and bones in an iron mortar
6. Chop two pounds of beef in the same manner
7. Mix the two meats together, adding salt and pepper from India
8. Place in a pot over active heat, occasionally adding fresh butter so they brown well and do not stick
9. When the meats are browned, gradually add the strained broth from the first preparation, stirring well
10. Let it boil for an hour while adding hot water
11. When complete, strain this broth and take one cup every two hours on the first day, then one good cup morning and evening on following days
12. Usually within eight to ten days, complete recovery is achieved

title: Camarões Cozidos Frios (Cold Boiled Shrimp)
body:
## Ingredients

- portion of shrimp
- salt
- peppers
- chopped parsley
- two onions
- grated nutmeg
- vinegar
- sweet oil
- English mustard flour
- one hard-boiled egg yolk
- one glass of wine

## Directions

1. Cook and shell a portion of shrimp
2. Add salt, peppers, chopped parsley, two onions cut in slices, grated nutmeg, vinegar and sweet oil
3. Mix everything and sprinkle with English mustard flour
4. Place on top a hard-boiled egg yolk dissolved in a glass of wine and serve

title: Camarões em Saladas (Shrimp in Salads)
body:
## Ingredients

- portion of shrimp
- water
- salt
- sweet oil
- pepper
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of shrimp in water and salt
2. Shell them and season with sweet oil, salt, peppers and vinegar
3. Serve cold

title: Camarões Ensopados com Palmito (Shrimp Braised with Palm Heart)
body:
## Ingredients

- palmito
- water
- salt
- portion of shrimp
- two spoons of fat
- one spoon of wheat flour
- chopped parsley
- one cut onion
- pepper
- one cup of white wine
- beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Chop a palmito and boil a little in water and salt
2. Remove the pieces with a skimmer and boil a portion of shrimp in the same water
3. Shell the shrimp after cooling
4. In a plate that can withstand heat, melt two spoons of fat, add one spoon of wheat flour, chopped parsley, a cut onion, pepper and salt
5. Add one cup of white wine and the shrimp with the palmito
6. Stir and pour beaten eggs over everything
7. Put in the oven, let it take good color and serve

title: Camarões Fritos (Fried Shrimp)
body:
## Ingredients

- portion of shrimp
- fat
- onion or parsley
- salt
- pepper
- lemon or sour orange juice

## Directions

1. Shell a portion of shrimp and fry in fat with chopped onion or parsley, salt and plenty of pepper
2. Let them toast well and serve squeezing lemon or sour orange juice over them

title: Camarões Guisados (Stewed Shrimp)
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- salt
- portion of washed shrimp
- butter
- parsley
- scallion leaves
- wheat flour
- one cup of water
- sour orange juice

## Directions

1. Place water and salt in a pan and when boiling add a portion of washed shrimp
2. When cooked, shell them and braise in butter with parsley and scallion leaves, sprinkling with wheat flour
3. Immediately add one cup of water and the juice of a sour orange
4. Let boil a little more and serve

title: Camarões Refogados com Tomates (Shrimp Braised with Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- portion of tomatoes
- parsley
- carrot
- peppers
- salt
- two spoons of fat
- portion of shrimp cooked in water and salt
- two cups of fish broth
- one egg yolk
- juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Fry a portion of tomatoes with parsley, chopped carrot, peppers and salt in two spoons of fat
2. Add a portion of shrimp cooked in water and salt and deshelled
3. Add two cups of fish broth
4. Add one egg yolk dissolved in a glass of lemon juice and serve

title: Cangica Cozida com Amendoim (Corn Porridge Cooked with Peanuts)
body:
## Ingredients

- a portion of cangica
- ground peanuts
- sugar
- water
- one bottle of milk

## Directions

1. Take a portion of cangica
2. Put to cook with ground peanuts, sugar and plenty of water
3. When cooked, add one bottle of milk
4. Boil a little more and serve

title: Cangica Cozida com Leite (Corn Porridge Cooked with Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- a portion of cangica
- milk
- three spoonfuls of cow's butter
- four spoonfuls of grated cheese

## Directions

1. Take a portion of cangica and boil in milk
2. When cooked, add three spoonfuls of cow's butter and four spoonfuls of grated cheese
3. Boil a little more and serve

title: Canhanha, Coió etc., Fritos (Fried Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- milk
- wheat flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean the fish, scald them with milk, roll them in wheat flour and fry them in butter on both sides until a good color.
2. Alternatively, clean them and pass them one by one through two beaten eggs mixed with one tablespoon of butter, then roll them in grated bread before frying.

title: Canhanha, Pescadinha, Coió and Marinha Cozidos (Boiled Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- sea water or well-salted water
- bread crumbs
- butter
- parsley, chopped
- mustard powder
- boiled potatoes

## Directions

1. After cleaning the fish, place a pot with sea water or well-salted water on the fire.
2. When boiling, place the fish in it.
3. Remove them immediately, let them drain and serve with fried breadcrumbs in butter with chopped parsley.
4. A little mustard powder can be added, along with some potatoes boiled in the same water.

title: Capivara Assada (Roasted Capybara)
body:
## Ingredients

- one capybara loin
- aguardente
- cloves
- parsley
- onions
- ginger
- marjoram
- thyme
- salt
- peppers
- juice from land orange or lemon juice

## Directions

1. After disinfecting the capybara loin, place it to soak for six hours in aguardente, cloves, parsley, onions, ginger, marjoram, thyme, salt, and peppers
2. Thread it onto a spit and roast over an open fire, moistening it with juice from land orange
3. When cooked, serve

title: Capivara Assada (Roasted Capybara)
body:
## Ingredients

- capybara loin
- aguardente
- cloves
- parsley
- onions
- ginger
- marjoram
- thyme
- salt
- peppers
- orange juice

## Directions

1. After disinfecting the capybara loin, soak it for six hours in aguardente, cloves, parsley, onions, ginger, marjoram, thyme, salt and peppers
2. Thread it onto a spit and roast over an open fire, basting it with orange juice
3. When cooked, serve it

title: Capivara Assada com Tayoba (Roasted Capybara with Tayoba)
body:
## Ingredients

- capybara meat
- milk serum
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- thyme or serpol
- cloves
- peppers
- tayoba leaves

## Directions

1. After disinfecting the capybara meat, place it to boil for one hour with milk serum, salt, parsley, onion leaves, thyme or serpol, cloves, and peppers
2. Remove it, dry it, and thread it onto a spit
3. Roast over an active fire at first, then moderate
4. Serve with tayoba leaves boiled in water and salt, placed on a plate and covered with the roasted meat

title: Capivara Assada com Tayoba (Roasted Capybara with Tayoba)
body:
## Ingredients

- capybara meat
- whey
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- thyme or serpol
- cloves
- peppers
- tayoba leaves
- breadcrumbs
- melted butter
- lemon and comari pepper sauce

## Directions

1. After disinfecting the capybara meat, bring it to a boil for one hour with whey, salt, parsley, onion leaves, thyme or serpol, cloves, and peppers
2. Remove and dry it, then thread it onto a spit
3. Roast over active fire at first, then moderate fire toward the end
4. Serve with tayoba leaves boiled in water and salt, placed on a plate, covered with breadcrumbs and melted butter, and serve with a sauce of lemon and comari peppers

title: Capivara Ensopada com Serralha (Capybara Stew with Sow Thistle)
body:
## Ingredients

- capybara meat
- gordura
- water
- aguardente
- salt
- parsley
- onion
- cloves
- pepper
- sow thistle

## Directions

1. Cut a piece of capybara meat, scald it, leaving it to cool in the same water
2. Remove and fry it in two spoonfuls of fat with a bowl of water, a glass of aguardente, salt, parsley, onion, cloves, and pepper
3. Leave it to boil until cooked
4. Serve with sow thistle finely chopped, scalded, and sautéed in a little fat after adding some water and salt

title: Capivara Guisada com Almeirões (Capybara Stewed with Chicory)
body:
## Ingredients

- capybara meat
- water
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onion leaves
- ginger
- cloves
- gordura
- onion
- lemon juice
- chicory

## Directions

1. Take a piece of capybara meat and boil it in water with salt, pepper, parsley, onion leaves, ginger, and cloves
2. When cooked, sauté it in two spoonfuls of fat with one chopped onion
3. Add a glass of lemon juice and a cup of water
4. Leave it to boil for another half hour
5. Serve over a plate of chicory that has been scalded, chopped, and sautéed in one spoonful of fat with a cup of water and salt; leave to boil a little longer and serve

title: Capivara Guisada com Carapicús (Capybara Stewed with Carapicús)
body:
## Ingredients

- capybara meat
- water
- salt
- peppers
- cloves
- thyme
- marjoram
- gordura
- carapicús
- vinegar
- water

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of capybara meat cut into pieces in water, salt, peppers, cloves, thyme and marjoram
2. When cooked, remove and sauté with one spoonful of fat, a portion of carapicús, salt, peppers, a glass of vinegar and a cup of water
3. Leave to cook over low fire for one hour and serve

title: Capivara Guisada com Raiz de Inhame (Capybara Stewed with Yam Root)
body:
## Ingredients

- capybara meat
- yam root
- water
- salt
- gordura
- onion
- lemon or orange juice
- egg yolks
- comari peppers
- parsley

## Directions

1. Put a piece of capybara meat and one or two yam roots in a pan with plenty of water and salt
2. Leave it to boil
3. When everything is cooked, remove and cut into pieces, both the meat and the yam
4. Then sauté them in a little fat and one chopped onion
5. Add a glass of lemon or orange juice and two egg yolks dissolved in a cup of water, comari peppers and parsley
6. After boiling for half an hour, serve

title: Capivara Guisada com Vagens (Capybara Stewed with Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- capybara meat
- water
- salt
- cornmeal
- gordura
- beans
- marjoram
- parsley
- onion leaves

## Directions

1. Boil a piece of capybara meat in water and salt
2. When cooked, cut it into pieces
3. In another pan, put one spoonful of cornmeal and another of fat, add the meat and fry, stirring
4. Add two cups of water, a portion of cut beans, salt, marjoram, parsley, and onion leaves
5. Boil until everything is cooked and serve

title: Capão Assado com Moranga (Capon Roasted with Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- one capon
- a handful of carapicús
- one onion
- parsley
- breadcrumbs
- milk
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- four egg yolks
- bacon strips
- squash with skin
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Fill the capon with a handful of carapicús, one onion, and parsley all finely chopped, mixed with a handful of breadcrumbs softened in milk and mashed with two spoons of butter, salt, pepper, and four egg yolks
2. Place the prepared capon in a pan on bacon strips with squash pieces with skin around it
3. Add two cups of meat broth and cover with other bacon strips
4. Let roast until cooked and serve with squash slices around it

title: Capão Assado com Pinhões (Capon Roasted with Pine Nuts)
body:
## Ingredients

- one capon
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- garlic
- cloves of India
- parsley
- onion leaves
- half pound of ham
- half pound of lamb meat
- three cooked eggs

## Directions

1. Rub the capon with salt and pepper, wrap it in a cloth dampened with vinegar
2. Add a clove of garlic mashed, some cloves of India, chopped parsley, and onion leaves
3. After four hours, remove it and fill with half pound of ham, half pound of lamb meat, three cooked eggs

title: Capão Assado com Pinhões (Roasted Capon with Pine Nuts)
body:
## Ingredients

- one capon
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- one clove of garlic, crushed
- cloves
- parsley, chopped
- onion leaves
- half pound of ham
- half pound of mutton meat
- three cooked eggs
- one onion
- pine nuts or chestnuts
- butter
- paper

## Directions

1. Rub the capon with salt and pepper, wrap it in a cloth moistened with vinegar
2. Add to it a crushed clove of garlic, some cloves, chopped parsley and onion leaves; let it sit for four hours
3. Remove the cloth and fill the capon with a mixture of half pound of ham, half pound of mutton meat, three cooked eggs, one onion, salt and pepper, all finely chopped
4. Place on a spit and roast wrapped in paper, anointed with butter
5. Meanwhile, cook a portion of pine nuts or chestnuts in water; once cooked, shell them
6. Serve the capon on a plate with the pine nuts or chestnuts arranged around it

title: Capão Assado no Espeto (Capon Roasted on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- one capon
- bacon, thin slices
- butter
- paper

## Directions

1. After the capon is killed and plucked in hot water, pass it over a smokeless flame to remove the remaining feathers
2. Open and clean the capon
3. Thread it on a spit
4. Cover the breast with a thin slice of bacon, and the entire body with paper anointed in butter
5. When roasted, remove the paper and bacon, and serve

title: Capão Assado no Espeto com Capa (Capon Roasted on Spit with Coating)
body:
## Ingredients

- one capon
- breadcrumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Prepare as capon roasted on spit
2. When roasted, remove the paper and bacon
3. Cover with breadcrumb crust and let roast a little more, moistening with melted butter until it is a good color, and serve with salads or some fruit preserve without pastry

title: Capão Assado no Forno (Capon Roasted in the Oven)
body:
## Ingredients

- one capon
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onion leaves
- carapicús
- mangaritos
- breadcrumbs
- bacon
- wine

## Directions

1. Fry the whole capon in butter, salt, pepper, parsley and onion leaves, turning it from time to time so it does not burn
2. Fill it with cooked and chopped carapicús and mangaritos, some grated bread, a spoon of butter, salt and pepper
3. Place in a pan, wrapped in bacon, in the oven with a glass of wine
4. Let it cook, then serve

title: Capão Ensopado (Braised Capon)
body:
## Ingredients

- one capon
- salt
- lemon juice
- fat
- four or five cups of meat broth
- pepper
- garlic
- onion
- chive leaves
- the giblets of the capon
- pieces of roasted beef or mutton
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Take the whole capon and rub it inside and out with salt and lemon juice
2. Place it in a pan with some fat, four or five cups of broth, salt, pepper, garlic, onion, chive leaves, the capon's giblets, and some pieces of roasted beef or mutton
3. When cooked, strain the sauce (if desired, thicken it with egg yolks) and serve with the sauce

title: Capão Ensopado com Arroz (Capon Braised with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- one capon, cut into pieces
- four spoons of fat
- half plate of chosen and washed rice
- one bottle of hot water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Brown the capon pieces in four spoons of hot fat
2. Remove them and heat the fat well, then add half plate of chosen and washed rice
3. Stir until all the rice is well coated in fat and heated
4. Add one bottle of hot water, salt, parsley, onion leaves and the capon pieces
5. Let everything boil well once, then let it dry near the fire
6. To serve, place the rice on a plate with the capon pieces on top, and cover everything with grated cheese

title: Capão Ensopado com Cará (Capon Braised with Cará Root)
body:
## Ingredients

- one capon, cut into pieces
- four spoons of hot fat
- one bottle of water
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- year-round onion leaves
- one good spoon of fine cornmeal
- one large cará, peeled and cut into pieces

## Directions

1. Brown the capon pieces in four spoons of hot fat
2. When almost fried, add one bottle of water, salt, pepper, parsley, year-round onion leaves, one good spoon of fine cornmeal, and one large cará peeled and cut into pieces
3. Let them boil until cooked, and serve in this state

title: Capão Ensopado com Nabos (Capon Braised with Turnips)
body:
## Ingredients

- one capon, cut into pieces
- three spoons of cow butter
- one bowl of water
- one spoon of wheat flour
- salt
- parsley
- onion
- one clove
- turnips, peeled and sliced thin
- two egg yolks
- one cup of white wine

## Directions

1. Cut the capon into pieces and fry them in three spoons of cow butter
2. Add one bowl of water, one spoon of wheat flour, salt, parsley, onion, one clove, and a portion of peeled turnips sliced thinly
3. Let cook, and finally add two egg yolks dissolved in one cup of white wine
4. Serve when cooked

title: Capão Ensopado com Ora-pro-nobis ou Quiabos (Capon Braised with Ora-pro-nobis or Okra)
body:
## Ingredients

- capon, cut into pieces
- two spoons of fat
- one bowl of water
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- parsley
- onion leaves
- one spoon of polvilho (cassava starch) kneaded with two egg yolks
- two cups of milk
- one handful of ora-pro-nobis or two dozen okras
- cornmeal angú

## Directions

1. Brown the capon pieces in two spoons of fat
2. Once half-cooked, add one bowl of water, salt, pepper, cloves, parsley, onion leaves and one spoon of polvilho kneaded with two egg yolks dissolved in two cups of milk
3. Let cook, and at the end add one handful of ora-pro-nobis or two dozen okras cut up
4. Serve with cornmeal angú

title: Capão Estufado com Gerumbeba (Braised Capon with Prickly Pear)
body:
## Ingredients

- one capon
- fruits of the prickly pear cactus
- half pound of chopped bacon
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- three cups of white wine
- one cup of water

## Directions

1. Fill the capon with the fruits of the prickly pear cactus, which have been previously passed through ashes or coals to clean them of their thorns
2. Peel the fruits and cut them up, and put them in a pan with half pound of chopped bacon, salt, pepper, nutmeg, three cups of white wine and one cup of water
3. Cover the pan with a seal and simmer over moderate fire until the fruits are cooked
4. Remove the fat from the sauce and serve with it

title: Capão Frito em Azeite (Capon Fried in Olive Oil)
body:
## Ingredients

- capon, cut into pieces
- sweet olive oil
- salt
- parsley
- pepper
- garlic
- onion
- juice of bitter orange
- butter
- spicy sauce or salad

## Directions

1. Cut the capon into pieces and soak them for four to six hours in sweet olive oil, salt, parsley, pepper, garlic, onion and juice of bitter orange
2. Melt one spoon of butter, and when well heated, add the capon pieces, frying them on both sides
3. Serve with some spicy sauce or salad

title: Capão Frito à Italiana (Capon Fried Italian Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- capon, cut into pieces
- vinegar
- sweet olive oil
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- parsley, chopped
- half clove of garlic
- clove
- grated cheese
- butter
- cold sauce

## Directions

1. Soak the capon pieces in vinegar, sweet olive oil, salt, pepper, onion, chopped parsley, half clove of garlic and clove
2. Roll them in grated cheese
3. Fry them in butter, serving with a cold sauce

title: Capão Recheado com Carapicús (Capon Stuffed with Carapicús)
body:
## Ingredients

- one capon
- one handful of carapicús
- half pound of ham
- one quarter of bacon
- the giblets of the capon
- one onion
- salt
- parsley
- pepper
- four cooked eggs
- one glass of wine

## Directions

1. Make a stuffing of one handful of carapicús, half pound of ham, one quarter of bacon, the capon's giblets, one onion, salt, parsley, pepper and four cooked eggs
2. Cook everything together with one glass of wine, stirring so it does not stick
3. When cooked, fill the capon's cavity and crop, lifting the skin between the neck and breast for this purpose
4. Place it in the oven in a pan over bacon slices, and bring to the table

title: Capão Refogado com Renovos de Samambaia (Capon Sautéed with Fern Fronds)
body:
## Ingredients

- one capon
- water
- salt
- pepper
- onion leaves
- marjoram
- fern fronds
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cut the capon into pieces and cook in water with salt, pepper, onion leaves, and marjoram
2. When cooked, remove and boil the fern fronds tied in bundles in this broth
3. Fry the capon pieces coated in breadcrumbs in butter
4. Remove the fern fronds, untie them, and place on a plate covered with breadcrumbs
5. Put the fried capon pieces on top and pour over all the butter in which the capon pieces were fried and serve

title: Capão Refogado e Recheiado (Braised and Stuffed Capon)
body:
## Ingredients

- one handful of bread crumbs, softened in milk
- one quarter of cow butter
- one quarter of raisins and carapicús, blanched and finely chopped
- one spoon of sugar
- nutmeg
- the giblets of the capon, blanched and finely chopped
- four egg yolks
- bacon, sliced
- grated breadcrumbs
- sweet preserve

## Directions

1. Knead together a handful of bread crumbs softened in milk, one quarter cow butter, one quarter raisins and blanched carapicús finely chopped, one spoon of sugar, nutmeg, the capon's giblets blanched and finely chopped, and four egg yolks
2. Fill the capon with this mixture and roast it in the oven in a pan lined with bacon slices
3. When cooked, roll it in grated breadcrumbs and return it to the oven to brown
4. Serve with some sweet preserve

title: Caramujos de Vinhas Cozidos (Boiled Snails)
body:
## Ingredients

- snails
- butter
- mushrooms
- manioc flour
- parsley
- garlic
- salt
- peppers
- white wine
- water

## Directions

1. Melt two tablespoons of butter
2. Mix in a handful of chopped mushrooms, one tablespoon of manioc flour, chopped parsley, one garlic clove, salt and peppers
3. Add one cup of white wine and another of water
4. Boil, add the snails and serve hot

title: Caramujos Recheados (Stuffed Snails)
body:
## Ingredients

- snails
- white wine
- cinnamon
- pepper
- clove
- nutmeg
- fish meat
- bread crumbs
- milk
- hard-boiled egg yolks
- onion
- parsley
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Remove snails from shells and boil for a moment in white wine with cinnamon, pepper, clove and grated nutmeg
2. Chop with an equal volume of cooked fish meat and knead with the same amount of bread crumbs softened in hot milk, two hard-boiled egg yolks for each dozen snails, one onion, a little parsley, all finely chopped and salt
3. Mix with one or two tablespoons of butter and fill the snail shells with this mixture
4. Place on a plate, heat in the oven and serve

title: Caramujos Refogados (Sautéed Snails)
body:
## Ingredients

- snails
- butter
- bread crumbs
- pepper
- nutmeg
- water
- egg yolks
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Melt two tablespoons of butter
2. Mix in a handful of bread crumbs, pepper, grated nutmeg and add the snails
3. Stir and add one cup of water and two egg yolks diluted in one glass of lemon juice
4. After boiling once more, serve

title: Caranguejos e Sirís com Leite (Crabs and Swimming Crabs with Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- crabs
- water
- salt
- two spoons of butter
- one spoon of wheat flour
- one cup of milk
- three egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut the legs of the crabs, leaving only the two claws
2. Cut also the tip of the tail and the part anterior to the head and cook in water and salt
3. Melt two spoons of butter and mix with one spoon of wheat flour
4. Immediately add one cup of milk and a little salt
5. When about to remove from fire, add three egg yolks
6. Place the crabs on a plate and pour the sauce over them and serve

title: Caranguejos e Sirís Cozidos (Boiled Crabs and Swimming Crabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- portion of crabs
- one bottle of white wine
- piece of butter
- three cut onions
- bay leaves
- salt
- peppers

## Directions

1. After washing well a portion of crabs, place in a casserole with one bottle of white wine, a piece of butter, three cut onions, bay leaves, salt and peppers
2. Let boil for half an hour and serve after draining and cooling

title: Caranguejos e Sirís Refogados (Crabs and Swimming Crabs Braised)
body:
## Ingredients

- portion of crabs
- water
- salt
- one spoon of butter
- one spoon of manioc flour
- one cup of white wine
- chopped parsley
- pepper
- crab meat

## Directions

1. Place a portion of crabs in a pan with water and salt
2. When cooked, shell them
3. Melt one spoon of butter and add one spoon of manioc flour, one cup of white wine, salt, chopped parsley and pepper
4. Put everything to boil, add the crab meat and serve after boiling a little more

title: Carapicús com Ovos (Mushrooms with Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- carapicús (mushrooms)
- agua (water)
- sal (salt)
- ovos (eggs)
- gordura (fat)
- vinho branco (white wine)
- pimenta (pepper)
- cebola (onion)

## Directions

1. Take a portion of carapicús boiled in water and salt
2. Drain them, mixing them with four eggs
3. Place them in a pan with fat to fry
4. Mix them and when the mixture begins to coagulate, add a cup of white wine, salt, pepper, and a diced onion
5. Let it boil for half an hour and serve

title: Carapicús e Cogumelos Refogados (Sautéed Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms (carapicús)
- fat
- water
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- onion leaves
- parsley

## Directions

1. Take a portion of mushrooms (carapicús), soak them, open them to clean them well of insects, and wash them well
2. Place them in two spoonfuls of very hot fat
3. Add half a bottle of water, one spoonful of vinegar, salt, pepper, onion leaves, and parsley
4. Boil for half an hour over moderate fire
5. Serve

title: Carapicús Guisados com Batatas (Mushrooms Braised with Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- carapicús (mushrooms)
- agua (water)
- sal (salt)
- gordura (fat)
- sumo de limão (lemon juice)
- batatas or mangaritos (potatoes or taro)
- farinha de trigo (wheat flour)
- pimenta (pepper)
- salsa (parsley)

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of carapicús in water and salt
2. Remove them with a skimming spoon and place them in a casserole with fat and a cup of lemon juice
3. Mix and add a portion of peeled and sliced potatoes or taro
4. Fry them in fat to which a little wheat flour has been added and two cups of water
5. Place over the fire, adding salt, pepper and chopped parsley
6. Having boiled over moderate heat, serve them

title: Carapicús Guisados com Pão (Mushrooms Braised with Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- manteiga (butter)
- pão ralado (bread crumbs)
- carapicús (mushrooms)
- agua (water)
- sal (salt)
- vinagre (vinegar)
- pimenta (pepper)

## Directions

1. Melt two spoons of butter
2. Mix them with a portion of bread crumbs and add a portion of carapicús cooked in water and salt, pepper, and a cup of vinegar
3. Mix well, let it boil still over moderate heat, and serve

title: Carapicús Guisados com Queijo (Mushrooms Braised with Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- carapicús (mushrooms)
- agua (water)
- sal (salt)
- vinagre (vinegar)
- gordura (fat)
- pimenta moida (ground pepper)
- cebola (onion)
- salsa (parsley)
- queijo ralado (grated cheese)
- aguardente or cognac (brandy or cognac)
- assucar (sugar)
- noz moscada (nutmeg)

## Directions

1. Braise a portion of carapicús boiled in water, salt and vinegar, and let them drain in two spoons of fat with ground pepper, onion and chopped parsley
2. Add a portion of grated cheese, mix everything with a spoon, pouring in a cup of brandy or better yet cognac
3. Let it boil still over coals and pour onto a plate
4. Sprinkle with sugar and nutmeg, and serve

title: Carapicús or Cogumelos Ensopados com Nata (Mushrooms Stewed with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- carapicús (mushrooms)
- leite (milk)
- cebola (onion)
- pimenta (pepper)
- sal (salt)
- manteiga (butter)
- farinha de trigo (wheat flour)
- nata (cream)
- ovo (egg)

## Directions

1. Blanch a portion of carapicús (mushrooms), let them cool and drain them
2. In a pot, bring milk to a simmer with a diced onion, pepper, and salt
3. Once cooked, add a spoon of butter mixed with a spoon of wheat flour, and a cup of cream with a beaten egg mixed in
4. Let them simmer a little longer and serve

title: Carapicús or Cogumelos Fritos (Fried Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- carapicús (mushrooms)
- vinho (wine)
- vinagre (vinegar)
- sal (salt)
- pimenta (pepper)
- gordura (fat)

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of selected and cleaned carapicús (mushrooms)
2. Give them a few boilings in a little wine and vinegar, adding salt and pepper
3. Once cooked, remove and drain them
4. Place them in a pan containing hot fat
5. Mix them a little, and adding the reduced broth, serve them

title: Carapicús ou Cogumelos Ensopados com Nata (Mushrooms Stewed with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms (carapicús)
- milk
- one onion
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- wheat flour
- one cup of cream
- one egg

## Directions

1. Blanch a portion of mushrooms (carapicús), allow them to cool and drain
2. Then place them to boil in milk, one chopped onion, pepper, and salt
3. When cooked, add one spoonful of butter mixed with one spoonful of wheat flour, and one cup of cream with one egg dissolved inside
4. Boil a little and serve

title: Carapicús ou Cogumelos Fritos (Fried Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms (carapicús)
- wine
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- fat

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of selected and cleaned mushrooms (carapicús)
2. Give them some boils in a little wine and vinegar, adding salt and pepper
3. When cooked, remove and drain them, placing them in a pan containing hot fat

title: Carapicús Refogados com Vinho (Mushrooms Sautéed with Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms (carapicús)
- butter
- wheat flour
- juice of one lemon
- half a bottle of red wine
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Select and wash a portion of mushrooms (carapicús)
2. Melt two spoonfuls of butter, adding to them one spoonful of wheat flour, one glass of lemon juice, half a bottle of red wine, salt, and pepper
3. Add the mushrooms afterward, and when they have boiled a little, serve

title: Caratinga Cozida (Cooked Caratinga)
body:
## Ingredients

- one caratinga
- water
- salt
- one spoon of bread
- milk
- peppers

## Directions

1. Cook a caratinga in water and salt until well cooked
2. Peel and mash it with one spoon of bread, mixing with milk and a little peppers and serve. Also it can be roasted in the coals.

title: Carne Assada no Espeto (Roasted Meat on Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- milk
- bacon
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- spicy sauce or tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Soak the meat for two days in curdled milk.
2. Lard it and put it to roast on the spit.
3. Brush melted butter on it frequently.
4. When removing from the fire, sprinkle ground breadcrumbs on top.
5. Serve with spicy sauce or tomato sauce.

title: Carne Assada Requentada (Reheated Roast Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- roast beef
- butter
- paper

## Directions

1. Wrap the roast beef in paper greased with butter
2. Pierce it on the spit
3. Heat it through
4. Serve covered with chopped herbs

title: Carne Assada Requentada (Reheated Roasted Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted meat
- butter
- paper
- herbs

## Directions

1. Wrap the roasted meat in buttered paper.
2. Put it on the spit and reheat it.
3. The meat reheated this way is as tender as on the first day.
4. When the pieces are very small, warm them on a grill to serve at the table covered with chopped herbs.

title: Carne Cozida Ensopada (Beef Cooked in Gravy)
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley root
- onion
- butter
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- beef broth
- cooked beef
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut into small pieces a parsley root and one onion, and let them fry in half a quarter of butter over low heat, without browning
2. Add a good spoon of wheat flour
3. Season as desired with salt, pepper, and nutmeg
4. Mix in a cup of beef broth, then leave to boil for ten minutes
5. Cut the cooked beef into small pieces and let them boil a little in the sauce
6. Thicken with two beaten egg yolks

title: Carne Cozida à Caseira (Home-Style Cooked Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beef
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chives
- beef broth
- bread crumb
- butter
- vinegar

## Directions

1. After the beef has been cooked and cooled, cut it into slices
2. Arrange the slices neatly on a metal plate
3. Dust with salt, pepper, parsley, and chopped chives
4. Pour over a cup of beef broth
5. Sprinkle on top breadcrumb fried in butter and a little vinegar
6. Place near the fire for twenty minutes
7. Then remove to serve at the table

title: Carne de Anta Refogada (Braised Tapir Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of fresh tapir meat
- water
- fat
- one chopped onion
- two tablespoons of wheat flour
- two cups of water
- salt
- pepper
- chopped parsley
- six tomatoes
- juice of one lemon
- slices of another peeled lemon
- one tablespoon of sugar

## Directions

1. Take a piece of fresh tapir meat, scald it and let it cool in the same water
2. Remove it and cut it into small pieces
3. Braise it with a little fat, one chopped onion, and dust with two tablespoons of wheat flour
4. Add two cups of water, salt, pepper, chopped parsley, six tomatoes, the juice of one lemon, slices of another peeled lemon, and one tablespoon of sugar
5. Let it boil for one hour over moderate heat and serve

title: Carne de Anta Sepultada à Moda de Caçador (Buried Tapir Meat Hunter's Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- a portion of tapir meat
- salt
- banana or caeté leaves
- peppers comaris
- lemon juice
- the juice that runs from the meat

## Directions

1. Take a portion of tapir meat and rub it with salt
2. Wrap it in banana or caeté leaves
3. Introduce the meat thus prepared into a hole made in the earth in which enough wood has been burned to heat it
4. Cover with earth, lighting another portion of wood on top
5. After two to three hours, remove the meat which will have acquired an excellent taste
6. Serve with a sauce made with comaris peppers, lemon juice, and the juice that runs from the meat when it is cut

title: Carne de Carneiro com Alcaparras (Mutton with Capers)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton meat, cut into pieces
- water
- butter
- bay leaves
- pepper from India
- small onions
- salt
- sardines, chopped
- capers
- lemon slices
- breadcrumb

## Directions

1. Cut the mutton meat into pieces and simmer in little water, butter, bay leaves, pepper from India, small onions and salt
2. A short time before it is cooked, add some chopped sardines, capers, slices of lemon and grated breadcrumb, and serve

title: Carne de Carneiro com Alcaparras (Mutton with Capers)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton, cut into pieces
- little water
- butter
- bay leaves
- peppercorns
- chives
- salt
- sardines, chopped
- capers
- lemon, sliced
- bread crusts, grated

## Directions

1. Cut the mutton into pieces and boil in little water with butter, bay leaves, peppercorns, chives and salt
2. A short time before the meat is cooked, add some chopped sardines, capers, sliced lemon and grated bread crusts
3. Serve

title: Carne de Carneiro com Maracujás (Mutton with Passion Fruit)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton meat
- water
- salt
- pulp of six to eight passion fruits

## Directions

1. Simmer the mutton meat in water and salt until cooked
2. At this time, simmer the pulp of six to eight passion fruits in a little

title: Carne de Carneiro com Maracujás (Mutton with Passion Fruit)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton
- water
- salt
- six to eight passion fruits, pulp
- butter
- cream
- bread crusts, grated
- sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the mutton in water and salt until cooked
2. Cook the pulp of six to eight passion fruits in a little butter with cream, grated bread crusts and sugar
3. Serve together with the meat

title: Carne de Carneiro com Pepinos (Mutton with Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton, cut into small pieces
- water
- salt
- cucumbers, peeled, deseeded and cut lengthwise
- vinegar
- sugar, half a spoon
- wheat flour, fried

## Directions

1. Cut the mutton into small pieces and cook in water with salt
2. Finally add peeled, deseeded and lengthwise-cut cucumbers
3. When everything is cooked, add a little vinegar, half a spoon of sugar and a little fried wheat flour

title: Carne de Carneiro Cozida de Olha (Boiled Mutton Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton with bones
- water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- small cabbage, split
- cassava root, split
- sweet potatoes
- pumpkin pieces
- lemon juice
- six comarí peppers, crushed
- mustard powder

## Directions

1. Take a piece of mutton with bones, soak in cold water for some time, then place on fire with cold water, salt, parsley, onion leaves, a small split cabbage, split cassava root, sweet potatoes and pumpkin pieces
2. Let everything boil until well cooked
3. Strain the broth, remove the fat and reduce to four cups
4. Mix in lemon juice, six crushed comarí peppers and mustard powder
5. Serve this sauce together with the stewed meat

title: Carne de Carneiro Dourada com Torradas (Golden Mutton with Toast)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds mutton
- half pound bacon interspersed with meat
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- onion
- parsley
- six egg yolks, beaten

## Directions

1. Take two pounds mutton and half pound bacon interspersed with meat, salt, pepper, nutmeg, onion and parsley; chop everything very fine and sauté in a little fat with one cup of wine
2. When cooked, pour this onto slices of bread placed on a plate
3. Cover everything with six beaten egg yolks
4. Place in the oven to finish cooking

title: Carne de Carneiro Encapada (Mutton in Batter Coat)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton, cut into slices
- salt
- pepper
- four spoons wheat flour
- three eggs, beaten
- one cup white wine
- four spoons sugar
- sugar and cinnamon for coating

## Directions

1. Cut the mutton into slices, season with salt and pepper, and grill on both sides
2. Make a thin batter of four spoons wheat flour, three beaten eggs, one cup white wine and four spoons sugar
3. Pour a third of this batter over the meat, and when it is grilled, add the second third of the batter, and at the end the final third (this is done so the batter cooks in layers)
4. Cover with sugar and cinnamon and serve

title: Carne de Carneiro Ensopada Parda (Brown Stewed Mutton)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton, cut into pieces
- fat, one spoon
- vinegar, one cup
- salt
- parsley
- peppercorns
- garlic, one clove
- onion leaves
- wheat flour
- sugar, burned
- two cups red wine
- sugar, one spoon
- dozen almonds, chopped
- raisins, scalded

## Directions

1. Cook the mutton cut into pieces with one spoon of fat and one cup of vinegar, salt, parsley, peppercorns, one clove of garlic and onion leaves
2. When almost cooked, add a little wheat flour until well toasted
3. Add burned sugar cooked in a spoon, two cups red wine, one spoon sugar, a dozen chopped almonds and scalded raisins
4. Boil the meat a little more in this sauce and serve

title: Carne de Carneiro Ensopada, Dourada (Stewed Golden Mutton)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton, cut into pieces
- fat, one spoon
- salt
- cloves
- nutmeg
- milk, a few cups
- four egg yolks
- milk, half cup

## Directions

1. Sauté the mutton cut into pieces in one spoon of fat with little salt, cloves and nutmeg
2. Then add a few cups of milk and boil near the fire until the meat is cooked
3. Add four egg yolks dissolved in half a cup of milk
4. Let it boil a little more and serve

title: Carne de Carneiro Estufada (Braised Mutton)
body:
## Ingredients

- four pounds mutton, chopped
- half pound bacon, chopped
- salt
- parsley
- marjoram
- pepper
- celery
- fat, melted
- two cups white wine
- lemon, sliced, peeled and without seeds
- carapicú

## Directions

1. Chop four pounds mutton with half pound bacon, salt, parsley, marjoram, pepper and celery; sauté in a little melted fat and when heated, add two cups white wine, peeled lemon slices without seeds and some carapicú
2. Let this finish cooking over coals and serve

title: Carne de Carneiro Estufada à Campestre (Braised Mutton Rustic Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- one portion mutton
- garlic cloves
- salt
- pepper
- fat, one spoon
- nutmeg, a little
- two bay leaves
- one cup wine
- one cup water

## Directions

1. Take a portion of mutton, lard it by inserting some garlic cloves; season with salt and pepper, and place in a pot with one spoon of fat, a little nutmeg, two bay leaves, one cup wine and one cup water
2. Cover the pot tightly and let boil with low fire for two to three hours
3. Serve with some sauce or salad

title: Carne de Carneiro Frita (Fried Mutton)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton slices
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chopped onion
- half spoonful of butter
- mustard sauce

## Directions

1. Cut and beat the slices, season with salt, pepper, parsley and chopped onion
2. Place a pot over high heat with half a spoonful of butter
3. When the butter is very hot, put the mutton slices in it
4. Fry both sides and serve with spicy mustard sauce
5. They can also be grilled with a little butter, cooking both sides

title: Carne de Carneiro Guisada (Stewed Mutton)
body:
## Ingredients

- four pounds mutton, chopped
- handful roasted and crushed peanuts
- salt
- cloves
- nutmeg
- butter, two spoons
- wheat flour, one spoon
- wine, one cup
- raisins, scalded, handful
- sugar, one spoon
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Chop four pounds mutton and mix with a handful of roasted and crushed peanuts, salt, cloves and nutmeg
2. Fry two spoons butter and one spoon wheat flour until dark amber color; add the meat at this point and let it heat, stirring always
3. Add one cup wine, a handful of scalded raisins, one spoon sugar and lemon juice
4. Let the stew boil over coals for half an hour and serve

title: Carne de Carneiro Guisada com Carapicús (Stewed Mutton with Carapicú)
body:
## Ingredients

- four pounds mutton, cut into pieces
- fat, one spoon
- water, one bowl
- carapicú, handful
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- cornstarch, one spoon
- juice of one terra orange

## Directions

1. Cut four pounds mutton into pieces and sauté in one spoon fat without letting them brown
2. Add one bowl of water, a handful of carapicú, salt, parsley, year-round onion leaves, one spoon cornstarch and the juice of one terra orange
3. Let boil over coals for two hours and serve with mineiro cornmeal mush

title: Carne de Carneiro Guisada com Couves (Stewed Mutton with Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds mutton
- quarter pound bacon
- cabbage, chopped, washed and squeezed
- water, one cup
- salt
- onion leaves

## Directions

1. Take two pounds mutton and a quarter pound bacon; fry everything together and add the chopped, washed and squeezed cabbage
2. Let sauté, stirring, then add one cup water, salt and some onion leaves; cover well
3. Let boil for one hour over coals and serve

title: Carne de Carneiro Picada e Frita (Chopped Fried Mutton)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds mutton, chopped (after removing nerves and skin)
- one pound pork with some fat
- two onions
- pepper
- salt
- cloves
- parsley
- handful crushed good biscuit powder
- one spoon cornstarch
- two eggs (yolk and white)
- one cup wine
- fat for frying

## Directions

1. Chop two pounds mutton (after removing nerves and skin) with one pound pork with fat, two onions, pepper, salt, cloves, parsley, a handful of crushed good biscuit powder, one spoon cornstarch, two eggs and one cup wine
2. Melt a little fat in a pot and when very hot, add the chopped mixture
3. When fried on one side, turn to fry the other side and serve with a spicy sauce

title: Carne de Carneiro Refogada com Arroz (Braised Mutton with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound of bacon
- mutton meat, cut into pieces
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onion leaves
- rice, chosen and washed
- one egg yolk
- water

## Directions

1. Fry the bacon and remove the cracklings
2. Add the mutton meat cut into pieces and braise them, adding salt, pepper, parsley and onion leaves until they take on a good color
3. Remove them and put the chosen, washed and drained rice in the hot fat
4. Stir so it does not burn, and when it has taken on enough heat, add a bowl of water in which one egg yolk has been mixed
5. Add the meat, letting everything cook near the fire until it dries out, then serve

title: Carne de Carneiro Refogada com Palmitos (Braised Mutton with Palm Hearts)
body:
## Ingredients

- four pounds of mutton meat, chopped
- salt
- parsley
- celery
- onion
- pepper
- butter
- meat broth or water
- palm hearts cooked in water and salt
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Take four pounds of mutton meat, chop it well, removing nerves and skins that appear
2. Season with salt, parsley, celery, onion and pepper
3. Braise in a little melted butter and add a bowl of meat broth or water
4. Let it boil over coals until the broth almost dries out
5. Add palm hearts cooked in water and salt over which place the braised mutton with the broth
6. Cover everything with grated cheese and put in the oven until it takes on a golden color, then serve

title: Carne de Carneiro Refogada em Vinho com Limão (Braised Mutton in Wine with Lemon)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton meat, cut into pieces
- water
- butter
- one spoonful of wheat flour
- one glass of white wine
- bay leaves
- onions
- lemon juice
- sugar
- cooked raisins

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into pieces and scald them in hot water
2. Braise them in a little butter and one spoonful of wheat flour until they are browned
3. Add one glass of white wine, some bay leaves, onions, lemon juice, sugar and cooked raisins

title: Carne de Carneiro à Americana (Mutton American Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of mutton
- vinegar, a little
- fat, one spoon
- pepper powder
- salt

## Directions

1. Take a piece of mutton and roast on a spit; when roasted, remove to be chopped
2. Mix the juice that dripped with a little vinegar, one spoon fat, pepper powder and salt, and place over fire
3. Add the chopped meat to this
4. After cooking for half an hour, serve

title: Carne de Carneiro à Parmesão (Mutton with Parmesan)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound of bacon
- two pounds of mutton meat, cut into small pieces
- salt
- pepper
- chopped parsley
- onion
- bread slices
- grated cheese
- butter
- one glass of wine

## Directions

1. Fry half a pound of bacon and when melted, add two pounds of mutton meat cut into small pieces
2. Add salt, pepper, chopped parsley and onion
3. When browned, place them on slices of bread covered with grated cheese, put in a buttered dish
4. Moisten with one glass of wine and finish cooking in the oven

title: Carne de Colonia com Feijão (Colonial Meat with Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of meat
- hot water
- parsley
- onion leaves
- cloves
- black beans
- pork belly

## Directions

1. Take a piece of meat, and after cleaning it, place it on the fire with already hot water.
2. Join with parsley, onion leaves, cloves, letting it boil for a quarter of an hour.
3. Remove it, and place it on the fire to cook with black beans and pork belly, in cold water.
4. After everything is cooked, serve it.

title: Carne de entrecosto com cogumelos (Beef Short Ribs with Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef short ribs
- butter
- wheat flour
- hot water
- brandy
- mushrooms
- pitted olives

## Directions

1. Free the meat from excess fat and cut into slices
2. Place these in a pan with a little butter
3. Once they are fried on both sides, remove them
4. Put a spoonful of wheat flour in the pan
5. When it takes color and is moistened in hot water and a glass of brandy, add the short rib slices again
6. Let them cook for three or four hours
7. Add some mushrooms and five minutes before serving, some pitted olives

title: Carne de Porco Cozida no Sangue (Pork Cooked in Blood)
body:
## Ingredients

- portions of pork meat
- salt
- bay leaves
- pepper
- pork blood
- strong vinegar
- clove
- cornmeal mush or cassava flour mush

## Directions

1. Cook portions of pork meat with salt, bay leaves, and pepper
2. Remove the meat and add the pork blood to the broth, having first dissolved it in strong vinegar and passed it through a sieve
3. Add some cloves and bring to a boil
4. Each time the blood appears to curdle, add a few drops of good vinegar
5. Finally return the meat to the vessel and after a few boils, serve with cornmeal mush or cassava flour mush

title: Carne de Porco de Matto Refogada com Gerumbeba (Wild Pig Meat Braised with Gerumbeba)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat from wild pig
- water
- salt
- pepper
- sprig of mint
- garlic
- parsley
- lemon juice
- two tablespoons pork fat
- breadcrumbs
- one cup water
- portion of gerumbeba fruits peeled
- half dozen tomatoes

## Directions

1. Soak the meat for twenty-four hours in water with salt, pepper, a sprig of mint, garlic, parsley, and lemon juice.
2. Fry it in two tablespoons of pork fat, turning it on all sides.
3. Remove and coat in breadcrumbs.
4. Return to the fat and add one cup of water, a portion of peeled gerumbeba fruits, and half a dozen tomatoes.
5. Let it boil for one hour and serve.

title: Carne de Porco do Matto Ensopada com Carás (Wild Pig Meat in Stew with Yams)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat from wild pig cut in pieces
- chopped bacon
- salt
- green peppers
- chopped parsley
- one clove garlic crushed
- nutmeg grated
- one yam peeled and cut in small pieces
- two egg yolks
- one tablespoon flour
- one glass lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut the pork meat in pieces and put in a pot over fire with chopped bacon, salt, green peppers, chopped parsley, one crushed garlic clove, a little grated nutmeg, and one yam peeled and cut in small pieces.
2. Let it boil and when cooked and the broth reduced, incorporate two egg yolks beaten with one tablespoon of flour and diluted in one glass of lemon juice.
3. Give it another boil and serve.

title: Carne de Porco do Matto Ensopada com Ora-pro-nobis (Stewed Wild Pork with Ora-pro-nobis)
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons wheat flour
- two tablespoons butter
- one onion, chopped
- two cups red wine
- one bottle water
- salt
- peppers
- parsley
- one tablespoon juice of orange
- wild pork, cut into pieces
- ora-pro-nobis leaves, peeled and cleaned

## Directions

1. Fry two tablespoons of wheat flour with two tablespoons of butter until they take color.
2. Add one chopped onion, two cups of red wine, one bottle of water, salt, peppers, parsley, and one tablespoon of juice of orange.
3. Boil a little, and then add the pork meat cut into pieces to this sauce.
4. Let it boil until cooked.
5. At this time, add a portion of ora-pro-nobis leaves, peeled and cleaned.
6. Boil once more and serve.

title: Carne de Porco do Matto Ensopada com Ora-pro-nobis (Wild Pig Meat in Stew with Ora-pro-nobis)
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons wheat flour
- two tablespoons butter
- one chopped onion
- two cups red wine
- one bottle water
- salt
- peppers
- parsley
- one tablespoon terra orange juice

## Directions

1. Fry two tablespoons of wheat flour with two tablespoons of butter until they take color.
2. Add one chopped onion, two cups of red wine, one bottle of water, salt, peppers, parsley, and one tablespoon of terra orange juice.
3. Boil a little.

title: Carne de Porco do Matto Ensopada com Repolho (Stewed Wild Pork with Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- wild pork, cut into large pieces
- half pound chopped bacon
- salt
- parsley
- one bunch mint
- onion leaves
- marjoram
- one tablespoon polvilho dissolved in a glass of vinegar
- one chopped cabbage, sautéed in a little fat

## Directions

1. Boil the pork cut into large pieces with half pound of chopped bacon, salt, parsley, one bunch of mint, onion leaves and marjoram.
2. When cooked, remove the meat and strain the broth.
3. Add to it one tablespoon of polvilho dissolved in a glass of vinegar and one chopped cabbage sautéed in a little fat.
4. Put the meat back in and let it boil over moderate heat until the broth dries out and serve.

title: Carne de Porco do Matto Estufada com Creme de Mostarda (Stewed Wild Pork with Mustard Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- one piece of pork meat
- salt
- pounded camarís peppers
- bacon pieces for larding
- breadcrumbs
- bacon slices
- one cup white wine
- two egg yolks, beaten
- one large cup milk
- one tablespoon mustard powder
- grated nutmeg
- one small teaspoon sugar

## Directions

1. Rub one piece of meat with salt and pounded camarís peppers.
2. Lard it with pieces of bacon and wrap it in breadcrumbs.
3. Put it in a pan with slices of bacon and one cup of white wine.
4. Cover the pan with the lid and let it cook in the oven.
5. When cooked, serve it with the sauce made of two beaten egg yolks boiled with one large cup of milk, one tablespoon of mustard powder, a little grated nutmeg and one small teaspoon of sugar.

title: Carne de Porco do Matto Guisada com Inhame (Wild Pork Stewed with Yam)
body:
## Ingredients

- one piece of pork meat
- half pound bacon
- salt
- Indian peppers
- parsley
- two onions, cut
- yam roots, cut into small pieces
- butter
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Cook one piece of meat with half pound of bacon, salt, Indian peppers, parsley, two cut onions and yam roots cut into small pieces.
2. When the meat is cooked, cut it into thick slices and fry them in butter.
3. Place the yams on the plate, sprinkle a little grated cheese on them, and pour over the butter in which the meat was fried and serve.

title: Carne de Porco do Matto Guisada com Mangaritos (Wild Pork Stewed with Mangaritos)
body:
## Ingredients

- one piece of wild pork
- water
- salt
- peppers
- parsley
- garlic
- mangaritos, peeled
- four tablespoons butter

## Directions

1. Take one piece of wild pork and boil it in water with salt, peppers, parsley, garlic and a portion of peeled mangaritos.
2. When the meat is cooked, remove it and cut it into small pieces, and the mangaritos into slices.
3. Fry both the meat and the mangaritos in four tablespoons of butter and serve.

title: Carne de Porco do Matto Guisada com Serralha (Wild Pork Stewed with Serralha)
body:
## Ingredients

- one piece of pork meat
- water
- salt
- ginger
- parsley
- onion leaves
- celery
- pepper
- pork fat
- serralha, chopped
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Boil in water with salt, ginger, parsley, onion leaves, celery and pepper, one piece of meat.
2. When cooked, remove it and fry it in pork fat, and add to the broth a portion of chopped serralha.
3. Boil a little and place it on a plate covered with breadcrumbs.
4. Put the meat in the middle and serve.

title: Carne de Porco do Matto Guisada com Vagens (Wild Pork Stewed with Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- one piece of pork meat
- water
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onion leaves
- butter
- one tablespoon cornmeal dissolved in half a cup of vinegar
- whole peeled beans

## Directions

1. Scald one piece of meat and let it cool.
2. Put it on the fire with water, salt, pepper, parsley, onion leaves.
3. When cooked, remove it and strain the broth.
4. Fry the meat in a little butter.
5. When fried, add the broth, thickening it with one tablespoon of cornmeal dissolved in half a cup of vinegar.
6. Add a portion of whole peeled beans and let it boil until everything is well cooked and the broth is almost dry and serve.

title: Carne de Porco do Matto Refogada com Bananas (Wild Pig Meat Braised with Bananas)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pork roast from wild pig
- bacon tempered with salt and pepper for larding
- one bottle wine or water
- parsley
- onion
- nutmeg
- one dozen terra bananas peeled

## Directions

1. Lard the pork roast with bacon tempered with salt and pepper.
2. Roast it for twenty minutes.
3. Remove and place in a pot with one bottle of wine or water, parsley, onion, nutmeg, and one dozen peeled terra bananas.
4. Boil until cooked.
5. Remove and serve with the strained sauce.

title: Carne de Porco Refogada com Abobora d'Agua (Pork Meat Braised with Water Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pork roast from wild pig
- whey from milk
- portion of water squashes cut in small pieces
- one tablespoon fine cornmeal
- one bottle water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- ginger

## Directions

1. Soak a pork roast from wild pig in whey for two days.
2. Remove and braise a portion of water squashes cut in small pieces in the same fat.
3. Add one tablespoon of fine cornmeal and finally one bottle of water.
4. Return the meat and let it boil with salt, parsley, onion leaves, and a little ginger.
5. Serve.

title: Carne de Porco Salgada (Salted Pork)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat
- salt
- saltpeter

## Directions

1. Use mostly the loin for this purpose
2. Cut the loin into pieces and rub them with crushed salt and saltpeter
3. Arrange them in a vessel and cover, taking care to press the meat pieces well so they do not get air
4. Place a good quantity of salt on top so the meat is completely covered
5. Place a white cloth over the vessel
6. After eight days the meat is sufficiently salted
7. This can be kept for three to four months, though the fresher the salt pork, the more flavorful it is
8. It frequently appears in the composition of all kinds of dishes with cabbage, lentils, or turnip pulp

title: Carne de Vacca Assada no Forno (Beef Roasted in the Oven)
body:
## Ingredients

- good cut of beef
- lard with strips of bacon dusted in Indian pepper
- strips of bacon
- half a bottle of red wine
- pepper
- salt
- garlic
- onions
- bay leaf
- butter
- paper

## Directions

1. Lard the beef with strips of bacon dusted in Indian pepper
2. Place in a pan lined with strips of bacon, with more strips prepared to cover it
3. Add half a bottle of red wine, pepper, salt, garlic, onions, and bay leaf
4. Cover the pan, securing it with strips of paper greased with butter and glued
5. Bake in the oven for six hours and serve

title: Carne de Vacca com Salsa (Beef with Parsley)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh beef
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- meat broth
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the fresh beef into thin slices.
2. Layer these in a pan one on top of the other with salt, pepper, and finely chopped parsley, one cup of meat broth.
3. Let them boil for half an hour over coals.
4. Then add to them one spoon of vinegar.

title: Carne de Vacca Cozida com Azeite (Beef Cooked with Oil)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beef
- parsley
- onion
- olive oil

## Directions

1. Cut the cooked beef into slices and place on a plate with whole scalded parsley and chopped onion
2. At the same time as the beef, serve in a sauce boat a cold sauce of oil or a restorative sauce

title: Carne de Vacca Cozida com Azeite (Beef Cooked with Olive Oil)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beef
- parsley
- onion
- olive oil

## Directions

1. Cut the cooked beef into slices and place on a plate with whole scalded parsley and chopped onion.
2. At the same time as the beef, serve in a sauce boat a cold sauce of olive oil, or a restaurant sauce.

title: Carne de Vacca Cozida e Encorpada com Gemas de Ovos (Beef Cooked and Thickened with Egg Yolks)
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley
- chives
- butter
- flour
- beef broth
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- cooked beef
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Chop parsley and chives, and place them with butter in a casserole
2. After they have heated, add a spoon of flour
3. Stir and pour over a cup of beef broth
4. Stir until boiling; season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg
5. Boil this sauce for a few minutes and place over the cooked beef cut into slices
6. At the moment of serving, thicken with egg yolks

title: Carne de Vacca Cozida no Leite (Beef Cooked in Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- milk
- salt
- sugar
- nutmeg
- bay leaves

## Directions

1. Take the washed beef and place it on the fire with milk that has already been boiled with salt
2. After it cools, add a little sugar, nutmeg, little salt, and bay leaves
3. Leave to boil

title: Carne de Vacca Cozida no Leite (Beef Cooked in Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- washed beef
- milk
- salt
- sugar
- nutmeg
- bay leaves
- wheat flour

## Directions

1. Take the washed beef and place it on the fire with milk already boiled with salt.
2. When cooled, add to it a little sugar, nutmeg, little salt, and bay leaves.
3. Let it boil over moderate fire for three to four hours and serve with its own sauce, thickened with wheat flour.

title: Carne de Vacca Cozida à Marujo (Beef Cooked Sailor-Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- small peeled onions
- wheat flour
- beef broth
- red wine
