- mushrooms or carapicús
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaves
- thyme
- cooked beef slices

## Directions

1. Place a little butter in a frying pan with small peeled onions and let them brown over low heat
2. Add a spoon of wheat flour and sauté the onions
3. Then add a cup of beef broth and another of red wine
4. Add some mushrooms or carapicús
5. Season with salt, pepper, bay leaves, and thyme
6. Leave to cook
7. Pour this sailor-style sauce over slices of cooked beef prepared beforehand
8. Leave to heat near the fire for half an hour before serving

title: Carne de Vacca Cozida à Mirandão (Mirandão-Style Cooked Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beef
- onion
- parsley
- salt
- garlic
- tomatoes
- lard
- vinegar

## Directions

1. After the meat is cooked, cut it into slices.
2. Chop one onion, parsley, add to it salt, garlic and tomatoes, and mix all of this.
3. Having previously on the fire a pan with hot lard, place in it a layer of mixed seasonings, another of meat slices, and so on, until filling it, with the last layer being seasonings.
4. In this state, cover the pan and as soon as the seasonings are cooked, add to them half a glass of vinegar, shake the pan for the sauce to penetrate, and serve.

title: Carne de Vacca Enrolada (Rolled Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- salt
- saltpeter
- clove
- fresh bacon
- twine
- water

## Directions

1. Take the beef, cut it into thin and long slices and sprinkle over them salt, a little saltpeter and ground clove.
2. Cover them with equal slices of fresh bacon with another layer of the same seasonings.
3. Roll these two slices then, the beef should be on the outside.
4. Tie these rolls with twine and after two hours boil them in water.
5. To put this meat on the table, after it cools cut it into transverse slices and serve with a cold sauce also.

title: Carne de Vacca Estufada (Beef Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beef cut into small pieces
- good piece of butter
- two spoons of wheat flour
- beef broth
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- parsley
- onion leaves
- English potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut the cooked beef into small pieces
2. Put these in a pan with a good piece of butter
3. When the butter is melted, add two spoons of wheat flour
4. Stir and add beef broth, salt, pepper, nutmeg, parsley, and onion leaves
5. Leave everything to boil for about one hour
6. Serve with cooked English potatoes

title: Carne de Vacca Estufada (Braised Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef, larded with pieces of fresh bacon
- pieces of fresh bacon
- onions, chopped
- salt
- pepper
- wheat flour
- one cup of broth
- tubaras (a type of vegetable)
- mushrooms
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Place the larded beef (filled with pieces of bacon) in a pot, garnished with pieces of bacon, chopped onions, salt, and pepper, and let it braise over moderate heat, keeping it well covered.
2. When cooked, remove the meat and fry a little wheat flour in fat; then add one cup of broth, tubaras, mushrooms, lemon juice and a little salt.
3. Let it boil and serve the meat with this sauce.

title: Carne de Vacca Estufada (Braised Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- larded beef
- bacon
- onions
- salt
- pepper
- wheat flour
- fat

## Directions

1. Put the larded beef (filled with pieces of bacon) in a pan lined with pieces of bacon, chopped onions, salt and pepper and let it braise over moderate fire, keeping it well covered.
2. When cooked, remove the beef and fry in fat a little wheat flour.

title: Carne de Vacca Frita (Fried Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of bacon cut into strips
- pieces of sausages or linguiça
- cooked beef cut into slices
- two spoons of broth
- one spoon of vinegar

## Directions

1. Fry pieces of bacon cut into strips with pieces of sausages or linguiça
2. When they acquire color, add the cooked beef cut into slices with two spoons of broth and one spoon of vinegar

title: Carne de Vacca Frita com Quibébe (Fried Beef with Quibebe)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef cut into small pieces
- melted fat
- salt
- peppercorns
- quibebe

## Directions

1. Cut the beef into small pieces
2. Fry in melted fat with salt and some peppercorns
3. Serve with quibebe

title: Carne de Vacca Guisada com Ora-pro-nobis (Beef Stewed with Ora-pro-nobis)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef cut into small thin slices
- melted fat
- one glass of water
- one spoon of brandy
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onion leaves
- one spoon of cornstarch
- ora-pro-nobis leaves
- one spoon of vinegar or juice of terra orange

## Directions

1. Fry the meat slices in a little melted fat
2. Add one glass of water and one spoon of brandy, salt, pepper, parsley, onion leaves, and one spoon of cornstarch
3. Leave to boil for one hour
4. Add the ora-pro-nobis leaves and one spoon of vinegar or orange juice
5. Give it another boil
6. Serve with cornmeal porridge

title: Carne de Vacca Secca com Ovos (Dried Beef with Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- dried beef
- eggs
- lard
- onions
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the dried meat for two or three hours.
2. Remove and let it cool.
3. Shred this cooked meat and mix it with eggs that should be well beaten to mix well.
4. Fry in lard with chopped onions, salt and pepper.

title: Carne de Vacca Tenra (Tender Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef cut
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- Indian cloves
- garlic
- bay leaf
- bacon strips
- peppercorns
- Indian cloves pounded
- bacon strips
- cups of water
- paper
- sauce for meat

## Directions

1. Soak the beef cut for three days in vinegar to which salt, pepper, Indian cloves, garlic, and bay leaf have been added
2. Make holes the thickness of a finger, filling them with bacon strips covered with pepper and pounded Indian cloves
3. Place the prepared meat in a pan lined with bacon and a few cups of water
4. Cover and secure the lid with glued paper strips
5. Braise in the oven
6. Serve with some meat sauce

title: Carne de Vacca Tostada (Toasted Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- bacon cut into small pieces
- bread crumbs
- mushrooms
- onion
- parsley
- garlic
- salt
- pepper
- meat broth
- cognac or white wine
- butter

## Directions

1. Fry bacon cut into small pieces.
2. Upon it scatter a little bread crumbs, mushrooms cut into small pieces, onion, parsley, one clove of chopped garlic, salt and pepper.
3. Add slices of beef which are then covered with mushrooms, onions, parsley, salt, pepper and bread crumbs.
4. Moisten these slices with meat broth and braise over gentle fire.
5. If desired to toast them, place over lively fire; otherwise serve with some sauce.
6. One can also, instead of bacon, add a little butter and include before cooking a little cognac or white wine.

title: Carne de Vacca à Moda Commum (Beef in Common Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- one piece of beef from the hip side
- lard for larding
- four carrots
- four onions
- two cloves
- parsley
- chives
- veal knuckle
- bay leaf
- thyme
- salt
- pepper
- four cups of water
- one glass of good brandy

## Directions

1. Take a piece of beef from the hip side
2. Make holes in it with a knife and introduce strips of lard into these holes
3. Place in a casserole with four carrots, four onions, two cloves, parsley, chives, veal knuckle, bay leaf, thyme, salt, pepper, and four cups of water
4. Leave to boil for four to five hours, depending on the size of the piece
5. When half-cooked, add a glass of good brandy
6. Serve with the vegetables

title: Carne de Veado Ensopada com Quiabos (Venison Stew with Okra)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison meat
- two spoonfuls of fat
- one bowl of water
- salt
- parsley
- one onion, cut
- peppercorns
- half a dozen tomatoes
- one small spoonful of cassava starch dissolved in half a chalice of water
- coconut milk from Bahia
- lemon juice
- okra, cut in rounds

## Directions

1. Take the venison meat and braise it in two spoonfuls of fat
2. Add one bowl of water, salt, parsley, one cut onion, peppercorns, half a dozen tomatoes
3. Add one small spoonful of cassava starch dissolved in half a chalice of coconut milk from Bahia
4. Add lemon juice and a portion of okra cut in rounds
5. Let simmer until the broth is reduced to three cups and serve

title: Carne de Veado Frita com Pirão de Batatas (Fried Venison with Potato Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison meat
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- two onions, chopped
- potatoes
- milk or cream

## Directions

1. Cut the venison meat into thin slices and sprinkle with salt and pepper
2. Fry in butter with two chopped onions
3. In another place, cook a portion of potatoes, peel and mash them to form a homogeneous mass
4. Put this mass to simmer with very rich milk or preferably cream
5. Let simmer, stirring well to avoid burning on the bottom
6. When it reaches the necessary consistency, put it on a plate
7. Place the meat with its butter on top and serve

title: Carne de Veado Guisada com Favas (Venison Stewed with Fava Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison meat
- water
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onion leaves
- ginger, grated
- fava beans, fresh
- wheat flour
- one spoonful of butter
- one chalice of vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into pieces and let it boil with water, salt, some pepper, parsley, onion leaves, grated ginger, and a portion of fresh fava beans
2. When the meat is cooked, fry a little wheat flour in one spoonful of butter until it takes color
3. At this point add one chalice of vinegar and the broth in which the meat and beans were cooked
4. Let the broth reduce, return the meat and beans to it
5. Once everything is heated together, serve

title: Carne de Veado Guisado com Pepinos (Venison Stewed with Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison meat
- salt
- pepper
- parsley, minced
- ginger, grated
- water
- bread crumbs
- butter
- sugar
- one chalice of white wine
- two chalices of water
- lemon juice
- lemon, sliced
- two egg yolks
- cucumbers, peeled and cut in fillets

## Directions

1. Cut the venison meat into slices the thickness of a finger, beat them a little and season with salt, pepper, minced parsley and grated ginger
2. Put to boil for five minutes in water that is already hot on the fire
3. Remove and coat in bread crumbs and fry in butter
4. After frying on both sides, remove from the fat, in which pour a portion of bread crumb pieces with one spoonful of sugar; stir
5. Add one chalice of white wine and two of water, the juice of two lemons, one lemon cut in slices, two egg yolks, and a portion of peeled cucumbers cut in fillets
6. Let simmer a little, add the meat slices, and after simmering a little, serve

title: Carne de Veado Refogada com Chuchús (Venison Stewed with Chayote Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison meat
- vinegar
- salt
- onion
- celery
- ginger
- cloves from India
- parsley
- marjoram
- fat
- wheat flour
- one bottle of white wine
- chayote squash

## Directions

1. Soak the meat for two days in vinegar, salt, onion, celery, ginger, cloves from India, parsley and marjoram
2. Remove and braise in fat, turning the pieces to brown on all sides
3. Sprinkle with wheat flour
4. Add one bottle of white wine and a portion of peeled and chopped chayote squash
5. Let simmer on coals until cooked and serve

title: Carne de Veado Refogada com Vagens (Venison Stewed with Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison meat
- fat
- two spoonfuls of wheat flour
- beans, chopped
- one bottle of water
- salt
- pepper
- marjoram
- parsley
- onion leaves
- lemon, peeled and sliced

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into slices and fry in fat on both sides until they take on good color
2. Remove them and pour two spoonfuls of wheat flour into the fat; stir
3. Add a portion of chopped beans
4. Add one bottle of water, salt, pepper, marjoram, parsley, onion leaves and slices of peeled lemon
5. Let simmer on low fire and serve

title: Carne Sepultada (Earth-Buried Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- salt
- banana leaves or caeté leaves

## Directions

1. Dig a hole in the earth deep enough to contain the piece of meat to be cooked.
2. Once ready, place in it the piece of meat seasoned with salt and well wrapped in banana leaves or caeté leaves, so that it does not have contact with the earth.
3. Cover with earth and on top of this light a strong fire.
4. The heated earth communicates its heat to the meat, which in this manner cooks slowly and becomes very succulent. Six to eight hours are necessary to cook the meat, depending on the size of the piece.

title: Carpa, Piaba etc. Assadas na Grelha (Carp, Piaba etc. Grilled)
body:
## Ingredients

- one fish
- salt
- pepper
- two tablespoons butter
- one tablespoon wheat flour
- salt
- pepper
- one cup of water
- one cup of sour orange juice

## Directions

1. Clean and scale one fish; open it along the back in strips, dust with salt and pepper, and grill
2. On the other hand, melt two tablespoons of butter over coals
3. Mix one tablespoon of wheat flour, salt, pepper, and one cup of water
4. Let it boil a little, add one cup of sour orange juice, place on a plate, put the fish on top, and serve

title: Carpa, Piaba etc. Cozidas com Leite (Carp, Piaba etc. Boiled in Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two fish
- milk
- salt
- two tablespoons fat
- two tablespoons cassava flour
- one cut onion
- peppers

## Directions

1. Take one or two fish, remove the scales, clean and wash them, cut into pieces and boil in milk and salt
2. When cooked, melt two tablespoons of fat in a vessel over coals
3. Mix two tablespoons of cassava flour, one cut onion and peppers
4. Add the milk and finally the fish
5. Let it boil for another half hour and serve

title: Carpa, Piaba, etc. Cozidas (Boiled Carp, Piaba, etc.)
body:
## Ingredients

- one fish
- one bowl of water
- one cup of white wine
- salt
- peppers
- onions
- parsley
- one cup of vinegar
- one garlic clove
- two cloves
- vinegar
- sweet oil
- one egg yolk

## Directions

1. Clean and wash one fish without removing the scales
2. In another pan, boil one bowl of water, one cup of white wine, salt, peppers, onions, parsley
3. When boiling, add the fish, which should be completely covered
4. In another pan, boil one cup of vinegar, one garlic clove, and two cloves, and pour hot over the fish
5. When the fish is cooked, remove the skin with the scales and serve cold with its own strained and reduced broth, mixing vinegar, sweet oil and one cooked egg yolk broken up in the cold sauce

title: Carpa, Piaba, etc. Cozidas com Leite (Carp, Piaba, etc. Cooked with Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two fish
- milk
- salt
- two spoons of fat
- two spoons of manioc flour
- one onion
- peppers

## Directions

1. Take one or two fish, remove the scales, clean and wash them, cutting them into pieces and boiling them in milk and salt
2. When cooked, melt two spoons of fat in a vessel over coals
3. Mix two spoons of manioc flour, one chopped onion and peppers
4. Add the milk and finally the fish
5. Let it boil for another half hour and serve

title: Carpa, Piaba, etc. Ensopadas (Carp, Piaba, etc. Braised)
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces of lard
- half a pound of beef
- one or two fish steaks
- one bowl of water
- one cup of red wine
- one onion
- one chalice of vinegar
- one small spoon of arrowroot
- one spoon of sugar

## Directions

1. Cut four ounces of lard and half a pound of beef into small pieces and place them with the steaks of one or two fish
2. Put them to cook with one bowl of water, one cup of red wine, one chopped onion, one chalice of vinegar
3. Let it boil, and when the fish is cooked, remove it
4. Add to the broth one small spoon of arrowroot and one spoon of sugar
5. When the broth is reduced, pour it over the fish and serve

title: Carpa, Piaba, etc. Ensopadas (Carp, Piaba, etc. Stewed)
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces of bacon
- half pound of beef
- one or two fish in portions
- one bowl of water
- one cup of wine

## Directions

1. Cut four ounces of bacon and half a pound of beef into small pieces, and place with the portions of one or two fish
2. Place to cook with one bowl of water and one cup of wine

title: Carpa, Piaba, etc. Fritas (Carp, Piaba, etc. Fried)
body:
## Ingredients

- medium-sized or small fish
- salt
- ground pepper
- manioc flour
- fat

## Directions

1. Only medium-sized or small fish should be fried
2. Clean them, remove the scales, split them down the middle so that the two halves remain attached at the back
3. Sprinkle with salt, ground pepper and wrap them in manioc flour
4. Fry them in fat until cooked and of good color, and serve

title: Carpa, Piaba, etc. Fritas em Restilo (Carp, Piaba, etc. Fried in Restilo)
body:
## Ingredients

- salted fish
- cachaca
- pork fat
- one onion
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- manioc flour

## Directions

1. This recipe is mainly for fish that have been preserved salted
2. Soak them for four hours, then dry them well, and place them in a casserole
3. Pour cachaca over them, heating the casserole and adding fire to burn the cachaca and let it burn completely
4. In another part, fry a little pork fat with one split onion, chopped parsley, salt, pepper and a little manioc flour
5. Pouring this over the fish placed on the plate, serve

title: Carpa, Piaba, etc. Guisadas (Carp, Piaba, etc. Stewed)
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three fish
- water
- salt
- parsley
- pepper
- a dozen tomatoes
- two spoons of fat
- three to four egg yolks
- one spoon of lemon juice

## Directions

1. Boil two or three scaled and cleaned fish in water, salt, parsley and pepper
2. When cooked, fry a dozen tomatoes in two spoons of fat
3. Add the fish broth mixed with three to four egg yolks beaten with one spoon of lemon juice
4. Boil a little and pour the fish into this sauce
5. Let it cook for another half hour over coals and serve

title: Carpa, Piaba, etc. Guisadas com Quiabos (Carp, Piaba, etc. Stewed with Okra)
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four fish
- water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- peppers
- cloves
- sour orange juice
- two spoons of fat
- two spoons of manioc flour
- okra

## Directions

1. Scale, clean and cut three or four fish into pieces and boil them in water, salt, parsley, onion leaves, peppers, cloves and sour orange juice
2. In another part, fry two spoons of fat and add two spoons of manioc flour
3. Then add the strained and reduced fish broth, a portion of okra cut into rounds and the fish
4. Let them boil for about an hour over very gentle heat and serve

title: Carpa, Piaba, etc. Refogadas em Môlho Branco (Carp, Piaba, etc. Sautéed in White Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- some onions
- two tablespoons cow butter
- one bottle of white wine
- fish cut in portions
- portion of carapicús
- one spoonful of vinegar
- two tablespoons butter
- two tablespoons wheat flour
- three egg yolks
- one tablespoon sugar

## Directions

1. Sauté some onions cut in half in two tablespoons of cow butter
2. Add one bottle of white wine, the fish cut in portions, a portion of carapicús, and one spoonful of vinegar
3. Let it boil, and when the fish is cooked, remove it
4. To the broth add two tablespoons of butter and two of wheat flour, three egg yolks beaten in one tablespoon of sugar
5. Let it boil a little more, add the fish and serve

title: Carpa, Piaba, Papaterra, Piáu Refogados (Sautéed Carp, Piaba, Papaterra, and Piáu Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons cow butter
- two large onions
- one fish cut in pieces
- one bottle of red wine
- handful of breadcrumbs
- salt

## Directions

1. Melt two tablespoons of cow butter, add two large onions cut in half and one fish cut in pieces
2. Add one bottle of red wine
3. Let it boil over moderate fire
4. When cooked, add a handful of breadcrumbs, little salt, boil a bit more and serve

title: Carurá de Carne de Vacca (Beef Caruru)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- fat
- salt
- water
- mimoso farina
- one dozen okra
- pepper
- parsley
- green onion

## Directions

1. Cut the beef into small pieces and fry for a quarter hour in fat and salt.
2. Then add to it one cup of water, one spoon of mimoso farina, one dozen okra cut into slices, salt, two or three bird peppers, parsley, green onion.
3. Let it boil for one hour.
4. Serve with farina angu or manioc flour broth.

title: Cará com Açúcar (Yam with Sugar)
body:
## Ingredients

- one yam
- water
- salt
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cook one yam in water and salt
2. When cooked, peel and cut into slices the thickness of a finger
3. Put these slices on a plate and cover them with layers of sugar
4. Put in the oven and when it has taken color, serve

title: Cará do Ar Cozido (Boiled Aerial Yam)
body:
## Ingredients

- aerial yams
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook a quantity of aerial yams in water and salt
2. When cooked, peel them and serve

title: Cará do Ar Frito (Fried Aerial Yam)
body:
## Ingredients

- aerial yams
- lard or butter

## Directions

1. Cook a quantity of aerial yams
2. When cooked, peel them
3. Cut them in thin slices and fry them in lard or butter

title: Cará Ensopado (Stewed Yam)
body:
## Ingredients

- one onion
- parsley
- fat
- one yam peeled and cut into pieces
- one bottle of water
- salt
- one small spoon of fine cornmeal

## Directions

1. Fry one onion and a little chopped parsley in fat
2. Add one peeled and cut yam in pieces, one bottle of water, salt, one small spoon of fine cornmeal
3. Let it boil until cooked and the broth reduced, and serve

title: Cará Refogado (Braised Yam - Alternate Recipe)
body:
## Ingredients

- some yams
- bitter orange or lemon
- cold water
- hot fat
- onions
- salt
- garlic
- Indian peppers

## Directions

1. Peel some yams
2. Wash them with bitter orange or lemon and then in cold water
3. Break them into small pieces and put them in a pan with hot fat, onions, salt, garlic and Indian peppers
4. Add a little water, enough to cook the yam
5. Stir from time to time to thicken the broth and serve

title: Cará Refogado (Braised Yam)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a quarter of bacon
- one yam peeled and chopped
- one cup of meat broth
- one cup of white wine
- parsley
- pepper
- nutmeg
- one egg yolk
- juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Fry half a quarter of bacon
2. When the fat is melted, remove the cracklings and add one peeled and chopped yam
3. Stir and add one cup of meat broth, one cup of white wine, parsley, a little pepper and nutmeg
4. Boil until the yam is cooked, incorporating it with one egg yolk dissolved in one spoon of lemon juice, and serve

title: Castanhas do Pará, Pinhões e Sapucáias (Brazil Nuts, Pine Nuts and Sapucaia Nuts)
body:
## Ingredients

- Brazil nuts, pine nuts, or sapucaia nuts
- lard
- salt
- nutmeg
- milk

## Directions

1. Boil these fruits
2. When cooked, peel them and crush them in a mortar with lard, salt and nutmeg
3. Dilute the mixture with milk
4. Boil this pulp for half an hour and serve
5. Alternative: these fruits can also be boiled or roasted in hot coals and served peeled

title: Castanhas, Pinhões, etc. Guisados (Stewed Chestnuts, Pine Nuts, etc.)
body:
## Ingredients

- nuts (chestnuts, pine nuts, etc.)
- lard
- white wine
- grated cheese
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cook a quantity of these fruits
2. Peel them and crush them in a mortar
3. Sauté this mixture in melted lard
4. Immediately add two cups of white wine, grated cheese and the juice of a lemon
5. Boil for about a quarter of an hour and serve

title: Cavalla Frita (Fried Mackerel)
body:
## Ingredients

- one fish
- butter
- garlic
- salt
- pepper
- one spoon wheat flour
- two cups water
- lemon juice
- some capers

## Directions

1. Clean a fish and place it in a casserole with butter, garlic, salt and pepper
2. Fry the fish, turning it two or three times, and remove it
3. Add one spoon of wheat flour to the butter, stir
4. Add two cups of water, some lemon juice, and some capers
5. Let it boil and serve with the fish

title: Cavalla Guisado (Stewed Mackerel)
body:
## Ingredients

- a portion of sea water
- some fennel leaves
- one clean fish
- two spoons fresh butter
- one spoon wheat flour
- one cup milk
- one egg yolk
- one cup white wine
- some grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of sea water with some fennel leaves
2. When boiling, add the cleaned fish
3. Cook for half an hour and remove it
4. Place the fish near the fire to keep it warm
5. Melt two spoons of fresh butter and mix with one spoon of wheat flour
6. Add one cup of milk and one egg yolk beaten with one cup of white wine
7. Add some grated nutmeg and serve

title: Cavalla Rechado (Stuffed Mackerel)
body:
## Ingredients

- a handful of small fish (carapicús)
- water
- parsley
- one onion
- two spoons butter
- a handful of bread crumb soaked in milk
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- two clean fish
- paper leaves
- oil

## Directions

1. Boil a handful of carapicús in water, drain and chop finely
2. Mix with some parsley, one onion, two spoons of butter and a handful of bread crumb soaked in milk
3. Season with salt, pepper and nutmeg
4. Fill two cleaned fish with this mixture
5. Wrap them in oiled paper leaves
6. Roast on the grill for a quarter of an hour over moderate fire, turning many times
7. When well cooked, remove the paper and serve

title: Cavalla, Olho de Boi and Caranha Assados (Roasted Large Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- large fish
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- parsley, chopped
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean the fish and fill it with butter mixed with salt, pepper and chopped parsley.
2. Wrap it in a sheet of thick paper.
3. Roast it on a grill over moderate fire.
4. When cooked, serve it moistened with lemon juice.

title: Cavalla, Olho de Boi etc. Cozidos (Boiled Large Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- large fish
- water
- salt
- melted butter
- wheat flour
- parsley, chopped
- fish broth
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean and open a fish along the back for its entire length.
2. Boil a portion of water with plenty of salt.
3. When boiling, place the fish in it.
4. After half an hour, remove the fish.
5. Make a sauce of melted butter mixed with wheat flour and chopped parsley.
6. Add two cups of fish broth.
7. Let it boil a little more and serve with lemon juice on top.

title: Cavalla, Olho de Boi etc. Fritos (Fried Large Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- large fish
- butter
- peeled onions
- salt
- pepper
- wheat flour
- water
- lemon juice
- capers

## Directions

1. Clean a fish and place it in a casserole with butter, peeled onions, salt and pepper.
2. Fry the fish, turning it two or three times and remove it.
3. Add one tablespoon of wheat flour to the butter and stir.
4. Add two cups of water, some lemon juice and several capers.
5. Let it boil and serve.

title: Cebolas Assadas (Roasted Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of mutton meat
- a little chicken breast
- one raw egg
- salt
- pepper
- some boiled onions with centers removed
- grated bread
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Chop a quarter of mutton meat with a little chicken breast
2. Mix with one raw egg, salt and pepper
3. Fill some boiled onions with centers removed with this mixture
4. Arrange on a plate and cover with grated bread
5. Pour some melted butter on top, put in the oven until it takes good color, and serve

title: Cebolas Cozidas (Cooked Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two dozen onions
- two to three spoons of fresh butter
- half a bottle of water
- salt
- pepper
- one spoon of good vinegar
- one cup of wine

## Directions

1. Remove the skins from one or two dozen onions and put them in a pan with two or three spoons of fresh butter
2. Add half a bottle of water, salt and pepper
3. Let it boil for five minutes over a lively fire, then put the pan over coals until the broth is reduced
4. Remove the onions, put them on a plate and add to the sauce one spoon of good vinegar and one cup of wine
5. After boiling a little, pour this sauce over the onions and serve

title: Cenouras Ensopadas (Stewed Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- water
- butter
- wheat flour
- white wine
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of carrots in water and cut them into slices
2. Melt butter and wheat flour separately
3. Add the carrots to this mixture
4. Add two cups of white wine, salt, and pepper
5. Leave to boil

title: Cenouras Ensopadas (Stewed Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- some carrots
- water
- one spoon of butter
- one spoon of wheat flour
- two cups of white wine
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Boil some carrots in water and cut them into slices
2. On the other hand, melt one spoon of butter and another of wheat flour
3. Put the carrots in this mixture, add two cups of white wine, salt and pepper
4. Let it boil

title: Cenouras Guisadas (Braised Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- fat
- salt
- pepper
- sugar
- broth
- white wine

## Directions

1. Scrape a portion of carrots and cut them into slices
2. Fry them in fat with a little salt, pepper, and a spoon of sugar, turning them from time to time so they do not burn
3. When well fried, remove from fire and add a cup of broth and another of white wine
4. Finish cooking and serve with roasts of game

title: Cenouras Guisadas - Outro Modo (Braised Carrots - Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- wheat flour
- onion
- parsley
- fat
- carrots
- sugar
- salt
- water
- lemon juice
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Sauté a spoon of wheat flour, a chopped onion, and a little parsley in two spoons of fat
2. Add a portion of carrots cut into slices, a spoon of sugar, salt, two cups of water, and a glass of lemon juice
3. Leave to boil over moderate heat
4. When cooked, incorporate with an egg yolk and serve

title: Cenouras Guisadas com Hervilhas (Carrots Braised with Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- wheat flour
- butter
- water
- white wine
- salt
- parsley
- coriander
- young carrots
- green peas

## Directions

1. Sauté half a spoon of wheat flour in two spoons of butter
2. Add two cups of water, a glass of white wine, salt, parsley, and coriander
3. After boiling for an instant, add a portion of young carrots and green peas
4. Leave to cook and serve

title: Cenouras Preparadas com Manteiga (Carrots Prepared with Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- Spanish sauce

## Directions

1. Take a portion of carrots, scrape and cut them into very fine slices
2. After boiling once, drain them
3. Place them in a casserole with a little butter, salt, broken pepper, and a little nutmeg
4. Sauté over the fire, adding a good spoon of reduced Spanish sauce (in this case do not let them boil)
5. Add chopped chives and parsley and serve

title: Cenouras Refogadas (Braised Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- some carrots
- fresh butter
- one spoon of wheat flour
- salt
- onion leaves
- one cup of water
- pepper

## Directions

1. Peel some carrots by scraping and cut them into slices
2. Braise them in fresh butter with one spoon of wheat flour, a pinch of salt, chopped onion leaves
3. Add one cup of water, salt and pepper
4. Let it boil a little and serve with roasted poultry

title: Cenouras Refogadas com Nata (Braised Carrots with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- some carrots
- butter
- water
- salt
- parsley
- one cup of cream
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Braise in very little butter some carrots, adding water, salt, chopped parsley
2. When cooked, add one cup of cream, letting it finish cooking near the fire
3. Before serving, add two egg yolks dissolved with one spoon of cream

title: Cevadinha (Barley)
body:
## Ingredients

- a portion of barley
- water
- salt
- two spoonfuls of butter
- two cups of milk cream

## Directions

1. Cook a portion of barley in water and salt
2. When cooked, add two spoonfuls of butter and two cups of milk cream
3. Serve

title: Cevadinha Frita (Fried Barley)
body:
## Ingredients

- a portion of barley
- milk
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cook a portion of barley in milk
2. When cooked, let the milk coagulate
3. Cut afterwards into slices
4. Fry in butter
5. Serve covered with breadcrumbs

title: Chocolate (Chocolate)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of chocolate
- twelve cups of water

## Directions

1. Grate half a pound of chocolate
2. Place it in a vessel
3. Add twelve cups of water
4. Place over fire and let it boil until it thickens
5. Stir with a proper chocolate whisk
6. Serve

title: Chocolate com Leite (Chocolate with Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of chocolate
- half a pound of sugar
- two egg yolks
- two bottles of milk
- ground cinnamon
- half a spoonful of flower water

## Directions

1. Grate half a pound of chocolate
2. Place it in a vessel with half a pound of sugar and two beaten egg yolks
3. Add two bottles of milk
4. Beat with a proper chocolate whisk
5. Place over fire to boil, continuing to beat
6. After it has boiled, add a little ground cinnamon and half a spoonful of flower water
7. Remove from fire and serve hot

title: Chocolate com Âmbar (Chocolate with Amber)
body:
## Ingredients

- chocolate
- amber

## Directions

1. Prepare chocolate in the manner already indicated
2. Add one scruple of amber for each pound of chocolate
3. This chocolate is excellent for restoring strength lost through sexual excess or sleep loss, for those suffering in humid weather, for those with dulled thoughts, and for those with a fixed idea that troubles and prevents their peace

title: Chouriço (Portuguese Pork Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- some onions, finely chopped
- rendered lard
- pork fat cut into small pieces
- pork blood
- milk
- salt
- seasonings

## Directions

1. Chop some onions very finely and cook them in rendered lard
2. When cooked, add a portion of lard cut into small pieces and mix with the pork blood, milk, salt and seasonings
3. Mix everything well
4. Fill cleaned intestines with this mixture, tying at one end
5. Prick with a pin from time to time if there is too much air inside
6. When the intestine is full, tie the other end
7. Cook these sausages in water for only a few minutes until done
8. To know if they are cooked, remove one from the vessel and prick it with a pin; if blood no longer comes out and fat comes out instead, it is cooked
9. Remove from the vessel and let cool
10. When you wish to eat them, place them on the grill, being careful to prick them so they do not burst
11. They may also be fried if desired

title: Chouriço Allemão (German-style Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork blood
- vinegar
- pork ears
- snout
- tongue
- heart
- lungs
- parts of belly skin with little fat
- water
- salt
- liver
- onions, chopped
- marjoram
- basil
- thyme
- pepper
- cloves
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Prepare the pork blood by placing a little vinegar in the vessel so it does not clot
2. Cut the ears, snout, tongue, heart, lungs and parts of belly skin that have little fat
3. Put everything to boil in a pan with water and salt
4. When everything is cooked, cut it into small pieces
5. Mix it with the blood and mashed liver, salt, chopped onions, marjoram, basil, thyme, pepper, cloves and nutmeg
6. Fill well-washed intestines with this mixture
7. After boiling, store them

title: Chouriço Branco (White Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve onions, cut into small pieces
- caul fat, rendered
- bread crumb soaked in milk
- roasted bird meat
- bread crumb
- caul fat cut into small pieces
- salt
- pepper from India, crushed
- garlic
- chives
- parsley
- six raw egg yolks
- half bottle of milk

## Directions

1. Cut twelve onions into small pieces and cook them in rendered caul fat
2. Add bread crumb boiled in milk and the meat of a roasted bird
3. Chop everything well and pound with bread crumb, using equal parts of bread crumb, bird meat, and caul fat cut into small pieces
4. Add salt, crushed pepper from India, garlic, chives, parsley, six raw egg yolks and half bottle of milk
5. Mix everything in a pan where the cooked onions are
6. Fill intestines with this mixture, leaving them an empty space so they do not burst
7. Tie well and put to cook in milk mixed with half water, not allowing it to boil
8. Remove from heat when cooked and let cool
9. When you wish to eat them, place them over coals or on the grill after wrapping them in paper coated with butter
10. Instead of bird meat, any other meat may be substituted

title: Chouriço de Figado de Porco (Pork Liver Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pork liver
- six onions
- rich meat broth
- eight ounces of caul fat, cut into pieces
- one quarter of a bottle of rich milk
- half bottle of blood
- salt, ground
- piri-piri pepper
- pepper from India
- parsley
- chives
- thyme
- winter savory
- coriander

## Directions

1. Take one liver, chop it well
2. Cook six onions in rich meat broth, chop them and mix with the already chopped liver
3. Add eight ounces of caul fat cut into pieces and one quarter of a bottle of rich milk and half a bottle of blood prepared as already described
4. Season everything with ground salt, piri-piri pepper, pepper from India, parsley, chives, thyme, winter savory and coriander
5. Place over a gentle fire only to warm through, stirring so that the blood does not stick to the bottom of the vessel
6. Fill intestines with this mixture in the manner already explained
7. After the sausages are ready, cook them

title: Chouriço de Fígado de Porco (Pork Liver Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pork liver
- six onions
- fatty meat broth
- eight ounces of caul fat, cut into pieces
- one quarter of a bottle of fatty milk
- one half bottle of blood, prepared as previously stated
- salt
- pimenta comari
- pimenta da India
- parsley
- chives
- thyme
- marjoram
- cilantro

## Directions

1. Chop the liver well.
2. Cook six onions in fatty meat broth, chop them, and mix with the already chopped liver.
3. Add eight ounces of caul fat cut into pieces, one quarter bottle of fatty milk, and one half bottle of blood that has been prepared as previously stated.
4. Season the whole mixture with ground salt, pimenta comari, pimenta da India, parsley, chives, thyme, marjoram, and cilantro.
5. Place over low heat only to warm through, stirring so that the blood does not stick to the bottom of the vessel.
6. Fill the casings with this mixture in the same manner as previously explained.
7. Once the sausages are ready, cook them in meat broth.
8. After draining, store them.
9. When you want to eat them, roast them on the grill wrapped in paper greased with butter.

title: Chouriço de Vitella (Calf Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- tripe
- calf blood
- fresh bacon
- calf tongue
- calf heart
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- marjoram
- onion
- parsley
- thyme
- water

## Directions

1. Clean and turn the tripe inside out
2. Fill with a mass made of calf blood passed through a sieve, a piece of fresh bacon cut, the tongue and heart of the calf cut in long strips, salt, pepper, cloves, marjoram, onion, parsley and thyme, all finely chopped
3. Cook the sausages in water and salt
4. Before serving, fry in a little fat

title: Chouriço do Sangue (Blood Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork blood
- vinegar
- a dozen onions, chopped
- four bottles of blood
- three pounds of caul fat, chopped
- parsley
- chives, chopped
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- cloves
- bay leaves
- thyme
- marjoram
- one bottle of milk

## Directions

1. Prepare the pork blood by placing a little vinegar in the vessel beforehand and mixing well to prevent the blood from clotting
2. Cook a dozen chopped onions
3. Add four bottles of blood, three pounds of chopped caul fat, parsley, chopped chives, salt, pepper, nutmeg, cloves, some bay leaves, thyme, marjoram, and one bottle of milk
4. Mix everything well so that the fat does not become lumpy
5. Introduce this mixture into well-washed intestines, tying one end
6. When full, tie the other end, leaving a small empty space so they do not burst
7. Cook and keep in hot water, being careful that it does not boil
8. When they begin to harden and when pricked with a pin release fat, they are cooked
9. Remove from heat, let drain, and dry with a cloth
10. If desired, give them a nice gloss by coating them with a piece of lard
11. When you wish to eat them, prick them so they do not burst and place them on the grill over a gentle fire
12. They may also be cut into rounds and fried in fat

title: Chouriço à Mineira (Minas Gerais-style Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork blood
- salt
- caul fat, cut into small pieces
- garlic, chopped
- onion, chopped
- parsley
- chives, chopped
- marjoram
- thyme
- winter savory
- piri-piri pepper
- cloves
- pepper from India
- cinnamon, finely ground
- half pound of refined sugar
- cassava flour or cornmeal, sifted

## Directions

1. Prepare the blood in a vessel in which you will have put a little salt, and stir so it does not clot
2. Cut the caul fat into small pieces
3. Finely chop the garlic, onion, parsley, chives, marjoram, thyme, winter savory and piri-piri pepper
4. Add to this cloves, pepper from India, finely ground cinnamon and half pound of refined sugar
5. Thicken the blood with cassava flour or sifted cornmeal
6. Proceed in the manner already explained

title: Chouriços de Frango (Chicken Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- skin of four to five chickens, plucked and cleaned
- breast meat
- thighs
- half a pound of bacon, all finely chopped
- one old bread, grated
- salt
- pepper
- clove
- parsley
- marjoram
- Madeira wine
- water and salt
- bacon slices
- one glass of white wine
- cooked fava beans

## Directions

1. Take the skin of four to five chickens, after plucking and cleaning.
2. Fill them with a mixture made of breast meat, thighs, half a pound of bacon, all finely chopped, one old bread grated, salt, pepper, clove, parsley and marjoram.
3. Moisten with Madeira wine and boil them a little in water and salt.
4. Then place them in a pan with slices of bacon and one glass of white wine.
5. Boil them over coals until cooked, and serve with sautéed fava beans.

title: Churasco de Carne de Anta (Grilled Tapir Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- a good portion of tapir meat with skin attached
- salt
- lemon juice
- comaris peppers

## Directions

1. Cut a good portion of tapir meat, leaving the skin attached to the meat
2. Rub it with plenty of salt
3. Place it in the oven to roast
4. Serve with the skin serving as a platter, accompanied by a sauce of lemon juice and comaris peppers

title: Churrasco à Moda de Sertão (Backlands-Style Grilled Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- good piece of beef from the breast and rib section, without removing the hide
- salt
- garlic
- butter

## Directions

1. Take a good piece of beef from the breast and rib section without removing the hide; pass over it a little salt ground with garlic and butter; place it on a spit, exposing it to an ardent fire.
2. The meat becomes well toasted on the outside and inside barely cooked with a little blood still present. This is how most backlands people prepare their churrasco; some, however, roast it in the oven when they have one at their disposal, which rarely happens in those desert regions.
3. Serve at table without sauce, if desired, or then with a spicy sauce.

title: Chá (Tea)
body:
## Ingredients

- tea leaves
- hot water

## Directions

1. Place two teaspoons of tea in a pot
2. Pour one cup of hot water over it
3. Bring the pot to the table with another pot of boiling water
4. Pour the boiling water over the tea in the quantity needed
5. Cover the pot and let it steep for two minutes
6. Serve

title: Coatí Assado no Espeto (Coati Roasted on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- one coati, skinned and cleaned
- salt
- pepper juice
- tomato juice
- chicken blood

## Directions

1. Rub the coati with salt, pepper juice and tomato juice
2. Leave with these seasonings for three hours to absorb the flavors
3. Anoint with chicken blood
4. Place on a spit and roast over high heat at first, then moderate to take on good color and serve

title: Coatí Lardeado e Assado no Forno (Coati Larded and Roasted in the Oven)
body:
## Ingredients

- two chicken breasts, chopped
- two onions
- four boiled eggs
- one peeled cassava root
- two ounces of bacon
- parsley
- peppers
- a dozen tomatoes
- salt
- vinegar
- bacon strips for larding
- bacon slices for lining the pan

## Directions

1. Chop two chicken breasts, two onions, four boiled eggs, one peeled cassava root, two ounces of bacon, parsley, peppers, a dozen tomatoes and salt
2. Fill the coati with this stuffing
3. After cleaning, rub with salt and vinegar
4. Lard the loin with fine strips of bacon
5. Place in a pan lined with bacon slices and roast over moderate heat
6. When roasted, serve with purslane salad

title: Coatí Refogado com Abóbora d'Água (Coati Sautéed with Water Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen tomatoes
- two spoons of fat
- one chopped onion
- parsley
- a portion of water squash, chopped in small pieces
- one coatí, cut in pieces
- one cup of water
- the same amount of sour orange juice

## Directions

1. Pass half a dozen tomatoes through two spoons of fat, one chopped onion and parsley; then add a portion of water squash chopped into small pieces and one coatí cut in pieces
2. Add afterwards one cup of water and the same amount of sour orange juice

title: Coatí Refogado com Abóbora d'Água (Coati Stewed with Water Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen tomatoes
- two spoons of fat
- one chopped onion
- parsley
- a portion of water squash chopped into small pieces
- one coati cut into pieces
- one cup of water
- the same amount of sour orange juice
- one small spoon of cassava starch
- one small spoon of sugar

## Directions

1. Strain the tomatoes in two spoons of fat, with a chopped onion and parsley
2. Add a portion of water squash chopped into small pieces and a coati cut into pieces
3. Add one cup of water, another cup of sour orange juice, one small spoon of cassava starch and one of sugar
4. Leave to boil on low heat until cooked and serve

title: Coatí Refogado de Escabeche (Coati Stewed in Escabeche)
body:
## Ingredients

- one coati, somewhat old
- one bottle of aguardente
- one cup of vinegar
- parsley
- salt
- peppers
- cloves
- garlic
- bay leaves
- ginger
- cow's butter
- onions
- one spoon of wheat flour
- one cup of wine
- one egg yolk

## Directions

1. Wrap the coati in cloth and place it in a bottle of aguardente with one cup of vinegar, parsley, salt, peppers, cloves, garlic, bay leaves, and ginger
2. Leave in this marinade for twenty-four hours, turning it two or three times
3. Cut the coati into pieces and fry with some onions in cow's butter
4. Sprinkle with one spoon of wheat flour and add one cup of wine in which an egg yolk has been dissolved
5. Leave to boil near the fire until the meat is very cooked and serve

title: Cobra Assada (Roasted Snake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one snake
- salt
- pepper juice
- garlic
- sour orange juice
- egg yolks
- bacon slices
- onion
- parsley
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Take a snake and rub with salt, pepper juice, garlic, and sour orange juice
2. Pass through egg yolks and place in a casserole on bacon slices, covering with others
3. Place chopped onion, well-chopped parsley and grated nutmeg on top
4. Roast over coals, covering, and placing a lid on top as well
5. It will be roasted in about one hour and serve with some compote

title: Cobra Frita (Fried Snake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one snake, cut into pieces
- sour orange juice
- salt
- parsley
- onion
- cloves
- ginger
- fat
- one chopped onion
- breadcrumbs
- boiled yam root or cassava

## Directions

1. Place the snake cut into pieces in a marinade of sour orange juice, salt, parsley, onion, cloves, and ginger
2. After six hours, fry it in plenty of fat with a chopped onion and a portion of breadcrumbs
3. Serve over cooked yam or cassava root boiled in water and salt

title: Cobra Guisada (Snake Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- one snake
- water
- salt
- peppers
- parsley
- onion
- marjoram
- cloves
- nutmeg
- ginger
- one spoon of wheat flour
- two spoons of fat
- one bottle of boiled milk
- two egg yolks
- one spoon of sugar

## Directions

1. Boil a snake in water with salt, peppers, parsley, onion, marjoram, cloves, nutmeg and ginger
2. When cooked, remove and separate the meat from the bones
3. Stew the meat with one spoon of wheat flour and two spoons of fat, not allowing it to fry too much
4. Add one bottle of boiled milk with two egg yolks and one spoon of sugar
5. Leave to boil for a quarter hour and serve

title: Cobra, Lagarto ou Rã Refogados (Snake, Lizard or Frog Stewed)
body:
## Ingredients

- one snake, cut into pieces
- two spoons of fat
- one chopped onion
- one spoon of wheat flour
- one cup of water
- salt
- parsley
- peppers
- a little grated nutmeg
- two egg yolks
- one cup of wine

## Directions

1. Cut a snake into pieces and stew with two spoons of fat and a chopped onion
2. Sprinkle with one spoon of wheat flour and one cup of water, salt, parsley, peppers, and a little grated nutmeg
3. Leave to boil near the fire until cooked
4. Incorporate the broth with two egg yolks dissolved in a cup of wine and serve

title: Coelho Assado no Espeto em Calda de Açúcar (Roasted Rabbit on a Spit in Sugar Syrup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit
- fat for wrapping
- one pound of sugar syrup
- half pound of roasted and crushed peanuts
- cloves
- cinnamon
- one handful of breadcrumbs
- eight egg yolks
- four spoonfuls of grated cheese
- cinnamon powder

## Directions

1. Wrap a rabbit in paper greased with fat and roast it on the spit
2. Remove the bones and chop the meat very finely
3. Mix the meat in one pound of sugar syrup, half pound of roasted and crushed peanuts, cloves and cinnamon
4. When ready, add one handful of breadcrumbs, eight egg yolks, four spoonfuls of grated cheese
5. Mix and place in a compote dish, covering with ground cinnamon
6. Serve as a dessert

title: Coelho Assado no Espeto à Caçador (Rabbit Roasted on a Spit Hunter Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- salt
- bacon lard
- pan drippings
- onion
- parsley
- cloves
- pepper
- ginger
- saffron

## Directions

1. Rub a rabbit with salt, wrap it in fine slices of bacon lard, and roast it on a spit
2. When roasted, remove it and pour the drippings that fell in the dripping pan into a sauce with one chopped onion, parsley, cloves, pepper, ginger and a little saffron
3. Leave to cook over a low fire
4. Place the rabbit on a plate, pouring the prepared sauce over it
5. Make several cuts in different places with the tip of a knife so that the sauce penetrates the rabbit and serve it

title: Coelho Assado no Espeto à Princesa (Rabbit Roasted on a Spit Princess Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- salt
- peppers
- tomatoes
- bacon lard
- breadcrumbs
- milk cream
- cloves

## Directions

1. Remove the head of a rabbit and rub the body with salt, peppers and tomatoes, leaving it in these seasonings for five to six hours
2. Then lard it with slices of bacon, trimming their ends flush with the meat
3. Then wrap the rabbit in breadcrumbs, thread it onto a spit, roast it, basting it with milk cream to which some cloves have been added

title: Coelho Assado no Espeto à Princeza (Roasted Rabbit on a Spit à Princesse)
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit
- salt
- peppers
- tomatoes
- lardoon slices of bacon
- breadcrumbs
- cream of milk
- cloves
- cinnamon
- sugar
- lemon

## Directions

1. Remove the head of a rabbit and rub the body with salt, peppers and tomatoes, leaving it in these seasonings for five to six hours
2. Lard it with thin slices of bacon, trimming their ends flush with the meat
3. Wrap the rabbit in breadcrumbs, thread it on the spit
4. Roast it, basting it with cream of milk to which some cloves, cinnamon and a little sugar have been added
5. When roasted, serve it with slices of lemon placed on top, and its sauce served separately

title: Coelho com Torradas (Rabbit with Toast)
body:
## Ingredients

- three spoonfuls of fat
- one onion
- parsley
- rabbit pieces
- salt
- cumin
- lemon slices
- orange juice
- bread slices
- butter
- grated cheese
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Fry an onion and a little chopped parsley in three spoonfuls of fat
2. Add the pieces of the rabbit cut up, with salt, cumin, slices of lemon and orange juice, and let them boil
3. When cooked, place on slices of bread fried in butter, which will have been placed at the bottom of a plate, and on which a layer of grated cheese will have been placed
4. Place on top of this a portion of melted butter

title: Coelho Ensopado com Gerumbeba (Rabbit Stewed with Prickly Pears)
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit
- salt
- garlic
- peppers
- tomatoes
- three spoonfuls of fat
- prickly pears
- one spoonful of wheat flour
- water or meat broth

## Directions

1. Cut the rabbit into quarters and soak these in salt, garlic, peppers, and crushed tomatoes for twenty-four hours
2. Sauté with the same seasonings in three spoonfuls of fat
3. Add a portion of peeled prickly pears, mix and sprinkle with one spoonful of wheat flour
4. Add a bowlful of water or meat broth and serve

title: Coelho Ensopado com Grão de Bico (Rabbit Stewed with Chickpeas)
body:
## Ingredients

- three spoonfuls of wheat flour
- three spoonfuls of butter
- rabbit pieces
- one cup of vinegar
- one bowlful of water
- salt
- parsley
- onion
- pepper
- chickpeas
- two egg yolks
- half cup of water
- three spoonfuls of sugar

## Directions

1. Toast three spoonfuls of wheat flour in three spoonfuls of butter until browned
2. Add the pieces of a rabbit and immediately add one cup of vinegar, one bowlful of water, salt, parsley, onion, pepper, and a portion of chickpeas
3. Let it cook and when cooked and the broth reduced, incorporate the broth with two egg yolks beaten in half cup of water and three spoonfuls of sugar
4. Serve

title: Coelho Frito (Fried Rabbit)
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit pieces
- water
- salt
- parsley
- breadcrumbs
- egg yolks
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil the pieces of a rabbit in water, salt and parsley
2. When cooked, remove and let cool
3. Roll the pieces in breadcrumbs, cover with egg yolks, fry in butter, and serve

title: Coelho Guisado com Guandús (Rabbit Stewed with Pigeon Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit
- water
- salt
- parsley
- ginger
- peppers
- pigeon peas
- two spoonfuls of butter kneaded with one spoonful of wheat flour
- two egg yolks
- one cup of vinegar
- two spoonfuls of sugar

## Directions

1. Boil a rabbit in water, salt, parsley, ginger, peppers, and a portion of pigeon peas
2. Remove the rabbit and reduce the broth
3. At the end add two spoonfuls of butter kneaded with one spoonful of wheat flour, and two egg yolks beaten in one cup of vinegar mixed with two spoonfuls of sugar
4. After it has boiled a little, add the rabbit, continue to boil a little and serve

title: Coelho Guisado com Leite (Rabbit Stewed with Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit
- water
- salt
- half pound of veal
- one onion
- nutmeg
- one bottle of milk
- bread crumbs
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook the rabbit in water and salt, remove the bones, chop the meat very finely with half pound of veal, one onion, salt, and grated nutmeg
2. On another part, bring one bottle of milk to boil with a portion of bread crumbs and a little salt, letting it reduce to one third
3. Add the chopped meat at this time, and as soon as it heats serve it

title: Coelho Guisado com Molho Pardo (Rabbit Stewed with Brown Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit
- bacon sheets
- one bottle of water
- one bottle of red wine
- salt
- parsley
- two onions
- one clove of garlic
- bay leaves
- peppers
- coriander
- rabbit liver
- two chicken livers

## Directions

1. Wrap a rabbit in sheets of bacon and roast it in the oven
2. Cut it into pieces and place them in the following sauce: boil one bottle of water, another of red wine, with salt, parsley, two sliced onions, one clove of garlic, bay leaves, peppers, coriander, the rabbit's liver and two chicken livers
3. When the broth is reduced to half, strain it, having placed the rabbit pieces in this sauce until they heat through, and serve

title: Coelho Lardado e Assado no Espeto (Larded and Roasted Rabbit on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit
- thin strips of bacon
- white wine
- two spoonfuls of butter
- salt
- parsley
- garlic
- onions
- peppers

## Directions

1. Lard the entire rabbit with thin strips of bacon
2. After removing the head, thread it on the spit and roast it, moistening it with white wine to which two spoonfuls of butter, salt, chopped parsley, garlic, sliced onions and peppers have been added
3. When roasted, serve it

title: Coelho Recheado e Ensopado (Stuffed and Stewed Rabbit)
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit
- half pound of pork meat
- carapicús
- one onion
- four hard-cooked egg yolks
- salt
- peppers
- nutmeg
- thread
- one bottle of water
- one bottle of wine
- parsley
- marjoram
- coriander
- garlic
- cloves
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- lemon

## Directions

1. Take a rabbit, remove its bones, leaving only the head
2. Chop half pound of fatty pork meat, carapicús, one onion, four hard-cooked egg yolks, salt, peppers, and nutmeg
3. Fill the rabbit with this mixture, sew the opening with thread, and cook it with one bottle of water, another of wine, salt, parsley, marjoram, coriander, garlic, and cloves
4. When cooked, remove and wrap it in breadcrumb crusts passed in butter
5. Pour the strained and reduced broth on top, and some slices of peeled lemon, and serve

title: Coelho Refogado com Agrião (Rabbit Stewed with Watercress)
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- pork fat
- onion
- salt
- peppers
- parsley
- white wine
- watercress

## Directions

1. Cut the rabbit into pieces and sauté them in a little pork fat until well browned
2. Add one chopped onion, salt, peppers, parsley, and a cup of white wine
3. When the pieces are cooked, sauté a portion of chopped and scalded watercress in the same sauce
4. Place the watercress at the bottom of the plate and the pieces on top and serve

title: Coelho Refogado com Alcachofras (Rabbit Stewed with Artichokes)
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- gordura
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- garlic
- water
- wine
- artichoke bottoms
- starch
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut a rabbit into pieces and pass them in hot fat with one chopped onion, salt, pepper, parsley and a clove of garlic
2. Add a cup of water and another of wine
3. Leave to boil until cooked
4. When cooked, add some artichoke bottoms cooked in water and salt and cut into slices, and a small spoonful of starch dissolved in water and lemon juice
5. Leave to boil and serve

title: Coelho Refogado com Batatinhas (Rabbit Stewed with Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- gordura
- rabbit
- potatoes
- water
- salt
- peppers

## Directions

1. Fry one onion in fat
2. When the onion is fried, sauté the pieces of a rabbit in this fat with a portion of raw peeled potatoes cut into slices
3. Add one cup of water, salt and peppers
4. Leave to cook over low fire and serve

title: Coelho Refogado com Cebolas (Rabbit Stewed with Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- gordura
- small onions
- rabbit
- red wine
- parsley
- salt
- peppers
- bay leaf
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Fry in three spoonfuls of fat a dozen small whole onions
2. When browned, add a rabbit cut into pieces
3. Stir, adding one bottle of red wine, parsley, salt, peppers, bay leaf, and nutmeg
4. Leave to cook near the fire and serve

title: Coelho Refogado à Bahiana (Rabbit Stewed Bahian Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- gordura
- wheat flour
- broth
- orange juice
- comari peppers
- pimentos
- ginger
- salt
- parsley
- carapicús

## Directions

1. After cleaning and cutting the rabbit, place it in a casserole with four spoonfuls of hot fat
2. When browned, remove it, lightly coat it in wheat flour, and put it back in the casserole with one cup of broth, another of orange juice, some comari peppers, two pimentos, a little grated ginger, salt, parsley, a portion of selected carapicús, and the fat in which the rabbit was fried
3. Boil a little and when well cooked, serve it

title: Coelho à Portuguesa (Rabbit Portuguese Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- water
- wine
- salt
- parsley
- marjoram
- nutmeg
- onions
- gordura or azeite doce
- pepper
- vinegar
- broth

## Directions

1. Cut a rabbit into pieces and boil them in water, wine, salt, parsley, marjoram and nutmeg
2. On the other hand, cut a portion of onions into slices and fry them in a casserole with two spoonfuls of fat or sweet oil, salt, and pepper
3. When beginning to boil, add one spoonful of vinegar and another of broth, not stirring, so that the onions remain whole
4. Add the cooked rabbit, heat and serve

title: Comida Branca (White Almond Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of sweet almonds
- eight bitter almonds
- milk
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take one pound of sweet almonds and eight bitter almonds; crush them well after preparing.
2. Bring milk and sugar to a boil, and dissolve the almonds in the boiling milk, then pass through a fine sieve.
3. Return to the fire and let cook, stirring, and reduce.
4. When thick, place on a dish and serve.

title: Comida Branca Fria (Cold White Almond Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- almonds prepared as for comida branca
- isinglass
- water

## Directions

1. Prepare the almonds as already described for comida branca and pass through a fine sieve.
2. Take isinglass broken into pieces and beaten in a cup of water; let the isinglass cook for two and a half hours and mix it with the cream which should be lukewarm.
3. Pour into a vessel or mold that is placed in water to cool.

title: Compota de Amoras (Blackberry Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds blackberries
- one pound sugar syrup at thread stage
- cinnamon
- fennel
- clove

## Directions

1. Take two pounds of blackberries and boil them in little water until they break apart
2. Add one pound of sugar syrup at thread stage, cinnamon, fennel and clove
3. Give it another boil
4. Serve with roasts

title: Compota de Araçã, Goiaba, Maracujá (Guava, Passion Fruit Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- fruit pulp with seeds
- one pound sugar syrup at thread stage
- cinnamon
- clove
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Take the pulp with seeds from these fruits
2. Put one pound of pulp in half the sugar syrup at thread stage with cinnamon, clove and nutmeg
3. Boil a little
4. Serve with roasts

title: Compota de Bananas (Banana Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- one dozen bananas
- cow butter
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Peel and fry one dozen bananas in cow butter
2. When fried, place on a plate
3. Sprinkle with cinnamon
4. Serve

title: Compota de Joáz (Joáz Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds joáz
- cinnamon
- clove
- one pound sugar

## Directions

1. Take two pounds of joáz and boil them in little water with cinnamon and clove
2. When cooked, strain through a sieve and add the syrup of one pound of sugar
3. Boil until it forms a thin porridge
4. Serve

title: Compota de Maçãs com Passas (Apple Compote with Raisins)
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- one pound of apple pulp
- one quarter pound sugar syrup
- one quarter pound washed raisins
- one quarter pound peeled and chopped almonds
- fennel
- one cup orange flower water

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of apples cut in pieces until they break apart and the water evaporates
2. Pass through a sieve
3. Mix one pound of this pulp with one quarter pound of sugar syrup, one quarter pound of washed raisins, one quarter pound of peeled and chopped almonds, a little fennel and one cup of orange flower water
4. Give it another boil
5. Serve

title: Compota de Sumo de Laranja (Orange Juice Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- oranges
- cinnamon
- clove
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel a portion of oranges and remove the seeds
2. Put them in a pan to boil, stirring with a breadcrumb spoon, a little cinnamon and clove
3. Pass through a sieve and thicken the juice until it forms a thin porridge
4. Serve; if the oranges are sour, add half a quarter pound of sugar for each orange juice strained through a sieve

title: Conserva de Porco Fresco à Brasileira (Fresh Pork Preserve Brazilian Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat
- garlic wine
- water

## Directions

1. Cut the pork meat into portions and place in garlic wine for twenty-four hours
2. Then place in a vessel to cook in little water, being careful to turn the pieces so they cook evenly
3. Allow the water to reduce, at which point the fat from the portions melts and the meat takes on a suitable color
4. Remove the portions and place them in an earthenware or glazed vessel
5. Pour over them the melted fat in which the portions were fried, ensuring they remain well covered with fat
6. When wishing to use them, remove the desired portions with a fork, being careful to level the fat so it covers the remaining pieces
7. When wishing to serve them, fry them in fat and serve with one of the sauces indicated for meat

title: Conservação da Carne em Salmoura (Preservation of Meat in Brine)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into pieces and place in a barrel between layers of salt
2. After two days, remove and hang in the air and let dry
3. Place again between layers of salt
4. To use this meat, give it one boiling with water
5. Pour out this water and place the meat in cold water for twelve to eighteen hours
6. In this state it can be used

title: Conservação da Carne em Salmoura à Ingleza (Preservation of Meat in Brine English Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- salt
- brown sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into pieces
2. Scald and dry it and rub with salt
3. Hang it in the air and then place in layers between salt and brown sugar inside a barrel
4. Cover and keep stored
5. Use one quarter of sugar for six pounds of salt

title: Conservação da Carne Frita (Preservation of Fried Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- water
- salt
- fat

## Directions

1. Boil the meat cut into pieces in little water and salt
2. Remove and dry with a cloth
3. Fry a portion of fat
4. Add the meat and let it cook until browned
5. Remove and place in pans
6. Pour hot fat on top
7. To use it, simply heat it

title: Conservação da Manteiga Fresca (Preservation of Fresh Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- fine salt
- saltpeter
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Take a portion of butter and wash several times in cold water, kneading with a wooden spoon until the water comes out clear
2. Squeeze the butter so it has no moisture
3. Mix four parts fine salt, one of ground saltpeter and one of white sugar
4. Take one ounce of the mixture for each pound of butter, with which it is well incorporated and placed in pieces in a pot, pressing well so it contains no air
5. By this process it keeps fresh and good-tasting for a long time

title: Conservação das Vagens Frescas (Preservation of Fresh Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh beans
- water

## Directions

1. Pick the beans on a sunny day; select the most ripened and perfect ones, place in a wide-mouth glass jar; when the jar is full, place it to boil in a pot with water, letting the water reach to the mouth of the jar; let it boil for half an hour and seal the jar well; the beans can thus keep fresh for a year and more.

title: Conservação de Carapicús (Preservation of Carapicú)
body:
## Ingredients

- carapicú
- vinegar
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Select and wash the carapicú
2. Give them one boiling in vinegar and leave to drain
3. When dry, keep between layers of salt
4. To use them, soak for some hours in cold water and wash well

title: Conservação de Carapicús e Cogumelos (Preservation of Carapicú and Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- carapicú
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Select and clean the carapicú
2. Place in a pan with water and much salt and bring to a boil
3. After boiling, remove and dry in the sun or oven and keep in a sack for when needed
4. When using them, soak for six hours in warm water, then boil them, and when drained they are ready to use

title: Conservação de Couves à Marinheira (Preservation of Cabbage Sailor Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- salt
- cloves
- vinegar
- brandy

## Directions

1. Chop a portion of fine cabbage
2. Scald in hot water and squeeze well
3. Place in a barrel or pan with plenty of salt and some cloves
4. Fill the pan with hot vinegar to which a quarter part of brandy is added
5. Cover the vessel and keep stored

title: Conservação de Hervilhas, Couve Flor e Vagens Cozidas (Preservation of Cooked Peas, Cauliflower and Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- peas or cauliflower or beans
- water
- salt
- fat
- onion
- wheat flour

## Directions

1. Take a portion of any of these vegetables and place in a pan with little water and salt and boil for a quarter hour
2. While still hot, pour into wide-mouthed jars, which should be almost full
3. Place these jars in a pot with water, the water reaching to the neck of the jars, and bring to a boil for fifteen to twenty minutes
4. Cover the jars well and keep stored
5. To use them, melt a little fat in a pan and fry with a little chopped onion and a little wheat flour
6. Add the vegetables and after cooking for half an hour, serve

title: Conservação de Marmelos, Maçãs e Peras (Preservation of Quinces, Apples and Pears)
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces, apples or pears
- twine
- paper

## Directions

1. Tie a piece of twine to the stem of each fruit that you want to pick, cutting the fruit above the knot
2. Place each fruit in a paper wrapper, leaving the ends of the twine tied to the stem of each fruit
3. Close the wrapper, hanging the fruits in an aired room
4. These fruits are conserved as well as possible, becoming even more flavorful with time

title: Conservação de Palmito e Espargos (Preservation of Palm Heart and Asparagus)
body:
## Ingredients

- palm heart
- asparagus
- water
- salt
- melted fat

## Directions

1. Cut a palm heart into pieces half a palm in length; for asparagus, remove the white part from below; boil either of these vegetables in water and salt for a quarter of an hour; pour out the water and dry the vegetables with a towel; place in a pan, pour melted fat over the top, and keep.

title: Conservação de Pepinos Pequenos e Beterrabas em Vinagre (Preservation of Small Cucumbers and Beets in Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- small cucumbers or beets
- strong vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- mustard
- chives
- spirits of wine

## Directions

1. Take a portion of small cucumbers or beets, wash them and place in a pan with strong vinegar and salt; bring to a boil once; remove the pan, let cool, and after three days, strain the vinegar which is then boiled with pepper, mustard, chives, and poured hot over the cucumbers or beets; add a glass of spirits of wine and place in a wide-mouth glass jar which is sealed and kept.

title: Conservação de Todas as Qualidades de Frutas (Preservation of All Kinds of Fruits)
body:
## Ingredients

- fruit
- cornmeal flour

## Directions

1. Pick the fruits before they are fully ripe and on a dry day
2. Place on straw in a well-aired and dry room
3. If the days are cold, place a brazier in the room
4. Then spread a layer of sifted cornmeal flour in a barrel
5. Place a layer of fruits on top so they do not touch each other
6. Fill the barrel in this manner, cover well and keep in a warm and dry place

title: Conservação do Leite Cozido (Preservation of Cooked Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- crystallized soda

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of milk
2. Add to each bottle of milk one eighth of crystallized soda
3. Pour hot into bottles and cover well
4. Place these afterward in a pot with water and boil for half an hour
5. Remove the bottles and seal immediately
6. By this method the milk keeps fresh for one to two months

title: Conservação do Peixe Fresco (Preservation of Fresh Fish) - Method 1
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh fish
- bread crumb
- spirits of wine

## Directions

1. As soon as the fish is caught, soak a portion of bread crumb in spirits of wine and fill the throat and mouth of the fish with it; in this manner the fish is preserved alive in a torpor for fifteen to twenty days; removing the bread from its mouth and placing it in water, it will revive in a few minutes.

title: Conservação do Peixe Fresco em Vinagre (Preservation of Fresh Fish in Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh fish
- salt
- vinegar
- pepper
- clove
- onion
- parsley

## Directions

1. As soon as the fish is caught, scale and clean it well without breaking it; apply plenty of salt, leaving it in this brine for three to four hours; cut into portions, placing them in a pan that is boiling with vinegar, pepper, clove, onion and parsley; after three to four boils, remove with a skimmer, place in a barrel, and pour cold strained vinegar over the top; cover and keep.

title: Conservação dos Melões Frescos (Preservation of Fresh Melons)
body:
## Ingredients

- melons
- ash

## Directions

1. Pick the melons
2. Place in a dark aired room heated by a brazier or in the sun for two or three days
3. Place the melons in a box with sifted ash so they do not touch each other
4. Thus they can be preserved three months and more

title: Conservação dos Ovos Frescos (Preservation of Fresh Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- fat
- eggs

## Directions

1. Melt a portion of fat and pour not very hot over the eggs placed in a barrel

title: Conservação dos Ovos Frescos (Preservation of Fresh Eggs) - Method 2
body:
## Ingredients

- virgin lime
- water

## Directions

1. Mix a portion of virgin lime with twenty times as much water, let it settle, decant the liquid, and pour it over the eggs; in this manner, fresh eggs are preserved for months and years.

title: Conservação dos Ovos Frescos (Preservation of Fresh Eggs) - Method 3
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- melted tallow
- bran

## Directions

1. Select a portion of eggs; dip each one in a vessel containing melted tallow not too hot; then place them in bran and they will keep for more than a year without spoiling.

title: Conservação dos Peixes em Gordura (Preservation of Fish in Fat)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt
- bay leaves
- pepper
- clove
- melted fat or sweet oil

## Directions

1. Take some fish, salt them after removing the scales and entrails; leave them in this brine for four hours; remove them, and if the fish are large, cut into portions, wash them and place in the sun until dry; place afterwards in a barrel with bay leaves, peppers, clove, and pour melted fat almost cold or sweet oil over the top until covered; seal the barrel and keep.

title: Conservação dos Tomates (Preservation of Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe tomatoes
- salt

## Directions

1. Select ripe, large tomatoes without defect; place in a barrel a thin layer of salt, over this a layer of tomatoes, over that another of salt and so on until the barrel is full; seal and keep.

title: Coração de Vacca Assado (Roasted Beef Heart)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef heart
- restorative sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the heart into slices
2. Place on the grill and cook
3. Serve with a restorative sauce

title: Coração de Vacca Ensopado (Braised Beef Heart)
body:
## Ingredients

- bacon, sliced
- parsley
- small onions
- half a bay leaf
- one glass of white wine
- small whole onions
- carrots, sliced
- lean bacon
- salt
- broken Indian pepper

## Directions

1. Take some bacon cut into slices and mash it with parsley, small onions and half a bay leaf, all finely chopped
2. Fill the beef heart with this prepared bacon
3. Place in a casserole a glass of white wine, small whole onions, carrots cut into slices of lean bacon, salt, and broken Indian pepper
4. Put the heart in the casserole, cover carefully with hot coals on top, placing it over low fire
5. Let it cook slowly for five to six hours and serve

title: Cordeiro Assado Dourado (Golden Roasted Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roasted lamb
- onions
- parsley
- bread slices
- butter
- toasted breadcrumbs or grated cheese
- four egg yolks
- one tablespoon sugar

## Directions

1. Mince leftover roasted lamb with onions and parsley
2. Place on bread slices fried in butter
3. Cover with toasted breadcrumbs, grated cheese, or both
4. Pour four beaten egg yolks mixed with one tablespoon sugar over the top
5. Place the plate in the oven or over coals
6. Serve

title: Cordeiro ou Cabrito com Molho Branco (Lamb or Kid with White Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons butter
- two tablespoons cornmeal
- pinch of cornstarch
- vinegar
- one bowl broth
- parsley
- onion leaves
- salt
- nutmeg
- roasted lamb or kid

## Directions

1. Melt two tablespoons butter
2. Add two tablespoons cornmeal, a pinch of cornstarch, vinegar, one bowl broth, parsley, onion leaves, salt, and nutmeg
3. Boil a little and strain through a sieve
4. Add the roasted lamb or kid, cut into slices
5. When hot, serve

title: Cordeiro ou Cabrito Refogado (Braised Lamb or Kid)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb or kid loin or quarter
- water
- salt
- pieces of bacon
- parsley
- onion
- garlic clove
- two tablespoons wheat flour
- two tablespoons butter
- one cup white wine
- juice of one lemon
- sugar
- raisins
- lemon slices, peeled and pitted

## Directions

1. Place lamb or kid loin or quarter in a pan with water, salt, pieces of bacon, parsley, onion, and garlic clove
2. Boil until cooked
3. Fry two tablespoons wheat flour with two tablespoons butter until dark brass-colored
4. Add the strained broth, one cup white wine, lemon juice, sugar, raisins, and lemon slices
5. Add the meat and boil over moderate heat until the broth is reduced to few cups
6. Serve with this sauce

title: Cordeiro ou Cabrito à Italiana (Lamb or Kid Italian Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb or kid meat
- sweet olive oil
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- garlic
- onion leaves
- bay leaves
- marjoram
- one bottle Malmsey wine
- two egg yolks
- juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Marinate the meat in sweet olive oil, salt, pepper, parsley, garlic, onion leaves, bay leaves, and marjoram for twelve hours
2. Place over fire with one bottle Malmsey wine
3. Leave over coals for six hours
4. Thicken the sauce with two egg yolks
5. Add lemon juice
6. Serve the meat with this sauce

title: Corá ou Mingáão de Milho Verde (Corn Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- ears of fresh corn
- milk
- sugar
- cinnamon
- cloves
- fennel

## Directions

1. Take ears of fresh corn that are still milky and sweet, shell and grate them
2. Pass the obtained juice through a sieve
3. Add a little milk, sugar, cinnamon, cloves, and fennel
4. Boil over moderate heat until reduced to half
5. Pour into a tureen to cool
6. Cover with sugar and cinnamon
7. For better appearance, place in the oven to toast slightly
8. Serve cold

title: Coscorrones (Fried Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of milk
- one egg white beaten
- salt
- half tablespoon of sugar
- two tablespoons of tapioca starch

## Directions

1. Mix two cups of milk with one beaten egg white, a little salt, half a tablespoon of sugar, and two tablespoons of tapioca starch

title: Coscorões (Fried Pastry Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of milk
- one beaten egg white
- a little salt
- half a spoonful of sugar
- two spoonfuls of cornstarch
- plenty of fat

## Directions

1. Mix two cups of milk with one beaten egg white, a little salt, half a spoonful of sugar, and two spoonfuls of cornstarch
2. Heat fat in a cassole on the fire
3. Dip a mold into the dough, holding it by the handle; the mold becomes coated with the dough which is left in the fat
4. When cooked, which is known when the dough entirely releases from the mold, remove it and repeat this operation until the dough is finished

title: Costella de Vacca Assada (Roasted Beef Rib)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of rib below the shoulder
- sweet oil
- salt
- broken Indian pepper
- lemon juice
- butter

## Directions

1. Take a piece of rib below the shoulder, remove the bones except the right rib bone
