2. Crush well and soak the short ribs in sweet oil, salt, and broken Indian pepper
3. Then place on the grill over a gentle fire, turning occasionally to avoid burning
4. After half an hour, remove from fire and serve at table with lemon juice and a little butter

title: Costella de Vacca com Substancia de Carne (Beef Rib with Meat Essence)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef rib
- beef meat essence

## Directions

1. Prepare as in the good cook's recipe
2. Leave to roast for three-quarters of an hour
3. Pour diluted beef meat essence over the top

title: Costella de Vacca do Bom Cozinheiro (Good Cook's Beef Rib)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef ribs
- butter
- hot coals

## Directions

1. Fry the ribs greased with butter on both sides
2. Cover the casserole with the lid and place hot coals on top
3. Leave it to cook, without adding anything else, neither water nor sauce, over moderate fire for two hours
4. Serve the ribs with no other sauce beyond their own juice contained in the casserole

title: Costella de Vacca à Italiana (Beef Rib Italian-style)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef ribs
- beef broth
- white wine
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- Indian cloves
- macaroni
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Boil the rib meat for three hours in beef broth and white wine over gentle fire with sufficient seasoning of salt, pepper, nutmeg, and Indian cloves
2. Remove the casserole from fire and strain the broth, then thicken it
3. On the other hand, cook macaroni in beef broth, and when cooked, remove it and mix with a good portion of thin broth
4. Place in the bottom of the casserole, and pour the sauce in which the rib meat was cooked over everything

title: Costelletas Assadas (Roasted Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- cutlets
- salt
- pepper
- garlic
- lemon juice
- butter

## Directions

1. Take the cutlets and season with salt, pepper, a little garlic and lemon juice
2. Let them soak in these substances and beat a little
3. Coat with a little butter and roast on a grill, being careful to turn them
4. Serve with a spicy sauce à Robert or any other

title: Costelletas de Porco Assadas (Roasted Pork Chops)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork chops
- sweet olive oil
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- bay leaf
- clove
- pickled cucumbers

## Directions

1. Marinate the pork chops in a mixture of sweet olive oil, salt, pepper, parsley, bay leaf, and clove for three days.
2. Fry them in a skillet or roast them on a grill.
3. Top with a Roberto sauce, with preserved cucumbers cut into rounds.
4. Alternatively, use a spicy sauce, a tomato sauce, or a sorrel pulp sauce.

title: Costelletas de Porco do Matto Assadas na Grelha (Grilled Wild Pork Chops with Coating)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork chops with loin attached, one finger thick
- salt
- pepper
- mustard powder
- breadcrumbs
- melted butter
- melted butter mixed with one glass white wine and mustard powder

## Directions

1. Separate each chop with its piece of loin, one finger thick.
2. Beat it and trim it.
3. Marinate in salt, pepper and mustard powder.
4. Wrap in breadcrumbs and pass in melted butter.
5. Grill on both sides and serve with melted butter mixed with one glass of white wine and mustard powder.

title: Costelletas de Porco do Matto Assadas na Grelha (Grilled Wild Pork Chops)
body:
## Ingredients

- wild pork chops
- pork fat
- salt
- spicy mustard sauce

## Directions

1. Pass the wild pork chops in pork fat and grill on both sides.
2. Sprinkle with salt and send to the table with a spicy mustard sauce.

title: Costelletas de Porco à Milaneza (Pork Chops Milanese)
body:
## Ingredients

- six fresh pork chops, trimmed
- butter
- breadcrumb powder
- grated cheese
- pimenta da India
- garlic clove
- chives
- meat substance
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Take six fresh trimmed pork chops and spread them with butter.
2. Coat them with breadcrumb powder and grated cheese.
3. Place them in a skillet with melted butter, salt, pimenta da India, a garlic clove, and chives.
4. Sauté the chops only until they take on heat from both sides.
5. Place them in a vessel with fire below and above, and let them cook for a quarter hour.
6. Remove from heat, drain, and remove the fat from the sauce left in the vessel.
7. Melt a little meat substance and a little tomato sauce, reduce this sauce, and place the chops inside to cook slowly.
8. Serve with pasta cooked in water and salt, or with a tomato sauce.

title: Costelletas de Vitella Afogadas (Braised Calf Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf cutlets
- butter
- broth or water
- onion
- parsley
- lemon juice
- salt
- pepper
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut the cutlets so each piece has a bone not too long
2. Fry in a little butter on both sides until they take color
3. Add some broth or water, onion, parsley, lemon juice, salt and pepper
4. Let cook
5. When cooked, remove them and strain the broth which is thickened with egg yolks to serve with the cutlets

title: Costelletas de Vitella com Môlho Pardo (Calf Cutlets with Brown Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf cutlets
- butter
- wheat flour
- onion
- broth
- salt
- nutmeg
- cloves
- sugar
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Fry the cutlets in a little butter until they are browned on both sides and remove them
2. In the same butter, add a spoonful of wheat flour and a chopped onion which is fried until toasted
3. Add several cups of broth, salt, nutmeg, cloves, a little sugar and vinegar
4. Simmer the cutlets in this sauce until they are cooked

title: Costelletas de Vitella Guisadas (Braised Veal Chops)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal chops
- salt
- pepper
- cabbage leaves
- onion

## Directions

1. Cut the chops and braise them in boiling water
2. Add salt, pepper, some cabbage leaves, and onion
3. Let them cook
4. Remove the chops and strain the broth, letting it reduce
5. Remove the fat from the broth
6. Serve the chops with the broth poured over them

title: Costelletas de Vitella Guisadas (Stewed Calf Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf cutlets
- fine bacon for larding
- water
- salt
- pepper
- cabbage leaves
- onion

## Directions

1. With the cutlets cut, lard them with fine bacon and place in some water that is already boiling
2. Add salt, pepper, some cabbage leaves, onion and let cook
3. Remove the cutlets and strain the broth which is left to reduce and from which the fat is removed and then served over them

title: Costelletas Empanadas (Breaded Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- cutlets
- grated bread crusts
- chopped parsley
- salt
- pepper
- melted fat or butter

## Directions

1. Roll the cutlets in a good layer of grated bread crumbs mixed with chopped parsley, salt and pepper
2. Moisten with melted fat or butter
3. Roast on a grill, adjusting the heat so they do not burn
4. Serve with spicy sauce

title: Costelletas Empapeladas (Cutlets in Paper)
body:
## Ingredients

- cutlets
- sweet oil or melted lard
- onions
- carapicú
- salt
- bacon
- bread crumbs
- butter
- eggs
- butter-greased paper

## Directions

1. Moisten the cutlets with sweet oil or melted lard
2. Cover on both sides with two onions, a handful of carapicú, a little salt, bacon, all very finely chopped, bread crumbs and a spoonful of butter
3. Pass through a liquid made of four beaten eggs
4. Wrap in grated bread crumbs
5. Finally wrap in well-buttered paper and go to roast in a large shallow pan placed over coals
6. Serve thus with the paper

title: Costelletas Lardeadas (Larded Chops)
body:
## Ingredients

- chops
- strips of beef tongue
- pieces of palmito (heart of palm)
- pieces of salt pork
- remaining palmito
- one onion
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- one glass of water
- one glass of orange wine
- 4 to 8 gilos

## Directions

1. Lard the chops alternately with strips of beef tongue and pieces of palmito
2. Place them to cook in a casserole with some pieces of salt pork, the rest of the palmito, a cut onion, parsley, salt, pepper, a glass of water and another of orange wine
3. Just before serving, add 4 to 8 gilos

title: Costelletas à Goyana (Goiás-style Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- cutlets
- cornmeal flour
- melted fat
- egg yolks
- grated cheese
- squash sprouts or samambaia sprouts

## Directions

1. Cut the cutlets
2. Coat in cornmeal flour, passing them in melted fat first, then in beaten egg yolks and again in cornmeal flour mixed with grated cheese
3. Fry in fat and serve with squash or samambaia sprouts

title: Cotia Assada (Roasted Agouti)
body:
## Ingredients

- one agouti
- salt
- peppers
- tomato juice
- bacon strips
- onions
- parsley
- garlic
- half cup of water
- one spoonful of orange juice

## Directions

1. After removing the skin of the agouti and cleaning it, rub it with salt, peppers and tomato juice, leaving it in this seasoning for six hours so the meat becomes well impregnated
2. Place it in a pan over strips of bacon with sliced onions, parsley and one clove of garlic, moistening it with half cup of water and one spoonful of orange juice
3. Roast it over a moderate fire and serve it with its defatted sauce

title: Cotia Assada (Roasted Agouti)
body:
## Ingredients

- agouti
- salt
- peppers
- tomato juice
- bacon lard
- onions, chopped
- parsley
- garlic clove
- half cup water
- one spoon orange juice

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the agouti and clean it thoroughly
2. Rub it with salt, peppers, and tomato juice, leaving it in this seasoning for six hours so the meat becomes well impregnated
3. Place it in a pan over bacon lard with chopped onions, parsley, and one garlic clove
4. Moisten it with half a cup of water and one spoon of orange juice
5. Roast over moderate fire
6. Serve with the defatted gravy

title: Cotia Ensopada com Caras (Agouti Stew with Taro)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoon wheat flour
- one onion, chopped
- one spoon brown sugar
- agouti
- one bottle water
- half cup orange juice
- one peeled and chopped taro
- half dozen tomatoes
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- garlic clove

## Directions

1. Brown one spoon of wheat flour, one chopped onion, and one spoon of brown sugar until they take on a dark amber color
2. Add the agouti cut into pieces and turn them to fry on both sides
3. Add one bottle of water with half a cup of orange juice, one peeled and chopped taro, half a dozen tomatoes, salt, pepper, parsley, and one garlic clove
4. Let it boil for one hour
5. When cooked, serve

title: Cotia Ensopada com Carás (Agouti Stewed with Taro)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoonful of wheat flour
- one sliced onion
- one spoonful of brown sugar
- agouti
- taro

## Directions

1. Sauté one spoonful of wheat flour, one sliced onion and one spoonful of brown sugar until they take on a dark amber color
2. Add the agouti cut into pieces and turn to fry

title: Cotia Estufada com Carapicus (Braised Agouti with Carapicus)
body:
## Ingredients

- agouti
- handful of carapicus
- four ounces bacon
- half dozen tomatoes
- parsley
- comarí peppers
- two hard-boiled eggs
- garlic clove
- one onion
- grated ginger
- one spoon sugar
- lemon juice
- bacon lard strips
- one cup water
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Flay and clean the agouti
2. Prepare a filling with a handful of carapicus, four ounces of bacon, half a dozen tomatoes, parsley, comarí peppers, two hard-boiled eggs, one garlic clove, one onion, grated ginger, and one spoon of sugar, moistened with a little lemon juice
3. Fill the agouti with this mixture
4. Place it in a pan lined with bacon lard strips, one cup of water, a portion of carapicus, salt, and pepper
5. Cover well and cook over coals for two to three hours
6. Serve

title: Cotia Frita com Mandioca (Fried Agouti with Manioc)
body:
## Ingredients

- agouti
- salt
- pepper
- cornmeal from cangica (cornmeal flour)
- egg yolks
- fat, two spoons
- cooked manioc, cut in long strips

## Directions

1. Cut the agouti into pieces and sprinkle with salt and pepper
2. Wrap them in cornmeal from cangica
3. Pass them in egg yolks
4. Cover again with cornmeal from cangica
5. Fry in two spoons of fat with some cooked manioc cut in long strips
6. Turn them so they fry on both sides and take on good color
7. Serve

title: Cotia Guisada com Grelos de Abóbora (Agouti Stew with Squash Shoots)
body:
## Ingredients

- agouti
- water
- salt
- peppers
- ginger
- squash shoots, destalked and bundled
- dozen tomatoes
- two spoons butter
- one cup broth
- one spoon dried breadcrumb powder
- salt
- peppers
- one small chopped onion

## Directions

1. Boil the agouti cut into pieces in water and salt
2. Add peppers, ginger, and a portion of squash shoots destalked and bundled in bundles
3. When cooked, remove the meat and the shoots
4. In another pan, brown a dozen tomatoes in two spoons of butter with one cup of broth, one spoon of dried breadcrumb powder, salt, peppers, and one small chopped onion
5. Let it boil, add the meat and shoots to this sauce
6. Serve after it has boiled a little

title: Cotia Refogada com Chuchús (Agouti Sautéed with Chayotes)
body:
## Ingredients

- one agouti
- three spoonfuls of fat
- chayotes
- one cup of water
- one goblet of brandy
- one spoonful of lime juice
- one small spoonful of cassava starch
- salt
- parsley
- peppers
- ginger

## Directions

1. Take an agouti, clean it and cut it into pieces, and fry them in three spoonfuls of fat
2. When fried, remove them and sauté in the same fat a portion of peeled chayotes
3. Add one cup of water, one goblet of brandy, one spoonful of lime juice, one small spoonful of cassava starch, salt, parsley, peppers, and a little grated ginger
4. Let it boil over embers until the meat is cooked and serve

title: Couve Flor com Queijo (Cauliflower with Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- water
- salt
- butter
- Flemish cheese
- white sauce
- breadcrumbs
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Peel cauliflower and place in a casserole with boiling water, salt and a little butter
2. Fifteen minutes are enough to cook them
3. Remove with a skimmer and let drain
4. Cover with finely grated Flemish cheese and place on a plate that can go on the fire
5. Pour over a thick white sauce, sprinkle with cheese and breadcrumbs
6. Pour melted butter on top and in this state place over low heat; twenty minutes are enough to prepare them

title: Couves Ensopadas (Cabbage in Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- couves (cabbage)
- vinagre (vinegar)
- gordura (fat)
- farinha de trigo (wheat flour)
- agua (water)

## Directions

1. Chop a portion of cabbage and blanch it with a little vinegar
2. Separately, melt two spoons of fat with a spoon of wheat flour, and when hot, add the cabbage and a cup of water
3. Having boiled for a quarter of an hour over not very moderate heat, serve it with beef or pork

title: Couves Flor Fritas (Fried Cauliflower)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- water
- salt
- butter
- thin batter

## Directions

1. Proceed as in the previous article, taking care only that the cauliflower is not entirely cooked
2. Pass them two or three times through hot butter
3. Let cool well
4. Soak in thin batter and then fry, taking care that they achieve a good color
5. Remove, let drain and serve

title: Couves Picadas (Chopped Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- couves (cabbage)
- agua (water)
- gordura (fat)
- sal (salt)

## Directions

1. Chop a portion of selected cabbage and wash it with cold water
2. Melt a spoon of fat and salt, add the cabbage, add a little water, let it boil and serve

title: Couves Refogadas Inteiras (Whole Cabbage Braised)
body:
## Ingredients

- folhas de couves (cabbage leaves)
- gordura (fat)
- sal (salt)
- pimentas comarís (peppers)

## Directions

1. Select a portion of cabbage leaves, break them with your nails, wash them, and braise them in a spoon of fat
2. Add a little salt and peppers, mix, and add half a bottle of water
3. Let it boil a little longer and serve

title: Coxas de Gallinha Recheiadas (Stuffed Chicken Legs)
body:
## Ingredients

- legs of four chickens
- half a pound of ham, chopped
- a little veal
- onion
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- two cups of meat broth
- two egg yolks
- lemon slices
- one glass of Madeira wine

## Directions

1. Cut the legs of four chickens, clean them and remove the bones
2. Stuff them with chopped half a pound of ham, a little veal, onion, parsley, salt and pepper
3. Cook them to take their primitive form
4. When browned, add two cups of meat broth thickened with two egg yolks, some lemon slices and one glass of Madeira wine
5. Simmer near the fire and serve afterwards

title: Coxas de Ganso com Quibebe (Goose Legs with Quibebe)
body:
## Ingredients

- six goose legs
- bacon, finely chopped
- salt
- parsley
- pepper
- broth
- onion

## Directions

1. Take six goose legs, open them, remove the bones, and fill the cavity with finely chopped bacon, salt, parsley and pepper
2. Then tie the legs and wrap each one in a slice of bacon, roast them in a pan with one cup of broth, one onion, salt and parsley, placing it either in the oven or over coals, also putting coals on top of the lid
3. When cooked, serve with quibebe

title: Croquetes de Carne de Porco Fresca (Fresh Pork Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover cooked or roasted pork
- salt
- garlic
- parsley
- pimenta da India
- bay leaf
- thyme
- vinegar
- two raw eggs
- dried bread crumbs or toasted bread crumb
- wheat flour

## Directions

1. Take the leftover cooked or roasted pork and chop it finely with a cleaver.
2. Season it with salt, garlic, parsley, pimenta da India, bay leaf, and thyme, all finely chopped.
3. Add two tablespoons of vinegar, knead well with two raw eggs, adding a little dried bread crumbs or toasted bread crumb.
4. If you do not have bread crumbs or dried bread, substitute with two tablespoons of wheat flour.
5. From this mixture, form little balls and fry them in butter.
6. Serve with sprigs of parsley or a Robert sauce.
7. These can be prepared in the same manner with beef, veal, mutton, poultry, or game.

title: Cuscúz (Cornmeal Pudding with Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a plate of fine cornmeal
- one cup of milk
- a quarter of a cheese cut into pieces
- one spoonful of sugar
- one spoonful of butter
- two egg yolks
- a little fennel
- some raisins
- a little grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Moisten half a plate of fine cornmeal with one cup of milk
2. Mix in a quarter of cheese cut into pieces, one spoonful of sugar, one spoonful of butter, two egg yolks, a little fennel, some raisins, and a little grated nutmeg
3. Place this dough in the cuscuzeiro and proceed as in the Cuscúz de Seminário

title: Cuscúz de Seminário (Cornmeal Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a plate of fine cornmeal
- water to moisten it
- a little salt
- one spoonful of sugar
- a little fennel

## Directions

1. Moisten half a plate of fine cornmeal with as much water as needed to moisten it without binding it
2. Mix in a little salt, one spoonful of sugar, and a little fennel
3. Place everything in a cuscuzeiro, pressing the dough a little, and with this cover a deep pot, with the cuscuzeiro entering the pot without reaching the water that is in the pot
4. Seal all the gaps that remain between the pot and the cuscuzeiro with a little cornmeal paste
5. Let it boil and when cooked, turn the cuscuzeiro upside down over a plate so the dough can come out whole, and serve

title: Cuscúz Frito (Fried Cornmeal Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked cuscúz
- a little fat
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut the cuscúz into slices
2. Fry in a little fat
3. Dust both sides with sugar and cinnamon
4. Serve with roasted meats or tea

title: Dourado e Salmão Cozidos (Dorado and Salmon Boiled)
body:
## Ingredients

- one dorado
- water
- salt
- parsley
- lemon juice
- one spoon of fat
- fish broth
- half a spoon of arrowroot

## Directions

1. Take one dorado, remove the scales and entrails, boil it with water, salt, parsley, a little lemon juice
2. When cooked, melt one spoon of fat, add the fish broth mixed with half a spoon of arrowroot
3. Boil a little, place the fish inside, and after another boil, serve

title: Dourado e Salmão de Escabeche (Dorado and Salmon Escabeche)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish cut into steaks
- sour orange or vinegar juice
- salt
- chopped onion
- garlic
- pepper
- clove
- a piece of lard
- one or two spoons of cornmeal
- carapicus or chopped mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cut the fish into steaks and place them in a soak in sour orange or vinegar juice, salt, chopped onion, garlic, pepper and clove
2. Then fry a piece of lard and add one or two spoons of cornmeal (fine cornmeal)
3. Stir, add the fish with all its sauce and a portion of carapicus or chopped mushrooms
4. Let it boil over moderate heat and serve

title: Dourado e Salmão Refogados (Dorado and Salmon Braised)
body:
## Ingredients

- one fish cut into steaks the thickness of a finger
- a little chopped lard
- some onions
- chopped parsley
- salt
- peppers
- one garlic clove
- three to four cloves
- two cups of water
- two egg yolks
- one cup of wine
- capers (optional)

## Directions

1. Place the fish steaks, a little chopped lard, some onions, chopped parsley, salt, peppers, one garlic clove, three to four cloves and two cups of water in a pot and bring to boil
2. Cover and when cooked, remove it and strain the broth thickened with two beaten egg yolks and one cup of wine, and serve
3. If capers are available, add two to three spoons of them

title: Dourado etc. Cozido com Arroz (Dorado etc. Cooked with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a bushel of chosen rice
- one bottle of water
- pieces of lard
- salt
- parsley
- some peppers
- two onions
- one dorado head
- parsnips
- nutmeg
- a dozen tomatoes
- milk (optional)

## Directions

1. Put half a bushel of chosen rice in a pot of sufficient capacity with one bottle of water, some pieces of lard, salt, parsley, some peppers and two onions
2. Place a dorado head filled with chopped parsnips, seasoned with salt and nutmeg and mixed with a dozen tomatoes, on this rice
3. Cook for one hour over moderate heat until the rice is cooked and dried
4. Place the rice on a plate and put the head on top, pouring milk over it (if desired), and serve

title: Eel and Mossum Braised (Enguia e mossum guisados)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh eels
- White wine
- One onion
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Melted butter

## Directions

1. Boil fresh eels for a few minutes and rub to clean them without removing the skin
2. Cook in white wine with one halved onion, salt and pepper
3. When the strained broth is reduced, serve hot
4. Or, if desired, after placing the fish on a plate, let cool, cover with chopped parsley, pour a little melted butter on top, and serve

title: Eel and Mossum Cooked (Enguia e mossum cozidos)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two bottles of water
- Salt
- Parsley
- Pepper
- Onion leaves
- Cloves
- One eel
- Manioc flour or bread crumbs
- Melted butter
- Sweet olive oil
- Vinegar
- Parsley pounded

## Directions

1. Put two bottles of water in a pan with salt, parsley, pepper, onion leaves, and cloves
2. When boiling, add pieces of eel after removing the skin
3. Let boil for a quarter of an hour
4. Remove and drain
5. Sprinkle with manioc flour or bread crumbs
6. Pour melted butter on top
7. Serve cold with a sauce of sweet olive oil, vinegar and pounded parsley

title: Eel and Mossum Sautéed (Enguia e Mossumrefogados)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two or three small eels
- Fat
- One onion
- One spoon of wheat flour
- Salt and pepper
- One bottle of water
- One chalice of sour orange juice

## Directions

1. Cut the eels into pieces after removing the skin
2. Sauté these pieces in fat until cooked
3. Remove and place on a plate
4. In the same butter, fry the onion, wheat flour, a little salt and pepper
5. Add one bottle of water and one chalice of sour orange juice
6. Let it boil and pour hot over the eels
7. Serve

title: Eel or Mossum Braised (Enguia ou mossum estufados)
body:
## Ingredients

- One or two fresh eels
- Bacon slices
- Beef
- Pork or chopped ham
- Carapicús or mushrooms
- One garlic clove
- One bay leaf
- Salt
- Pepper
- Sliced lemon without skin
- One bottle of white wine

## Directions

1. Scald one or two fresh eels
2. Place in a casserole lined with bacon slices, a piece of beef, a piece of pork or chopped ham, a handful of carapicús or mushrooms, one garlic clove, one bay leaf, salt, pepper, slices of peeled lemon, and one bottle of white wine
3. Let boil over coals
4. When cooked, serve with the strained and reduced sauce

title: Eel or Mossum in Escabeche (Enguia ou mossum d'escabeche)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two to three small eels
- One bottle of water
- One bottle of white wine
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bay leaves
- Parsley
- Cow butter
- Olive oil
- Vinegar
- Parsley pounded

## Directions

1. Remove the skins from two to three small eels and cut into pieces
2. In another part, boil one bottle of water, one bottle of white wine, salt, pepper, bay leaves, parsley, and cow butter
3. Scald the fish with this broth and let soak for three hours
4. Then let boil for half an hour
5. Remove the fish and serve cold with a cold sauce of olive oil, vinegar and chopped parsley

title: Eel or Mossum Stewed (Enguia ou mossum ensopados)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two to three eels
- White wine
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Bay leaves
- Two onions
- Carapicús
- Three egg yolks
- One cup of orange or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Remove the skins from two to three eels and cut into pieces
2. Cook in white wine, salt, pepper, cloves and bay leaves
3. Remove cooked pieces and place on a plate kept warm by the fire
4. In another part, fry two halved onions and chopped carapicús, salt and pepper, without browning
5. Add the strained broth and finish cooking
6. Thicken with three beaten egg yolks mixed with one cup of orange or lemon juice
7. Pour over the fish and serve hot

title: Eels and Mossum Roasted on the Grill (Enguias e mossum assados na grelha)
body:
## Ingredients

- One eel
- Water
- White wine
- Parsley
- Onions
- Bay leaves
- Salt and pepper
- Fat or melted butter
- Manioc flour
- Beaten egg yolk
- One chalice of lemon juice
- One egg yolk

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the eel and cut into pieces
2. Boil for half an hour with equal parts water and white wine, parsley, onions, bay leaves, salt and pepper
3. Let cool and remove the fish
4. Pass through fat or melted butter
5. Coat in manioc flour
6. Pass through beaten eggs
7. Cover again with manioc flour
8. Roast on the grill, turning the pieces so they cook on all sides
9. Serve with the reduced broth mixed with one egg yolk dissolved in one chalice of lemon juice

title: Eggs Beaten and Thickened with Cornmeal (Ovos Batidos e Engrossados com Fubá)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- parsley
- onion
- salt
- Indian pepper
- garlic
- cornmeal
- borage leaf
- fat
- onion sauce
- Comarí peppers

## Directions

1. Beat a portion of whole eggs well, adding parsley, well-chopped onion, salt, well-ground Indian pepper and garlic; after beating well, add cornmeal until forming a half-hard paste
2. Take one spoon of this paste and place in a frying pan, using spoons to finish the paste and removing the eggs from the pan as they fry; place these eggs on a plate and serve with a sauce made of onions, chopped parsley and Comarí peppers
3. If desired, add a blanched borage leaf to the paste, and thus prepared, place it to fry in very hot fat, continuing until the paste is finished, and serve with the above sauce
4. In the same manner, cooked pieces of chicken or hen can be prepared, already cooked, cleaned and with bones removed, artichoke leaves, heart of palm, fern, asparagus sprouts, etc.

title: Eggs Parmesan (Ovos à Parmesã)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight whole eggs
- four egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- three spoons of grated cheese
- two spoons of fat
- breadcrumbs
- sugar
- cinnamon powder

## Directions

1. Beat eight whole eggs, four yolks, with salt, pepper and three spoons of grated cheese
2. In another pan, melt two spoons of fat
3. When hot, pour in the eggs; fry, stirring, until fried
4. Place on a plate, cover with breadcrumbs, sugar and cinnamon powder, and serve

title: Eggs with Minced Cold Meat (Ovos com Carne Fria Picadinha)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- parsley
- onions
- chives
- salt
- Indian pepper
- garlic

## Directions

1. Beat a portion of whole eggs; after beating well, add parsley, well-chopped onions and chives, salt, Indian pepper, and one clove of garlic; mix everything well

title: Empada de Bacalhão à Mineira (Salt Cod Pie, Minas Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- salt cod
- aguardente
- white wine
- peppers
- onions
- parsley
- mushrooms
- salt
- vinegar
- eggs

## Directions

1. Boil the salt cod for half an hour in aguardente
2. Remove, chop it, and after chopping, boil it in white wine with peppers, onions, and parsley
3. Let the broth dry out
4. Mix in a handful of mushrooms boiled in water, salt, vinegar, and some chopped hard-boiled eggs
5. Place this dough in the mold, bake and serve

title: Empada de Bagres (Catfish Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- pie dough
- salt cod cooked in white wine
- hard-boiled eggs
- bacon
- onions
- catfish
- fat
- artichoke hearts
- spicy sauce

## Directions

1. Take a box of pie dough and place on the bottom a hash made of salt cod cooked in white wine, hard-boiled eggs, bacon, and onions
2. Place on top of it a portion of catfish fried in fat and mixed with artichoke hearts that have been cooked
3. Cover with the rest of the hash, place the lid, and bake in the oven, serve with a spicy sauce

title: Empada de Camarões (Shrimp Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- a large piece of fish without bones
- a portion of shrimp
- a quarter of a palm heart
- a handful of mushrooms
- parsley
- one onion
- peppers
- salt
- one cup of wine
- two hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Take a large piece of fish without bones, a portion of shrimp, a quarter of a palm heart, a handful of mushrooms, parsley, one onion, peppers, and salt
2. Boil everything together with one cup of wine
3. When cooked, add two chopped hard-boiled eggs
4. Place this dough in the pie box and bake in the oven, serve cold or hot

title: Empada de Carne de Vitela (Veal Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of veal
- a little butter
- meat stock
- Madeira wine
- seasonings
- one egg

## Directions

1. Take a piece of veal and brown it with a little butter
2. Cut it afterward into small pieces that are placed in a cassole with meat stock, the same amount of Madeira wine, and seasonings
3. Let it boil for half an hour and fill the pie box with this dough
4. Cover it and place in the oven
5. When cooked, beat an egg and brush the dough with it to take on good color, and serve

title: Empada de Coelho or Tatú (Rabbit or Armadillo Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit
- red wine
- cloves
- nutmeg
- sage
- onion
- salt
- peppers
- carapicús (a type of game bird)
- six egg yolks
- one chopped onion
- lemon juice
- pastry casing

## Directions

1. After cleaning the rabbit, cook it in red wine with cloves, nutmeg, sage, onion, salt and peppers
2. Remove the meat from the bones, cut into pieces and mix with a portion of carapicús, six egg yolks and one chopped onion
3. Moisten with lemon juice
4. Bake in a pastry casing and when well baked, serve

title: Empada de Galinha à Mineira (Chicken Pie, Minas Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- two chickens
- four spoonfuls of fat
- four cups of water
- salt
- parsley
- onions
- peppers
- one palm heart
- chicken livers and gizzards
- eight hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Brown two chickens with four spoonfuls of fat
2. Cut them afterward into pieces and add four cups of water, salt, parsley, onions, peppers, and a palm heart cut up
3. Let it boil over coals until the broth dries out
4. Remove the bones and add the chicken livers and gizzards, and eight hard-boiled eggs
5. Fill the mold with this hash, bake in the oven, and serve hot

title: Empada de Gallinha (Chicken Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken pieces
- melted fat
- one cup of meat broth
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaf
- clove
- onion
- half a garlic clove
- half a palm heart, chopped and scalded
- quarter pound of wheat flour
- half a pound of cow butter
- three eggs
- half a spoonful of sugar
- one egg yolk

## Directions

1. Take the chicken pieces and sauté them in melted fat
2. Add one cup of meat broth, salt, pepper, bay leaf, clove, onion, half a garlic clove and half a palm heart, chopped and scalded in hot water
3. In another place make a dough with a quarter pound of wheat flour, half a pound of cow butter, three eggs, half a spoonful of sugar
4. Form a box with this dough, or using a mold, line the interior walls of it with this dough, well buttered
5. Place the chicken and cooked palm heart inside, make a lid with the same dough, cover it and place it in the oven
6. When cooked, remove it and coat the exterior cap with the broth of a beaten egg and return it to the oven for some time
7. This egg serves to make the cap glossy, and this is called gilding the cap

title: Empada de Jacú (Guan Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- one guan
- four hard-boiled eggs
- chicken breast
- half a palm heart
- salt
- ground Indian pepper
- white wine
- pie dough

## Directions

1. Open the bird along the back, remove the bones, chop the meat and the liver of the bird
2. Chop four hard-boiled eggs, salt, chicken breast, and half a palm heart
3. Season everything with salt, ground Indian pepper, and boil in white wine
4. When almost cooked, wrap it in a thick layer of pie dough, bake in the oven and serve

title: Empada de Lombo de Veado (Venison Loin Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison loin
- water
- salt
- white wine
- ginger
- parsley
- marjoram
- four pounds of wheat flour
- half a pound of butter
- four eggs
- milk

## Directions

1. Boil a venison loin in water and salt for half an hour, and following that, in white wine, ginger, parsley, and marjoram, until cooked
2. Afterward, knead four pounds of wheat flour with half a pound of butter, four eggs, and as much milk as is needed to make a dough
3. Wrap it in this dough and bake in the oven

title: Empada de Mandim (Fish Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish cut into pieces
- milk
- salt
- parsley
- grated cheese
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut the fish into pieces and boil in milk, salt, and parsley
2. When cooked, place it in the mold, filling the gaps with grated cheese and small pieces of butter
3. Bake in the oven and serve hot

title: Empada de Mocotó de Porco (Pig's Feet Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- four pig's feet
- water
- salt
- one onion
- chicken breast
- four hard-boiled eggs
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- puff pastry dough
- white wine

## Directions

1. Boil four pig's feet in water and salt until cooked
2. Remove the bones and cut the meat into small pieces, mix with one onion, chicken breast, and four hard-boiled eggs, all well chopped
3. Season with salt, peppers, and grated nutmeg, and fill the pie mold made of puff pastry with this dough
4. Bake in the oven and when cooked, remove it, lift the lid a little and moisten the meat with a little white wine
5. Finish baking and serve

title: Empada de Ovos (Egg Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve eggs
- four tablespoons grated cheese
- two tablespoons fresh butter
- salt
- ground pepper

## Directions

1. Beat a dozen eggs with four tablespoons of grated cheese and two tablespoons of fresh butter
2. Pour everything into a frying pan and fry, stirring until it hardens
3. Season with salt and ground pepper
4. Fill the pastry form with this mixture
5. Leave to bake in the oven and serve

title: Empada de Pombos (Pigeon Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- three pigeons
- fat
- parsley
- chopped onion
- salt
- peppers
- puff pastry
- quarter pound bacon
- four chopped eggs
- one cup wine

## Directions

1. Fry three pigeons in fat with parsley, chopped onion, salt and peppers
2. Place the pigeons in a puff pastry casing over a quarter pound of bacon and four chopped and sautéed eggs with one cup of wine
3. Cover the pigeons with the same chopped mixture
4. Cover, bake in the oven and serve

title: Empada de Presunto (Ham Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- water
- a little lemon juice
- pastry casing

## Directions

1. Take the ham and after removing the skin, soak it in water with a little lemon juice
2. After twenty-four hours have passed, remove it, dry it and wrap it in a pastry casing for empadas
3. Go to the oven and bake for two to three hours depending on the size of the ham
4. Serve with the same casing

title: Empada de Pássaros Miúdos (Small Birds Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- one dozen small birds
- white wine
- salt
- sage
- onion
- garlic
- cloves
- pepper
- half pound fresh bacon
- four cooked eggs
- bird offal
- peppers

## Directions

1. Clean a dozen small birds, roast them a little on the spit
2. Then stew them in white wine with salt, sage, onion, garlic, cloves and pepper
3. When cooked, place them in a pastry box, interlayering with a chopped mixture made of half pound fresh bacon, four cooked eggs, the birds' offal and peppers
4. Moisten everything with the wine in which the birds were cooked, strain and reduce
5. Bake in the oven and serve hot

title: Empadas de Peixes Miúdos (Small Fish Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat from one roasted chicken
- four cooked eggs
- one onion
- salt
- one garlic clove
- peppers
- small fried fish
- butter

## Directions

1. Chop the meat from a roasted chicken with four cooked eggs, one onion, salt and one garlic clove
2. Add salt and peppers
3. Place part of this chopped mixture at the bottom of a pastry box
4. Place a layer of small fish fried in butter on top, covering with a layer of the chopped mixture
5. Continue placing the fish alternately until the pastry is filled
6. When full, cover with its lid, bake in the oven and serve

title: Empadinhas de Ostras (Oyster Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- breast of one chicken
- liver of one chicken
- one onion
- a little parsley
- salt
- one tablespoon butter
- half cup white wine
- puff pastry
- two or three oysters
- a little butter
- one egg yolk

## Directions

1. Chop the chicken breast and liver with an onion and a little parsley and salt
2. Simmer a little in one tablespoon of butter and half cup white wine
3. Make some small boxes of puff pastry
4. Line the bottom with some of this chopped mixture
5. Place two or three oysters on top with a little butter
6. Cover and brush the outside with beaten egg yolk
7. Put in the oven and serve when baked

title: Empadinhas de Ostras (Small Oyster Pies)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken breast and liver
- one onion
- a little parsley
- salt
- one spoonful of butter
- half a cup of white wine
- puff pastry dough
- oysters
- butter
-  egg yolk

## Directions

1. Chop the breast and liver of a chicken with one onion, a little parsley, and salt
2. Boil briefly in one spoonful of butter and half a cup of white wine
3. Make some small boxes of puff pastry, line the bottom with a little of this hash
4. Place on top two or three oysters with a little butter, cover it and brush the top of the crust with egg yolk

title: Enguia do Mar Cozida (Boiled Sea Eel)
body:
## Ingredients

- one sea eel
- water
- salt
- parsley
- bread crusts
- butter

## Directions

1. Remove the skin of the fish and cut into pieces
2. Boil in well-salted water with parsley
3. When cooked, remove and place on a plate
4. Cover with a portion of bread crusts grated and fried in butter
5. Serve

title: Enguia do Mar Ensopada (Sea Eel Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- one sea eel
- one spoon fat
- one bowl water
- salt
- peppers
- parsley
- two spoons cornmeal
- a handful of carapicús
- one cup lemon juice

## Directions

1. Remove the skin of the fish and remove the loins and cut into pieces
2. Sauté in one spoon of fat, turning and avoiding browning
3. Add one bowl of water, salt, peppers and parsley
4. Add two spoons of cornmeal and a handful of chopped carapicús
5. Let everything boil
6. Add one cup of lemon juice and serve

title: Enguia do Mar Estufada (Braised Sea Eel)
body:
## Ingredients

- one sea eel
- salt
- peppers
- one onion
- one cup cream
- one spoon mustard sauce

## Directions

1. Skin the fish and remove the loin and cut into pieces
2. Place in a casserole with salt, peppers and one chopped onion
3. Roast over coals, turning from time to time until perfectly cooked
4. Add one cup of cream and one spoon of mustard sauce
5. Let it boil once more and serve with the fish

title: Enguia do Mar Refogada (Sea Eel Sautéed)
body:
## Ingredients

- one sea eel
- sea water
- one celery root
- parsley
- butter
- wheat flour
- one cup white wine

## Directions

1. Remove the skin of the fish and cut into pieces
2. Boil in sea water with one celery root and parsley
3. When cooked, remove and place in a pan with butter
4. Dust with wheat flour and fry a little
5. Add one cup of white wine
6. Let it boil a little more and serve with its sauce

title: Enguia do Mar Refogada com Caranguejos (Sea Eel Sautéed with Crabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- one sea eel
- sea water
- one sour orange
- two spoons butter
- one or two crabs
- one onion
- bay leaves
- cloves

## Directions

1. Remove the skin and cut the fish into pieces
2. Cook in sea water with one peeled and halved sour orange
3. When cooked, remove and sauté with two spoons of butter
4. Add one or two crabs crushed with shell, one onion, bay leaves and cloves
5. Strain this butter and pour over the fish
6. Serve

title: Enguia e Mossum Refogados (Eel and Mossum Braised)
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three small eels
- fat
- one onion
- one spoon of wheat flour
- salt
- pepper
- one bottle of water
- one chalice of sour orange juice

## Directions

1. Cut two or three small eels into pieces after removing the skin
2. Braise these pieces in fat until cooked
3. Remove them and place them on a plate
4. In the same butter, fry one onion, one spoon of wheat flour, a little salt and pepper
5. Add one bottle of water and one chalice of sour orange juice, let it boil, pour it hot over the eels and serve

title: Enguia ou Mossum Assados no Espeto (Eel or Mossum Roasted on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two eels
- two onions
- one potato or carrots
- parsley
- thyme
- salt
- peppers
- two bowls of water
- one bottle of white wine
- manioc flour or breadcrumbs
- butter
- paper

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from one or two eels and place them in a pot with two onions, one potato or carrots, parsley, thyme, salt, peppers, two bowls of water and one bottle of white wine
2. Let it boil for half an hour, remove the pot from the fire and let it cool
3. Remove the fish and cover them with a layer of manioc flour or breadcrumbs
4. Tie them to a spit wrapping them in paper greased with butter
5. Roast them over regular heat for half an hour, remove the paper and serve with the sauce well reduced

title: Enroladas de Carne de Vacca (Beef Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef, sliced thin
- sardines, chopped
- onions, chopped
- ground pepper
- nutmeg
- salt
- piece of bacon the size of a finger
- butter
- wheat flour
- water

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into thin slices; beat these slices with the back of a knife, and add to them chopped sardines and onions, ground pepper, nutmeg, salt and a piece of bacon the size of a finger.
2. Roll them in such a way that the bacon remains in the center, tie them with thread, and place the prepared rolls in butter and let them braise over coals.
3. When cooked, remove them, pouring over the butter one spoon of wheat flour; let it brown, then add to it two cups of water and salt, putting the rolls in this sauce where they finish cooking.
4. Before serving, remove the thread.

title: Entrecosto com pirão (Beef Short Ribs with Cassava Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef short ribs
- brandy
- onions
- parsley
- bay leaf
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- butter
- cassava flour or potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut the short rib meat into slices and place them for twenty-four hours in an infusion of brandy in a dish with slices of onions, parsley, bay leaf, salt, pepper and nutmeg
2. Half an hour before serving, melt a little butter over a lively fire
3. When well hot, add the meat slices
4. Turn them and serve with cassava flour paste or potatoes

title: Entrecosto de Espeto (Spit-Roasted Rib)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of well-meaty rib
- salt
- ground pepper
- dried bread crumbs, ground

## Directions

1. Take a well-meaty piece of rib, spread it with salt and ground pepper, and roast it on a spit with its own sauce.
2. Once roasted, cover the meaty part with ground dried bread crumbs, let it cook a bit more, and serve.

title: Entrecosto de Porco do Matto Assado no Espeto (Wild Pork Rib Roasted on the Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- one rib
- salt
- vinegar
- juniper berries
- melted butter mixed with red wine
- breadcrumbs
- parsley
- chopped onion

## Directions

1. Take one rib and marinate it in salt, vinegar and juniper berries for three hours.
2. Thread it on the spit and roast it, basting with melted butter mixed with red wine.
3. When almost roasted, cover it with breadcrumbs, parsley and chopped onion.
4. When cooked and of good color, serve it with the sauce that dripped into the dripping pan.

title: Entrecosto de Vacca (Beef Short Ribs)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of beef short ribs
- salt
- ground pepper

## Directions

1. Take a piece of beef short ribs, remove the tendons, and cut into slices the thickness of a finger
2. Beat for a while, rubbing the slices with salt and ground pepper
3. Place on the grill over live fire until the ribs are roasted

title: Entrecosto de vacca (Beef Short Ribs)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef short ribs
- salt
- ground pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Take a piece of beef short ribs, remove the tendons, and cut into slices the thickness of a finger
2. Beat them for a while, rubbing the slices with salt and ground pepper
3. Place on the grill over a lively fire until the ribs are roasted
4. Serve with a chef's sauce, fried potatoes, or a spicy sauce

title: Escabeche Oysters (Ostras de Escabeche)
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters, opened
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- white wine

## Directions

1. Take opened oysters and heat them over hot coals to scald them in their own water
2. Remove them and place in a pan
3. Sprinkle with salt, pepper, and nutmeg
4. Pour white wine over them so that it covers them
5. Keep them to be served cold

title: Escaldado (Poached)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoonful of fat
- one bottle of hot water
- salt
- some onion leaves
- eggs according to number of servings
- manioc flour or cornmeal

## Directions

1. Fry one spoonful of fat until it stops crackling
2. Pour one bottle of hot water, salt, and some onion leaves into it
3. When this broth is boiling, add a portion of eggs according to the number of people to be served
4. When the egg whites are cooked and the yolks soft, remove with a skimmer and place on a separate plate
5. Make a porridge with this broth using manioc flour or cornmeal, placing the poached eggs on top

title: Espargos Guisados com Nata (Asparagus Braised with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- water
- salt
- butter
- one spoonful of wheat flour
- one cup of good milk or cream
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Boil asparagus in salted water and let drain
2. Separate 24 tips and sauté in melted butter
3. At the same time, boil one spoonful of wheat flour with one cup of good milk or cream
4. Season with salt and pepper
5. Pour over the asparagus and serve

title: Espargos à Maneira de Hervilhas (Asparagus in the Manner of Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus shoots
- water
- salt
- butter
- pepper
- wheat flour
- meat broth
- nutmeg
- sugar
- three egg yolks

## Directions

1. Take asparagus shoots and cut into small pieces
2. Place in a casserole with boiling salted water
3. When they have boiled once, remove with a skimmer and let drain
4. Afterwards place in cold water, being careful not to crush them
5. Half an hour before serving, let drain and sauté with a little butter, salt and broken Indian pepper
6. Sprinkle with a good pinch of wheat flour, moisten with a little broth and add salt and peppers (if lacking, use grated nutmeg and sugar)
7. Let boil, thicken the sauce with three egg yolks and serve

title: Fartes de Carne de Porco (Pork Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- portion of leftover pork
- pork belly rind or ham rind
- salt
- pepper
- spices

## Directions

1. Take a portion of leftover pork with pork belly rind or ham rind, and cut everything into small pieces and season as one seasons sausages.
2. Take a portion of this meat wrapped in pork belly rind or in strips of ham.
3. Sew at both ends and make as many as the meat yields.
4. Place them in brine for eight days.
5. After that time, take them to the smokehouse and let them dry.
6. When you want to eat them, soak them in water for twenty-four hours.
7. After that, place them to cook in plenty of water and serve.

title: Fatias de Carne de Carneiro com Carapicús (Mutton Slices with Capers)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton slices
- white wine
- water
- strips of bacon
- onions
- cloves
- salt
- pepper
- one bay leaf
- one spoonful of butter
- one spoonful of wheat flour
- one spoonful of vinegar
- carapicús (capers), scalded, chosen, chopped and cooked in water and salt

## Directions

1. Cut and beat the slices, cook them partly in white wine and water in a pot lined with strips of bacon
2. Add onions, cloves, salt, pepper and a bay leaf
3. When cooked, remove the slices and the fat from the broth
4. In another pan, melt one spoonful of butter, mix it with one spoonful of wheat flour, one spoonful of vinegar and a portion of scalded, chosen, chopped and cooked capers
5. Add this to the meat broth, and when the broth is well reduced, serve this pulp with the slices

title: Fatias Douradas (Gilded Slices)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- white wine
- three egg yolks
- two tablespoons sugar
- butter
- cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut bread into slices
2. Soak in white wine, remove and let drain
3. Pass the slices through three beaten egg yolks mixed with two tablespoons of sugar
4. Fry in butter
5. Cover with cinnamon and sugar
6. Serve hot

title: Favas Cozidas (Cooked Fava Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- a portion of fava beans
- water
- salt
- one quarter pound of chopped bacon
- one chopped onion
- parsley
- vinegar or sour orange juice

## Directions

1. Take a portion of fava beans
2. Soak in cold water for some hours
3. Remove and put to cook in water and salt
4. After boiling, remove the pan from the heat and strain the water
5. Fry one quarter pound of chopped bacon with one chopped onion, parsley and salt
6. Add the fava beans
7. Stir and add the water in which the fava beans were cooked
8. Let boil until dried and serve
9. If desired, add one spoonful of vinegar or sour orange juice at this time

title: Favas Cozidas (Stewed Fava Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- fava beans
- salt
- water
- lard
- onion
- parsley
- vinegar or sour orange juice (optional)

## Directions

1. Take a portion of fava beans and soak them in cold water for several hours.
2. Remove them and put them to cook in water and salt.
3. Once boiled, remove the pan from the fire and drain the water.
4. In another part, fry a quarter of chopped lard with a chopped onion, parsley and salt.
5. Add the fava beans and stir, then add the water in which the fava beans cooked.
6. Let it boil until it dries, and serve.
7. If desired, add a spoonful of vinegar or sour orange juice at this point.

title: Favas do Reino Refogadas (Sautéed Royal Fava Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- royal fava beans
- fat
- water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Take a portion of royal fava beans and boil them in water so that you can remove the skin that covers them.
2. By this method, squeezing between the fingers, it comes off easily.
3. In another part, fry a little fat, add half a bottle of water, salt, parsley, onion leaves and the cooked and peeled fava beans.
4. Boil for a quarter of an hour, add a glass of vinegar and serve.

title: Favas Novas com Leite (New Fava Beans with Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- young fava beans
- butter
- wheat flour
- cream or whole milk
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. After scalding and draining, put them in a casserole with a little butter.
2. Sprinkle a little wheat flour on top and sauté.
3. Moisten with cream or whole milk, let cook slowly.
4. Thicken with egg yolks and serve.

title: Favas Refogadas (Sautéed Fava Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- a portion of fresh green fava beans
- one spoonful of fat
- some chopped onion leaves
- one cup of water
- salt
- parsley
- a little lemon juice or vinegar

## Directions

1. Take a portion of fresh green fava beans
2. Fry one spoonful of fat with some chopped onion leaves
3. Add one cup of water and the fava beans
4. Boil for half an hour
5. Add salt, parsley and a little lemon juice or vinegar
6. Serve

title: Feijoada (Feijoada)
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- water
- salt
- lard
- sausages
- pork
- dried meat
- colonial meat
- onions
- garlic

## Directions

1. Put the chosen and washed beans in a pan with water, salt, a piece of lard, some sausages, pork, dried meat, colonial meat, two split onions and a clove of garlic.
2. Let boil four to five times, and once cooked and the water reduced, serve.

title: Feijão Branco Ensopado (Stewed White Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- white beans
- fat
- reduced broth
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves

## Directions

1. Cook a portion of white beans.
2. Once cooked, fry a spoonful of fat in another part.
3. Add the drained beans and stir.
4. Add the reduced broth, salt, parsley and onion leaves.
5. Let it boil a little and serve.

title: Feijão Branco Guisado (Braised White Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- white beans
- butter
- wheat flour
- onion
- vinegar
- salt
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cook a portion of white beans.
2. In another part, fry two spoonfuls of butter, two of wheat flour and a chopped onion.
3. Add two spoonfuls of vinegar and stir.
4. Add the cooked beans and broth, salt and parsley.
5. Let it boil and serve.

title: Feijão Branco Guisado com Ovos (Braised White Beans with Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- fat
- cornmeal
- onion
- white beans
- water
- salt
- eggs
- sour orange juice (optional)

## Directions

1. Fry two spoonfuls of fat with a spoonful of cornmeal and a sliced onion.
2. Add the white beans cooked in water and salt and drained.
3. Stir and add the bean broth.
4. While boiling, break some eggs into it so that they remain whole.
5. If desired, add a glass of sour orange juice and serve.

title: Feijão Marumbé Refogado (Sautéed Marumbé Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- marumbé beans
- onion
- fat
- water

## Directions

1. Sauté a sliced onion in two spoonfuls of fat.
2. Add half a bottle of water and the washed beans.
3. Let boil until the beans are cooked and serve.

title: Feijão Marumbé à Mineira (Marumbé Beans Miner's Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- marumbé beans
- water
- salt
- fat
- parsley
- onion leaves

## Directions

1. Cook a portion of marumbé beans in water and salt.
2. Once cooked, drain the water.
3. Heat a little fat with parsley and onion leaves and add the beans.
4. Stir and add the broth, let boil and serve.

title: Feijão Preto em Tutú (Black Beans Tutú)
body:
## Ingredients

- black beans
- fat
- salt
- garlic
- onion
- chili pepper
- bean broth
- cornmeal or cassava flour
- sausages
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Cook a portion of beans, drain and season with plenty of fat, salt, garlic, onion, chili pepper.
2. Add the bean broth and a portion of cornmeal or cassava flour.
3. Stir well over the fire until the flour is cooked and serve, pouring a slice of fried or roasted sausages and some rounds of potatoes on top.

title: Feijão Preto em Tutú à Baiana (Black Beans Tutú Bahian Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- black beans
- water
- salt
- lard
- parsley
- onion
- chili peppers
- garlic
- hot water
- cassava flour
- onion leaves

## Directions

1. Cook a portion of beans in water and salt.
2. Once cooked, drain the broth.
3. Fry a piece of chopped lard with parsley, onion, chili peppers, salt and a clove of garlic.
4. Add the beans and stir, mashing.
5. Add a portion of hot water and cassava flour, stirring until it forms a half-firm paste.
6. Put on the plate, covering with a branch of parsley and chopped onion leaves, and pouring a half portion of fat on top.
7. Serve with pork loins, roasted suckling pig or fried sausages.

title: Feijão Preto à Moda Brazileira (Black Beans Brazilian Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- black beans
- water
- fat
- onion leaves
- garlic
- salt

## Directions

1. Choose and wash a portion of black beans, drain and put to boil in water for six to eight hours, adding hot water from time to time as needed.
2. Once cooked, drain the water.
3. In another part, melt two spoonfuls of fat, add some onion leaves, a clove of garlic and salt.
4. Then add the beans, stir with a wooden spoon, mash the beans well and add the broth that drains off.
5. Boil until the water almost dries and serve.

title: Feijão Preto à Moda dos Colonos (Black Beans Colonist's Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- black beans
- onion
- butter
- parsley
- salt

## Directions

1. Once the beans are cooked, drain the water.
2. In another part, fry a chopped onion in butter with parsley and salt.
3. Add the beans without mashing and serve.

title: Figado de Vitella Assado (Roasted Calf's Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf liver
- wheat flour
- one egg
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Cut the liver into slices
2. Pass it first in wheat flour, then in beaten egg, and afterwards again in wheat flour
3. Roast it on the grill and serve with melted butter

title: Figado de Vitella Guisado (Another Way) (Stewed Calf's Liver - Another Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf liver
- white wine
- salt
- pepper
- clove
- parsley
- marjoram
- pieces of salt pork
- egg yolks
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Soak the liver in white wine with salt, pepper, clove, parsley and marjoram for twenty-four hours
2. Place on the fire with the seasonings, adding some pieces of salt pork
3. Let it boil
4. When cooked, remove it and strain the broth
5. Thicken with egg yolks, add a little lemon juice and serve

title: Figado de Vitella Guisado (Stewed Calf's Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf liver
- melted hot butter
- one tablespoon wheat flour
- one onion
- one tablespoon white wine
- salt
- pepper
- cooking herbs

## Directions

1. Cut the liver into slices and place in a casserole with melted hot butter
2. Let it fry on both sides
3. Then add one tablespoon of wheat flour, a cut onion, stir everything
4. Add one tablespoon of white wine, salt, pepper, cooking herbs and let it boil over moderate heat

title: Figado de Vitella Lardeado (Larded Calf's Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf liver
- hot water
- white wine
- carrots
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- bundle of cooking herbs

## Directions

1. Lard the liver after scalding it in hot water
2. Let it cool, then place on the fire with white wine, carrots, onion, salt, pepper and a bundle of cooking herbs
3. Let it cook over moderate heat and serve

title: Figado de Vitella Recheado (Stuffed Calf's Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf liver
- salt pork
- water
- salt
- grated bread crumbs
- sugar
- one tablespoon vinegar

## Directions

1. Take the liver and make some holes in it, which are filled with salt pork
2. Let it cook in water and salt
3. Remove the foam from time to time
4. Finally add grated bread crumbs, a little sugar and one tablespoon of vinegar

title: Figado de Vitella Refogado (Braised Calf's Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf liver
- wheat flour
- butter
- meat broth
- one glass of wine
- one bay leaf
- slices of lemon
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the liver into slices two fingers thick
2. Lard them, sprinkle with wheat flour, and fry in butter in a casserole
3. Add a little meat broth, a glass of wine, a bay leaf, slices of lemon and salt
4. Let them boil over coals for half an hour and serve

title: Figado de Vitella à Parmesão (Calf's Liver Parmesan)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf liver
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chive leaves
- one garlic clove
- sweet oil or butter
- wheat flour
- grated cheese
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Cut the liver into very fine slices and season with salt, pepper, parsley, chive leaves and one garlic clove
2. Fry in a casserole with sweet oil or butter
3. When cooked, place on the plate
4. Sprinkle with wheat flour and grated cheese
5. Pour a little melted butter over it
6. Place over coals, let it heat well and serve

title: Frango Assado na Grelha com Mangaritos (Grilled Chicken with Mangaritos)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- salt
- parsley
- pepper
- garlic juice
- vinegar
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- mangaritos

## Directions

1. Take the chicken and cut it into four pieces
2. Soak the pieces with salt, chopped parsley, pepper, a little garlic juice and a spoonful of vinegar
3. Wrap in breadcrumbs and roast on the grill, dripping a little butter on it from time to time
4. Turn to roast on both sides at the same time
5. Fry some mangaritos in butter and after cutting them in slices, serve them around the roasted chicken pieces

title: Frango Assado no Espeto e Recheado (Roasted Chicken Stuffed on the Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- two hard-boiled eggs
- chicken giblets
- parsley
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- thin slice of bacon

## Directions

1. Chop finely two hard-boiled eggs, the chicken giblets and a little parsley and mix with a spoonful of breadcrumbs and another of butter, salt and pepper
2. Fill the chicken with this stuffing
3. Roast on the spit, covering it with a very thin slice of bacon
4. Serve with salad after roasting it properly

title: Frango Assado no Espeto e Recheado (Stuffed Chicken Roasted on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cooked eggs, finely chopped
- chicken giblets
- parsley
- bread crumbs
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- thin bacon strip
- salad

## Directions

1. Mix two finely chopped cooked eggs, chicken giblets, and parsley with one spoon of bread crumbs and one spoon of butter
2. Season with salt and pepper
3. Stuff the chicken with this filling
4. Roast on a spit covered with a very thin bacon strip
5. When properly roasted, serve with salad

title: Frango Assado no Espeto em Papelotes (Chicken Roasted on a Spit in Paper)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- butter
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Prepare the chicken and place on a spit covered with paper greased with butter
2. Roast over coals
3. When roasted, remove the paper
4. Coat with bread crumbs
5. Pour melted butter on top
6. Turn over the coals until golden, then serve

title: Frango Assado no Forno (Roasted Chicken in the Oven)
body:
## Ingredients

- handful of bread crumb softened with milk
- two small spoonfuls of butter
- two dozen peanuts crushed
- two spoonfuls of sugar
- four egg yolks
- nutmeg
- saffron
- salt
- parsley
- clove

## Directions

1. Knead a handful of bread crumb softened with milk and two small spoonfuls of butter
2. Mix with two dozen crushed peanuts and two spoonfuls of sugar, four egg yolks, a little nutmeg and saffron
3. Fill the chicken with this stuffing
4. Put it in a pan, wrapped in slices of bacon, after seasoning with salt, parsley and clove
5. Roast in the oven and after removing the bacon, serve with its sauce and some salad

title: Frango Desossado Assado no Forno (Boned Chicken Roasted in the Oven)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- two pounds of veal
- half pound of bacon
- two onions
- chicken giblets
- two hard-boiled eggs
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- parsley
- marjoram
- aguardente do reino
- slices of bacon
- two glasses of white wine
- two cups of water
- onion leaves
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Open the chicken through the back and remove all the bones, leaving only the neck, wings, legs and hip bone
2. Chop two pounds of veal with half a pound of bacon, two onions, the chicken giblets, two hard-boiled eggs, salt, pepper, nutmeg, parsley and marjoram
3. Moisten with a little aguardente do reino and fill the chicken with this stuffing
4. Wrap it in slices of bacon and place in a pan with two glasses of white wine, two cups of water, salt, onion leaves and the juice of a lemon
5. Cook until done, remove the chicken, strain the sauce, remove the fat and pour it over the chicken to serve

title: Frango Ensopado com Cará (Chicken Stew with Cará)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- quarter pound of chopped bacon
- cará
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- pepper
- three egg yolks
- polvilho
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take a chicken cut into pieces and place on the fire in a pan with a quarter pound of chopped bacon and a cará cut into pieces with salt, parsley, onion leaves and pepper
2. Let them boil until cooked
3. Thicken the sauce with three egg yolks and a pinch of polvilho dissolved in two spoonfuls of lemon juice and serve the chicken

title: Frango Ensopado com Cenouras (Chicken Stew with Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- two chickens
- carrots
- parsley
- wheat flour
- butter
- vinegar
- water
- sugar
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Cut two chickens into pieces and boil them
2. When almost cooked, add a portion of cut carrots with parsley and a spoonful of wheat flour kneaded with two spoonfuls of butter and a spoonful of vinegar dissolved in four cups of water
3. Let them cook near the fire until everything is done
4. Add half a spoonful of sugar and an egg yolk and serve at table

title: Frango Frito com Bananas (Fried Chicken with Bananas)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- butter
- wheat flour
- egg yolks
- dozen bananas
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Roast a chicken on the spit, greased with butter and when almost cooked, remove it and cut into pieces
2. Coat the pieces in wheat flour, then pass through beaten egg yolks, then again in wheat flour and fry in butter
3. On the other hand, take a dozen bananas split in half and coat in wheat flour, fry them equally in butter
4. Serve the fried bananas with the chicken pieces on top and covered with sugar and cinnamon

title: Frango Frito com Capa (Fried Chicken with Coating)
body:
## Ingredients

- young chicken
- salt
- wheat flour
- beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Cut a young chicken into four pieces and sprinkle with salt
2. Wrap first in wheat flour, then in beaten eggs and again in wheat flour
3. Fry on both sides in butter over high heat and serve the chicken with salads or marmalade

title: Frango Frito com Hervilhas (Fried Chicken with Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- water
- salt
- onion leaves
- young peas
- fat
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cook a chicken cut into pieces in water, salt and onion leaves
2. When cooked, boil fresh peas in the same water
3. On the other hand, fry the chicken pieces in two spoonfuls of fat after rolling them in breadcrumbs

title: Frango Frito com Hervilhas (Fried Chicken with Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- water
- salt
- onion leaves
- fresh peas
- two spoons of lard
- breadcrumbs
- one spoon of wheat flour

## Directions

1. Cook the chicken, cut into pieces, in water with salt and onion leaves
2. When cooked, boil fresh peas in the same water
3. In another part, fry the chicken pieces coated in breadcrumbs in two spoons of lard
4. When browned on both sides, remove them
5. In the same lard, add one spoon of wheat flour, mix well
6. Immediately add the peas with all the broth
7. Let them finish drying and serve with the peas around the chicken pieces

title: Frango Frito com Palmitos (Fried Chicken with Palm Hearts)
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- palm heart
- water

## Directions

1. Cut the chickens into four pieces and dust with salt and pepper
2. Sauté in butter, coat the pieces in breadcrumbs and finish frying in butter
3. In another part, boil a palm heart cut into pieces in water and salt
4. Arrange on a plate covered with a layer of breadcrumbs
5. Place the fried chicken pieces on top
6. Pour over all the sauce in which the chickens were fried and serve
7. Can be served in the same manner with asparagus or squash shoots

title: Frango Guisado (Stewed Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- water
- one onion
- half pound of bacon
- salt
- parsley
- cloves
- pepper
- a little saffron
- vinegar
- a handful of bread crumb
- eight egg yolks
- two glasses of wine
- one spoon of sugar

## Directions

1. Boil water with cut onion, chopped bacon, salt, and parsley
2. When boiling, add the chicken, cloves, pepper, saffron, and vinegar
3. When the chicken is cooked, remove and cut it, leaving the bones aside
4. Strain the broth and reduce it
5. Mix with a handful of bread crumb, eight egg yolks, two glasses of wine, and one spoon of sugar
6. Return the chicken pieces to this sauce
7. Let it boil a little more and serve at the table

title: Frango Guisado com Carapicú (Stewed Chicken with Carapicú)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- quarter pound of bacon
- salt
- parsley
- pepper
- cloves of India
- onion
- a handful of carapicús
- juice of one bitter orange
- one spoon of tapioca
- one spoon of sugar
- three egg yolks
- one glass of wine or water

## Directions

1. Cook chicken cut into pieces with chopped bacon, salt, parsley, pepper, cloves of India, and onion
2. When cooked, remove it and strain the broth, reducing it by half
3. Add a handful of carapicús, juice of one bitter orange, one spoon of tapioca, one spoon of sugar, and three egg yolks mixed in one glass of wine or water
4. Bring the sauce to a boil, add the chicken pieces, finish cooking, and serve with simple cornmeal mush or with the sauce

title: Frango Guisado com Palmitos (Stewed Chicken with Palm Hearts)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- one palm heart
- water
- half pound of bacon
- salt
- parsley
- one onion
- a little basil
- two spoons of wheat flour
- one spoon of vinegar
- one spoon of sugar
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook chicken and palm heart cut into pieces and tied in a bundle in water with half pound of bacon, salt, parsley, one onion, and a little basil
2. When cooked, remove the chicken pieces and palm heart, strain the broth and reduce it
3. Mix in two spoons of wheat flour, one spoon of vinegar, one spoon of sugar, two egg yolks
4. When everything is cooked, add back the chicken pieces and palm heart and serve after they have warmed through

title: Frango Recheiado (Stuffed Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- chicken giblets
- a handful of carapicús
- half dozen sardines
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- one onion
- two cloves of India
- water
- garlic
- sweet oil
- vinegar
- bacon strips

## Directions

1. Open the chicken along the back and remove the bones
2. Fill it with giblets, chopped carapicús, half dozen sardines, little salt, pepper, parsley, one onion, and two cloves of India
3. Soak it in water with garlic, salt, pepper, sweet oil, and vinegar
4. After four to five hours have passed, wrap it in bacon strips and roast on the grill, serving it with its own sauce

title: Frango Refogado com Creme (Chicken Braised with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken, cut into four parts
- butter
- one bottle of milk
- half spoon of cornstarch
- salt
- nutmeg
- cinnamon
- four egg yolks
- one spoon of sugar
- one cup of wine

## Directions

1. Fry the chicken pieces with a little butter
2. Boil one bottle of milk with half spoon of cornstarch, salt, nutmeg, and cinnamon
3. Beat four egg yolks with one spoon of sugar
4. Slowly add the hot milk to the egg mixture
5. Return to the fire, add the chicken pieces and one cup of wine
6. When cooked, serve

title: Frango Refogado com Palmitos (Chicken Braised with Palm Hearts)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken, cut into pieces
- hot fat
- one finely chopped and washed palm heart
- two cups of water
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- ora-pro-nobis leaves
- one small spoon of cornstarch dissolved in cold water
- one spoon of lemon juice

## Directions

1. Braise the chicken pieces in hot fat until half fried; remove them
2. In the same fat, braise the finely chopped palm heart; stir
3. Add two cups of water and the chicken pieces
4. Season with salt, pepper, and parsley
5. Add ora-pro-nobis leaves and the cornstarch dissolved in cold water
6. Add lemon juice
7. Let simmer near the fire until dried; serve with cornmeal porridge

title: Frango Refogado com Pepinos (Chicken Braised with Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- two chickens, cut into pieces
- cow butter
- salt
- pepper
- one spoon of wheat flour
- three cups of water
- one cup of vinegar
- whole mustard
- one onion
- one dozen peeled cucumbers without seeds
- toasted bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Fry the salted and peppered chicken pieces in cow butter until almost cooked; remove them
2. Roll the pieces in toasted bread and fry again until well browned on both sides; remove them
3. In the same butter, add one spoon of wheat flour, then three cups of water and one cup of vinegar
4. Season with salt, pepper, and whole mustard
5. Add the onion and let simmer
6. Add one dozen peeled, seeded cucumbers, cut lengthwise into four pieces
7. Let everything simmer a little more
8. Arrange the cucumbers on a plate covered with bread crumbs
9. Place the chicken pieces on top, pour the sauce over all, and serve

title: Frango Refogado com Raiz de Inhame (Chicken Braised with Yam Root)
body:
## Ingredients

- two chickens, cut into four pieces
- butter
- two spoons of wheat flour
- one cup of wine
- one cup of water
- two spoons of sugar
- salt
- cinnamon
- one yam root, sliced and pre-cooked for two to three hours in water and salt

## Directions

1. Brown the chicken pieces in butter; remove them
2. In the same butter, braise two spoons of wheat flour until it becomes a light golden color
3. Add one cup of wine and one cup of water
4. Add two spoons of sugar, salt, and cinnamon
5. Return the chicken pieces to the sauce along with the pre-cooked yam root slices
6. Let everything simmer for half an hour and serve

title: Frango Refogado em Molho Branco (Chicken Braised in White Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken, cut into pieces
- wine with garlic
- hot butter
- one cup of water
- one spoon of wheat flour
- juice of one lemon
- lemon slices without seeds
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Soak the chicken pieces in garlic wine for some time
2. Braise in hot butter until browned
3. Add one cup of water and one spoon of wheat flour
4. Add lemon juice and lemon slices
5. Let simmer until cooked
6. Thicken the sauce with two egg yolks and serve

title: Frangos Assados com Creme (Roasted Chicken with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- one fat chicken
- butter
- bread crumbs
- rich milk
- two egg yolks
- one spoon of sugar
- one spoon of wine
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Wrap the chicken in paper greased with butter and roast on a spit
2. When almost done, remove the paper
3. Wrap the chicken in bread crumbs and continue roasting
4. Moisten with rich milk
5. When cooked and golden, thicken the drippings with two egg yolks
6. Add one spoon of sugar, one spoon of wine, and nutmeg
7. Let simmer briefly and serve the chicken with this sauce

title: Frangos Assados Lardeados (Roasted Larded Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- young fat chickens
- thin strips of bacon
- butter
- green peas

## Directions

1. Lard the young fat chickens with thin bacon strips
2. Cover with paper greased with butter
3. Roast on a spit over a lively fire
4. Serve with its own drippings and green peas

title: Frangos Assados no Burralho (Chicken Roasted in Embers)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- chicken giblets
- ham
- bacon
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- half dozen peeled pine nuts
- bacon strips
- pine nuts for cooking

## Directions

1. Fill the chicken with a mixture of giblets, ham, bacon, parsley, salt, pepper, and peeled pine nuts, all finely chopped
2. Wrap it in bacon strips and cover with paper
3. Place with pine nuts to roast in the embers, putting coals on top
4. When roasted, peel the pine nuts and place on a plate
5. Place the chicken in the center
6. Remove the paper and bacon, and serve

title: Frangos Cozidos na Capa (Chicken Cooked in a Crust)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- salt
- pepper
- chicken giblets
- onion
- hard-boiled eggs
- parsley
- wheat flour
- slices of bacon

## Directions

1. Take a chicken and rub it with salt and pepper
2. Fill it with the giblets, an onion, some hard-boiled eggs, salt and chopped pepper
3. Wrap it in slices of bacon
4. Cover with a good layer of wheat flour
5. Boil in salted water
6. When cooked, remove the bacon crust and serve with a spicy sauce

title: Frangos de Caramelos (Caramelized Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- salt
- nutmeg
- parsley
- sugar
- sauce from roasting
- glass of red wine

## Directions

1. Season a chicken with salt, nutmeg and chopped parsley
2. Rub it well with these seasonings and roast on the spit, covered with paper
3. When roasted, dissolve one pound of sugar in the sauce that dripped down
4. Add a glass of red wine to it and place the chicken in this broth
5. Let it boil a little and serve, sprinkling it with cinnamon

title: Frangos Dourados (Golden Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- salt
- pepper
- paper
- butter
- egg yolks
- young peas

## Directions

1. Roast a chicken on the spit after rubbing with salt and pepper and wrapping in paper greased with butter
2. When roasted, remove it and cut into four pieces
3. Wrap the pieces in beaten egg yolks and fry in butter
4. Sprinkle the rest of the eggs on top and when fried on both sides, serve with fresh young peas

title: Frangos Ensopados com Guándus (Chicken Stew with Pigeon Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- two chickens
- salt
- parsley
- onion
- water
- half plate of pigeon peas
- butter
- wheat flour
- vinegar
- chicken broth

## Directions

1. Cook two chickens cut into four pieces with salt, parsley, onion and little water
2. On the other side, boil half a plate of pigeon peas with water and salt
3. Melt two spoonfuls of butter and mix two spoonfuls of wheat flour, two of vinegar and the broth from the chickens
4. After boiling a little, add the chicken pieces and the cooked pigeon peas without their water and let them boil for half an hour more before serving

title: Frangos Ensopados com Quiabos (Chicken Stew with Okra)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- fat
- water
- okra
- polvilho
- salt
- parsley
- onion
- juice of one terra orange
- cornmeal mush

## Directions

1. Sauté the pieces of a chicken in two spoonfuls of fat until golden on both sides
2. Add half a bottle of water, a portion of cut okra, half a spoonful of polvilho, salt, parsley, onion and the juice of a terra orange
3. Let them boil until cooked and serve with cornmeal mush

title: Frangos Refogados com Alcachofras (Chicken Braised with Artichokes)
body:
## Ingredients

- two chickens, cut into pieces
- lard or fat for frying
- one spoon of wheat flour
- one bottle of water
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- one onion
- artichoke bottoms
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Fry the chicken pieces in fat; when browned on both sides, remove them
2. Add one spoon of wheat flour to the fat; stir and add one bottle of water
3. Season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg
4. Add the sliced onion and artichoke bottoms along with the fried chicken pieces
5. Simmer near the fire until everything is cooked
6. Thicken the sauce with two egg yolks and serve

title: Frangos Refogados com Alcachofras (Chickens Sautéed with Artichokes)
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of two chickens
- fat for frying
- one spoon of wheat flour
- one bottle of water
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- one onion, cut
- artichoke bottoms

## Directions

1. Fry the pieces of two chickens in fat.
2. When browned on both sides, remove them and add one spoon of wheat flour to the fat.
