3. Fry a piece of chopped bacon, add the purée and stir, putting the mass on a plate
4. Put on top bread cut in small pieces fried in lard until well toasted and a chopped onion, and serve

title: Hervilhas Seccas em Quibebe (Dried Peas Quibebe)
body:
## Ingredients

- pea purée
- cups of milk cream
- two spoons of sugar
- breadcrumbs
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Take the pea purée, fry in cups of milk cream with two spoons of sugar
2. Boil over coals, stirring until it hardens
3. Put on a plate, and having covered with breadcrumbs and melted butter, serve

title: Hervilhas Seccas Refogadas (Sautéed Dried Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- a portion of dried peas
- water and salt
- a piece of bacon
- one chopped onion
- a little sugar

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of dried peas in water and salt
2. When cooked, fry a piece of bacon and one chopped onion
3. Add a little sugar and the cooked dried peas
4. Let it boil a little and serve

title: Hervilhas Verdes com Cenouras (Green Peas with Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- a portion of green peas
- new carrots, chopped
- a little butter
- about half a bottle of water
- salt
- a little white wine
- a little sugar

## Directions

1. Take a portion of green peas and new carrots, chopped and brown in a little butter
2. Add about half a bottle of water and salt
3. When boiled a little, add a little white wine and a little sugar
4. Continue to boil and serve

title: Inhame Cozido (Boiled Yam)
body:
## Ingredients

- yam
- water
- salt
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the root of a yam in water and salt until well cooked, because if not it will burn the throat
2. After cooking, peel and serve with powdered sugar
3. Alternatively, cut into slices, place on a plate for the oven, cover with sugar, and let roast until it takes color

title: Inhame Ensopado (Yam Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- yam
- water
- salt
- cheese
- fat
- wheat flour
- pepper
- milk

## Directions

1. Cook the root of a yam and peel and cut it into pieces
2. Cut some pieces of cheese and sauté them together in a casserole with two spoons of fat
3. Add a little wheat flour, a little salt, pepper, and a cup of rich milk
4. Leave to boil for half an hour and serve
5. Instead of milk, one can use a cup of water with a spoon of vinegar, and thicken with an egg yolk

title: Inhame Refogado (Sautéed Yam)
body:
## Ingredients

- yams
- water
- salt
- fat
- garlic
- onion
- Indian pepper

## Directions

1. Cook a portion of well-washed yams without peeling them in water and salt
2. After cooking, remove the skins and cut into small pieces
3. Sauté in fat with salt, garlic, onion, and Indian peppers
4. Add a little water, boil a little, and serve

title: Inhuma, Pato Silvestre, etc. Assados no Burralho (Wild Duck Roasted in Coals)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bird
- liver of the bird
- half pound of pork meat
- half quarter of bacon
- salt
- parsley
- onion, chopped
- one dozen tomatoes
- two cloves
- peppers
- thin slice of bacon
- banana leaves
- spicy sauce

## Directions

1. Chop the liver of the bird with half pound of pork meat and half quarter of bacon
2. Season with salt, parsley, chopped onion, one dozen tomatoes, two cloves and peppers
3. Stuff the bird with this mixture
4. Wrap it in a thin slice of bacon
5. Wrap it then in banana leaves
6. Place it in hot coals and let it roast for two to three hours
7. Remove, unwrap from the leaf and bacon
8. Serve with a spicy sauce

title: Inhuma, Pato Silvestre, etc. Assados no Espeto (Wild Duck Roasted on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bird
- salt
- pepper juice
- garlic
- onion
- orange of the land
- boiled rice
- pieces of bacon
- sugar
- white wine
- thin strips of bacon for larding
- banana leaves

## Directions

1. Rub the bird with salt, pepper juice, garlic, onion and orange of the land, leaving it with this seasoning for three to four hours
2. Fill it with boiled rice, pieces of bacon, salt and sugar melted in white wine
3. Lard the breast with thin strips of bacon
4. Wrap it in banana leaves
5. Place it on a spit and roast for two hours
6. When cooked, serve with some salad

title: Inhuma, Pato Silvestre, etc. Ensopados com Taioba (Wild Duck Stewed with Taioba)
body:
## Ingredients

- two spoons of butter
- one spoon of fine cornmeal
- one bowl of water
- one bird, cut into pieces
- one taioba root, cut into pieces
- salt
- parsley
- peppers
- half dozen tomatoes
- a little ginger, grated
- one glass of juice of orange of the land
- two egg yolks
- one spoon of brown sugar
- one glass of red wine

## Directions

1. Melt two spoons of butter with one spoon of fine cornmeal
2. Add one bowl of water, one bird cut into pieces, one taioba root cut into pieces, salt, parsley, peppers, half dozen tomatoes, a little grated ginger and one glass of orange juice
3. Let it simmer until cooked
4. Before serving, add two beaten egg yolks mixed with one spoon of brown sugar and one glass of red wine

title: Inhuma, Pato Silvestre, etc. Estufados com Pepinos (Wild Duck Braised with Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- one whole bird
- three spoons of fat
- wheat flour
- one cup of water
- one cup of red wine
- salt
- parsley
- marjoram
- peppers
- tomatoes
- four large cucumbers, peeled and cored

## Directions

1. Braise one whole bird in three spoons of fat
2. Dust it with wheat flour and add one cup of water and one cup of red wine
3. Add salt, parsley, marjoram, peppers, tomatoes and four large cucumbers, peeled, cut into four pieces and cored
4. Cover the pan well
5. Let it cook over coals for two hours
6. Serve. Alternatively, the cucumbers can be chopped and mixed with the tomatoes to stuff the bird before cooking in the same manner

title: Inhuma, Pato Silvestre, etc. Fritos (Fried Wild Duck)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bird, cut into pieces
- salt
- peppers
- tomatoes
- one garlic clove
- one onion, pounded
- breadcrumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut the bird into pieces and soak them with salt, peppers, tomatoes, one garlic clove and one pounded onion
2. After six hours, when the meat has taken on the flavor of the seasonings, dry it and roll it in breadcrumbs
3. Fry on both sides in butter
4. When cooked, serve with the same butter

title: Inhuma, Pato Silvestre, etc. Guisados com Vagens (Wild Duck Stewed with Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bird
- salt
- water
- parsley
- onion leaves
- one quarter of bacon
- one pound of veal meat
- one small spoon of cassava starch
- a portion of beans, chopped
- marjoram
- one onion, chopped
- one glass of lemon juice

## Directions

1. Boil the bird with salt, water, parsley, onion leaves, one quarter of bacon and one pound of veal
2. When cooked, remove it and cut it into pieces
3. Reduce the broth and thicken it with one small spoon of cassava starch
4. Place the meat back in the pan with a portion of chopped beans, marjoram, one chopped onion and one glass of lemon juice
5. Let it simmer until everything is cooked
6. Serve

title: Inhuma, Pato Silvestre, etc. Refogados com Mangalós (Wild Duck Braised with Mangalós)
body:
## Ingredients

- two spoons of wheat flour
- four spoons of butter
- one onion, chopped
- one bird, cut into pieces
- a portion of mangalós, cooked in water and salt
- one glass of vinegar
- parsley
- onion leaves
- one sprig of marjoram

## Directions

1. Braise two spoons of wheat flour with four spoons of butter until the flour is golden
2. Add one chopped onion and the bird cut into pieces
3. Stir and add a portion of cooked mangalós, one glass of vinegar, parsley, onion leaves and one sprig of marjoram
4. Let it simmer for one hour over moderate fire
5. Serve

title: Inhuma, Pato, etc. Estufados com Arroz (Wild Duck Braised with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter of bacon, chopped fine
- one plate of rice, picked and washed
- one bottle of water
- one bird, cut into pieces
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- one dozen tomatoes
- one glass of wine

## Directions

1. Fry one quarter of finely chopped bacon
2. When the fat is melted and hot, add one plate of picked and washed rice
3. Stir and add one bottle of water, one bird cut into pieces, salt, parsley, onion leaves, one dozen tomatoes and one glass of wine
4. Cover the pan well
5. Let it simmer for two hours over moderate fire
6. When cooked, serve

title: Inhuma, Pato, etc. Guisado com Repolho (Wild Duck Stewed with Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bird
- water
- salt
- four spoons of fat
- one white cabbage, chopped
- half bottle of water
- parsley
- onion leaves
- one glass of lemon juice

## Directions

1. Boil the bird with as much water and salt as needed
2. When almost cooked, remove it and cut it into pieces
3. Braise the pieces in four spoons of fat
4. When browned, remove them and braise one chopped white cabbage in the same fat
5. Stir and add half bottle of water, salt, parsley, onion leaves and one glass of lemon juice
6. Add the bird pieces
7. Let it simmer until the broth is reduced
8. Serve

title: Inhuma, Pato, etc. Refogados com Samambaia (Wild Duck Braised with Fern Shoots)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bird
- fat for browning
- one onion, chopped
- one spoon of wheat flour
- a portion of fern shoots (boiled in water and salt)
- one bottle of water
- salt
- peppers
- parsley
- onion leaves

## Directions

1. Take the bird and after cleaning, braise it in fat, turning it on all sides
2. Cut it into pieces and place them back in the same fat after having browned a chopped onion and one spoon of wheat flour in it
3. Add a portion of fern shoots (boiled in water and salt), one bottle of water, salt, peppers, parsley and onion leaves
4. Let it simmer until the broth reduces
5. Serve the pieces of bird over the fern shoots arranged on a plate

title: Irara, Onça e Tamandua Assados (Roasted Irara, Jaguar, and Anteater)
body:
## Ingredients

- young meat from irara, jaguar, or anteater
- lemon juice
- tomatoes, crushed
- comarí peppers
- garlic clove
- onion with cloves

## Directions

1. The meat of these three animals is prepared in the same way
2. The meat should be very young
3. After cleaning, soak it in lemon juice with crushed tomatoes, comarí peppers, one garlic clove, and one onion studded with cloves
4. Leave the meat for twenty-four hours to absorb the seasoning flavors and become more tender
5. Pass it in hot fat and place it on the spit
6. Roast over moderate fire
7. Serve

title: Irara, Onça e Tamandua Ensopados (Stewed Irara, Jaguar, and Anteater)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat from irara, jaguar, or anteater
- strong vinegar
- salt
- parsley
- comarí peppers
- ginger
- nutmeg
- mustard seeds, ground
- fat
- one spoon wheat flour
- two onions
- two cups water
- one glass vinegar

## Directions

1. Soak the meat in strong vinegar, salt, parsley, comarí peppers, ginger, nutmeg, and a portion of ground mustard seeds
2. After twenty-four hours, remove the meat and fry it in fat, turning to toast on all sides
3. Cut the meat into small pieces
4. Brown one spoon of wheat flour, two onions in the same fat
5. Add two cups of water and one glass of vinegar
6. Let it boil a little and add the meat to this sauce
7. Let it boil near the fire until cooked
8. Serve

title: Irara, Onça e Tamandua Guisados (Braised Irara, Jaguar, and Anteater)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of meat from irara, jaguar, or anteater
- two bowls water
- one cup vinegar
- one cup brandy
- salt
- peppers
- parsley
- onion leaves
- ginger
- garlic clove
- two cloves
- dozen tomatoes
- three spoons fat
- three egg yolks

## Directions

1. Take a piece of meat and boil it in two bowls of water, one cup of vinegar, one cup of brandy, salt, peppers, parsley, onion leaves, ginger, one garlic clove, two cloves, and a dozen tomatoes
2. When cooked, remove the meat and brown it in three spoons of fat
3. Add the broth reduced to four cups and thickened with three egg yolks
4. Serve

title: Jacú, Mutúm, etc., Ensopados com Cará do Ar (Game Birds Stewed with Aerial Yam)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bird (jacú, mutúm, etc.)
- one chopped onion
- one spoonful of sugar
- two spoonfuls of fat
- one bowl of water
- one spoonful of fine cornmeal
- one aerial yam, peeled and cut into pieces
- salt
- hot peppers
- parsley
- cumin
- one cup of local orange juice

## Directions

1. Cut the bird into pieces and sauté with one chopped onion and one spoonful of sugar in two spoonfuls of fat, turning the pieces to brown on both sides.
2. Add one bowl of water, one spoonful of fine cornmeal, the peeled and cut aerial yam, salt, hot peppers, parsley, and cumin.
3. Add one cup of local orange juice.
4. Let it boil for one hour and serve when cooked.

title: Jacú, Mutúm, etc., Ensopados com Quiabos (Game Birds Stewed with Okra)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bird (jacú, mutúm, etc.)
- two spoonfuls of fat
- one bowl of water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- some hot peppers (pimentas comaris)
- one glass of juice from local oranges
- two egg yolks
- one small spoonful of tapioca starch
- okra, cut into pieces

## Directions

1. Take the bird and fry it briefly in two spoonfuls of fat, turning it on all sides.
2. Cut it into pieces and return them to the pan.
3. Add one bowl of water, salt, parsley, onion leaves, and some hot peppers.
4. Let it boil for three quarters of an hour.
5. Mix one glass of local orange juice with two egg yolks, one small spoonful of tapioca starch, and add along with the cut okra.
6. Let it finish cooking and serve.

title: Jacú, Mutúm, etc., Guisados (Game Birds Braised)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bird (jacú, mutúm, etc.)
- water
- salt
- parsley
- one glass of brandy
- two spoonfuls of fat
- one chopped onion
- a little wheat flour
- one lemon

## Directions

1. Boil the bird for one hour in water with salt, parsley, and one glass of brandy.
2. Cut it into pieces and sauté in two spoonfuls of fat with one chopped onion and a little wheat flour.
3. Add the reduced cooking broth and let boil for one quarter of an hour.
4. Before serving, squeeze the juice of one lemon over the meat and serve with the sauce well reduced.

title: Jacú, Mutúm, etc., Guisados com Batatinhas (Game Birds Braised with Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bird (jacú, mutúm, etc.)
- a portion of peeled potatoes
- water
- salt
- hot peppers
- parsley leaves
- onion
- ginger
- marjoram
- two spoonfuls of butter
- four egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil the bird with a portion of peeled potatoes in water with salt, hot peppers, parsley leaves, onion, ginger, and marjoram.
2. When cooked, remove the bird and cut it into pieces.
3. Sauté the pieces in two spoonfuls of butter.
4. Add the strained and well-reduced cooking broth and let boil for one quarter of an hour more.
5. Mash the potatoes into a homogeneous paste, mix with four egg yolks, and fry in a casserole in two spoonfuls of butter.
6. Serve the meat over the potato preparation.

title: Jacú, Mutúm, etc., Refogados com Tomates (Game Birds Sautéed with Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bird (jacú, mutúm, etc.)
- water
- salt
- parsley
- hot pepper
- some cabbage leaves
- cloves
- one glass of local orange juice
- breadcrumbs
- fat
- one dozen tomatoes
- one cup of wine
- a handful of carapicús, chopped
- one spoonful of sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the bird into pieces and let boil in water with salt, parsley, hot pepper, some cabbage leaves, cloves, and one glass of local orange juice.
2. When cooked, remove the pieces, coat them in breadcrumbs, and sauté in fat, turning to fry on both sides.
3. When fried, add one dozen tomatoes, one cup of wine, the reduced and strained cooking broth, a handful of chopped carapicús, and one spoonful of sugar.
4. Let boil for another half hour over coals and serve.

title: Lagarto Assado (Roasted Lizard)
body:
## Ingredients

- one lizard
- vinegar
- melted fat
- salt
- parsley
- cloves
- peppers
- butter
- wheat flour

## Directions

1. Soak a lizard in vinegar with a little melted fat, salt, parsley, cloves, and peppers for twelve hours
2. Wrap it in paper greased with butter and place on a spit
3. Roast over moderate heat
4. Remove the paper and dust with wheat flour, dripping fat on top
5. Let it brown and serve with salad or compote

title: Lagarto Ensopado com Ervilhas (Lizard Stewed with Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- one lizard, cut into pieces
- two spoons of fat
- one chopped onion
- wheat flour
- peas

## Directions

1. Cut the lizard into pieces and stew in two spoons of fat with a chopped onion
2. Sprinkle with wheat flour and add peas

title: Lagarto Ensopado com Hervilhas (Lizard Stewed with Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- one lizard, cut into pieces
- two spoonfuls of fat
- one onion, sliced
- wheat flour
- one glass of white wine
- one bottle of water
- salt
- parsley
- marjoram
- a portion of green peas

## Directions

1. Cut the lizard into pieces and braise in two spoonfuls of fat with a sliced onion
2. Dust with wheat flour
3. Add one glass of white wine and one bottle of water, salt, parsley, marjoram, and a portion of green peas
4. Let everything boil over moderate heat for one hour and serve

title: Lagarto Frito com Quibebe (Fried Lizard with Pumpkin Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- one lizard, cut into pieces
- hot water
- salt
- lemon juice
- fat
- one onion, minced
- wheat flour
- quibebe (pumpkin stew)

## Directions

1. Cut a lizard into pieces and scald with very hot water, salt, and a little lemon juice
2. Let cool in this water
3. Fry in as much fat as needed, with a minced onion and dusted with wheat flour
4. Place on a plate with quibebe and serve

title: Lagarto Guisado com Repolho (Lizard Stewed with Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- one lizard
- water
- salt
- peppers
- tomatoes
- parsley
- onion
- carapicús (a vegetable), sliced
- fat
- one glass of vinegar
- cabbage

## Directions

1. Boil a lizard in water with salt, peppers, tomatoes, parsley, onion, and a portion of sliced carapicús
2. When cooked, remove and braise with one spoonful of fat, one glass of vinegar, and the strained broth
3. Let it boil until the broth is reduced
4. Serve with cabbage, adding a little vinegar

title: Lagarto Refogado (Stewed Lizard)
body:
## Ingredients

- one clean lizard
- hot vinegar
- fat
- one cup of water
- half a dozen tomatoes
- salt
- parsley
- peppers
- one chopped onion
- one clove of garlic
- two egg yolks
- one cup of white wine

## Directions

1. Take a clean lizard and scald it in a little hot vinegar
2. Cut it into pieces and stew in fat, adding one cup of water, half a dozen tomatoes, salt, parsley, peppers, a chopped onion, and a clove of garlic
3. Leave to boil over regular heat for one hour
4. Incorporate the broth with two egg yolks dissolved in a cup of white wine and serve

title: Lagarto Refogado (Stewed Lizard)
body:
## Ingredients

- one lizard, cleaned
- vinegar, hot
- fat
- one cup of water
- half a dozen tomatoes
- salt
- parsley
- peppers
- one onion, sliced
- one garlic clove
- two egg yolks
- one glass of white wine

## Directions

1. Take a cleaned lizard and scald it in a little hot vinegar
2. Cut it into pieces and braise in fat, immediately adding one cup of water, half a dozen tomatoes, salt, parsley, peppers, a sliced onion, and one garlic clove
3. Let it boil over regular heat for one hour
4. Incorporate the broth with two egg yolks dissolved in one glass of white wine and serve

title: Lagosta Assada (Roasted Lobster)
body:
## Ingredients

- portion of lobsters
- water
- salt
- one onion
- parsley
- handful of breadcrumbs
- four egg yolks
- one spoon of butter

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of lobsters in water and salt and let cool
2. When cold, open them, remove the vein from the tail and chop with an onion and parsley
3. Mix with a handful of breadcrumbs, four egg yolks and one spoon of butter
4. Make small cakes with this mixture, fry in butter and serve

title: Lagosta Guisada (Stewed Lobster)
body:
## Ingredients

- portion of lobsters
- water
- salt
- two spoons of butter
- parsley
- one onion
- handful of chopped carapace
- pepper
- clove
- one cup of wine
- one cup of vinegar
- one cup of water

## Directions

1. Take a portion of lobsters and cook in water and salt
2. When cooked, let cool and shell them
3. Braise in two spoons of butter with parsley, one onion and a handful of chopped carapace
4. Add salt, pepper, clove, one cup of wine, one cup of vinegar and one cup of water
5. Let everything boil over moderate heat and serve

title: Lagosta ou Homard Assadano Espeto (Lobster Roasted on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- lobster
- butter
- white wine
- salt
- peppers

## Directions

1. Tie the lobster firmly on the spit
2. Roast over a live fire, basting it with melted butter, white wine, salt and peppers
3. When cooked (known when the hard shell becomes tender), serve with its sauce

title: Lagostas ou Homárds Recheadas (Stuffed Lobsters)
body:
## Ingredients

- lobsters
- salt
- water
- bread crumbs
- milk
- butter
- egg yolks
- sugar
- nutmeg
- wheat flour
- white wine
- cream

## Directions

1. Boil lobsters in salted water
2. Remove the tail and shell
3. Fill the shell with a mixture made from bread crumbs softened in milk, kneaded with butter, egg yolks, sugar, grated nutmeg and wheat flour as needed to form a paste
4. Peel the tails
5. Melt two tablespoons of butter, add one tablespoon of wheat flour, two egg yolks dissolved in two tablespoons of white wine, one cup of cream, a little sugar and grated nutmeg
6. Boil everything, add the tail and stuffed shell with the mixture
7. Boil for another quarter hour and serve

title: Lagostas Recheadas (Stuffed Lobster)
body:
## Ingredients

- portion of lobsters
- water
- salt
- handful of breadcrumbs
- milk
- one spoon of butter
- two egg yolks
- one spoon of sugar
- nutmeg
- wheat flour

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of lobsters in water and salt
2. When cooked, remove the tail and shield
3. Fill the shield with a mixture made from a handful of breadcrumbs softened in milk and kneaded with one spoon of butter, two egg yolks, one spoon of sugar, a little grated nutmeg and wheat flour enough to make a paste
4. Shell the tails afterwards

title: Lambari Braised (Lambaris estufados)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two spoons of butter
- Parsley chopped
- Lambaris
- Salt
- Pepper
- Carapicús
- Potatoes
- One glass of wine

## Directions

1. Put two spoons of butter with chopped parsley in a frying pan
2. Place the fish seasoned with salt and pepper, with chopped carapicús or sliced and peeled potatoes, and one glass of wine
3. Place over coals and let boil until the fish are cooked and the broth is reduced
4. Serve

title: Lambari Fried (Lambaris fritos)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lambaris
- Wheat flour
- Cow butter or fat
- Salt
- Ground pepper
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Coat lambaris in wheat flour
2. Fry in cow butter or fat until well browned
3. Place on a plate
4. Top with parsley, ground pepper, and serve
5. Or if desired, pour the butter or fat in which they were fried on top

title: Lambari Fried with Cheese (Lambaris fritos com queijo)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lambaris
- Salt
- Pepper
- Grated cheese
- Fat

## Directions

1. Fry a portion of lambaris
2. Place on a plate
3. Sprinkle with salt and pepper
4. Cover with a layer of grated cheese
5. Pour fat on top
6. Place the plate in the oven until it takes color
7. Serve

title: Larded Leg of Mutton Cooked with Vegetables (Perna de carneiro lardeada, cozida com legumes)
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- bacon slices
- ham slices
- beans or peas
- parsley
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- orange juice

## Directions

1. Beat the leg after removing fat and trimming the bone
2. Lard it with slices of bacon and ham
3. Scald in hot water and boil for five minutes, then place in cold water
4. Put on the fire with beans or peas, parsley, one onion, salt and pepper
5. Boil until cooked
6. Separate the broth and reduce to two cups of liquid
7. Add the juice of one orange and return the meat to it
8. Let it boil over coals until well browned and begins to stick to the bottom of the vessel
9. Serve with the vegetables

title: Lebre Estufada à Chefe de Cozinha (Braised Hare Chef's Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- loins from two hares
- salt
- garlic
- chopped parsley
- thyme (serpol)
- pepper
- bay leaves
- onions
- basil
- cumin
- lemon juice
- bacon lard
- bacon strips
- half a bottle of brandy (aguardente)
- equal amount of white wine
- two pounds of chopped veal

## Directions

1. Soak the hare loins in salt, garlic, chopped parsley, thyme, pepper, bay leaves, onions, basil, cumin, and lemon juice for twenty-four hours.
2. Remove the loins, dry them, and lard them with bacon strips.
3. Place them in a pan lined with bacon strips with half a bottle of brandy and an equal amount of white wine.
4. Let everything boil for three to four hours over a brisk fire with two pounds of chopped veal.
5. Strain and reduce the sauce, pour it over the hare, and serve cold.

title: Lebre Estufada à Chefe de Cozinha (Braised Hare, Chef's Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- loins from two hares
- salt
- garlic
- chopped parsley
- thyme (serpol)
- pepper
- bay leaves
- onion leaves
- marjoram
- cumin
- lemon juice
- bacon lard strips
- half bottle brandy
- half bottle white wine
- two pounds chopped veal meat

## Directions

1. Remove the loins from two hares
2. Soak them in salt, garlic, chopped parsley, thyme, pepper, bay leaves, onion leaves, marjoram, cumin, and lemon juice
3. After twenty-four hours, remove the loins, dry them, and lard them with bacon
4. Place them in a pan lined with bacon lard strips with half a bottle of brandy and the same amount of white wine
5. Let everything boil for three to four hours over brisk fire with two pounds of chopped veal meat
6. Strain and reduce the sauce, pour it over the loins
7. Serve cold

title: Lebre Guisado com Molho Preto (Hare Stew with Black Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- bacon lard strips
- one cup water
- one cup wine
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- bay leaf
- marjoram
- cumin
- onion leaves
- hare liver
- two hen livers
- hen blood
- orange juice

## Directions

1. Cut the hare into pieces and boil it with bacon lard strips, one cup of water, one cup of wine, salt, pepper, parsley, bay leaf, marjoram, cumin, and onion leaves
2. When cooked, remove it and pass the broth through a sieve
3. Add the hare liver, the blood, and the liver of two hens mixed together in orange juice
4. Heat, stirring continuously
5. Place the hare in the sauce
6. When everything is hot, serve

title: Lebre Lardeada e Assada no Espeto (Larded and Roasted Hare on the Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- salt
- pepper
- hen's blood
- bacon lard
- two spoons melted butter
- half spoon English mustard
- one glass red wine

## Directions

1. Rub the hare with salt, pepper, and hen's blood, exposing it to the flame to dry the blood
2. Lard it with bacon
3. Roast it on the spit
4. Make a sauce with two spoons of melted butter, half spoon of English mustard, and one glass of red wine
5. Use this sauce to baste the hare while roasting
6. Serve

title: Lebre Preparada Instantaneamente (Instantly Prepared Hare)
body:
## Ingredients

- one still-warm hare
- four ounces of bacon cut into pieces
- a bunch of seasoning herbs
- onions
- salt
- abundant pepper
- one and a half bottles of strong red wine

## Directions

1. Cut the still-warm hare into pieces.
2. Combine the blood with the hare, four ounces of bacon cut into pieces, a bunch of seasoning herbs, onions, salt, and abundant pepper in a large vessel, along with one and a half bottles of strong red wine.
3. Place the vessel over a brisk fire so that the fire surrounds it, and with the first boil the wine will catch fire; one can add a little brandy to make this effect quicker.
4. After the wine finishes burning, wrap the pieces in six spoonfuls of butter, then in wheat flour.
5. Place them back in the vessel and let the sauce reduce.
6. In half an hour the hare will be ready.

title: Lebre Preparada Instantaneamente (Instantly Prepared Hare) - Version 2
body:
## Ingredients

- one still-warm hare
- four ounces of bacon
- seasoning herbs
- onions
- salt
- pepper
- one and a half bottles of strong red wine
- six spoonfuls of butter
- wheat flour

## Directions

1. Cut the still-warm hare into pieces and combine with blood in a large vessel.
2. Add four ounces of bacon, a bunch of seasoning herbs, onions, salt, pepper, and one and a half bottles of strong red wine.
3. Place over brisk fire so the flames surround the vessel; at first boil the wine will catch fire (add a little brandy if desired for quicker effect).
4. After the wine burns off, wrap pieces in six spoonfuls of butter, then in wheat flour.
5. Return to vessel and reduce the sauce.
6. Ready in half an hour.

title: Lebre Refogada com Carapicus ou Cogumelos (Hare Sautéed with Carapicus or Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- two spoons fat
- salt
- pepper cloves
- nutmeg
- six tomatoes
- one chopped onion
- one spoon wheat flour
- handful of chosen and washed carapicus
- one cup hot water
- one glass wine
- orange juice

## Directions

1. Cut the hare meat into pieces
2. Sauté in two spoons of fat with salt, pepper cloves, nutmeg, six tomatoes, one chopped onion, one spoon of wheat flour, and a handful of chosen carapicus washed in hot water
3. When everything is impregnated with fat, add one cup of hot water, one glass of wine, and a little orange juice
4. Boil everything for one hour near the fire
5. Serve

title: Leg of Mutton Braised (Perna de carneiro estufada)
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- thick bacon
- salt
- pepper
- chopped parsley
- garlic juice
- beef broth
- small turnip, peeled
- onion
- garlic clove
- bay leaves
- two cloves
- brandy
- ham or beef tongue

## Directions

1. Remove the bone from the leg
2. Lard the cut sides with thick bacon seasoned with salt, pepper, chopped parsley and garlic juice
3. Cook the meat and lard the outside with strips of ham or beef tongue
4. Place in a pan lined with thin bacon slices, with a bowl full of beef broth, one small peeled turnip, one onion, one garlic clove, bay leaves, two cloves, salt, pepper, and a glass of brandy
5. Let cook on moderate heat from below and above
6. When cooked, serve with vegetables, pouring over it the strained and reduced broth

title: Leg of Mutton Braised with Vegetables
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- fresh peas, beans, or squash greens
- salt
- parsley
- clove
- onion leaves
- lean bacon or ham

## Directions

1. Beat the leg well after removing fat and trimming the bone
2. Scald it in hot water, letting it boil for five minutes in this water
3. Remove it and place in cold water, leaving it there until cool
4. Put it back to cook with a portion of fresh peas, beans or squash greens, salt, parsley, one clove, onion leaves of the season, and a piece of lean bacon or ham
5. When all is cooked, separate the broth and evaporate it until reduced to two cups of liquid
6. Return the leg to it, leaving it to cook over coals, turning it until it begins to stick to the bottom of the pan and is well browned
7. Serve with the vegetables

title: Leite de Amendoas ou de Pevides de Melancias (Almond Milk or Watermelon Seed Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound sweet almonds or watermelon seeds
- milk for soaking
- six cups milk
- sugar
- orange flower water

## Directions

1. Take half pound of sweet almonds or watermelon seeds
2. Pound them, moistening them with a little milk
3. Take six cups of milk, place within them the almonds or watermelon seeds
4. Add a little sugar and orange flower water, and pass everything through a fine sieve
5. Bring to boil over a gentle fire and reduce
6. Pass once more through a sieve, pour onto a plate, and let it cool to serve

title: Leite de Amendoas ou de Pevides de Melancias (Almond or Watermelon Seed Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of sweet almonds or watermelon seeds
- six glasses of milk
- a little sugar
- orange blossom water

## Directions

1. Take half a pound of sweet almonds or watermelon seeds; crush them, moistening them with a little milk.
2. Take six glasses of milk, put them with the almonds or watermelon seeds, add a little sugar and orange blossom water, and pass everything through a fine sieve.
3. Bring to a boil over a gentle fire and reduce.
4. Pass through a sieve again, pour into a dish, and let cool before serving.

title: Leite e Ovas de Carpa, Piaba, etc. Ensopados (Milt and Roe of Carp, Piaba, etc. Braised)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish milt and roe
- water
- salt
- vinegar
- wheat flour
- butter
- one cup of white wine
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Place the milt and roe of fish in cold water for twenty-four hours
2. In another vessel, boil a bottle of water with salt and a little vinegar
3. When boiling, place the pieces of milt and roe inside
4. Let them boil for a quarter of an hour, remove them and wrap them in wheat flour
5. Fry them in butter, turning them, and when the flour is browned, add one cup of white wine, salt, pepper, nutmeg
6. Let it boil over coals for a moment and serve

title: Leitão Assado e Recheado à Mineira (Roast Stuffed Suckling Pig, Minas Gerais Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 suckling pig, one month old
- salt
- garlic
- pig's tongue
- pig's offal (liver, heart, etc.)
- pig's blood
- pepper
- parsley
- onion leaves
- marjoram
- cardamom leaves
- cooked pine nuts, shelled
- pieces of sausage
- lard
- parsley sprigs for garnish
- 1 ripe peeled lemon for the mouth

## Directions

1. Kill the piglet by stabbing with a small knife under the left arm, being careful not to make too large an incision.
2. Collect the blood without adding anything to it, allowing it to coagulate.
3. Scald the pig with boiling water and ash, using the back of a knife to remove hairs from the back.
4. Remove the nails and after thorough washing, if any hairs remain, singe them over straw fire.
5. Make an opening in the belly just large enough to disembowel it.
6. Through this opening, remove the tongue (which should be detached from the mouth with a cut underneath) and the offal.
7. Wash again, then rub inside and out with salt and garlic, allowing the flavors to penetrate.
8. Leave in the same vessel for twenty-four hours.
9. Boil the tongue and offal in salted water along with the coagulated blood.
10. When cooked, chop into thin slices and sauté with salt, pepper, garlic, parsley, onion leaves, marjoram, cardamom leaves (all finely chopped), cooked shelled pine nuts, and pieces of sausage.
11. Cook until the juices have reduced.
12. Stuff the pig's belly with this filling through the opening and sew it shut.
13. Make a hole under the front legs, fold the hind legs toward the back and tie them with a cord.
14. Make a sauce of lard, salt, and garlic; rub the pig with it.
15. Place in a roasting pan and put in the oven with moderate heat.
16. When fully cooked and golden brown, remove from the oven and make a cut in the skin of the neck.
17. Place on a serving platter garnished with parsley sprigs and put a ripe peeled lemon in the pig's mouth.
18. Serve hot, though some prefer it cold.

title: Leitão Assado no Espeto à Mineira (Roast Suckling Pig on a Spit, Minas Gerais Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 suckling pig, cleaned and washed
- salt
- garlic
- lard
- sweet oil (if roasting over coals)

## Directions

1. Split the cleaned suckling pig in half lengthwise, with one half retaining the head.
2. Rub both halves with salt and garlic and leave to marinate for twelve hours.
3. Thread each half onto a spit, passing it between the ribs and skin; for the half with the head, the spit point should exit through the mouth.
4. Tie the leg to the spit with a cord and make a small hole in the skin through which to insert your hand.
5. Place in the oven, resting the spit point on a support with the other end against the oven wall.
6. Turn frequently and baste with a sauce made of lard, salt, and garlic until golden brown.
7. Serve each half on a platter lined with watercress leaves, topped with thin slices of peeled lemon.
8. Alternatively, the pig may be roasted over coals with careful attention to moderate the fire, basting with the sauce but using sweet oil instead of lard.

title: Leitão Assado no Espeto à Mineira (Spit-Roasted Suckling Pig Minas Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- suckling pig
- salt
- garlic

## Directions

1. The suckling pig is cleaned and washed as was already explained above.
2. Split it into two halves, with one of them having the head, that is, cut lengthwise, rub it with salt and garlic, leave it in a marinade for twelve hours.
3. Thread each of these halves on a spit, passing the spit between the ribs and the skin.
4. As for the half where the head adheres, the point of the spit comes out through the mouth.
5. Tie the foot to the spit with a cord, and make a small hole in the skin into which the hand is inserted.
6. In this state, it goes to the oven, resting the point of the spit on a support which was proportioned.

title: Leitão Assado à Chefe de Cozinha (Roasted Suckling Pig Chef Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- suckling pig
- chef-style sauce
- sweet olive oil

## Directions

1. Take a suckling pig after it has been prepared and cleaned, singe it in the flame, and garnish with a chef-style sauce, sew its belly to contain the sauce.
2. Thread it lengthwise on a spit and tie at the ends of the spit.
3. One hour and a half before serving, place it on the fire, and from time to time brush it with a brush dipped in sweet olive oil.
4. Moderate the heat so that the suckling pig gets a nice browning on all sides.
5. At the time of serving, remove it from the spit and make a cut in the skin of the neck so that the skin cracks between the teeth, which is what gives charm to the suckling pig.
6. Take it to the table, placing a chef-style sauce in one tureen and a diluted meat substance sauce in another.

title: Leitão em Gelatina (Suckling Pig in Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 suckling pig, partially boiled
- ground meat for stuffing, seasoned
- thin slices of ham
- thin slices of bacon
- fish (tubaras or carapicús)
- hard-boiled eggs
- bacon strips for wrapping
- white cloth
- string
- half meat broth and half white wine
- salt
- broken pepper
- roots
- onions
- parsley
- chives
- garlic
- clove
- thyme
- bay leaf
- marjoram

## Directions

1. Boil the suckling pig briefly, then remove all bones and spread it on cloth.
2. Place a layer of seasoned ground meat on top.
3. Layer thin slices of ham over the meat, then a layer of thin bacon slices.
4. Add a layer of fish (tubaras or carapicús), then a layer of hard-boiled eggs.
5. Top with another layer of seasoned ground meat.
6. Wrap the pig (being careful not to disturb the layers) with bacon strips and cover with white cloth tied with string.
7. Cook in equal parts meat broth and white wine for three hours.
8. Add salt, broken pepper, roots, onions, parsley, chives, garlic, clove, thyme, bay leaf, and marjoram.
9. Allow to cool in the cooking vessel and serve cold.

title: Leitão Ensopado à Mineira (Braised Suckling Pig, Minas Gerais Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cleaned suckling pig, cut into pieces
- lard
- salt
- garlic
- Comarí pepper
- chopped parsley
- hot water
- spiced sauce on the side

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned pig into pieces and place in a vessel already containing hot lard.
2. Add salt, garlic, Comarí pepper, and chopped parsley and reduce the heat.
3. After cooking for half an hour, add some hot water and continue cooking.
4. When fully cooked and little liquid remains, remove from heat.
5. Serve with a spiced sauce on the side.

title: Lentilhas Ensopadas (Stewed Lentils)
body:
## Ingredients

- a portion of lentils
- water and salt
- two spoons of butter
- a little salt
- one chopped onion
- one cup of vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of lentils in water and salt
2. When cooked, fry two spoons of butter, a little salt, one chopped onion
3. Add the lentils with broth and one cup of vinegar
4. Let them boil a little more and serve

title: Lingoada Assada na Grelha (Grilled Flounder)
body:
## Ingredients

- one flounder
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- butter
- wheat flour
- white wine
- capers

## Directions

1. Clean the fish and dry to remove moisture
2. Anoint with butter, salt, pepper and nutmeg
3. Roast on the grill over moderate fire
4. Serve with a white sauce made of melted butter, flour, white wine and capers

title: Lingoada Cozida (Boiled Flounder)
body:
## Ingredients

- one flounder
- sea water
- parsley
- butter
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Clean the fish and boil in sea water
2. When cooked, remove and dry
3. Place on a plate and sprinkle with chopped parsley
4. Pour melted butter on top
5. Serve with potatoes boiled in the same water

title: Lingoada Ensopada (Flounder Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- one flounder
- butter
- wheat flour
- a handful of carapicús
- white wine
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Remove the skin and spine of the fish and cut into strips
2. Sauté in butter and remove and drain well
3. Add one spoon of wheat flour to the butter and stir
4. Add a handful of carapicús cooked in white wine
5. Season with salt, pepper and nutmeg
6. Let it boil, add the fish and boil for another quarter of an hour
7. Serve

title: Lingoada Estufada (Braised Flounder)
body:
## Ingredients

- two spoons butter
- parsley
- onion
- carapicús
- one or two roasted fish
- more parsley
- carapicús
- butter

## Directions

1. In a heat-resistant plate, place two spoons of butter and a thick layer of chopped parsley and onion mixed with chopped carapicús
2. Place one or two roasted fish on top
3. Cover with another portion of herbs and carapicús and another piece of butter
4. Cover the plate with another plate filled with coals
5. Place over fire, cook and serve in the same plate while hot

title: Lingoada Estufada com Vinho (Braised Ling with Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons of butter
- one or two cleaned fish sprinkled with salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- one cup of white wine

## Directions

1. Put two tablespoons of butter in a dish that can resist the action of fire and one or two cleaned fish sprinkled with salt, pepper and grated nutmeg
2. Add one cup of white wine, and after covering, boil it between two fires and serve it very hot

title: Lingoada Frita (Fried Flounder)
body:
## Ingredients

- one flounder
- three eggs
- one spoon wheat flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean the fish perfectly
2. Mix three beaten eggs with one spoon of wheat flour to make a thick paste
3. Place a portion of butter in a frying pan and heat well
4. Coat the fish in the egg and flour paste and fry over high heat
5. Serve

title: Lingoada Frita de Escabeche (Fried Marinated Ling)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- sweet oil
- salt
- peppers
- grated nutmeg
- melted butter
- breadcrumbs
- beaten eggs
- tomatoes
- wine

## Directions

1. Remove the skin of the fish, split it down the middle, also remove the bones, and soak it in sweet oil with a little salt, peppers and grated nutmeg for four hours, turning it from time to time
2. Remove and dry it with absorbent paper, and coat it with melted butter wrapping it then in breadcrumbs, beaten eggs and breadcrumbs again
3. Fry it in butter or roast it on the grill, and serve with a sauce made of tomatoes sautéed in butter with salt and peppers
4. Add one or two cups of wine, boil it a little and serve

title: Lingoada Frita Encapotada (Fried Breaded Ling)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish meat
- salt
- pepper
- two eggs
- one tablespoon of wheat flour
- one tablespoon of melted butter
- butter for frying
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut the fish meat into strips two fingers in width, dry them with a towel, and sprinkle with a little salt and pepper
2. Beat two eggs with one tablespoon of wheat flour and one tablespoon of melted butter
3. Pass the fish in this batter, and then fry it in butter
4. Place on a plate, squeeze lemon juice over it and serve

title: Lingoada ou Azevia Estufada (Braised Ling or Pollock)
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons of butter
- a thick layer of chopped parsley and onion mixed with chopped carapicús (small fish)
- one or two roasted fish
- more herbs and carapicús
- another piece of butter

## Directions

1. In a dish that can withstand the action of fire, put two tablespoons of butter and a thick layer of parsley and onion chopped and mixed with chopped carapicús
2. Place one or two roasted fish on top and cover with another portion of herbs and carapicús, and another piece of butter
3. Cover the dish with another filled with hot coals, and placed over the fire, cook it, and serve it in the same dish still hot

title: Lingoada Recheada (Stuffed Ling)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- milk
- wheat flour
- butter
- chopped parsley
- lemon juice
- tomatoes

## Directions

1. Remove the skin of the fish only from the dark side; after cutting the head, insert a knife to loosen the spine
2. Leave the fish prepared soaked in milk; remove and let drain
3. Dust with wheat flour and fry in butter
4. Through the hole where the head was missing, remove the dorsal spine that is now loosened, and fill the cavity with butter kneaded with chopped parsley, wheat flour and lemon juice
5. Put everything in a dish, bake in the oven, and serve with a tomato sauce

title: Lingoada Refogada (Sautéed Flounder)
body:
## Ingredients

- one flounder
- two spoons butter
- parsley
- onions
- salt
- peppers
- nutmeg
- two cups white wine

## Directions

1. Clean the fish and place in a casserole with two spoons of butter
2. Add chopped parsley, cut onions, salt, peppers and nutmeg
3. Turn the fish and add two cups of white wine
4. Let it boil over coals and serve when cooked

title: Lingua de Carneiro Dourada (Gilded Lamb Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- one lamb tongue
- salt
- cardamom powder
- two cups of milk
- four egg yolks
- two spoonfuls of wheat flour
- half a spoonful of sugar
- butter

## Directions

1. Peel and cook one tongue, then cut it into slices
2. Season these slices with a little salt and cardamom powder and wrap them in a thin batter made of two cups of milk, four egg yolks, two spoonfuls of wheat flour and half a spoonful of sugar
3. Thus prepared, fry the slices on both sides in butter, place them in a casserole and pour the rest of the egg batter on top, letting it fry

title: Lingua de Carneiro Ensopada com Tomates (Lamb Tongue Stewed with Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb tongues
- water
- slices of bacon
- salt
- marjoram
- parsley
- onion leaves
- one dozen tomatoes
- cooked rice

## Directions

1. With the tongues peeled, cut them into slices and let them boil with water and some slices of bacon, salt, marjoram, parsley and onion leaves
2. When cooked, remove them from the broth and let this reduce; leaving only two cupfuls, add one dozen tomatoes
3. Give it a boil, mash the tomatoes and put the tongues back in the vessel
4. After taking one boil, serve with cooked rice

title: Lingua de Carneiro Guisada (Braised Lamb Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb tongue
- thin strips of bacon
- meat broth
- salt
- pepper
- year-round onion leaves
- parsley
- tapioca starch
- one spoonful of vinegar
- one handful of ora-pro-nobis
- cornmeal mush from Minas

## Directions

1. Lard the tongue after boiling and removing the skin with thin strips of bacon and cook it in meat broth with salt, pepper, year-round onion leaves and parsley
2. When cooked, strain the broth through a sieve; thicken it with a little tapioca starch, add one spoonful of vinegar and one handful of ora-pro-nobis
3. Put the tongue back into this sauce and let it boil another half hour over coals and serve with cornmeal mush from Minas

title: Lingua de Porco Ensacada (Encased Pig's Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pig's tongue
- salt
- wheat flour
- aromatic herbs
- 1 beef intestine casing

## Directions

1. Boil the pig's tongue over high heat, then remove the skin covering it.
2. Place the tongue in a vessel with abundant salt, wheat flour, and aromatic herbs so it is well covered with this mixture.
3. After leaving it in this pickle for three to four days, place the tongue in a beef intestine casing, ensuring there are no air pockets, and tie both ends of the casing securely.
4. Hang in smoke and leave for one month or more.
5. Cook in meat broth and serve cold.

title: Lingua de vacca assada em camadas (Beef Tongue Roasted in Layers)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue, cooked or roasted
- broth with vinegar
- pickled peppers
- parsley
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- bread crumb
- white wine

## Directions

1. Take a tongue already cooked or roasted and cut into very thin slices
2. Place at the bottom of the serving dish a little broth with vinegar, minced pickled peppers, parsley, onion (all finely chopped), salt, pepper and bread crumb
3. Place a layer of tongue slices on top, over this another layer of bread crumb, and so on until finished
4. Place the dish in the oven, not very hot, and let it cook until the bottom layer begins to roast
5. Serve after moistening with a few spoonfuls of broth and white wine

title: Lingua de Vacca Assada Guarnecida (Roasted Beef Tongue with Garnish)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- six spoonfuls of broth
- bacon slices
- bouquet of aromatic herbs
- one or two onions
- cloves

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook the tongue with six spoonfuls of broth, bacon slices, a bouquet of aromatic herbs, one or two onions, and cloves
2. When the broth is reduced to a quarter of its original amount, let it cool
3. Fill the tongue with bacon strips
4. Thread it onto a spit and roast for one hour
5. Serve with a spicy sauce

title: Lingua de vacca assada guarnecida (Roasted Garnished Beef Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- broth
- bacon strips
- aromatic herbs
- onions
- Indian cloves
- bacon
- spicy sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook the tongue with six spoonfuls of broth, bacon strips, an herb sauce, one or two onions and Indian cloves
2. When the broth is reduced to a quarter, let it cool
3. Fill the tongue with bacon strips, thread it on the spit and let it roast for an hour
4. Serve with a spicy sauce

title: Lingua de vacca com cogumelos (Beef Tongue with Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- mushroom sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare as in the previous recipe, placing on top a mushroom sauce

title: Lingua de vacca com môlho picante (Beef Tongue with Spicy Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- carrots
- onions
- celery
- salt
- spicy sauce

## Directions

1. Boil the tongue for four to five hours with some carrots, onions, celery and salt
2. Remove the skin and split it down the middle
3. Serve placed in one of the indicated spicy sauces in which it should be heated

title: Lingua de vacca ensopada branca (White Beef Tongue Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- wheat flour
- butter
- water
- vinegar
- sugar
- raisins
- almonds
- lemon slices
- beef tongue, cooked

## Directions

1. Knead two spoonfuls of wheat flour with a spoonful of beef butter, dissolved in water and vinegar
2. Add a little sugar, scalded raisins, peeled almonds and some slices of lemon and the slices of cooked tongue
3. Let all boil for half an hour and serve

title: Lingua de vacca ensopada parda (Brown Beef Tongue Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- wheat flour
- water
- vinegar
- sugar
- raisins
- almonds
- beef tongue, cooked

## Directions

1. Toast a spoonful of wheat flour until well browned
2. Dissolve it in a cup of water and a spoonful of vinegar
3. Add a little sugar, already scalded raisins and peeled almonds
4. Let it boil for half an hour with the tongue, already cooked and cut into slices
5. Serve thus

title: Lingua de vacca escarlate (Scarlet Beef Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- pepper
- saltpeter
- salt

## Directions

1. Take a tongue and after cleaning it, roast it superficially over a very lively fire so that the skin can be removed
2. Rub it with a little pepper, saltpeter and salt ground
3. Place it in a pan covered with salt under a board with a weight on top
4. After fifteen days it is removed and hung in the air
5. To cook it, place it in hot water for two hours, then put it in a pan full of water with some onions, Indian cloves and bay leaf, but without adding salt or pepper
6. Let it cook for six to seven hours over moderate fire and then remove it
7. Let it drain well to go to the table after it cools

title: Lingua de vacca à caseira (Homestyle Beef Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- bacon strips
- broken Indian pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- Indian cloves
- chopped chives
- veal
- onions
- thyme
- bay leaf
- meat broth
- butter
- wheat flour
- pickled cucumbers
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Take a beef tongue, blanch it, and after leaving it for some time in cold water, remove the excess fats and superfluous flesh
2. Fill it with bacon strips seasoned with broken Indian pepper, salt, nutmeg, Indian cloves and finely chopped chives
3. Cook it with bacon and a piece of veal
4. Then add onions, thyme, bay leaf and Indian cloves
5. Moisten with meat broth and let it cook over a very gentle fire for five hours
6. When serving, remove the skin, cut the tongue in two parts, and place on the serving dish
7. Toast some minced onions in butter, mix with wheat flour dissolved in the tongue broth, add a little vinegar and broken Indian pepper
8. When this sauce is reduced to half, pour it over the tongue on which pickled cucumbers have been previously placed

title: Lingua de vacca à hespanhola (Spanish Beef Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue, cooked
- lemon peel
- cinnamon
- sugar
- wine
- broth

## Directions

1. The tongue, after being well cooked, is opened down the middle without dividing entirely and placed in a pan containing lemon peel, cinnamon, sugar, half a bottle of wine and the same amount of broth
2. Let it boil for half an hour over coals and serve

title: Lingua Guisada (Stewed Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue, already boiled and cleaned
- one cup of broth
- half bottle of wine
- salt
- pepper
- parsley, chopped
- two egg yolks
- broth
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Take an already boiled and cleaned tongue and cut it into thin slices
2. Boil for one hour over moderate heat in one cup of broth and half a bottle of wine with little salt and pepper
3. Place the pieces on a serving platter
4. Add to the sauce a little chopped parsley and two egg yolks diluted in a little broth and vinegar
5. Let it boil once and pour the sauce over the slices before serving

title: Lingua guisada (Stewed Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue, boiled
- broth
- wine
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take a tongue already boiled, cleaned as above and cut into thin slices
2. Boil it for an hour over moderate fire in a cup of broth and half a bottle of wine, little salt and pepper

title: Lingua picada com môlho (Chopped Beef Tongue with Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- cold water
- hot water
- bacon strips
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- parsley
- chives
- wheat flour
- butter
- onions
- mushrooms
- sugar
- vinegar
- capers

## Directions

1. Place the tongue in cold water for twenty-four hours, changing the water several times
2. Scald it in hot water, scraping it to remove the hard skin
3. Cook it for five hours with small pieces of bacon, pepper, salt, nutmeg, parsley and chopped chives
4. Remove from the fire and split it without dividing into pieces
5. Place on a serving dish
6. Strain the broth and thicken it with wheat flour, fry it in butter
7. Add onions, mushrooms, pepper and after boiling for five minutes, serve with the tongue
8. Or you may add to this sauce a little sugar and vinegar, or serve the tongue with a capers sauce or another thickened sauce

title: Linguas de Carneiro com Abóbora d'Agua (Lamb Tongues with Water Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb tongues
- water
- salt
- water squash
- lard
- two spoonfuls of fine cornmeal
- one bowlful of water or meat broth
- parsley
- celery root
- one onion

## Directions

1. Cook the tongues in water and salt and peel them
2. Cut them into cubes and fry them in melted lard with two spoonfuls of fine cornmeal until the cornmeal takes on a copper color
3. Add a portion of water squash cut equally into cubes
4. Mix and as it takes on the heat of the fire, add one bowlful of water or meat broth, salt, parsley, celery root, and one cut onion
5. Let it boil for one hour over moderate fire and serve

title: Linguas de Carneiro Douradas e Empasteladas (Gilded and Pastry-Enclosed Lamb Tongues)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb tongues
- one dozen sardines in barrels or fresh
- brandy
- six egg yolks
- two egg whites
- one cup of milk
- two spoonfuls of wheat flour or fine cornmeal
- one spoonful of sugar
- nutmeg
- butter
- lemon
- salad

## Directions

1. Boil the tongues until cooked, peel them and split them into four strips lengthwise
2. Take one dozen sardines which are placed on a plate, covered with brandy, and the plate placed over a vessel containing boiling water to heat it; add brandy and let it burn until it extinguishes on its own
3. Mix six egg yolks and two egg whites well beaten, one cup of milk, two spoonfuls of wheat flour or fine cornmeal, one spoonful of sugar and a little nutmeg
4. Put a little butter in the bottom of a casserole and fry it, pouring into it half of the mixture just prepared and let it fry
5. Place on this fried mixture, after removing the casserole from the fire, the strips of tongues and the sardines broken into pieces with some slices of peeled lemon without seeds, all in alternating order
6. Pour the rest of the mixture on top, cover the casserole and place it to roast in an oven or over coals, also covering the lid with coals
7. Serve with some salad

title: Linguas de Porco Fumadas e Ensacadas (Smoked and Encased Pig's Tongues)
body:
## Ingredients

- several pig's tongues
- salt
- saltpetre
- marjoram
- bay leaf
- thyme
- juniper berries
- beef intestine casings
- weight for pressing
- water
- wine
- parsley
- onions
- bay leaf
- marjoram

## Directions

1. Scald several pig's tongues to remove their skins.
2. Layer them on top of each other, rubbing each with salt, saltpetre, marjoram, bay leaf, thyme, and juniper berries.
3. Place a weight on top to press them and cover the vessel to exclude air.
4. Leave in a cool place for eight days.
5. Remove the tongues from the brine and allow them to drain.
6. Introduce them into casings, tying both ends securely.
7. Place in smoke.
8. When ready to serve, cook in water, wine, parsley, onions, bay leaf, and marjoram.
9. Allow to cool and serve.

title: Linguiças de Salchichas (Veal Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight pounds veal meat
- four pounds pork fat
- four pounds pork meat
- salt
- saltpeter
- pepper
- cloves
- marjoram
- garlic
- casing

## Directions

1. Cut all meat finely and pass through a sieve
2. Season with salt, a little saltpeter, pepper, cloves, marjoram, and crushed garlic
3. Leave in the vessel until the next day
4. Fill the casings and hang in smoke
5. Serve raw or fried in butter

title: Linguiças à Brazileira (Brazilian Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat from the leg and shoulder, cut into small pieces
- salt
- garlic
- India pepper
- Comarí peppers
- parsley
- chives
- marjoram
- bay leaf
- marjoram (segorelha)
- 1 bottle of wine or aguardente
- fine intestine casings
- orange
- corn meal (fubá)
- cold water

## Directions

1. Cut meat from the leg and shoulder into small pieces and season with salt, garlic, India pepper, Comarí peppers, parsley, chives, marjoram, bay leaf, and marjoram.
2. Leave to marinate in this mixture for twenty-four hours.
3. Prepare fine intestine casings by washing with orange and corn meal, then soaking in cold salted water for twenty-four hours.
4. When filling, drain the casings, spread them on cloth to dry, and fill them with the marinated meat pieces.
5. Pack the meat as tightly as possible, pricking the casing occasionally with a pin to release trapped air between the meat layers.
6. Once the casing is full, tie the other end.
7. Place the prepared sausages in smoke for three days.
8. After this time, store in a dry place.
9. Serve fried, grilled, or cooked with rice; they also enter into the composition of various stuffings.

title: Linguiças à Maneira de Sonhos (Pork Sausages Dream-Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork meat
- Fresh pork fat without skin
- Pepper
- Salt
- Thyme
- Parsley
- Bay leaf
- Cloves
- Pork caul

## Directions

1. Take equal amounts of pork meat and fresh pork fat without skin
2. Chop everything as finely as possible
3. Season with pepper, salt, thyme, parsley, bay leaf, and cloves
4. Take small pieces of this mixture and cover them with pieces of pork caul, giving them an elongated and flat shape
5. Roast them over a low fire on the grill and when cooked, serve with cooked cabbage, pigeon peas, and mangalós
6. Alternatively, they can be fried and served on a plate, pouring over them a sauce made with the fat in which they were fried with a little white wine added, letting it boil until reduced to half

title: Livro de Vitella com Macarrão (Calf's Stomach with Pasta)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf stomach
- water
- salt
- portion of pasta
- grated cheese
- melted butter
- sugar
- ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Clean the stomach well and cut it into pieces one palm in length
2. Place it to cook in water and salt with a portion of pasta
3. When the stomach and pasta are cooked, drain them
4. Put them in a serving dish and cover with a layer of grated cheese
5. Pour melted butter over them
6. Sprinkle with sugar and ground cinnamon
7. Place in the oven to roast for one hour
8. Serve the calf's stomach this way

title: Livro de Vitella com Quiabos (Calf's Stomach with Okra)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf stomach
- water
- salt
- parsley
- onion
- one dozen okras
- two egg yolks
- one tablespoon starch
- one tablespoon vinegar
- one cup cold water

## Directions

1. Clean the stomach well and cut it into small pieces
2. Boil in water, salt, parsley and onion
3. When almost cooked, add one dozen okras cut in slices
4. Let it finish cooking, and before removing from the fire add two egg yolks mixed with one tablespoon of starch, one of vinegar and one cup of cold water
5. Serve with cornmeal mush

title: Livro de Vitella Frito (Fried Calf's Stomach)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf stomach
- water
- salt
- some maniocs
- one onion
- fat

## Directions

1. Cook the stomach in water and salt with some maniocs
2. Remove from the pot and drain it
3. Chop it into small pieces and cut the maniocs into slices
4. Add them with a cut onion to a frying pan containing hot fat
5. Stir and serve

title: Livro ou Bucho de Vitella (Calf's Stomach)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf stomach
- calf hands and feet
- orange
- water
- salt
- onion
- pepper
- two tablespoons wheat flour
- same amount of butter
- one tablespoon vinegar
- lemon juice
- yolks of three eggs

## Directions

1. Take the stomach, hands and feet of the calf
2. After washing well, scald in hot water, rub with orange to make them white, and cook in water with salt, onion and pepper
3. When cooked, remove the bones from the hands and feet, cut them into pieces, as well as the tripe
4. Pour two tablespoons of wheat flour, the same amount of butter, one tablespoon of vinegar, lemon juice and the yolks of three beaten eggs into the broth, boiling for a little while
5. Return the feet and tripe and after boiling for some more time, serve

title: Lombinho Assado no Espeto (Roasted Loin Chops on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- loin chops
- sweet oil
- pepper
- salt
- parsley
- bay leaf
- onions
- butter
- meat juice
- vinegar
- chives
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Trim and cut the loin chops, but not too thin so they resist the fire
2. Place these slices in a marinade of sweet oil, pepper, salt, parsley, bay leaf and onions for twelve hours
3. Thread them onto a spit, covering the skin part with paper oiled with butter
4. Roast over a brisk fire so the heat penetrates them
5. Remove the paper several moments before serving
6. Remove them from the spit while still rare
7. Serve with a sauce made of meat juice, vinegar, chives, salt and pepper, or a tomato sauce

title: Lombinho com Codeas de Pão (Loin Chops with Bread Crusts)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roasted loin chop
- bread crusts
- butter
- parsley
- capers
- pickles

## Directions

1. Cut into slices the leftovers of roasted loin chop that remained
2. Heat within the juice without boiling
3. Make bread crusts of the same size
4. Fry them in butter until they take color
5. Place on a plate layers of crusts and loin alternately
6. Pour over them the loin juice or melted butter with parsley and some capers or pickles

title: Lombinho com Codeas de Pão (Loin with Bread Slices)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roasted loin
- bread
- butter
- loin juice or melted butter
- parsley
- capers or pickled cucumbers in vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the leftover roasted loin into slices and heat them in the juice without boiling.
2. Cut bread into slices of the same size and fry them in butter until they brown.
3. Layer the bread slices and loin slices on a plate, one on top of the other.
4. Pour over them the loin juice or melted butter with parsley and some capers, or pickled cucumbers in vinegar.

title: Lombinho de Espeto (Spit-Roasted Pork Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- Three pork loins
- Wine with garlic
- Meat substance
- Pepper sauce

## Directions

1. After preparing and larding three loins, place them in wine with garlic for twenty-four hours
2. After this time, drain them and stuff them with meat substance
3. Serve with a pepper sauce

title: Lombinho de Vacca com Macarrão (Beef Loin with Pasta)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef loin
- butter
- beef broth
- pasta
- ham
- mushrooms
- beef broth
- white wine

## Directions

1. Cut the loin into slices the thickness of a finger.
2. Fry them in butter, removing them before they are fully cooked.
3. Finish cooking them in a few spoonfuls of beef broth.
4. When sufficiently cooked, remove and place on a platter, leaving a space in the middle for the pasta.
5. Cook the pasta in broth for the necessary time.
6. Soak mushrooms in a mixture of one cup of beef broth and another cup of white wine for a quarter of an hour.
7. Mix the cooked pasta with half a quarter of ham and the same amount of mushrooms cut into small pieces, along with their soaking liquid.
8. Fill the space on the platter with the pasta mixture.

title: Lombinho de Veado Refogado (Braised Venison Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- two venison loins
- salt pork
- vinegar
- salt
- onion
- parsley leaves
- bay leaves
- twelve tomatoes
- venison meat pieces
- half a cup of water
- half a bottle of white wine
- two spoonfuls of butter
- one spoonful of wheat flour
- carapicús

## Directions

1. Lard the two venison loins with salt pork and soak them for forty-eight hours in vinegar, salt, onion, parsley and bay leaves
2. Put them in a pan between lard pieces with twelve tomatoes and some venison meat pieces, half a cup of water and half a bottle of white wine
3. Let simmer for two to three hours and remove the meat
4. In another place, melt two spoonfuls of butter with one spoonful of wheat flour, adding the strained meat broth and some boiled carapicús
5. Let simmer until the broth is reduced to three or four cups
6. Pour this gravy over the loins and serve

title: Lombinhos de Porco Fresco (Fresh Pork Loins)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork loins
- Pork fat strips
- Veal meat slices
- Carrots
- Onions
- Cloves
- Parsley
- Onion leaves
- Bay leaf
- Butter
- Meat broth

## Directions

1. Remove the loins in their full length and prepare them, larding them with fat
2. They may be left long or given a round shape
3. Place strips of pork fat in a casserole with some slices of veal meat, two carrots, onions, two cloves, parsley, onion leaves, and bay leaf
4. Place the loins on top and cover with a sheet of paper spread with butter
5. Add half a bottle of meat broth
6. Place the casserole over the fire and set fire on top
7. When ready to plate, drain them and place meat substance on top
8. Serve with chicory, sliced cucumbers, cassava fries, or with a tomato sauce or hot sauce

title: Lombo de Carneiro Assado em Papel (Lamb Loin Roasted in Paper)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb loin
- salt
- parsley
- pepper
- garlic
- onion
- butter
- white wine
- water
- one egg yolk
- one dozen okras

## Directions

1. Take the loin, season it with salt, parsley, pepper, garlic and onion, all pounded together to form a paste
2. Wrap it in paper greased with butter and roast it on a spit over coals, removing the paper a short time before serving so it takes on color
3. Serve with a sauce made from the broth that dripped in the vessel placed under the spit, to which are added one glass of white wine, another of water, one egg yolk and one dozen okras
4. Bring to the fire and after taking two boils, serve with the roast

title: Lombo de Carneiro Assado no Espeto (Lamb Loin Roasted on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb loin
- fine strips of bacon
- fine pepper
- salt
- garlic
- butter
- white wine
- one onion
- parsley

## Directions

1. Lard the loin with fine strips of bacon wrapped in fine pepper, salt and pounded garlic
2. Place it on a spit, pouring on it from time to time a spoonful of the sauce that dripped under the spit in a casserole in which were placed one spoonful of butter, two glasses of white wine, one onion and a little chopped parsley

title: Lombo de Carneiro Assado no Forno (Lamb Loin Roasted in the Oven)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb loin
- bacon strips
- salt
- ground Indian pepper
- garlic juice
- lemon juice
- one onion
- parsley
- breadcrumbs
- green beans or roasted potatoes

## Directions

1. Lard the loin with bacon in thin strips, sprinkle a little salt, ground Indian pepper over it, a little garlic juice and lemon juice, one chopped onion and parsley
2. Leave for two hours with these seasonings to take on the flavor
3. Then wrap it in grated bread and place it in a casserole which is placed in an oven or over coals and let it roast for two hours
4. Serve with green beans or roasted potatoes

title: Lombo de Lingoada Frito (Fried Ling Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- ling loins
- parsley
- onions
- two tablespoons of melted butter

## Directions

1. Remove the head and tail, remove the dark skin, and take the meat from one side, doing the same from the opposite side
2. If the fish is large, cut the meat into two or three strips
3. After cleaning and drying with a towel, place them in a casserole with chopped parsley and onions
4. Cover completely the fish pieces with this mixture
5. Add two tablespoons of melted butter and having fried a little more, serve hot with this sauce

title: Lombo de Pescada Ensopado (Stewed Hake Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- hake loins
- salt
- pepper
- melted butter
- breadcrumbs
- beaten eggs
- white wine
- broth

## Directions

1. Remove the loins from a good fish, sprinkle them on all sides with salt and a little pepper
2. Pass them in melted butter, and cover with a layer of breadcrumbs
3. Pass them in beaten eggs and wrap again in another layer of breadcrumbs
4. Roast them on the grill over a very low fire, and when well browned, put them on a plate
5. On the other hand, melt two tablespoons of butter, mix with half a cup of white wine and one cup of broth
6. Boil, pour very hot over the fish, and serve

title: Lombo de Pescada Estufado (Braised Hake Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- hake loins
- cow butter
- dozen tubaras (small fish)
- white wine

## Directions

1. Take the loins of fish and cut them into pieces
2. Sauté them in cow butter and remove them
3. Sauté a dozen tubaras in the same fat, add one cup of white wine and let it boil
4. When cooked, add the hake loins

title: Lombo de Pescada Estufado (Braised Hake Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- Hake loins
- Cow butter
- A dozen tubaras (small fish)
- One teacup white wine

## Directions

1. Take the hake loins and cut them into pieces
2. Sauté them in cow butter and remove
3. Sauté a dozen tubaras in the same fat
4. Add one teacup of white wine and let it boil
5. When cooked, add the pieces of fish
6. Serve after boiling once more

title: Lombo de Pescada Frito (Fried Hake Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- Hake fish
- Salt
- Pepper
- Chopped parsley
- Butter

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the fish
2. Take out the loins
3. Season with salt, pepper and chopped parsley
4. Fry in butter
5. Serve when cooked

title: Lombo de Porco Assado (Roasted Pork Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh pork loin
- Sweet olive oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Onions
- Cloves
- Bay leaf
- Hot pepper sauce
- Butter
- Onions for stew
- Wheat flour
- Meat broth
- White wine

## Directions

1. Marinate a fresh pork loin in sweet olive oil for three days with salt, pepper, parsley, onions, cloves, and bay leaf
2. Skewer it and when ready, serve with a hot pepper sauce
3. Alternatively, roast it in a casserole with a little butter, adding an onion
4. Serve with an onion stew made as follows: cut onions into slices, pass them over the fire with a piece of butter until almost cooked, add a pinch of wheat flour and stir until they take on a good color, moisten with meat broth, white wine, salt and Indian pepper, letting it boil until the onions are cooked and no sauce remains

title: Lombo de Porco Assado no Forno (Pork Loin Roasted in the Oven)
body:
## Ingredients

- one loin
- strips of bacon and pieces of garlic for larding
- salt
- pounded camarís peppers
- sour orange juice
- tomatoes
- potatoes, peeled

## Directions

1. Take one loin, lard it with strips of bacon and pieces of garlic.
2. Marinate in salt, pounded camarís peppers, sour orange juice and tomatoes for six hours.
3. Place it in a pan with some peeled potatoes and let it roast in the oven.
4. When roasted, serve with the potatoes arranged around the loin.

title: Lombo de Porco do Matto Assado com Pinhões (Wild Pork Loin Roasted with Pine Nuts)
body:
## Ingredients

- one loin
- salt
- green peppers
- lemon juice
- garlic
- chopped parsley
- onions
- bacon slices
- peeled pine nuts

## Directions

1. Take one loin, season it with salt, green peppers, lemon juice, garlic, finely chopped parsley and onions.
2. Wrap it in slices of bacon and place it in a pan with a portion of peeled pine nuts.
3. Put it in the oven or over coals, also covering the lid with coals.
4. When roasted, serve with the pine nuts in the same plate.

title: Lombo de Porco do Matto Assado no Espeto (Wild Pork Loin Roasted on the Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- one loin
- salt
- sour orange juice
- tutú de feijão
- salads

## Directions

1. Take one loin, rub it with salt and sour orange juice.
2. Thread it on the spit and roast it.
3. In the first fifteen minutes it should be over a flame, and afterwards over a bed of coals, without moistening it.
4. When roasted, serve with tutú de feijão and salads.

title: Lombo de Porco do Matto Estufado com Beringelas (Braised Wild Boar Loin with Eggplants)
body:
## Ingredients

- one wild boar loin
- parsley
- onions
- bay leaves
- one pound of chopped bacon
- one bottle of red wine
- orange wine (if good red wine is unavailable)
- one dozen eggplants

## Directions

1. Soak the loin in parsley, onions, and bay leaves for six hours
2. Place in a pot with the chopped bacon and red wine (or orange wine if red wine is unavailable)
3. Add the eggplants
4. Cover the pot with the lid
5. Braise over a moderate fire until everything is cooked
6. Serve with the sauce degreased and incorporated with two egg yolks diluted in a little lemon juice

title: Lombo de Porco do Matto Estufado com Beringelas (Wild Pork Loin Stewed with Eggplants)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pork loin
- parsley
- onions
- bay leaves
- one pound chopped bacon
- one bottle red wine
- orange wine
- one dozen eggplants

## Directions

1. Let one pork loin marinate for six hours in parsley, onions, bay leaves.
2. Place it in a pan with one pound of chopped bacon, one bottle of red wine, or if not good quality, orange wine, and one dozen eggplants.
3. Cover the pan and boil over moderate heat.

title: Lombo de Porco do Matto Estufado com Carapicús (Wild Pork Loin Stewed with Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- one loin
- salt
- lemon juice
- bacon for larding wrapped in salt and pepper
- one cup water
- one cup white wine
- carapicús (mushrooms)
- six tomatoes
- one chopped onion
- marjoram
- parsley

## Directions

1. Rub one loin with salt and lemon juice, and lard it with bacon wrapped in salt and pepper.
2. Place it in a pan on a slice of bacon with one cup of water, one cup of white wine, a portion of carapicús, six tomatoes, one chopped onion, marjoram and parsley.
3. Cover the pan with the lid and cook in the oven or over moderate heat.
4. In two to three hours it will be cooked, and then serve it over the carapicús with its sauce.

title: Lombo de Porco do Matto Guisado com Carás (Wild Boar Loin Stewed with Yams)
body:
## Ingredients

- one wild boar loin
- one yam
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- pepper
- lemon juice from two lemons
- half a quart of butter or fat
- corn flour
- carapicú (a type of fish) chopped

## Directions

1. Boil the loin with the yam cut into pieces, salt, parsley, onion leaves, pepper, and lemon juice from two lemons
2. When cooked, remove and cut into pieces, as well as the yam
3. Fry afterwards in half a quart of butter or fat
4. After frying, dust with corn flour and pour the strained broth over it
5. Add chopped carapicú to the broth
6. Let everything boil until the broth is well reduced
7. Serve

title: Lombo de Porco do Matto Guisado com Chuchús (Wild Boar Loin Stewed with Chayotes)
body:
## Ingredients

- one wild boar loin
- water
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onion leaves
- one chalice of white wine
- one handful of bread crumb
- two tablespoons of cow butter
- chayotes
- one egg yolk
- one cup of milk cream

## Directions

1. Cut the loin into pieces and boil in water with salt, pepper, parsley, onion leaves, white wine, and bread crumb
2. When cooked, remove and fry in two tablespoons of cow butter
3. Add the strained broth through a sieve and peeled and cut chayotes
4. Let cook until the broth is reduced
5. Incorporate one egg yolk beaten in one cup of milk cream
6. Serve

title: Lombo de Porco do Matto Guisado com Favas (Wild Boar Loin Stewed with Broad Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- one wild boar loin
- water
- salt
- pepper
- two cloves
- bay leaves
- one sprig of ground ivy
- parsley
- onion leaves
- half a quart of finely chopped bacon
- fresh broad beans
- three egg yolks
- one tablespoon of wheat flour
- half a cup of sour orange juice

## Directions

1. Cut the loin into pieces and boil in water with salt, pepper, cloves, bay leaves, ground ivy, parsley, and onion leaves
2. When cooked, strain the broth and fry the meat with half a quart of finely chopped bacon
3. Add fresh broad beans and immediately the broth
4. Let boil until the beans are cooked and the broth is reduced
5. Incorporate three egg yolks beaten with one tablespoon of wheat flour and half a cup of sour orange juice
6. Serve

title: Lombo de Porco à Moda de Bolonha (Pork Loin Bologna-Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork loin with loin attached
- Sage leaves
- Vinegar
- Onions
- Carrots
- Lemon zest
- Cloves
- Water or meat broth
- Herbs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Redcurrant jelly

## Directions

1. Take a pork loin with the loin still attached
2. Cover it with sage leaves and tie both ends with twine to give it a round shape
3. Place it in a terrine, cover with vinegar, and add some onions and carrots cut into slices, lemon zest, and some cloves
4. Let it marinate for two to three days
