5. After this time, place the loin in a casserole, adding half of the marinade liquid, two cups of water or meat broth, two onions, two carrots, and a bundle of aromatic herbs, salt and pepper
6. Cover the casserole, place it over a strong fire which is then reduced, letting it cook for two to three hours
7. Remove the twine and sage leaves, place it on a plate, and serve with a portion of redcurrant jelly in a separate terrine
8. Alternatively, the sauce in which the loin was cooked may be poured over it

title: Lombo de Vacca Assado em Espeto (Beef Loin Roasted on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef loin, tender
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaves
- cloves
- cebolinhos (small onions)
- Indian pepper or comari

## Directions

1. The beef loin, being tender, is always roasted on a spit. Remove the fat and skin, then take it to a sauce of vinegar, salt, pepper, bay leaves, and cloves, and after twelve hours, place it on a spit and let it roast for an hour and a half or two hours, depending on the thickness of the piece, always turning it.
2. Serve with a sauce made from the juice that dripped from the meat mixed with vinegar, cebolinhos, Indian pepper or comari. One should never lard this loin, as it loses its juice through the cuts made for this purpose, and consequently remains without flavor.

title: Lombo de Vacca Cheio (Stuffed Beef Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef loin
- lard strips
- sweet oil
- onion
- parsley sprigs
- bay leaves
- broken India pepper

## Directions

1. Take a beef loin and pierce it with lard strips
2. Place it in a serving dish with a glass of sweet oil, an onion cut into rounds, some parsley sprigs, some bay leaves and broken India pepper
3. Turn it frequently
4. After two hours, having drained it, thread it onto a spit
5. Cover with a sheet of paper oiled with sweet oil
6. Place near the fire, basting with the sauce it was in
7. After three-quarters of an hour, remove the paper so it takes color
8. Five minutes before serving, pour the beef essence over it
9. Place in a serving dish with potato pulp prepared with white sauce

title: Lombo de Vacca Cheio de Toucinho à Moda de Manchester (Manchester-Style Beef Loin Filled with Bacon)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef loin
- slices of bacon
- sweet oil
- onion
- parsley sprigs
- bay leaves
- broken Indian pepper
- paper
- small potatoes
- olives or tubaras

## Directions

1. Take a beef loin which is pierced with slices of bacon, placing it on a platter with a glass of sweet oil, an onion cut into rings, some sprigs of parsley, some bay leaves and broken Indian pepper. Turn it many times.
2. Two hours later, place it to drain and after being covered with a sheet of paper greased with sweet oil, place it on the spit. Go to the fire, always coating it with that sauce in which it was soaked.
3. Three quarters of an hour later, remove the paper, and five minutes before going to table, pour over it the substance from the same beef, placing it then on a platter garnished with small potatoes, olives or tubaras.

title: Lombo de Vacca com Môlho de Tomates (Beef Loin with Tomato Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef loin
- lard
- butter
- carrots
- onions
- bay leaf
- aromatic herbs
- chives
- cloves
- good broth

## Directions

1. Prepare and stuff a beef loin with lard and tie it in a roll, using string to maintain its shape
2. Butter a casserole and place the loin in it with carrots, onions, bay leaf, aromatic herbs, chives, two cloves and a spoonful of good broth
3. Cover with a sheet of paper oiled with butter and let it simmer
4. Place over gentle heat both above and below; allow it to take color, leaving it to cook the necessary time
5. Remove the vegetables and fat, and let it cook a little longer until the broth becomes thick, to baste the loin
6. Place in a serving dish and pour tomato sauce over it

title: Lombo de Vacca com Tubaras (Beef Loin with Tubaras)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roasted loin
- tubaras
- beef broth
- white wine
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cut the leftover roasted loin into small slices.
2. Cut the tubaras into an equal amount of small slices.
3. Place everything in a casserole with one cup of broth and another cup of white wine.
4. Let it simmer for half an hour over moderate heat.
5. Sprinkle salt, pepper, and nutmeg to taste.
6. Serve as is.

title: Lombo de Vacca em Vinho Madeira (Beef Loin in Madeira Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef loin
- lard pieces
- beef slices
- veal slices
- onions
- aromatic herb sprigs
- Madeira wine, half a bottle
- beef broth, half a glass
- Spanish sauce, one good glass

## Directions

1. Prepare the loin, rolled and tied as previously described
2. Place it in a casserole in the bottom of which have been placed some pieces of lard, slices of beef and veal, onions and a sprig of aromatic herbs
3. Cover the loin with some lard slices
4. Pour over it half a bottle of Madeira wine and half a glass of beef broth
5. Let it simmer briefly, then cook over gentle heat for about two hours
6. When cooked, pass the broth through a silk sieve
7. Pour a good glass of Spanish sauce over it and add the strained juice
8. Reduce everything to the consistency of beef broth essence and pour over the loin already placed in the serving dish

title: Lombo de Vacca Encapotado (Beef Loin in Pastry Crust)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef loin
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaf
- celery
- wheat flour
- eggs
- beef butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. After the loin has been seasoned with salt, pepper, bay leaf and celery for twenty-four hours, roast it on a spit
2. Once roasted, make a dough of wheat flour, eggs, beef butter, a little sugar and salt
3. Cover the loin with a thin layer of this dough, wrapping it in paper oiled with butter
4. Roast it for another hour
5. Finally remove the paper and serve with the sauce of your choice

title: Lombo de Vacca Estufado à Moda do Caçador (Beef Loin Braised Hunter Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef loin
- ham slices
- rabbit meat strips
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- lard slices
- beef broth, one spoonful
- dry Madeira wine, two glasses
- lard, one pound chopped finely
- onions, two
- parsley sprigs
- thyme
- bay leaves
- valeriana (blessed herb)
- cloves
- roasted ham slices
- rabbit thighs
- butter

## Directions

1. Take a good piece of loin and prepare it by filling it with ham slices and rabbit meat strips seasoned with salt, pepper and nutmeg
2. Cover the loin entirely with lard slices
3. After tying it, place it in a covered pot over heat
4. Add a spoonful of meat broth, two glasses of dry Madeira wine, a pound of finely chopped lard, two onions, a sprig of parsley, thyme, bay leaves, valeriana, cloves, some salt, pepper, some roasted ham slices and rabbit thighs
5. Cover with a sheet of paper oiled with butter
6. Two hours before serving, bring to a boil; then let the loin drain
7. Remove the strings and pass the broth through a silk sieve, removing the fat, leaving it reduced to half the meat essence
8. Add two spoonfuls of Spanish sauce and half a glass of Madeira wine to the broth and remove from heat
9. At this point add a little fresh butter and, divided into three parts, two-thirds are placed in the serving dish where the loin is placed, basted with meat essence, and the rest of the sauce is poured into a sauce boat garnished with rabbit loins

title: Lombo de Vacca Refogado com Tubaras (Beef Loin Braised with Tubaras)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef loin
- tubaras (a type of vegetable)
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut the loin into pieces the size of a square inch and place them in a casserole greased with butter. Cut in the same manner half a dozen tubaras which are added to the meat and covered with melted butter.
2. After everything is cooked, drain the butter; pouring onto a platter the meat and tubaras, pour over it a sauce.

title: Lombo de Vacca Retogado com Tubaras (Braised Beef Loin with Turnips)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef loin
- turnips, about half a dozen
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut the loin into pieces the size of a square inch
2. Place them in a casserole dish lined with butter
3. Cut the turnips in the same manner and add them to the meat
4. Cover with melted butter
5. Once everything is cooked, drain the butter
6. Pour the meat and turnips into a serving dish
7. Pour Spanish sauce over it, to which a glass of Madeira wine has been previously added

title: Lombo de Vacca à Mineira (Minas Gerais-Style Beef Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of beef
- parsley
- pepper
- vinegar
- melted fat
- paper
- tomatoes
- onions
- comari pepper
- salt
- garlic

## Directions

1. Take a piece of beef that is beaten strongly with a mallet. Make a sauce of parsley, pepper, vinegar, and a little melted fat, and coat the piece of meat with this sauce.
2. Let it remain there for two hours; then cover it with paper greased with fat, placing it on a sheet metal tray two fingers in height, which is placed over a grill with coals underneath; from time to time, turn it from one side to the other.
3. When you see that nothing more drips from the meat, remove the paper, letting it brown appropriately, and being careful to turn from time to time to avoid burning.
4. Place it on a platter pouring over it a sauce made of tomatoes, onions, a little vinegar, comari pepper, salt and garlic.

title: Lombo de Vacca à Tranceza (French-Style Beef Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- good piece of beef loin
- pieces of bacon
- garlic wine
- butter
- paper
- wheat flour

## Directions

1. Take a good piece of beef loin, from which the membrane is removed; beat it with a mallet, piercing it with pieces of bacon; let it soak in garlic wine for twenty-four hours.
2. After it has drained and been dried with a cloth, place it on a spit, cover it with a sheet of paper greased with butter, let it roast for an hour and a quarter, moistening it every ten minutes with the juice that drips from it.
3. One hour later, remove the paper and let it take color, placing it on the plate which should contain a restorative sauce, pouring over it the substance from the meat itself or a sauce of tubaras.

title: Lombo de Veado Assado com Capa de Ovos (Roasted Venison Loin with Egg Coating)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison loin
- salt
- lemon juice
- onion slices
- parsley
- cloves
- ginger
- bacon
- breadcrumbs
- four egg yolks
- one tablespoon of sugar
- cinnamon powder
- lemon juice
- white wine

## Directions

1. Rub the loin with salt and lemon juice
2. Cover it with slices of onion, parsley, cloves and ginger
3. Wrap it and put it on the spit
4. When roasted, remove it from the spit and remove the bacon that wrapped it as well as the seasonings
5. Place in a large casserole and cover with the following sauce: a handful of breadcrumbs, four egg yolks, one tablespoon of sugar, cinnamon powder, a little salt, the juice of one lemon, white wine as much as needed to form a thick porridge
6. Put it thus in the oven and serve with some compote or salad

title: Lombo de Veado Assado na Grelha (Grilled Venison Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison loin
- thin strips of bacon
- salt
- pimento peppers
- garlic
- onions
- bitter orange juice
- mustard
- white wine

## Directions

1. Lard the loin with thin strips of bacon
2. Sprinkle it with salt and anoint it with the juice of pimento peppers, garlic, chopped onions, and bitter orange juice
3. Leave it in these seasonings for six hours
4. Grill it over live fire on both sides to take on good color
5. Serve with its own sauce to which a little ground mustard or preferably English mustard is added; if the sauce is scanty, add a glass of white wine

title: Lombo de Veado Ensopado com Tayoba (Venison Loin Stewed with Taro)
body:
## Ingredients

- three tablespoons of butter
- two tablespoons of wheat flour
- venison loin
- one bottle of water
- salt
- few pimento peppers
- parsley
- onion leaves
- one root of taro or yam

## Directions

1. Melt three tablespoons of butter and brown two tablespoons of wheat flour in it
2. Then add the loin cut into slices
3. Add one bottle of water, salt, few pimento peppers, parsley, onion leaves and one root of taro or yam cooked in water and salt and cut into slices
4. Let boil for one hour over moderate heat and serve

title: Lombo de Veado Estufado com Couve Flor (Venison Loin Braised with Cauliflower)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison loin
- white wine
- salt
- pimento peppers
- onions
- garlic
- ginger
- marjoram
- basil
- bacon strips
- cauliflower or cabbage

## Directions

1. Soak the loin in white wine, salt, pimento peppers, onions, garlic, ginger, marjoram and basil for twenty-four hours
2. Remove and wrap it in strips of bacon
3. Place it in a pot with a portion of cauliflower, or lacking that, very finely chopped cabbage, holding the lid down well
4. Boil over regular heat until cooked and the broth reduced, and serve

title: Lombo de Veado Estufado com Hervilhos (Venison Loin Braised with Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison loin
- pieces of bacon seasoned with salt
- pimento peppers from India
- garlic
- bacon strips
- two cups of water
- one onion
- cloves
- cinnamon
- new peas
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Lard the venison loin with some thick pieces of bacon seasoned with salt, pimento peppers from India and pieces of a garlic clove
2. Place it in a pot over strips of bacon with two cups of water, one onion studded with cloves and cinnamon
3. Cover the pot well and let boil over coals until cooked
4. On the other hand, boil a portion of new peas in water and salt
5. After draining, braise them in the loin's broth and serve with the loin

title: Lombo de Veado Guisado com Mangalós (Venison Loin Stewed with Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison loin
- thin strips of bacon
- water
- salt
- parsley
- pimento pepper
- onion leaves
- ginger
- portion of beans
- one tablespoon of wheat flour
- one cup of sour orange juice
- one tablespoon of sugar

## Directions

1. Lard the loin with thin strips of bacon
2. Boil it in water, salt, parsley, pimento pepper, onion leaves and ginger
3. Put it on the spit and roast over strong fire until almost roasted
4. Cook in the broth a portion of beans, adding to them one tablespoon of wheat flour dissolved in one cup of sour orange juice, one tablespoon of sugar, until the broth is reduced and the beans become almost dry
5. Then add the sauce that dripped from the roasted loin and serve, pouring the beans on top of the loin

title: Lombo de Veado Refogado com Feijão Branco (Braised Venison Loin with White Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison loin
- four tablespoons of fat
- two tablespoons of wheat flour
- one tablespoon of vinegar
- one bottle of water
- salt
- some pimento peppers
- parsley
- one onion
- nutmeg
- white beans or guandús

## Directions

1. Braise the loin in four tablespoons of fat
2. After cooking, dust it with two tablespoons of wheat flour
3. Add one tablespoon of vinegar, one bottle of water, salt, some pimento peppers, parsley, one onion cut up, grated nutmeg, and a portion of white beans or guandús that have been soaked in cold water for two hours
4. Let everything boil for two to three hours over moderate heat and serve

title: Lombo de Veado Refogado com Feijão Branco (Venison Loin Stewed with White Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison loin
- four spoonfuls of fat
- two spoonfuls of wheat flour
- one cup of vinegar
- one bottle of water
- salt
- peppercorns
- parsley
- one onion, cut
- nutmeg, grated
- white beans or pigeon peas

## Directions

1. Braise the loin in four spoonfuls of fat
2. After it is cooked, sprinkle it with two spoonfuls of wheat flour
3. Add one cup of vinegar, one bottle of water, salt, some peppercorns, parsley, one cut onion, grated nutmeg, and a portion of white beans or pigeon peas

title: Lombo de Veado à Caçador (Venison Loin Hunter's Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison loin
- salt

## Directions

1. Those who appreciate game do not like venison meat with many side dishes and seasonings
2. Only rub the loin with salt and roast it on a spit over a moderate fire to cook slowly and take on good color
3. Eat it, squeezing lemon juice over it

title: Lombo de Veado à Princeza (Venison Loin Princess Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison loin
- salt pork
- white wine
- salt
- parsley
- pepper
- nutmeg
- ginger
- cloves from India
- butter
- bread crumbs
- white wine

## Directions

1. Take the venison loin, lard it with thin strips of salt pork, and soak it in white wine with salt, parsley, pepper, nutmeg, ginger and cloves from India
2. After three days, remove it, having wrapped it in paper greased with butter
3. Roast it on a spit and when roasted, remove the paper and wrap it in bread crumbs
4. Pass it through melted butter and let it roast until it takes on good color
5. Serve with the gravy that dripped, to which one chalice of white wine is added; give it a boil and strain before serving

title: Lombo Estufado e Guarnecido de Cenouras (Braised Loin Garnished with Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef loin
- carrots

## Directions

1. Prepare and braise a good piece of loin as usual, after basting it with its own essence
2. Place it in a serving dish garnished with small carrots, also basted with the same meat essence
3. Pour the rest of the sauce strained through cloth into a sauce boat, removing the fat so it remains reduced to half the meat essence

title: Lombo Guisado (Stewed Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef loin
- butter
- Spanish sauce, two spoonfuls
- mushrooms or carapicús
- turnip sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the loin into thin slices
2. Place these in a casserole, covering them with a good piece of butter
3. Cook over fairly strong heat so they cook without boiling
4. When they are firm under the finger, drain the butter
5. Add two spoonfuls of Spanish sauce and let them cook slowly for several minutes
6. Place in a serving dish garnished with mushrooms or carapicús and a turnip sauce or any other

title: Lontra e Ariranha Assadas (Roasted Otter and Giant Otter)
body:
## Ingredients

- otter or giant otter
- sour orange juice (laranja da terra azeda)
- salt
- parsley
- onions
- pepper
- nutmeg
- butter or fat
- paper

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the otter.
2. After cleaning and washing, soak in sour orange juice, salt, parsley, onions, pepper, and nutmeg for twenty-four hours.
3. Wrap in paper spread with butter or fat and spit-roast over moderate fire.
4. When the meat is roasted, serve.

title: Lontra e Ariranha Ensopadas (Otter and Giant Otter Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- otter or giant otter meat
- hot fat
- one bottle of water
- white beans
- salt
- parsley
- onion
- wheat flour
- vinegar
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into slices and beat slightly.
2. Pass through hot fat.
3. Add one bottle of water, a portion of white beans, salt, parsley, and onion.
4. Let boil until the meat and beans are cooked.
5. Add one spoonful of toasted wheat flour mixed with a little vinegar and an egg yolk.
6. Give it another boil and serve.

title: Lontra e Ariranha Guisadas (Braised Otter and Giant Otter)
body:
## Ingredients

- half or one quarter of an otter or giant otter
- water
- salt
- pepper
- cumin
- parsley leaves
- onions
- one spoonful of wheat flour
- one spoonful of sugar
- two spoonfuls of fat
- one chopped onion
- one cup of water
- half a dozen tomatoes

## Directions

1. Boil half or one quarter of an otter or giant otter in water with salt, pepper, cumin, parsley leaves, and onions.
2. When the meat is cooked, remove the bones and cut into pieces.
3. Fry one spoonful of wheat flour and one spoonful of sugar in two spoonfuls of fat with one chopped onion until browned.
4. Add the meat, stir, and add one cup of water and half a dozen tomatoes.
5. Let everything boil for another half hour and serve.

title: Lúcio, Trahira, etc. Assados e Recheados (Pike, Trahira, etc. Roasted and Stuffed)
body:
## Ingredients

- one large fish
- half a dozen small fish, cleaned with bones removed and chopped
- half a dozen cooked shrimp
- one onion
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- carapicú
- bread crumb soaked in hot milk
- butter
- one cup of hot wine
- one glass of vinegar
- one tablespoon of mustard sauce
- yolk of one cooked egg

## Directions

1. Clean the fish and fill it with the following mixture: half a dozen cleaned and chopped small fish, half a dozen cooked shrimp, one onion, parsley, salt, pepper, carapicú, and bread crumb soaked in hot milk, all well mashed
2. Dust the fish with salt and pepper
3. Wrap it in a buttered paper leaf and tie
4. Thread the fish onto a spit and roast over moderate fire
5. When roasted, make a sauce of one cup of hot wine, one glass of vinegar, one tablespoon of mustard sauce, salt, pepper and the yolk of one cooked egg
6. Boil the sauce briefly and serve it

title: Lúcio, Trahira, etc. Assados na Grelha (Pike, Trahira, etc. Grilled)
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned fish
- paper
- butter
- parsley
- chopped onion
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Clean the fish and wrap them in well-buttered paper
2. Cook them on the grill
3. When roasted, remove the paper and fill the belly with a little butter mixed with chopped parsley, onion, salt and pepper
4. Place the fish on a plate and keep them in a warm place until serving

title: Lúcio, Trahira, etc. Cozidos (Pike, Trahira, etc. Boiled)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- water
- white wine
- salt
- peppercorns
- bay leaves
- clove
- parsley
- onions
- two tablespoons of butter
- vinegar
- sweet oil
- cassava flour

## Directions

1. Boil the fish in equal parts water and white wine, ensuring the fish is covered with liquid
2. Add salt, peppercorns, bay leaves, clove, parsley, and onions, and two tablespoons of butter
3. When cooked, remove the fish and serve with vinegar, sweet oil, salt, pepper and a broth made from the fish cooking liquid and cassava flour

title: Lúcio, Trahira, etc. Cozidos em Gelea (Pike, Trahira, etc. Boiled in Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish, cut three fingers thick
- four ounces of lard, chopped
- half a pound of beef
- salt
- parsley
- pepper
- onion leaves
- one glass of white wine
- water
- one cup of white wine
- one tablespoon of sour orange juice

## Directions

1. Cut the fish into three-finger-thick pieces
2. Chop four ounces of lard and half a pound of beef
3. Add salt, parsley, pepper, onion leaves, one glass of white wine and equal amount of water
4. Place in a pan and boil until the fish is cooked
5. Remove the fish, strain the broth and reduce it until well incorporated
6. Pass the fish pieces through this broth and place them on a plate over the remaining sauce
7. Add one cup of white wine and one tablespoon of sour orange juice to dissolve the remaining broth
8. Serve with the same fish

title: Lúcio, Trahira, etc. Fritos (Pike, Trahira, etc. Fried)
body:
## Ingredients

- small fish
- salt
- ground pepper
- cassava flour
- lard
- chopped onion

## Directions

1. Choose small fish and dust them with salt and ground pepper
2. Roll them in cassava flour and fry in abundant lard with chopped onion
3. When browned, serve hot

title: Lúcio, Trahira, etc. Guisados (Pike, Trahira, etc. Stewed)
body:
## Ingredients

- one large fish, cut into steaks
- water
- salt
- peppercorns
- parsley
- onion leaves
- sour orange juice
- dozen tomatoes
- two tablespoons of lard
- okra
- corn flour porridge

## Directions

1. Boil one large fish cut into steaks, adding water, salt, peppercorns, parsley, onion leaves and sour orange juice
2. When cooked, fry a dozen tomatoes in two tablespoons of lard
3. Add the broth and fish, along with okra cut into rounds
4. Let boil for a quarter hour and serve with corn flour porridge

title: Macaco Assado no Espeto (Roasted Monkey on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- monkey
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice
- bacon strips
- butter or fat
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Pluck the monkey and clean it well.
2. Rub with salt, pepper, and lemon juice, leaving it in this seasoning for two to three hours.
3. Lard with fine strips of bacon and roast on a spit, wrapped in paper spread with butter or fat.
4. When cooked, remove the paper and roll in bread crumbs.
5. Continue roasting, moistening with butter, until it takes on good color, and serve.

title: Macaco Assado no Forno (Oven-Roasted Monkey)
body:
## Ingredients

- monkey
- sour orange juice (laranja da terra azeda)
- salt
- pepper
- garlic
- marjoram
- parsley
- bacon strips
- one glass of wine

## Directions

1. Soak the monkey in sour orange juice with salt, pepper, garlic, marjoram, and parsley for twenty-four hours, turning it two or three times.
2. Lard with fine strips of bacon, place in a pan, moisten with one glass of wine.
3. Roast in the oven or over coals; when roasted, serve.

title: Macaco Cozido com Aboboras (Monkey Stewed with Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- monkey cut into pieces
- water
- salt
- pepper
- one garlic clove
- parsley
- half a pound of bacon
- one pound of veal

## Directions

1. Boil the monkey cut into pieces in water with salt, pepper, one garlic clove, parsley, half a pound of bacon, and one pound of veal.

title: Macaco cozido com aboboras (Stewed Monkey with Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- one monkey, cut into pieces
- water
- salt
- peppers
- one clove of garlic
- parsley
- half a pound of bacon
- nine pounds of veal

## Directions

1. Boil the monkey cut into pieces in water with salt, peppers, one clove of garlic, parsley, half a pound of bacon and nine pounds of veal
2. When cooked, remove the meat and strain the broth, reducing it to three or four cups
3. Add one squash with still-soft skin, peeled and chopped
4. Give it one boil and add the monkey meat and the veal cut into pieces with some peeled lemon slices, and two egg yolks beaten in a glass of sour orange juice
5. After boiling for one more hour, serve

title: Macaco cozido com bananas (Stewed Monkey with Bananas)
body:
## Ingredients

- one monkey
- water
- salt
- a dozen land bananas with skin
- two tablespoons of wheat flour
- the same amount of butter
- one cup of white wine
- two tablespoons of sugar
- one tablespoon of lemon juice

## Directions

1. Remove the head from the monkey and boil it in water and salt with a dozen land bananas with skin
2. When the meat is cooked, brown two tablespoons of wheat flour in the same amount of butter, and before it colors, add one cup of white wine with two tablespoons of sugar and one of lemon juice
3. After boiling a little, add the meat without bones and cut into pieces
4. Let it boil once more, and pour everything over the bananas peeled and placed whole on a plate, and serve

title: Macaco Estufado com Gerumbeba (Monkey Braised with Prickly Pears)
body:
## Ingredients

- monkey, cleaned and without head
- one veal or pork liver
- two ounces of bacon
- one onion
- parsley
- two egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- one spoonful of sugar
- bacon strips
- prickly pears (gerumbelas), peeled
- one cup of water
- one dozen tomatoes

## Directions

1. Take a cleaned monkey without head and fill it with a stuffing made with one veal or pork liver, two ounces of bacon, one onion, a little parsley, all finely chopped and mashed with two egg yolks, salt, pepper, nutmeg, and one spoonful of sugar.
2. Place it in a pan lined with bacon strips with a portion of peeled prickly pears, one cup of water, and one dozen tomatoes.
3. Cover the pan and let boil for three hours over a moderate fire.
4. When cooked, serve with the strained and degreased sauce.

title: Macaco frito com salada de batatas (Fried Monkey with Potato Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- one monkey, cut into pieces
- lemon juice
- salt
- pepper
- wheat flour
- lard
- potatoes
- vinegar
- parsley
- onions
- fat

## Directions

1. Cut the monkey into pieces, except the head
2. Soak them in lemon juice with salt and pepper for six hours
3. Remove them, wrap them in wheat flour, and roast them on a grill, dripping a little lard on them from time to time
4. At this time, take some potatoes which are peeled and cut into slices
5. Add half a cup of good vinegar, a little salt, peppers, chopped parsley and onions
6. Add one tablespoon of fat, stir and serve cold with the fried monkey

title: Macaco Refogado com Pepinos (Monkey Braised with Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- monkey, cleaned and without head
- three spoonfuls of fat
- half a dozen cucumbers
- six onions
- one cup of water
- one glass of sour orange juice
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- chopped parsley
- two egg yolks
- one glass of wine

## Directions

1. Braise the cleaned monkey without head in three spoonfuls of fat.
2. Remove it and in the same fat put half a dozen cucumbers and six onions cut into pieces.
3. Add one cup of water, one glass of sour orange juice, salt, pepper, cloves, chopped parsley, and the pieces of braised monkey.
4. Let boil for one hour.
5. Incorporate the sauce with two beaten and dissolved egg yolks in one glass of wine, and serve.

title: Macaroni Tostado (Toasted Macaroni)
body:
## Ingredients

- macaroni
- good broth
- one onion
- cloves
- salt
- broken pepper
- slices of bread
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- Dutch or Minas cheese, finely grated

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of macaroni in good broth with an onion, cloves, a little salt and broken pepper.
2. When the macaroni is nearly cooked, remove from fire and place in a deep dish surrounded by slices of bread spread with butter.
3. Sprinkle breadcrumbs on top and a good layer of finely grated Dutch or Minas cheese.
4. Pour a little butter on top and brown with a hot iron.

title: Macarrão de Morangas Verdes (Green Squash Pasta)
body:
## Ingredients

- green squash
- salt
- garlic
- vinegar
- parsley
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take some green squash, peel them and remove the seeds
2. Then chop them as finely as possible
3. Season with salt, garlic, vinegar, chopped parsley and plenty of pepper

title: Maccarão de Morangas Verdes (Green Squash Pasta)
body:
## Ingredients

- green squashes
- salt
- garlic
- vinegar
- chopped parsley
- plenty of Indian pepper
- hot fat

## Directions

1. Take green squashes, peel them and remove the seeds
2. Chop as finely as possible
3. Season with salt, garlic, vinegar, chopped parsley and plenty of Indian pepper
4. Sauté in a pan with very hot fat
5. Cover the pan and let it cook a little
6. Serve

title: Mandioca Assada (Roasted Cassava)
body:
## Ingredients

- cassava
- salt
- sugar
- butter
- fat or milk cream

## Directions

1. Roast one or two cassavas in the embers
2. When roasted, peel and arrange on a plate
3. Add a little salt and sugar, pour a little butter, fat or milk cream on top, and place in the oven or over coals, covering with a lid with coals on top
4. When browned, serve hot

title: Mandioca Frita (Fried Cassava)
body:
## Ingredients

- cassava
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves

## Directions

1. Roast in embers or cook in water two cassavas
2. Peel and cut into slices
3. Fry in a casserole with a little salt, parsley, and chopped onion leaves, and serve

title: Mandí Cozido (Boiled Salted Catfish)
body:
## Ingredients

- salted fish
- water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- marjoram
- peppercorns
- lemon juice
- flour

## Directions

1. Soak the salted fish in cold water for six hours
2. Boil in water with salt, parsley, onion leaves, marjoram, peppercorns and lemon juice
3. When cooked, strain the broth and thicken it with flour
4. Pour the broth onto a plate, place the fish on top and serve

title: Mandí Ensopado (Catfish Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- one salted catfish, cut into steaks
- water
- two tablespoons of lard
- one tablespoon of corn flour
- peppercorns
- parsley
- two cups of milk

## Directions

1. Boil one salted catfish cut into steaks
2. When cooked, melt two tablespoons of lard and mix with one tablespoon of corn flour, peppercorns and parsley
3. Add two cups of milk
4. Place the fish in this sauce and add the strained and reduced cooking broth
5. Let boil until the broth is well reduced and serve

title: Mandí Frito (Fried Catfish)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish, cut one finger thick
- salt
- pepper
- lard
- wheat flour or corn flour
- chopped parsley
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut the fish into one-finger-thick steaks and sprinkle with salt and pepper
2. Fry in lard on both sides
3. Roll in wheat flour or corn flour mixed with chopped parsley and fry again
4. When golden, serve with lemon juice on top

title: Mandí Refogado (Sautéed Catfish)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh catfish
- lard
- chopped onion
- corn flour
- water
- salt
- parsley
- marjoram
- two egg yolks
- one cup of lemon juice
- cassava flour

## Directions

1. Take fresh catfish, remove the entrails and cut into steaks
2. Sauté in lard with chopped onion
3. Dust with corn flour and add half a bottle of water, salt, parsley and marjoram
4. Let boil for one hour
5. Incorporate two egg yolks beaten in one cup of lemon juice
6. Boil a little more and serve with cassava flour broth

title: Mangalós Guisados (Stewed Mangalós)
body:
## Ingredients

- mangalós
- water and salt
- a piece of chopped bacon
- a little water
- a little sour orange juice

## Directions

1. Boil the mangalós in water and salt
2. When cooked, drain the water; fry a piece of chopped bacon and add the mangalós
3. Add a little water and a little sour orange juice and serve

title: Mangalós Refogados (Sautéed Mangalós)
body:
## Ingredients

- a portion of mangalós
- water and salt
- a dozen tomatoes
- parsley
- onion leaves
- ground pepper
- lard
- one spoon of sour orange juice

## Directions

1. Cook a portion of mangalós in water and salt
2. When cooked, pass through a sieve
3. Fry a dozen tomatoes, parsley, onion leaves, ground pepper and lard
4. Add the mangalós purée and one spoon of sour orange juice
5. Stir, put on a plate, and serve with fried sausages

title: Mangaritos Assados (Roasted Mangarito)
body:
## Ingredients

- mangarito
- salt
- butter
- wheat flour
- egg yolks
- milk

## Directions

1. Peel a portion of mangarito and place sprinkled with salt in a pan that goes in the oven until roasted
2. When roasted, melt two spoons of cow butter with one of wheat flour
3. Add two beaten egg yolks and two cups of rich milk
4. Let boil for some moments, pour the mangarito in this sauce, give it a boil, and serve

title: Mangaritos Ensopados (Stewed Mangarito)
body:
## Ingredients

- mangarito
- fat
- wheat flour
- water
- lemon juice
- parsley
- salt
- onion leaves

## Directions

1. Peel a portion of mangarito and cut into pieces
2. Fry in two spoons of fat and one spoon of wheat flour
3. Add a bottle of water, a glass of lemon juice, parsley, salt, and onion leaves
4. Leave to boil over moderate heat and serve when cooked

title: Manjar de Galinha (Chicken Almond Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken
- two bottles milk
- half pound rice powder
- one pound sugar
- lemon peel
- cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cook a chicken in two bottles of milk
2. When cooked, remove the chicken, bone it and chop it very fine
3. Pound in a mortar until reduced to a paste
4. Return the paste to the milk with half pound of rice powder, one pound of sugar and chopped lemon peel
5. When the mixture is cooked and incorporated, pour on a plate
6. Cover with sugar and cinnamon, put in the oven to bake and toast and serve

title: Manjar de Pão (Bread Almond Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cooked chicken breasts
- quarter pound blanched almonds
- two and a half pounds sugar
- breadcrumbs
- one bottle milk
- four egg yolks
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Take two cooked chicken breasts and pound them with a quarter pound of blanched almonds, two and a half pounds of sugar and a portion of breadcrumbs
2. Put in a casserole with one bottle of milk
3. Let boil twice and when it has cooled a little, add four beaten egg yolks
4. Boil again, pour on a plate
5. Cover with sugar and ground cinnamon and serve

title: Manjar Dourado (Golden Almond Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound rice powder
- two bottles milk
- eight egg yolks
- one pound sugar
- cloves
- egg whites
- half pound sugar

## Directions

1. Cook one pound of rice powder in two bottles of milk with eight egg yolks, one pound of sugar and cloves
2. When everything is cooked and thick, arrange on a plate
3. Cover with beaten egg whites mixed with half pound of sugar so that it forms a meringue
4. Put in the oven and when browned, serve

title: Manjar Enfarinhado (Floured Almond Cream Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- almond cream mixture
- one pound butter
- four egg yolks
- two tablespoons sugar
- pastry casing
- melted butter
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. After making the almond cream mixture with eggs as already described, knead one pound of butter, four egg yolks and two tablespoons of sugar
2. Remove the mixture with a spoon and place inside a piece of pastry casing
3. Roll and press the edges cutting them with a pastry wheel
4. Fry these rolls in melted butter and serve with sugar and cinnamon

title: Manjar à Brasileira (Brazilian Almond Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound rice powder
- two bottles milk
- a little salt
- one pound sugar
- cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Boil one pound of rice powder with two bottles of milk, a little salt and one pound of sugar, stirring constantly
2. When cooked, remove with a spoon in spoonfuls and arrange on a plate
3. Put in the oven to bake and brown
4. Sprinkle with cinnamon and sugar and serve

title: Marreco assado com maçãs (Roasted Duck with Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- one clean duck
- two slices of bacon
- one onion
- two cloves
- one bay leaf
- salt
- pepper
- one cup of broth
- pulp of ten to twelve apples
- two dozen raisins
- two spoonfuls of sugar

## Directions

1. Take a clean duck and boil it for half an hour, wrapped in two slices of bacon, with one onion, two cloves, one bay leaf, salt, pepper and one cup of broth
2. Remove the duck and strain the broth, mixing it with the pulp of ten to twelve apples, two dozen raisins and two spoonfuls of sugar
3. Serve this pulp on a plate with the duck on top

title: Marreco assado no espeto (Duck Roasted on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- one duck
- breadcrumb crumb
- duck liver, chopped
- white wine
- salt
- pepper
- one onion
- two cloves

## Directions

1. After plucking, cleaning and washing, fill the duck with breadcrumb crumb mixed with the chopped duck liver, all moistened with white wine, seasoned with salt, pepper, one onion and two cloves
2. Roast it on a spit, at first over moderate fire so the excess fat has time to melt, and then over livelier fire so the roast takes the proper color
3. When cooked, serve with salads

title: Marreco assado no forno (Duck Roasted in the Oven)
body:
## Ingredients

- duck liver
- two cooked eggs
- handful of carapicú
- one onion
- salt
- pepper
- bacon slices
- one glass of white wine
- clove of India
- parsley
- salad or pulp pasta

## Directions

1. Chop the duck liver with two cooked eggs, a handful of carapicú and one onion; mix with salt and pepper and fill the duck
2. Place it in the oven in a pan lined with bacon slices, one glass of white wine, salt, clove of India, parsley
3. Let it boil until the meat is cooked and serve with some salad or pulp pasta

title: Marreco ensopado com cará (Duck Stewed with Taro)
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of duck
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- one bowl of meat broth
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- parsley
- one or two taro roots
- two egg yolks
- one spoonful of vinegar

## Directions

1. Wrap the pieces of duck in breadcrumbs and fry them in butter
2. Add a bowl of meat broth, little salt, pepper, onion, parsley, one or two taro roots if small
3. Let it boil until everything is cooked; remove the duck pieces and the taro cut in slices
4. Having reduced the broth to little liquid, thicken it with two egg yolks and one spoonful of vinegar, and return the meat and taro slices
5. Let it boil a bit more and serve

title: Marreco ensopado com chuchã (Duck Stewed with Chayote)
body:
## Ingredients

- one duck, cut in pieces
- three spoonfuls of butter
- two spoonfuls of wheat flour
- one bottle of water
- salt
- pepper
- onion leaves
- cane
- a portion of peeled and cut chayotes
- one glass of vinegar
- two spoonfuls of sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the duck in pieces and fry them with three spoonfuls of butter until almost roasted
2. Remove the pieces and in the same butter add two spoonfuls of wheat flour and stir until golden
3. Add a bottle of water, salt, pepper and onion leaves
4. Boil everything a little; add the cane and a portion of peeled and cut chayotes, one glass of vinegar and two spoonfuls of sugar
5. Boil for half an hour more and serve

title: Marreco ensopado com inhame (Duck Stewed with Yam)
body:
## Ingredients

- one duck, cut in pieces
- fat
- one spoonful of wheat flour
- one bowl of water
- salt
- pepper
- onion leaves
- marjoram
- one yam root
- one spoonful of lemon juice
- one lemon, cut and without seeds

## Directions

1. Cut the duck in pieces and pass them through fat until roasted on all sides
2. Add one spoonful of wheat flour and stir, then add one bowl of water, salt, pepper, onion leaves, marjoram and one yam root cooked beforehand in water and salt and cut in slices
3. Let it boil until everything is cooked, and before serving, add one spoonful of lemon juice and one lemon cut and without seeds

title: Marreco Estufado com Ameixas do Canadá (Braised Duck with Canadian Plums)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 duck
- Canadian plums, peeled and pitted, or preserved plums mixed with raisins and pieces of peeled quince
- half a pound of bacon cut into pieces
- two glasses of wine
- salt
- nutmeg
- cardamom
- cloves
- two dozen plums, peeled and pitted

## Directions

1. Take the duck and fill it with Canadian plums, peeled and pitted, or in their absence, with preserved plums mixed with raisins and pieces of peeled quince
2. Place the duck in a pan with half a pound of bacon cut into pieces, two glasses of wine, little salt, nutmeg, cardamom, cloves, and two dozen plums peeled and without pits
3. Seal the lid and cook over a regularly hot fire
4. After one hour, remove the duck and place it on a plate with the plums arranged around it
5. Strain the sauce, remove the fat, and serve it over the roast

title: Marreco estufado com ameixas do Canadá (Duck Braised with Canadian Plums)
body:
## Ingredients

- one duck
- Canadian plums, peeled and pitted, or preserved plums mixed with raisins and pieces of peeled quince
- half a pound of bacon, cut in pieces
- two glasses of wine
- salt
- nutmeg
- cardamom
- clove of India
- two dozen peeled plums without pits

## Directions

1. Take the duck and fill it with peeled and pitted Canadian plums, or in their absence with preserved plums mixed with raisins and pieces of peeled quince
2. Place the duck in the pan with half a pound of bacon cut in pieces, two glasses of wine, little salt, nutmeg, cardamom, clove of India, two dozen peeled plums without pits
3. Seal the lid and boil it over regularly hot fire

title: Marreco Estufado com Cará do Ar (Braised Duck with Air Yam)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 duck
- salt
- garlic juice
- onion juice
- ground pepper
- chopped parsley
- vinegar
- air yam, grated
- three tablespoons butter
- three tablespoons grated cheese
- little salt
- ground cinnamon
- bacon slices
- six to eight onions
- one cup of broth
- salt
- pepper
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Soak the duck with salt, garlic juice, onion juice, ground pepper, chopped parsley, and vinegar
2. After six to eight hours, remove it and fill it with grated air yam mixed with three tablespoons of butter, three of grated cheese, little salt, and ground cinnamon
3. Place the filled duck on a slice of bacon and cover with another slice
4. Add six to eight onions, one cup of broth, salt, pepper, and chopped parsley
5. Seal the lid and let it cook for an hour and a half over a regular fire
6. When cooked, remove the duck, strain the sauce, remove the fat, and pour it over the duck on a plate, arranging the onions around it

title: Marreco Frito com Choucroute (Fried Duck with Sauerkraut)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 duck, cut into pieces
- salt
- pepper
- breadcrumbs
- fat for frying
- fermented cabbage or fermented cabbage
- little water
- salt
- one cup of vinegar

## Directions

1. After soaking the duck pieces in salt and pepper, roll them in breadcrumbs
2. Fry in plenty of fat
3. When fried and of good color, remove them
4. In the same fat, place a portion of fermented cabbage or blanched cabbage, adding little water, salt, and one cup of vinegar
5. Let the cabbage braise and finish cooking near the fire, stirring occasionally to avoid burning
6. Serve with the fried duck placed on top

title: Marreco Frito com Guandús (Fried Duck with Pigeon Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 duck, cut into pieces
- salt
- pepper
- wheat flour
- egg yolks
- fat for frying
- one tablespoon wheat flour
- one glass of vinegar
- pigeon peas cooked in water
- salt
- onion leaves
- parsley leaves

## Directions

1. Cut the duck into pieces and rub with salt and pepper
2. Roll them in wheat flour and then pass through egg yolks
3. Fry in little fat over a lively fire
4. Remove the pieces and add one tablespoon of wheat flour to the same fat
5. Stir and add one glass of vinegar and a portion of pigeon peas cooked in water, salt, onion leaves, and parsley leaves
6. Let them boil over low fire until the water dries, and serve the pigeon peas with the fried duck on top

title: Marreco Frito com Mangaritos (Fried Duck with Mangaritos)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 young duck, cut into pieces
- orange juice
- salt
- parsley
- chopped onion
- some pimiento peppers
- fat for frying
- small mangaritos, peeled
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Cut a young and new duck into pieces and soak with orange juice, salt, parsley, chopped onion, and some pimiento peppers
2. After three hours, fry in plenty of fat along with a portion of small peeled mangaritos
3. Fry until of good color, then increase the fire and serve, sprinkling on top with some finely chopped parsley

title: Marreco Guisado com Gerumbeba (Braised Duck with Gerumbeba Fruit)
body:
## Ingredients

- gerumbeba fruits (fruits of the infernal fig tree)
- 1 duck
- rest of the fruits
- bacon slice
- one cup of meat broth
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- one chopped onion

## Directions

1. Take a portion of gerumbeba fruits (fruits of the infernal fig tree), place them on a coal bed, roast and peel them
2. Fill the duck with these fruits and place it in a pan with the remaining fruits on a slice of bacon
3. Add one cup of meat broth, salt, pepper, parsley, and one chopped onion
4. Let it boil over low fire until the meat is cooked
5. Serve with its own sauce, strained through a sieve or coarse cloth

title: Marreco Guisado com Quiabos (Braised Duck with Okra)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 duck, cut into pieces
- water
- salt
- onion
- parsley
- celery root
- two tablespoons butter
- one tablespoon wheat flour
- strained broth
- okra cut into rounds
- fried duck pieces
- juice of one lemon
- slices of another lemon, peeled

## Directions

1. Cut the duck into pieces and boil them in water with salt, onion, parsley, and celery root
2. When almost cooked, remove them and fry them in two tablespoons of butter, turning them until of good color
3. Remove them again and add one tablespoon of wheat flour to the same fat
4. Stir the strained broth in which the duck was cooked until reduced to four cups
5. Add a portion of okra cut into rounds, the fried duck pieces, the juice of one lemon, and slices of another peeled lemon
6. Serve at the table

title: Marreco refogado com azeitonas (Duck Braised with Olives)
body:
## Ingredients

- one duck
- one quarter of bacon, cut in small pieces
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onion leaves
- one cup of water or broth
- a portion of pitted and blanched olives

## Directions

1. Fry one duck with a quarter of bacon cut in small pieces, turning it until it takes a good color evenly
2. Add salt, pepper, parsley, onion leaves, one cup of water or broth, and finally a portion of pitted and blanched olives
3. Let it boil over low fire until the meat is cooked and serve

title: Marreco refogado com repolho (Duck Braised with Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cabbage, finely chopped
- water
- salt
- half a pound of bacon, chopped
- one duck
- sweet potatoes, peeled

## Directions

1. Boil finely chopped cabbage for a few minutes in water and salt
2. Remove from water and squeeze it to have as little water as possible
3. Place it in the pan with half a pound of chopped bacon and the duck filled with peeled sweet potatoes
4. When cooked, serve the cabbage on a platter and the duck on top

title: Massa Folhada para Empadas (Puff Pastry for Pies)
body:
## Ingredients

- pie dough
- butter

## Directions

1. Take the dough above, after being well kneaded, extend it on a board
2. Spread butter on top, roll it up and extend it again, buttering it once more
3. Repeat this operation eight to twelve times and make the crust for the pie in the manner already explained

title: Massa para Pasteis (Pastry Dough)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of wheat flour
- four egg yolks
- half a quarter of butter or fat
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Knead one pound of wheat flour with four egg yolks, half a quarter of butter or fat, and a little salt
2. After kneading well, extend over a board, spreading it with wheat flour so the dough does not stick to the board
3. Cut this dough with the rim of a glass to make round pieces and fill according to the taste of the pastries desired
4. If desired, brown them by coating with beaten egg yolk using a feather, and bake in the oven

title: Massas para Empadas (Pie Dough for Pies)
body:
## Ingredients

- four spoonfuls of wheat flour
- one spoonful of butter
- hot water
- two eggs
- salt

## Directions

1. Knead four spoonfuls of wheat flour with one spoonful of butter, hot water, two eggs, and salt
2. Knead everything until it has regular consistency
3. Extend half of it on a plate, with the edges of the dough being half an inch larger than the plate
4. With the other half extended, cover the filling and the dough of which the pie was made
5. Fold the edges of the lower paste over the upper layer and bake in the oven

title: Mate (Mate)
body:
## Ingredients

- mate
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take a portion of mate and place it in a bowl
2. Pour water over it and stir
3. Give each guest the bowl with the proper metal straw, which is inserted into the liquid
4. Each guest drinks as they wish
5. Always present a sugar bowl when serving mate, as some prefer it with sugar and others without

title: Maçãs com Arroz (Apples with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- seven apples
- marmalade or other preserves
- gelée
- rice prepared for rice pudding
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Peel seven apples and remove the core with a spoon.
2. Cook them in a syrup with half the juice of a lemon.
3. When the apples are cooked, drain them.
4. Spread a layer of marmalade or other preserves on a dish.
5. Place the apples on top, fill the cavity of each with gelée and cover with rice prepared as for rice pudding and serve.

title: Maçãs com Arroz (Apples with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- seven apples
- rice
- syrup
- juice of half a lemon

## Directions

1. Take seven apples, peel them and remove the core with a spoon
2. Let them cook in a pan in a syrup with half the juice of one lemon
3. When the apples

title: Meat Extract for Sauce (Extracto de Carne para Môlho)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- half a pound of bacon slices
- two pounds of beef, cut into small pieces
- beef broth
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- bouquet of herbs
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Line a casserole of considerable capacity with butter; cover the bottom with half a pound of slices of bacon and two pounds of beef, cut into small pieces, placed over a lively fire, and left to fry for a while with the casserole well covered, avoiding however that the bacon does not burn; add from time to time one spoonful of beef broth, then adding two bottles of beef broth, salt, pepper, cloves, a bouquet of herbs and some mushrooms; let everything cook over a moderate fire for three to four hours; remove the fat and strain through a cloth.

title: Mexilhão e Bribigão Cozido (Boiled Mussels and Cockles)
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- bread crumbs
- fat
- water
- parsley
- onion leaves
- salt
- pepper
- mussel meat

## Directions

1. Sauté a portion of mushrooms and a handful of bread crumbs in one tablespoon of fat
2. Add one cup of water, a little parsley, onion leaves, salt and pepper
3. Boil and add the mussel meat
4. Once heated, remove from fire and serve

title: Mexilhões Ensopados (Mussels in Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- mussels
- butter
- parsley
- onion
- wheat flour
- pepper
- egg yolks
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Wash and clean the mussels with a hard brush
2. Open them and remove the water by passing through a strainer
3. Reunite the meat in one shell and place in a pan with a little butter, parsley and chopped onion
4. When the butter is melted, add the water from the mussels, dust with half a tablespoon of wheat flour and a little pepper
5. Heat without letting the water boil
6. When serving, add three egg yolks diluted in one glass of lemon juice and send to table

title: Mexilhões Guisados (Stewed Mussels)
body:
## Ingredients

- carapicús
- onion
- parsley
- garlic
- olive oil
- white wine
- pepper
- nutmeg
- mussels

## Directions

1. Chop finely a handful of carapicús, one onion, a little parsley and one garlic clove
2. Put in a pot with two or three tablespoons of olive oil, sauté and add one cup of white wine, pepper and grated nutmeg
3. Boil a little
4. After the mussels have boiled in their own water, place the chopped mixture on a plate with the mussels in their shells and serve

title: Mexilhões Refogados (Sautéed Mussels)
body:
## Ingredients

- mussels
- fat
- pepper

## Directions

1. Open the mussels and remove one shell
2. Reunite the meat from one to the other
3. Add a little fat and pepper to each
4. Place in a pan, heat until they come to a boil and serve

title: Minced Mutton, Fried (Carne de carneiro picada e frita)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of mutton, sinews and skin removed
- one pound of pork with some fat
- two onions
- pepper
- salt
- cloves
- parsley
- one handful of powder of good polvilho biscuits
- one spoon of polvilho
- two eggs (yolk and white)
- one glass of wine
- some fat for frying

## Directions

1. Mince the mutton with the pork.
2. Melt a little fat in a pan and when well heated, add the minced meat.
3. Fry on one side, then turn to fry on the other side.
4. Serve with a spicy sauce.

title: Mingão da Uberaba (Uberaba Corn Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- fat
- salt
- milled cornmeal or fine cornmeal

## Directions

1. Make the same way as Ceará soup, but instead of wheat flour, use milled cornmeal or fine cornmeal

title: Mingão de Itabira (Itabira Corn Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- a good portion of butter
- one bowl of cornmeal
- water
- a little sugar
- cinnamon
- anise

## Directions

1. Fry a good portion of butter with one bowl of cornmeal
2. When it turns cinnamon color, add water and boil for one hour with a little sugar, cinnamon, and anise
3. Finally add one more bowl of milk

title: Mingão de Paulista (Paulista Corn Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- salt
- one spoonful of fat
- four spoonfuls of fine cornmeal
- a little cold water
- one parsley leaf

## Directions

1. Put a pot with water, salt, and one spoonful of fat on the fire
2. When boiling, gradually add four spoonfuls of fine cornmeal dissolved in a little cold water and one parsley leaf, stirring constantly
3. Let cook for a little more time and remove the pot from the fire

title: Mingão do Ceará (Ceará Corn Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- a little fat
- salt
- wheat flour

## Directions

1. Put water on the fire with a little fat and salt
2. As soon as it boils, add wheat flour dissolved in cold water, stirring until it finishes cooking

title: Mingão para purificar o sangue (Pottage to Purify the Blood)
body:
## Ingredients

- yam root
- whey
- watercress
- lettuce
- sugar
- bitter almonds
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Peel, wash and cut a yam root into small pieces
2. Cook it in water for two hours
3. Remove and reduce to a paste
4. Put a portion of milk whey on the fire with watercress and lettuce
5. Let this mixture boil for half an hour
6. Strain through thick cloth
7. Return to the fire
8. Mix in the yam paste, sugar, some crushed bitter almonds and a little nutmeg
9. Let it boil for one more hour
10. Serve

title: Mingão à Mineira (Minas-Style Cornmeal Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 6 tablespoons cornmeal
- 10 or 12 cups water
- salt
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 2 tablespoons rich milk

## Directions

1. Put cornmeal in a pot over the fire with water and a little salt
2. Let boil until the contents are cooked, stirring constantly before removing from heat
3. Add a tablespoon of sugar and two tablespoons of rich milk
4. Serve

title: Miolo de Vacca (Beef Brain)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef brain
- hot water
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- bay leaves
- garlic
- parsley
- celery root
- carrots

## Directions

1. First bring the brain to hot water and after cleaning of blood and the membrane that covers it, place for one or two hours in cold water
2. Cook in this same water with a small cup of vinegar, salt, pepper, cloves, bay leaves, garlic, parsley, celery root and carrots
3. Three-quarters of an hour are sufficient for it to be cooked
4. Serve with any sauce indicated for meats

title: Miolo de Vacca (Calf Brain)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf brain
- hot water
- cold water
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- bay leaves
- garlic
- parsley
- celery root
- carrots

## Directions

1. Bring the calf brain to hot water, then clean it of blood and the membrane that covers it.
2. Soak it in cold water for one or two hours.
3. Cook it in this same water with one cup of vinegar, salt, pepper, cloves, bay leaves, garlic, parsley, celery root and carrots.
4. Three quarters of an hour is sufficient for it to be cooked.
5. Serve with any sauce indicated for meats.

title: Miolo de Vacca Ensopado (Braised Calf Brain with Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf brain
- water
- salt
- mushrooms
- butter
- small onions
- wheat flour
- mushroom broth

## Directions

1. Boil the brain in water and salt.
2. Cook the mushrooms separately, cut into pieces in little water and one spoon of butter.
3. Then fry in butter a dozen small onions until they are golden.
4. Sprinkle over them one spoon of wheat flour, moistening them with one glass of the mushroom broth.
5. Add the brain cut into slices, leave it to boil for half an hour over coals, and serve.

title: Miolo de Vacca Frito (Fried Calf Brain)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf brain
- water
- salt
- wheat flour
- pepper
- mustard sauce
- butter

## Directions

1. Let the brain cool after being cooked in water and salt.
2. Cut it into slices that should be covered with wheat flour, pepper, salt and mustard sauce.
3. Fry it in butter on both sides.

title: Miolo de Vitella Assados (Roasted Veal Brains)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal brains
- water
- salt
- grated cheese
- sugar
- cinnamon
- cloves

## Directions

1. Boil the veal brains for half an hour in water and salt
2. After cleaning, let cool and slice onto a plate with grated cheese on the bottom
3. Add another layer of cheese on top, then sugar, cinnamon, and ground cloves
4. Cover the plate and place over coals with fire on top
5. Let roast until they take on a good color and serve

title: Miolo de Vitella Estufado (Braised Veal Brains)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal brains
- manioc flour
- butter
- wheat flour
- sugar
- one glass Madeira wine
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Scald the veal brains and cut into slices
2. Coat in manioc flour and fry in butter
3. In a separate pan, fry a little wheat flour in one tablespoon butter and another of sugar until the flour becomes golden
4. Add one glass Madeira wine
5. After boiling briefly, add the brains to this sauce after ten to twelve minutes
6. Serve with lemon juice and one tablespoon sugar

title: Miolo de Vitella Frio (Cold Veal Brains)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal brains
- water
- one tablespoon wheat flour
- one tablespoon butter
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Clean the veal brains as with beef brains
2. Cut into slices and cook for half an hour with one tablespoon wheat flour, one tablespoon butter, salt, nutmeg, and a little water
3. Make a sauce by melting two tablespoons butter and adding two tablespoons wheat flour, a little salt, parsley, pepper, and lemon juice
4. Serve with this sauce on top

title: Miolo de Vitella Guisado (Stewed Veal Brains)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal brains
- water
- butter
- one onion
- salt
- parsley
- one tablespoon wheat flour
- one glass wine
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Wash and scald the brains, then cut into slices
2. Fry in butter with one onion, salt, parsley, and one tablespoon wheat flour
3. Add one glass wine and peeled lemon slices without seeds
4. After boiling briefly, serve

title: Miolos de Carneiro com Ovos (Lamb Brains with Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb brains
- hot water
- four cooked eggs
- half a pound of ham
- two onions
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- two spoonfuls of fine cornmeal
- butter
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Scald the brains in hot water; after cooling, chop them with four cooked eggs, half a pound of ham, two onions, a little parsley, salt, pepper and two spoonfuls of fine cornmeal
2. Fry a little butter in a casserole; when hot, add the chopped mixture and when fried, turn it to fry on the other side
3. Cover with grated cheese and serve

title: Miolos de Carneiro Dourados (Gilded Lamb Brains)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb brains
- hot water
- two spoonfuls of butter
- one onion
- chopped parsley
- salt
- ground pepper
- grated cheese
- slices of bread
- eight beaten eggs
- sugar
- ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Scald the brains in hot water; after chopping, fry them in two spoonfuls of butter, one onion, chopped parsley, salt and ground pepper with an equal amount of grated cheese
2. Pour this fried mixture on the plate over slices of bread fried in butter, cover with grated bread and finally with eight beaten eggs
3. Place the plate in the oven to toast
4. Serve covered with sugar and ground cinnamon

title: Miolos de Vitella Fritos (Fried Veal Brains)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal brains
- dried breadcrumbs or crackers
- salt
- pepper
- onion leaves
- butter

## Directions

1. After cleaning and cooking the brains as described for cold brains
2. Coat in a layer of ground dried breadcrumbs or crackers, salt, pepper, and chopped onion leaves
3. Fry in butter over moderate heat

title: Miudos de Carneiro Cozidos (Boiled Lamb Offal)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb lungs
- heart
- liver
- tongue
- feet
- hot water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- peppers
- one spoonful of butter
- one spoonful of wheat flour
- one glass of white wine

## Directions

1. After cleaning the lungs, heart, liver, tongue and feet of the lamb, scald them in hot water and then cook them in water with salt, parsley, onion leaves and some peppers
2. When they are cooked, remove the bones from the feet, drain the broth which is thickened (reducing it first if necessary) with one spoonful of butter kneaded with another of wheat flour and one glass of white wine
3. Return the offal to this sauce

title: Miudos de Carneiro Cozidos (Stewed Lamb Offal)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb lungs
- lamb heart
- lamb liver
- lamb tongue
- lamb feet
- hot water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- peppers
- butter
- wheat flour
- white wine
- cornmeal (fubá) or cassava flour (farinha de mandioca) for serving

## Directions

1. After cleaning the lungs, heart, liver, tongue and feet of the lamb, scald them in hot water
2. Cook them then in water with salt, parsley, onion leaves and some peppers
3. When cooked, remove the bones from the feet and drain the broth
4. Thicken the broth (reducing it first if necessary) with one spoonful of butter kneaded with another of wheat flour and one glass of white wine
5. Return the offal to this sauce and let it boil a little
6. Serve with cornmeal porridge (angú de fubá de moinho)

title: Miudos de Carneiro Fritos (Fried Lamb Offal)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb lungs
- lamb liver
- lamb heart
- lamb kidneys
- lamb feet
- two onions
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- two cloves
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- eggs

## Directions

1. Clean and cook the lungs, liver, heart, kidneys and feet, removing the bones
2. Chop everything together with two onions, some parsley, salt, pepper and two cloves
3. Fry with two spoonfuls of butter
4. Place on a plate, cover with breadcrumbs and beaten eggs
5. Heat in the oven and serve in this arrangement

title: Miudos de Carneiro Refogados (Braised Lamb Offal)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb lungs
- liver
- heart
- kidneys
- feet
- water
- salt
- two spoonfuls of butter
- two spoonfuls of wheat flour
- one spoonful of sugar
- one onion
- two cups of red wine
- meat broth
- one dozen okras
- cornmeal mush or tapioca flour

## Directions

1. Take the lungs, liver, heart, kidneys and feet of the lamb, wash and clean everything and scald in hot water
2. Then cook in water and salt; when cooked, remove the bones from the feet and cut all the meat into small pieces
3. On the other hand, fry two spoonfuls of butter with two of wheat flour, one spoonful of sugar and one cut onion until they become dark copper color
4. Add two cups of red wine, the same amount of broth and one dozen okras cut into rounds; add the cooked offal and boil everything for half an hour over coals
5. Serve with cornmeal mush or tapioca flour porridge

title: Miudos de Vitella com Quiabos (Veal Giblets with Okra)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal tongue
- veal heart
- veal lungs
- veal liver
- water
- salt
- onions
- parsley
- chili pepper
- okra
- one glass wine
- lemon slices
- cornmeal mush for serving

## Directions

1. Cut the tongue and heart into slices
2. Chop the lungs into small pieces and pass the liver through a sieve
3. Place all in a pot with water, salt, sliced onions, parsley, and chili pepper
4. When everything is cooked and the water has almost dried up, add a dozen okra, one glass wine, and lemon slices
5. Give it another boil and serve with cornmeal mush

title: Mixed-Pickles (Mixed Pickles)
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- cauliflower
- carrots
- scallions
- young cucumbers
- chuchús
- palmetto
- corn ear tips
- guandú
- mangalos
- asparagus tips
- samambaia
- beans
- mustard seeds
- peppers of all kinds
- salt
- vinegar
- cloves
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Place in a pan a portion of peas, cauliflower, sliced carrots, scallions, young cucumbers, chuchús, chopped palmetto, tips of green corn ears, guandú, mangalos, asparagus tips, samambaia, beans, mustard seeds, peppers of all kinds, salt, vinegar, cloves, nutmeg
2. Boil for a quarter hour, remove from heat and let cool
3. The next day, heat again and while still hot, place in wide-mouthed jars, the vegetables should be covered with vinegar and covered, keep stored

title: Mocotó com Arroz (Beef Knuckle with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef hand or foot, cleaned
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- pepper
- rice

## Directions

1. Clean the beef hand or foot well and cut into pieces
2. Cook with salt, parsley, onion leaves, and a little pepper
3. When almost cooked, remove and strain the broth
4. Cook the rice in this broth
5. When the rice is almost dry, add the mocotó and serve

title: Mocotó de Porco Refogado (Braised Pork Trotters)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork trotters
- Pork fat
- Wheat flour
- Parsley leaves
- Onions
- Pork trotter broth
- Salt

## Directions

1. Boil the trotters until almost cooked, let them cool and remove the bones
2. In another pan, fry a little pork fat with wheat flour, parsley leaves, onions, pork trotter broth and a little salt
3. After it has boiled once, add the pork trotter meat to this sauce, letting it boil a bit more, and serve

title: Mocotó de Vacca (Beef Knuckle Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef hand or foot
- hot water
- bacon skins
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- bay leaf
- mustard
- vinegar or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Scald a beef hand or foot in hot water
2. After scraping and removing the hooves, cut into pieces at the joints
3. Place on the fire with some bacon skins, salt, pepper, parsley, and bay leaf
4. Boil vigorously for six hours
5. Remove the mocotó pieces and bacon skins, letting the broth thicken
6. When almost ready, add one spoonful of mustard and a little vinegar or lemon juice
7. Serve

title: Modo de Fazer Café com Leite (Method of Making Coffee with Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- coffee powder

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of milk, and when boiling, add the coffee powder to it, stir, strain through a cloth bag and serve; it is also used to place the strained coffee in one pot and the milk in another, so that each person can use it according to their taste.

title: Modo de Fazer Café à Moda Turca (Method of Making Coffee in the Turkish Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- powdered coffee
- water
- cold water

## Directions

1. Many use to prepare coffee in the Turkish style, which consists of boiling powdered coffee with water, and after it has boiled, adding a few drops of cold water, which precipitates the powder, and the liquid becomes as if it had been strained; this method is preferred in some countries.

title: Modo de Fazer o Café (Method of Making Coffee)
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- coffee

## Directions

1. Place a vessel with water over the fire to boil, and when boiling, place the coffee in a cloth strainer and pour boiling water over it; save the coffee that drips and serve.

title: Modo de Guisar as Sardinhas de Nantes (How to Cook Nantes Sardines)
body:
## Ingredients

- preserved sardines or fish in sweet oil
- vinegar
- mustard powder

## Directions

1. The fish may be eaten cold by adding a little vinegar and mustard powder
2. Alternatively, heat them with the oil, and when hot add a little vinegar and serve

title: Modo de Guisar as Sardinhas de Nantes ou Qualquer Outro Peixe Conservado em Azeite Doce (How to Prepare Nantes Sardines or Any Other Fish Preserved in Sweet Oil)
body:
## Ingredients

- Nantes sardines or preserved fish
- Vinegar
- Mustard powder

## Directions

1. They can be eaten cold by adding some vinegar and mustard powder
2. They can also be heated

title: Modo de Tirar o Mau Cheiro à Carne (Method of Removing Bad Smell from Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat with bad smell
- live coals without smoke

## Directions

1. Place the meat with bad smell in a pan; when it boils, remove the scum and place in the pan a portion of live coals without smoke; let it boil for five minutes and then remove the meat with a skimmer, after which it can be prepared as desired.

title: Modo de Torrar Café à Mineira (Method of Roasting Coffee in the Minas Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- washed and dry coffee
- brown sugar or refined sugar
- bread

## Directions

1. Place the washed and dry coffee in a small pan proper for roasting coffee; roast over a lively fire, stirring with a bread spoon until it is of suitable color; remove then from the fire and place in a coarse sieve to let the smoke out; put in the same pan one pound of brown sugar or refined sugar for four pounds of coffee; add the roasted coffee, stir well and let cool, then crush or grind the coffee and save it for daily use.

title: Modo de Torrar o Café (Method of Roasting Coffee)
body:
## Ingredients

- coffee
- sugar

## Directions

1. Wash the coffee well and dry it in the sun; the coffee should always be washed because many dye coffee with lead and other harmful substances; place in an iron box with a small door, secured by a handle, and place this over the fire until the coffee has taken on a suitable color, and keep it after grinding; in many places a spoon of sugar is added to each pound of coffee after it is ready.

title: Mogango ou Moranga Assado (Roasted Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- squash
- water
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Take a squash, cut it into eight or more pieces
2. Remove the seeds and cook in water
3. When the pieces are cooked, place them on a plate, cover them with breadcrumbs and place the plate over the oven
4. Let them roast and serve

title: Mogango ou Moranga Cheios (Stuffed Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe squash
- water
- beef
- salt
- garlic
- peppers
- vinegar
- lard
- olives
- sausage

## Directions

1. Take a ripe, dry squash
2. Open a hole at the bottom so the seeds come out through there
3. Cook it whole in water until very well cooked
4. Separately, chop with a knife a quantity of beef
5. Season with salt, garlic, peppers and vinegar
6. Sauté in very hot lard
7. Add some olives and pieces of sausage and let them cook
8. When cooked, fill the squash with this mixture and pieces of sausage
9. Line a frying pan with plenty of lard, place the filled squash in it and roast in the oven
10. When roasted, serve

title: Molho Allemão para Peixes (German Sauce for Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 cups of meat sauce stock
- 4 egg yolks
- a little nutmeg
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Take 8 cups of meat sauce stock
2. Mix with 4 egg yolks and a little nutmeg
3. Let it boil for fifteen minutes
4. If desired, add a little vinegar and serve, mainly for fish

title: Molho Amarello de Limão (Yellow Lemon Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- wheat flour
- egg yolks
- lemon rind
- a little sugar
- ground cinnamon
- nutmeg
- white wine

## Directions

1. Knead in the same proportion butter, wheat flour, egg yolks, and lemon rind, as in lemon sauce
2. Add a little sugar, ground cinnamon and nutmeg, and as much white wine as is necessary for the sauce
3. Put on the fire and leave to boil a little, stirring always
4. Serve for the same dishes as lemon sauce, and also for puddings and pasta

title: Molho Amarello de Ovos (Yellow Egg Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- a little butter
- half a spoon of flour
- six egg yolks
- a little nutmeg
- salt
- one spoon of vinegar
- one spoon of water

## Directions

1. Mix a little butter with half a spoon of flour and six egg yolks, a little nutmeg and salt
2. Add one spoon of vinegar and another spoon of water, or as much as is necessary
3. Leave to boil and serve with fish, artichokes, and little potatoes

title: Molho Amarelo de Limão (Yellow Lemon Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Wheat flour
- Egg yolks
- Lemon zest
- Sugar
- Ground cinnamon
- Nutmeg
- White wine

## Directions

1. Mix butter and wheat flour in equal proportions with egg yolks and lemon zest as in lemon sauce
2. Add a little sugar, ground cinnamon and nutmeg, and as much white wine as necessary for the sauce
3. Place over fire and let it boil a little, stirring constantly
4. Serve with the same dishes as lemon sauce, and also for puddings and pastas

title: Molho Apimentado (Peppered Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- a little wheat flour
- butter
- some onions
- chives
- bay leaf
- thyme
- parsley
- pepper
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Fry a little wheat flour with butter until it takes on a reddish-brown color
2. Remove from fire and add finely chopped onions, chives, bay leaf, thyme, parsley and pepper
3. Add several cups of beef broth
4. Let boil for fifteen minutes
5. Strain through a fine sieve and serve

title: Molho Bechamel (Bechamel Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoon of fat
- one spoon of tapioca starch
- salt
- pepper
- half a bottle of milk, cooked

## Directions

1. Fry one spoon of tapioca starch in one spoon of fat
2. Immediately add salt, pepper, and gradually half a bottle of cooked milk
3. Let boil a little and serve

title: Molho Bechamel (Béchamel Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 spoon of starch
- 1 spoon of fat
- salt
- pepper
- half a bottle of milk, already cooked

## Directions

1. Fry the starch in a spoon of fat
2. Add salt, pepper, and little by little half a bottle of already cooked milk
3. Let it boil for a little while and serve

title: Molho Bechamel Gordo (Rich Béchamel Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of bacon
- some carrots
- two onions
- some cups of meat sauce stock
- pepper
- nutmeg
- peppercorn from India
- bay leaves
- parsley
- salt
- 3 spoons of wheat flour
- 1 spoon of fat
- 1 cup of cream

## Directions

1. Fry some carrots and two onions with a piece of bacon
2. Add some cups of meat sauce stock, pepper, nutmeg, peppercorn from India, bay leaves, parsley and salt
3. Let it boil for an hour, strain it
4. Fry 3 spoons of wheat flour in a spoon of fat and add to the sauce
5. Add 1 cup of cream, let it boil a little more, and serve

title: Molho Branco (White Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of fat
- veal meat
- a piece of sausage
- 2 cups of meat broth
- 1 glass of wine
- 1 clove of garlic
- salt
- cloves
- pepper
- the crumb of a loaf of bread
- 3 cooked egg yolks

## Directions

1. Fry the veal meat and a piece of sausage in a piece of fat for half an hour
2. Add 2 cups of meat broth, 1 glass of wine, 1 clove of garlic, salt, cloves, and pepper
3. Add the crumb of a loaf of bread and 3 cooked and mashed egg yolks
4. Let it boil for two more hours, strain it and serve

title: Molho da Tartaria (Tartar Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- marjoram
- chives
- thyme
- salt
- French mustard
- pepper
- vinegar
- sweet oil

## Directions

1. Finely chop marjoram, chives and thyme
2. Add salt, French mustard, pepper and vinegar
3. Gradually add sweet oil, stirring constantly
4. If the sauce becomes thick, add a little more vinegar
5. If it is too salty, add more oil and mustard
6. Serve cold with cold roasted meats or cooked fish

title: Molho de Alcaparras (Caper Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 spoon of fat or cow butter
- 1 spoon of cornmeal
- 1 cup of broth or water
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 spoon of vinegar
- nutmeg
- some capers

## Directions

1. Heat a spoon of fat or cow butter in a saucepan
2. When melted, add 1 spoon of cornmeal and stir
3. Little by little add 1 cup of broth or water
4. After boiling, remove from heat and add the egg yolk dissolved in 1 spoon of vinegar
5. Add nutmeg and some capers
6. Let it boil once more and serve with meat or fish

title: Molho de Ameixas (Plum Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Plums
- Cornstarch
- Cinnamon
- Lemon zest
- Water
- White or red wine
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Pass a portion of plums through a sieve, removing the pits
2. Add a spoonful of cornstarch, cinnamon and lemon zest
3. Let it boil for one hour with little water
4. Strain, squeezing it
5. Add to the juice an equal amount of white or red wine and a little sugar
6. Let it boil for a little while
7. Serve with all puddings and pastas of all kinds

title: Molho de Ameixas (Plum Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- a portion of plums
- one spoon of cornstarch
- cinnamon
- lemon rind

## Directions

1. Pass a portion of plums through a sieve and remove the pits
2. Add one spoon of cornstarch, cinnamon and lemon rind
3. Leave to boil one hour with little water

title: Molho de Bom Gosto (Sauce of Good Taste)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Eggs with whites and yolks
- Bread of three ounces
- Milk
- Vanilla
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Beat a spoonful of butter with two eggs with whites and two more yolks
2. Mix with three ounces of bread, soaked in milk
3. Add necessary milk, a little vanilla and sugar
4. Let it boil for a little while
5. Pass through a sieve
6. Serve with any pudding

title: Molho de Camarão (Shrimp Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- some shrimp
- 1 spoon of fat or butter
- some spoons of meat sauce stock
- nutmeg
- pepper from India
