- a little starch
- a little vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook some shrimp
2. After cooked, cut them and mix with 1 spoon of fat or butter
3. Fry a little
4. Add some spoons of meat sauce stock, nutmeg and pepper from India
5. Let it boil a little and strain
6. If desired, thicken the sauce with a little starch and a little vinegar, and serve

title: Molho de Chefe de Cozinha (Chef's Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh butter
- finely chopped parsley
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- chopped chives
- juice of one lemon
- juice of green grapes
- juice of tomatoes
- or a little vinegar

## Directions

1. Place a little fresh butter on a plate with finely chopped parsley, salt, pepper, and nutmeg
2. Add chopped chives if desired
3. Add the juice of one lemon, juice of green grapes or tomatoes, or a little vinegar

title: Molho de Chocolate (Chocolate Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Egg yolks
- Chocolate powder
- White wine
- Sugar
- Lemon zest
- Ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Let a little butter melt
2. After it is almost melted, add three egg yolks, two spoonfuls of chocolate powder, and as much white wine as is necessary for the required quantity of sauce
3. Add a little sugar, lemon zest and ground cinnamon
4. Boil everything, stirring constantly, over coals
5. Serve for puddings and pastas

title: Molho de Florença (Florentine Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bowl
- four egg yolks
- salt
- pepper powder
- one chalice of sweet olive oil

## Directions

1. Place a bowl inside a pot filled with boiling water
2. Put four egg yolks with salt and pepper powder in the bowl
3. Add little by little one chalice of sweet olive oil, stirring strongly
4. Remove the bowl and serve with all kinds of fish
5. Take great care not to apply too much heat, because the egg yolks will curdle and the sauce will be ruined

title: Molho de Grumixama (Grumixama Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Grumixamas
- Cinnamon
- Lemon zest
- Sugar
- Cornstarch

## Directions

1. Pound and squeeze a portion of grumixamas
2. Boil the juice for half an hour with cinnamon, lemon zest, sugar and a little cornstarch
3. Serve for puddings and pancakes

title: Molho de Manteiga Preta (Black Butter Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- parsley
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Place butter in a skillet and heat until it turns black, without however burning
2. At this point, add whole parsley to fry and pour over the fish
3. Pour vinegar in the skillet, which will finish cooking, and as soon as it is hot, pour it over the fish and serve at once

title: Molho de Miudos (Offal Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- onion slices
- pieces of beef knuckle
- pork rind pieces
- chopped carrots
- garlic
- cloves
- water

## Directions

1. Cover the bottom of a pot with onion slices, pieces of beef knuckle, pork rind pieces, chopped carrots, garlic and cloves
2. Add a little water
3. Keep near the fire, well covered and over high heat until well cooked
4. Add more water
5. Remove the fat, skim and strain through a cloth

title: Molho de Nata (Cream Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Wheat flour or cornmeal
- Milk
- Parsley
- Nutmeg
- Sugar
- Salt
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Fry a little butter with wheat flour or cornmeal and four spoonfuls of rich milk
2. Add a little chopped parsley, nutmeg, a little sugar and salt
3. Boil and thicken with two egg yolks
4. Serve with puddings and pastries

title: Molho de Nata (Cream Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 spoons of fat
- 1 spoon of cornmeal
- a bunch of parsley
- chopped chives
- salt
- pepper from India
- a little nutmeg
- 1 cup of milk or cream

## Directions

1. Pour 2 spoons of fat in a pan
2. Add 1 spoon of cornmeal, a bunch of parsley, chopped chives, salt, pepper from India and a little nutmeg
3. Add 1 cup of milk or cream
4. Let it boil for fifteen minutes and serve for potatoes or cod

title: Molho de Nata Aromático (Aromatic Cream Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Rich milk
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Lemon zest
- Vanilla
- Salt
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Let some cups of rich milk boil with sugar, cinnamon, lemon zest, a little vanilla and a tiny bit of salt
2. Finally, thicken with two to three egg yolks

title: Molho de Ostras ou Mariscos (Oyster or Shellfish Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters or shellfish
- white sauce
- juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Boil oysters or shellfish, adding the water contained in their shells
2. They cook with low heat
3. After cooked, let them drain
4. Pour white sauce on top with the juice of 1 lemon
5. This sauce serves to put over roasted and cooked fish

title: Molho de Pimenta Allemão (German Pepper Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- a little cow butter
- 2 spoons of starch
- 1 chopped onion
- 2 bell peppers
- salt
- some cups of meat broth

## Directions

1. Heat a little cow butter in a pan
2. Add 2 spoons of starch, 1 chopped onion, and 2 bell peppers with salt
3. Stir and add some cups of meat broth
4. Let it boil for half an hour more and serve with fish and cooked meat

title: Molho de Pobre (Poor Man's Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- some onions
- parsley
- a little water or beef broth
- a little cornmeal
- one spoon of vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop onions and parsley finely
2. Place in a pot with a little water or beef broth, a little cornmeal, and one spoon of vinegar
3. Let boil until the onion is cooked
4. Use this sauce to warm up cold meats

title: Molho de Rabão (Radish Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- radish root
- one spoon of tapioca starch
- one spoon of vinegar
- a little salt
- sugar
- six cups of water

## Directions

1. Grate the radish root
2. Add one spoon of tapioca starch, one spoon of vinegar, a little salt, sugar, and six cups of water
3. Cook over coals until it begins to boil
4. Serve with cooked meats

title: Molho de Salsa (Parsley Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- wheat flour
- 1 spoon of fat
- meat broth
- finely chopped parsley leaves
- a little vinegar
- 1 spoon of spirits in which preserved peppercorn is kept

## Directions

1. Heat wheat flour and 1 spoon of fat in a saucepan until the flour takes on color
2. Add meat broth and finely chopped parsley leaves
3. Add a little vinegar and 1 spoon of spirits in which preserved peppercorn is kept
4. This sauce serves both for meat and for fish

title: Molho de Sardinhas (Sardine Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one dozen sardines in barrel
- six to eight chalices of broth
- pepper
- nutmeg
- parsley
- bay leaf
- a little salt
- vinegar
- lemon juice
- sour orange juice

## Directions

1. After well washing one dozen sardines in barrel and removing the bones, put them in a pot with six to eight chalices of broth, pepper, nutmeg, parsley, bay leaf, and a little salt
2. Leave to boil for one hour over moderate fire
3. Serve with vinegar, lemon juice, or sour orange juice, for game meats

title: Molho de Tomates (Tomato Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- ten or twenty tomatoes
- pepper
- one clove of garlic
- bay leaves
- thyme
- parsley
- onions
- butter
- one spoon of wheat flour

## Directions

1. Boil ten or twenty tomatoes with pepper, one clove of garlic, bay leaves, thyme, parsley and onions
2. After cooking, pass the pulp through a fine sieve
3. Place butter and one spoon of wheat flour in a pot
4. Mix in the tomato pulp
5. Let boil a little over coals and serve

title: Molho de Tubaras (Shark Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- a little sweet oil
- some sharks
- mushrooms
- half a clove of garlic
- parsley
- meat broth
- 1 glass of white wine
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Heat a little sweet oil on coals
2. Add some sharks, mushrooms, half a clove of garlic, and parsley
3. Add meat broth, 1 glass of white wine, salt and pepper
4. Let them cook, taking care to remove the fat that floats on the sauce, and serve

title: Molho de Tubaras (Tubara Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- a little sweet olive oil
- some tubaras
- mushrooms
- half a clove of garlic
- parsley
- a portion of beef broth
- a chalice of white wine
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Heat a little sweet olive oil over coals
2. Add some tubaras, mushrooms, half a clove of garlic, and parsley
3. Add a portion of beef broth and a chalice of white wine, salt, and pepper
4. Bring to cook, taking care to remove the fat that floats on the sauce
5. Serve

title: Molho de Tubaras à Italiana (Shark Sauce Italian Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 6 sharks
- 1 spoon of sweet oil
- 1 onion
- a little chopped parsley
- salt
- pepper
- cloves from India
- bay leaves
- some slices of lemon

## Directions

1. Fry 4 or 6 sharks in 1 spoon of sweet oil with 1 onion and a little chopped parsley, salt, pepper, cloves from India, bay leaves and some slices of lemon

title: Molho de Tubaras à Italiana (Tubara Sauce Italian Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- four or six tubaras
- one spoon of sweet olive oil
- one onion
- a little minced parsley
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- bay leaves
- some slices of lemon
- one chalice of white wine
- two spoons of sauce substance

## Directions

1. Fry four or six tubaras in one spoon of sweet olive oil with one onion and a little minced parsley, salt, pepper, cloves, bay leaves and some slices of lemon
2. After ten minutes, add one chalice of white wine and two spoons of sauce substance
3. Leave to boil near the fire for half an hour
4. Before serving, remove the lemon slices, parsley, onion, and finally the fat
5. Serve

title: Molho de Vinho com Nata (Wine and Cream Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Cream
- White or red wine
- Water
- Butter
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Valeriana

## Directions

1. Beat four egg yolks in a cup of cream
2. Add a glass of white or red wine and two glasses of water
3. Heat in a pot over coals until it begins to boil
4. Then add a spoonful of butter, three spoonfuls of sugar, a little cinnamon and valeriana
5. Serve with puddings and pastas

title: Molho de Vinho com Sardinhas (Wine Sauce with Sardines)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 box of Nantes sardines
- white wine
- half a bottle of meat broth
- some slices of lemon
- some cloves
- a little cow butter
- 1 chopped onion
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 cup of wine

## Directions

1. Take 1 box of Nantes sardines, remove the bones, clean them, and break them up in a cup of white wine
2. Add half a bottle of meat broth, some slices of lemon, and some cloves
3. Let it boil for half an hour
4. Add a little cow butter and 1 chopped onion
5. Let it boil a little more and strain through a sieve
6. Add 3 egg yolks dissolved in 1 cup of wine
7. Let it boil a little more and serve with fish or meat

title: Molho de Vinho Tinto (Red Wine Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Red wine
- Water
- Sugar
- Lemon zest
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Mix one bottle of red wine, half of water, two spoonfuls of sugar, lemon zest and five cold egg yolks
2. Place over coals, let it boil for fifteen minutes
3. Serve with rice pudding

title: Molho Doce de Vinho Tinto (Sweet Red Wine Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Red or white wine
- Bay leaves
- Cloves
- Cinnamon
- Sugar
- Cornstarch

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of red or white wine with some bay leaves, cloves, cinnamon and sugar
2. While boiling, add a spoonful of cornstarch dissolved in a cup of wine
3. Let it boil once more
4. Serve with pastas and puddings, or when cold with salami

title: Molho Estimulante de Tomates (Stimulating Tomato Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- two onions, chopped
- twenty or thirty tomatoes
- salt
- thyme
- bay leaves
- a little saffron
- some pimentos
- one or two spoons of beef broth
- one spoon of butter

## Directions

1. Fry two chopped onions, twenty or thirty tomatoes, salt, thyme, bay leaves, a little saffron, and some pimentos with one or two spoons of beef broth and one spoon of butter
2. After everything is cooked, pass through a sieve, pressing well to pass
3. Serve with meats and fish
4. Note: As this sauce is very stimulating, people with weak stomachs should abstain from it

title: Molho Excellente para Caças (Excellent Sauce for Game)
body:
## Ingredients

- the legs, wings and breast of game
- the rest of the game
- one spoon of sauce substance
- half a spoon of beef butter
- as much flour
- half a cup of red wine
- one small onion
- a bouquet of herbs
- two spoons of sweet olive oil
- the juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Remove the legs, wings and breast of the game, and pound the rest in a mortar
2. Add from time to time one spoon of sauce substance, passing the pulp through a sieve
3. Mix half a spoon of beef butter kneaded with as much flour, half a cup of red wine, one small onion and a bouquet of herbs which are removed afterwards
4. Leave everything to boil for half an hour, add two spoons of sweet olive oil and the juice of one lemon
5. Heat in this sauce, without boiling, the legs and breast of the game
6. Garnish the bottom of the plate with slices of bread fried in butter
7. Place the legs of the game on top, pour the sauce over and serve

title: Molho Frio de Ovos (Cold Egg Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 6 egg yolks
- 1 cup of vinegar
- 2 spoons of sweet oil
- juice of lemon
- broth of peppercorn
- a little sugar
- ground salt

## Directions

1. Mash 4 or 6 egg yolks
2. Add 1 cup of vinegar, 2 spoons of sweet oil, juice of lemon, broth of peppercorn, a little sugar and ground salt
3. Serve with cold fish and meats

title: Molho Frio para Peixes (Cold Sauce for Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- a portion of parsley
- chervil
- chives
- marjoram
- two hard-boiled egg yolks
- four spoons of sweet olive oil
- two spoons of vinegar
- two spoons of powdered mustard

## Directions

1. Blanch a portion of parsley, chervil, chives, and marjoram
2. Chop everything finely and pass through a sieve
3. Add two hard-boiled egg yolks and mix well
4. Add little by little four spoons of sweet olive oil, two spoons of vinegar and two spoons of powdered mustard
5. Put in a terrine and serve

title: Molho Genovez (Genoese Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- two spoons of cornstarch butter
- sufficient water
- parsley
- mushrooms
- chives
- one chalice of white wine

## Directions

1. Fry two spoons of cornstarch butter until they turn an amber color
2. Add sufficient water, finely minced parsley, mushrooms, and chives, and one chalice of white wine
3. Serve

title: Molho Gommado (Gummed Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- two spoons of cornstarch
- four spoons of water or beef broth
- a little nutmeg
- some parsley leaves
- onion
- one spoon of fat
- one clove of garlic
- a little sugar
- a little vinegar or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Put two spoons of cornstarch in a pot, dissolved in four spoons of water or beef broth
2. Add a little nutmeg, some parsley leaves, onion, one spoon of fat, and one clove of garlic
3. Leave to boil, stirring until it is cooked
4. Add a little sugar, a little vinegar or lemon juice
5. Serve

title: Molho Hollandez (Dutch Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- a little butter
- purified salt

## Directions

1. Put a little butter in a pot
2. After it is melted and a deposit has formed at the bottom of the pot which is very clear, pass it through a fine sieve
3. Mix in purified salt and beat lightly with a fork
4. Serve hot in a bowl. It is the true Dutch sauce, and is served with fish

title: Molho Incorporado de Vinho Tinto (Incorporated Red Wine Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Red wine
- Cloves
- Cinnamon
- Sugar
- Egg yolks
- Cornstarch

## Directions

1. Boil half a bottle of red wine with some cloves, cinnamon and sugar
2. Thicken with two egg yolks and a spoonful of cornstarch
3. Serve with puddings
4. This sauce needs only one boiling

title: Molho Indiatico de Kari (Indian Curry Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quarter of butter
- 1 spoon of powdered bell pepper (Kari)
- a little saffron
- nutmeg
- 2 spoons of wheat flour or cornmeal
- a little meat broth

## Directions

1. Heat 1 quarter of butter in a saucepan
2. Add 1 spoon of powdered bell pepper (Kari), a little saffron, nutmeg, 2 spoons of wheat flour or cornmeal, and a little meat broth
3. Let it boil for fifteen minutes over coals, strain through a fine sieve, add 1 more spoon of butter and serve

title: Molho Inglez para Caça (English Sauce for Game)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a bottle of milk
- the crumb of a loaf of bread
- twenty or thirty grains of pepper
- salt
- one spoon of beef butter

## Directions

1. Boil the crumb of a loaf of bread in half a bottle of milk
2. Pass through a sieve
3. Add twenty or thirty grains of pepper and salt
4. At the moment of serving, add one spoon of beef butter
5. Serve with feathered game

title: Molho Italiano (Italian Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- white wine
- a little parsley
- chives
- capers
- salt
- pepper
- one spoon of sweet olive oil
- one clove of garlic
- cloves
- one spoon of butter

## Directions

1. Boil with white wine a little parsley, chives, capers, all very finely minced
2. Add salt, pepper, one spoon of sweet olive oil, and if suitable, one clove of garlic and cloves
3. Leave to boil once more, remove from the fire and mix in one spoon of butter stirring until it melts
4. Serve

title: Molho Marseilles (Marseille Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- chives
- chervil
- one clove of garlic
- pepper
- vinegar
- sweet olive oil

## Directions

1. Put in a plate onion, chives, chervil, one clove of garlic, all finely minced and pepper
2. Dilute with vinegar and sweet olive oil
3. Mix a little and stir to combine

title: Molho para Feijão Branco e Vagens (Sauce for White Beans and Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- six egg yolks
- one spoon of wheat flour
- one cup of vinegar
- three cups of water
- a little butter
- salt
- a little sugar

## Directions

1. Mix six egg yolks with one spoon of wheat flour
2. Add one cup of vinegar and three cups of water
3. Add a little butter, salt and a little sugar
4. Boil and heat over coals, stirring until it boils
5. Pour over the cooked beans and serve

title: Molho para Guisados de Caça e Aves (Sauce for Stewed Game and Fowl)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoon of butter
- one spoon of wheat flour
- two chalices of meat extract
- as much red wine
- one whole onion
- a bouquet of aromatic herbs
- a little salt
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Put on the fire in a pot one spoon of butter and another of wheat flour, let it heat, and before browning add two chalices of meat extract and as much red wine, one whole onion and a bouquet of aromatic herbs (the onion and herbs are removed when serving), a little salt
2. Leave to cook half an hour, add the legs, wings and stomach of game or the legs, wings and giblets of fowl (already cooked) and let them heat in this sauce without cooking, adding lemon juice
3. Garnish the bottom of the plate with pieces of bread, on which place the game or fowl, pouring the sauce over and serve

title: Molho para Palmitos (Sauce for Hearts of Palm)
body:
## Ingredients

- a good piece of butter
- wheat flour or cornmeal
- a little nutmeg
- water from cooking the hearts of palm
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Fry a good piece of butter with wheat flour or cornmeal
2. Add a little nutmeg and as much water as needed from cooking the whole hearts of palm
3. Let boil for fifteen minutes
4. Thicken with two egg yolks
5. Pour over the whole hearts of palm and serve

title: Molho para Pastéis (Sauce for Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Cornstarch
- Vinegar
- Water
- Marmalade
- Lemon slices

## Directions

1. Fry a little butter until it takes on color
2. Add a little cornstarch, a little vinegar, water, a piece of marmalade dissolved in hot water, and some slices of lemon
3. Let it boil for one hour

title: Molho para Pastéis de Peixe (Sauce for Fish Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef broth
- Onions
- Cloves
- Bay leaves
- Herbs for seasoning
- Peppers
- Wine
- Sardines
- Capers
- Small onion
- Crackers

## Directions

1. Take four cups of good beef broth, four onions, some cloves, bay leaves, a bouquet of seasoning herbs and four to six peppers
2. Let it boil for half an hour
3. Pass through a sieve
4. Add two cups of wine, some chopped sardines, some spoonfuls of capers, a chopped small onion and four spoonfuls of crushed crackers
5. Let it boil another half hour
6. Serve

title: Molho para Pudim de Arroz (Sauce for Rice Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk
- Egg yolks
- Cinnamon
- Cornstarch
- Sugar
- Rose water or orange flower water

## Directions

1. Boil, stirring constantly, half a bottle of milk, four egg yolks, a piece of cinnamon, a small spoonful of cornstarch, a little sugar, a little rose water or orange flower water
2. Serve

title: Molho Pardo de Alcaparras com Vinho (Brown Caper Sauce with Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- corn meal or wheat flour
- red wine diluted with an equal amount of water
- chopped onions
- bay leaves
- a sprig of parsley
- marjoram
- serpol
- capers
- raisins
- lemon rinds
- Indian cloves
- salt
- sugar
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Make as brown caper sauce, but boil the ingredients in red wine diluted with an equal amount of water instead of beef broth

title: Molho Pardo à Mineira (Minas-style Brown Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken blood
- two spoons of vinegar
- two spoons of fat
- one bottle of water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- Indian cloves
- one spoon of corn meal or half spoon of polvilho
- a little sugar
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. When killing the chicken, catch the blood in a dish containing two spoons of vinegar
2. Fry the chicken cut into pieces in two spoons of fat
3. After fifteen minutes, add one bottle of water, salt, parsley, and onion leaves
4. When cooked, remove the chicken pieces from the broth
5. Add the blood and some Indian cloves, one spoon of corn meal or half spoon of polvilho, and a little sugar
6. Allow to boil for half an hour
7. If desired, thicken with two egg yolks
8. Add the chicken pieces again, bring to a boil once more, and serve

title: Molho Parisiense (Parisian Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 spoon of butter
- 3 spoons of wheat flour
- some pieces of onion
- chopped carrots
- parsley
- cooked milk
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Fry the flour in a spoon of butter
2. Add pieces of onion, chopped carrots, and parsley
3. Add cooked milk, salt, pepper, and nutmeg little by little
4. Let it boil for an hour over low heat and serve with poultry

title: Molho Picante Dourado (Golden Spicy Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- a little butter
- one spoon of polvilho
- two egg yolks
- one spoon of English mustard powder
- one cup of beef broth, wine, or vinegared water
- a little salt
- a little sugar

## Directions

1. Mix a little butter with one spoon of polvilho, two egg yolks, one spoon of English mustard powder, one cup of beef broth, wine or vinegared water as desired, a little salt, and a little sugar
2. Put in a saucepan over coals and allow to boil a little
3. Serve

title: Molho Picante Pardo (Brown Spicy Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoon of wheat flour or cornmeal
- one spoon of fat or butter
- one onion, chopped
- one spoon of mustard powder
- some slices of lemon
- salt
- beef broth as needed
- a little vinegar
- sugar
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Fry one spoon of wheat flour or cornmeal in one spoon of fat or butter until browned
2. Add one chopped onion, one spoon of mustard powder, some slices of lemon, salt and beef broth as needed
3. Add a little vinegar, sugar and lemon juice
4. Serve

title: Molho Picante Pardo (Brown Spicy Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoon of wheat flour or corn meal
- one spoon of fat or cow's butter
- one chopped onion
- one spoon of mustard powder
- a few slices of lemon
- salt
- beef broth
- a little vinegar
- sugar
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Fry one spoon of wheat flour or corn meal in one spoon of fat or cow's butter until browned
2. Add one chopped onion and one spoon of mustard powder
3. Add a few slices of lemon and salt
4. Add as much beef broth as needed
5. Add a little vinegar, sugar, and lemon juice
6. Serve

title: Molho por Excellencia (Sauce Par Excellence)
body:
## Ingredients

- some sardines
- 1 onion
- a piece of washed butter
- some spoons of wheat flour or starch
- a little bell pepper
- nutmeg
- meat broth
- juice of 2 lemons
- slices of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Wash some sardines and remove the bones; cut them well along with 1 onion
2. Heat a piece of washed butter in a saucepan
3. Once melted, add some spoons of wheat flour or starch, a little bell pepper, and nutmeg
4. Add the chopped sardines, meat broth, juice of 2 lemons, and slices of 1 lemon
5. Let it boil a little and serve

title: Molho Restaurante (Restorative Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- watercress
- chervil
- marjoram
- onion leaves
- beef broth
- pepper
- salt
- vinegar
- butter mixed with wheat flour

## Directions

1. Chop equal parts watercress, chervil, marjoram, and onion leaves
2. Place in a pot with beef broth, pepper, salt and vinegar
3. Let boil for fifteen minutes with these herbs
4. Remove from fire and add a little butter mixed with wheat flour
5. Stir until completely melted and serve

title: Molho Restaurante Frio (Cold Restorative Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- watercress
- chervil
- marjoram
- chives
- garlic
- parsley
- celery leaves
- basil
- capers
- anchovies
- egg yolk
- sweet oil
- vinegar
- mustard powder

## Directions

1. Chop finely: watercress, chervil, marjoram, chives, garlic, parsley, celery leaves, basil, capers, and anchovies
2. Pound all in a stone mortar
3. Add one egg yolk beaten with a little sweet oil
4. Add vinegar from time to time to prevent curdling
5. Continue pounding until the consistency of a sauce is reached
6. For a more restorative sauce, add a little mustard powder

title: Molho Roberto (Robert Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoon of wheat flour
- one spoon of butter
- three or four onions, chopped
- one spoon of vinegar
- French mustard

## Directions

1. Place in a pot one spoon of wheat flour and one spoon of butter
2. Heat until it takes on a russet color
3. Add three or four chopped onions
4. Continue heating until the flour becomes a reddish-brown color
5. Add one spoon of vinegar and French mustard
6. Mix everything together
7. Serve with cold pork loin or turkey

title: Molho Rozano (Rozano Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound of butter
- two pounds of ham
- one pound of beef
- chicken thighs
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- bay leaves
- two or three onions
- a dozen cooked egg yolks
- one carafe of milk

## Directions

1. Place over a low fire half pound of butter, two pounds of ham, one pound of beef, and chicken thighs, all cut into small pieces, with salt, pepper, cloves, bay leaves, and two or three onions
2. Fry everything well
3. Add a dozen cooked egg yolks, mashed
4. Add one carafe of milk little by little
5. Let cook for another hour, stirring continuously
6. Pass through a fine sieve and serve

title: Molho à la Daube (Daube Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- two spoons of vinegar
- two spoons of sweet oil
- a little pepper
- salt
- sugar
- lemon zest
- vinegar as needed

## Directions

1. Beat two spoons of vinegar, two spoons of sweet oil, a little pepper, the same amount of salt and sugar until well mixed
2. Add a little lemon zest and gradually as much vinegar as needed for the sauce
3. Serve cold with cold meats, whether pork or beef, and with fried fish

title: Molho à Provençale (Provençal Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- a little parsley
- one clove of garlic
- spoons of sweet olive oil
- a pinch of wheat flour
- beef broth
- white wine
- a bouquet of aromatic herbs

## Directions

1. Heat a little parsley and one clove of garlic in spoons of sweet olive oil
2. Add afterwards a pinch of wheat flour, and then beef broth, white wine, and a bouquet of aromatic herbs
3. Cook for half an hour, remove the fat, the garlic, and the bouquet of aromatic herbs
4. Serve

title: Molhos de Tubaras (Fish Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces of tubaras, clean
- four ounces of butter
- two cups of meat extract

## Directions

1. Fry four ounces of clean tubaras in four ounces of butter
2. Add two cups of meat extract
3. Let boil over moderate heat for one hour and serve

title: Moranga Cozida (Boiled Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- squash or pumpkin
- water
- salt
- lard
- vinegar
- onion

## Directions

1. Take a squash or pumpkin
2. Cut it into pieces
3. Remove the seeds and peel them
4. Boil the pieces in water and salt
5. When cooked, place them on a plate and serve with a sauce of melted lard, vinegar and chopped onion poured over top

title: Mulberry Sauce (Molho de Amoras)
body:
## Ingredients

- mulberries
- water
- salt
- beef broth or meat sauce substance
- spinach, chopped

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of mulberries with water and salt; remove them and let them drain; then place them in a portion of beef broth, or better the substance for sauce, and add a portion of chopped spinach, and serve with cooked fowl, etc.

title: Muquecas de Carne de Anta (Wrapped Tapir Pieces)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of tapir meat
- salt
- banana or caeté leaves
- lemon juice
- comaris peppers

## Directions

1. Take a piece of tapir meat and cut it into fine slices
2. Season with salt
3. Wrap each slice in banana or caeté leaves
4. Place in the embers until cooked
5. Serve with a sauce of lemon juice and comaris peppers

title: Muquecas de Carne de Vacca (Wrapped Beef Muquecas)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- salt
- garlic
- vinegar
- banana leaves or caeté leaves

## Directions

1. Cut a piece of beef the thickness of three fingers into slices.
2. Season the slices with salt and garlic, adding a little vinegar.
3. Wrap them in banana or caeté leaves so the meat does not get ashes on it.
4. Place them in hot embers, covering them properly.
5. Leave them for three to four hours, renewing the embers from time to time.
6. The meat prepared this way is very delicious and retains all its juice.

title: Mushroom Essence (Essencia de Cogumelos)
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- cloves

## Directions

1. Clean a portion of mushrooms, place them in a terrine over salt, and leave until the next day; after this, squeeze them well, pouring over the mass that remains in the sieve some water to extract more substance.
2. Boil this juice with a little pepper and cloves, skimming from time to time; strain and keep in small glass jars with two cloves and a bit of pepper, so that when desired, one can aromatize some sauce.

title: Mutton Chops with Cream (Costelletas de carneiro com creme)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton chops
- milk
- salt
- one bay leaf
- two cloves
- one palm heart cut in long pieces

## Directions

1. Put the beaten and trimmed chops in a pan with boiling milk.
2. Add salt, a bay leaf, two cloves, and palm heart cut in long pieces.
3. Simmer over moderate heat.
4. When cooked, remove the chops and palm heart and place on a platter.
5. Thicken the milk with four egg yolks and pour over the chops.
6. Serve.

title: Mutton Chops with Milk (Costelletas de carneiro com leite)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton chops
- milk
- salt
- whole Indian peppers
- parsley
- onion leaves
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- four eggs

## Directions

1. Put the chops in already boiling milk with salt, whole Indian peppers, parsley, and onion leaves.
2. When cooked, place the chops on a plate buttered with cow butter.
3. Cover with a portion of breadcrumbs and then with four beaten eggs.
4. Bake in the oven and when the eggs are cooked, serve.

title: Mutton Chops, Braised (Costelletas de carneiro refogadas)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton chops
- salt
- pepper
- wheat flour
- butter
- four glasses of white wine
- one onion
- one bunch of parsley
- one spoon of toasted wheat flour

## Directions

1. Beat the chops and trim them into a regular shape.
2. Season with salt and pepper and coat in wheat flour.
3. Brown on both sides in butter.
4. Add four glasses of white wine, sliced onion, and a bunch of parsley.
5. When cooked, remove the parsley and add one spoon of toasted wheat flour to thicken the sauce.
6. Add one spoon of vinegar and serve.

title: Mutton Chops, Fried (Costelletas de carneiro fritas)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton chops
- butter or melted fat
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- breadcrumbs
- onion finely chopped and fried in butter

## Directions

1. Cut, beat and trim the chops.
2. Dip in melted butter or fat.
3. Sprinkle with salt, pepper and ground nutmeg.
4. Cover with breadcrumbs and finely chopped onion fried briefly in butter.
5. Fry on both sides.
6. Serve with sauce or vegetables.

title: Mutton Chops, Fried with Coating (Costelletas de carneiro fritas com capa)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton chops
- salt
- pepper
- melted butter
- wheat flour
- egg yolks
- cow butter
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Season the chops with salt and pepper.
2. Dip in melted butter and wheat flour, then in egg yolks, and again in wheat flour.
3. Fry in cow butter.
4. Sprinkle with grated cheese and serve.

title: Mutton Chops, German Style (Costelletas de carneiro á allemã)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton chops with bone
- water
- salt
- pepper
- marjoram
- parsley
- onion leaves

## Directions

1. Cut the chops leaving one bone on each.
2. Beat well and shape into a rounded form with a knife.
3. Cook in water with salt, pepper, marjoram, parsley, and onion leaves.
4. When cooked, grill them.
5. Serve with an appropriate sauce for meat.

title: Mutton Chops, Grilled and Jellied (Costelletas de carneiro grelhadas e geladas)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton chops
- bacon strips
- butter
- meat extract or sauce

## Directions

1. Lard the chops by piercing them through on both sides with bacon strips.
2. Pass in butter on both sides and fry over high heat.
3. Trim the bacon ends level with the meat.
4. Let boil, covering with meat extract or sauce.
5. Keep near the fire for a quarter of an hour.
6. Serve.

title: Mutton Chops, Italian Style (Costelletas de carneiro á italiana)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton chops
- sweet olive oil
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare the chops and beat them well.
2. Soak them in a sauce of sweet olive oil with salt, pepper, and lemon juice.
3. After marinating, grill them.

title: Mutton Chops, Roasted and Covered (Costelletas de carneiro assadas e cobertas)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton chops
- salt
- parsley
- bay leaves
- onions
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- four eggs

## Directions

1. Cut, beat and trim the chops.
2. Bring them to boil, seasoned with salt, parsley, bay leaves and onions, until cooked.
3. Strain the broth and reduce it until thick.
4. Place the chops in a buttered pan.
5. Pour the sauce over them, sprinkle with breadcrumbs, and cover with four beaten eggs.
6. Bake in the oven and when the eggs are cooked, serve.

title: Mutton Chops, Stewed (Costelletas de carneiro em quisado)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton chops
- salt
- pepper
- chopped parsley
- wheat flour
- butter
- water
- one onion
- one handful of carapicús
- quarter of scalded raisins
- two spoons of sugar
- one spoon of wheat flour fried in butter
- one dozen sapucaia fruits, peeled and cut in long pieces

## Directions

1. Beat the chops and season with salt, pepper, and chopped parsley.
2. Coat in wheat flour and fry on both sides in butter.
3. Add a bowl of water and let it boil over coals with an onion and a handful of carapicús.
4. When cooked, add the scalded raisins, two spoons of sugar, one spoon of flour fried in butter, and the sapucaia fruits cut in long pieces.
5. Serve when ready.

title: Mutton Chops, Stewed (Costelletas de carneiro guisadas)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton chops
- salt
- fat
- water
- onion leaves
- parsley
- Indian peppers
- two egg yolks
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Beat the chops and season with salt.
2. Fry on both sides in a little fat.
3. Drain the fat and add two glasses of water, onion leaves, parsley, and Indian peppers.
4. Let boil and serve with its own sauce, thickened with two egg yolks and lemon juice.

title: Mutton Feet with Beans (Pés de carneiro com vagens)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton feet, cleaned
- water
- salt
- marjoram
- parsley
- onion leaves
- beans

## Directions

1. Place the cleaned feet to cook in water, salt, marjoram, parsley and onion leaves in equal parts
2. When almost cooked, fry the chopped beans in a little fat
3. Add the mutton feet with all the broth to the beans
4. Let them dry on moderate heat, then bring to table

title: Mutton Feet with Chayote (Pés de carneiro com chuchu)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton feet
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- bacon pieces
- chayotes, peeled and cut
- egg yolks
- orange juice

## Directions

1. Cut the mutton feet into pieces and cook with salt, parsley, onion leaves and pieces of bacon
2. When almost cooked, add peeled and cut chayotes
3. Let boil a little until the broth reduces
4. Thicken the broth with two beaten egg yolks, adding a little orange juice

title: Mutton Tongue, Grilled (Lingua de carneiro assada na grelha)
body:
## Ingredients

- one mutton tongue
- sweet olive oil
- salt
- pepper
- garlic water
- pickled cucumbers

## Directions

1. Boil the tongue to remove the thick skin that covers it.
2. Split lengthwise.
3. Soak in sweet olive oil with salt, pepper and garlic water.
4. After one hour, grill it.
5. Serve with pickled cucumbers.

title: Mutton Tongue, Roasted with Bacon (Lingua de carneiro assada com toucinho)
body:
## Ingredients

- one mutton tongue, skin removed
- salt
- ground pepper
- bacon cut in very fine slices

## Directions

1. Rub the tongue with salt and ground pepper.
2. Cut into fine, long slices.
3. Cover these slices on both sides with very fine slices of bacon, tying with thread.
4. Grill and serve.
5. Remove the thread and serve with roasted cassava or cará root.

title: Mutton Tongues with Water Squash (Linguas de carneiro com abobora d'agua)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton tongues, cooked and peeled
- melted fat
- two spoons of fubá mimoso
- water squash cut in cubes
- bowl of water or meat broth
- salt
- parsley
- celery root
- one onion

## Directions

1. Cut the cooked, peeled tongues into cubes and fry in melted fat with two spoons of fubá mimoso until the fubá begins to brown.
2. Add a portion of water squash also cut in cubes.
3. Stir and when taking on heat, add a bowl of water or meat broth, salt, parsley, celery root, and one sliced onion.
4. Let boil for one hour over moderate heat.
5. Serve.

title: Mãos de Vitella Cozidas (Cooked Veal Feet)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal feet
- sour orange
- water
- salt
- eggs
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean the veal feet and rub with sour orange to make them white
2. Cook in water and salt
3. When cooked, remove the bones from the feet
4. Thicken the broth with beaten eggs, add lemon juice
5. Let the feet boil a little more in this sauce and serve
6. They can also be fried or braised in sauce after cooking

title: Mólho Avinagrado para Lombo e Língua de Vaca (Vinegared Sauce for Loin and Beef Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoon of corn meal or roasted wheat flour
- two spoons of vinegar
- two cups of wine and four of water
- Indian cloves
- sugar
- raisins
- blanched and chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Boil one spoon of corn meal or roasted wheat flour until it becomes brown
2. Add two spoons of vinegar, two cups of wine and four cups of water
3. Add Indian cloves, sugar, raisins, and blanched almonds cut into small pieces
4. Allow everything to boil for half an hour
5. Serve

title: Mólho de Fricassé (Fricassée Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoon of fat
- one heaping spoon of wheat flour or corn meal
- two cups hot water
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onion
- cooked beef, chicken, or veal

## Directions

1. Put the fat in a saucepan and heat until very hot
2. Add, stirring, one heaping spoon of wheat flour or corn meal
3. Add two cups of hot water, salt, pepper, parsley, and onion
4. Add the cooked beef, chicken, or veal
5. Allow everything to cook for another hour over moderate heat
6. If desired, mushrooms, onions, artichokes, or peeled potatoes may be added to cook together

title: Mólho de Giló (Gilo Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four gilos cut into small pieces
- eight cups of beef substance
- a little salt
- four peppercorns
- juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Cut three or four gilos into small pieces in a saucepan
2. Add eight cups of beef substance, a little salt, four peppercorns, and the juice of one lemon
3. Boil and serve with roasted beef or pork

title: Mólho de Laranjas Verdes (Green Orange Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- green oranges
- salt
- ground Indian pepper

## Directions

1. Squeeze a quantity of green oranges
2. Strain the juice
3. Mix with a little salt and ground Indian pepper
4. Serve cold with cold roasted meat and roasted suckling pig

title: Mólho de Limão (Lemon Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- a small spoon of wheat flour
- a little cow's butter
- four egg yolks
- rind of one lemon
- one glass of white wine
- one spoon of vinegar

## Directions

1. Mash a small spoon of wheat flour with a little cow's butter, four egg yolks, and the rind of one lemon
2. Boil, stirring until cooked
3. Add one glass of white wine and one spoon of vinegar
4. Serve with beef tongue, chickens, ducks, etc.

title: Mólho de Mostarda Aperfeiçoado (Improved Mustard Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- three spoons of English mustard sauce
- three spoons of vinegar
- three spoons of water
- one spoon of butter
- three egg yolks
- two spoons of sugar

## Directions

1. Mix well three spoons of English mustard sauce, three of vinegar, three of water, one of butter, three of egg yolks, and two of sugar
2. Place over coals and heat, stirring constantly, until it begins to boil
3. Serve with meats of all kinds

title: Mólho de Pobre (Poor Man's Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- some onions
- parsley
- a little water or beef broth
- a little corn meal
- one spoon of vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop some onions and parsley finely
2. Put in a saucepan with a little water or beef broth, a little corn meal, and one spoon of vinegar
3. Allow to boil until the onion is cooked

title: Mólho Estimulante (Stimulating Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup vinegar
- serpol
- bay leaves
- one garlic clove
- onion
- pepper
- beef broth
- a little beef substance from roasted meat
- a little wheat flour fried with butter

## Directions

1. Put the vinegar, serpol, bay leaves, garlic clove, onion, and pepper in a saucepan
2. Allow everything to boil until reduced to two thirds
3. Add beef broth and a little beef substance from roasted meat
4. Add a little wheat flour fried with butter
5. Pass through a sieve
6. If desired, melt butter in the saucepan and thicken gradually with wheat flour without allowing it to color
7. Add the sauce mentioned above
8. Add seasonings cut into small pieces
9. Bring everything to boil
10. Pour over cutlets, reheated meats, etc.

title: Mólho Mineiro (Minas-style Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen fresh or preserved peppercorns
- juice of one gallego lemon
- six to eight spoons of beef broth

## Directions

1. Mash half a dozen fresh or preserved peppercorns
2. Squeeze the juice of one gallego lemon over them
3. Add six to eight spoons of beef broth
4. Serve with boiled meats, cabbage, etc.

title: Mólho Pardo (Brown Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- a little fat
- pieces of roasted beef
- a quantity of grated bread crusts
- onion leaves
- parsley
- salt
- one pimiento
- a few cups of beef broth or beef substance for sauces
- vinegar or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Fry in a little fat pieces of roasted beef and a quantity of grated bread crusts
2. After half an hour, add onion leaves, parsley, salt, one pimiento, and a few cups of beef broth or beef substance
3. Allow to boil for another half hour
4. Strain and, if desired, add a little vinegar or lemon juice before serving

title: Mólho Pardo de Alcaparras (Brown Caper Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- a few spoons of corn meal or wheat flour
- beef broth
- chopped onions
- bay leaves
- a sprig of parsley
- marjoram
- serpol
- a few spoons of capers
- a few raisins
- lemon rinds
- Indian cloves
- salt
- a little sugar
- one spoon of vinegar

## Directions

1. Toast a few spoons of corn meal or wheat flour until they become dark copper color
2. Boil in a small quantity of beef broth with chopped onions, bay leaves, a sprig of parsley, marjoram, serpol, a few spoons of capers, a few raisins, lemon rinds, Indian cloves, salt, a little sugar, and one spoon of vinegar
3. Serve with roasted ducks, beef tongue, game, etc.

title: Mólho à Franguinha (Chicken-style Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- ingredients as in fricassée sauce
- some egg yolks

## Directions

1. Make as fricassée sauce
2. Thicken the sauce with some egg yolks

title: Mólho à Orléans (Orleans-style Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- four spoons of vinegar
- pepper
- chopped onions
- butter
- wheat flour
- beef broth or water
- four pickled cucumbers
- one cooked carrot
- three hard boiled egg whites
- loins of three anchovies
- one spoon of capers

## Directions

1. Put four spoons of vinegar, pepper, chopped onions, and butter in a saucepan
2. Boil and add wheat flour fried in butter prepared beforehand until it takes a dark color
3. Dilute with beef broth or water
4. When serving, add four chopped pickles, one cooked carrot, three hard boiled egg whites cut into pieces, the loins of three finely chopped anchovies, and one spoon of capers
5. Heat slightly and serve with ducks, beef, or veal

title: Nabos Gelados (Glazed Turnips)
body:
## Ingredients

- turnips
- water
- meat broth
- white wine

## Directions

1. Peel and cut turnips into pieces
2. Boil in water for a few minutes, then cook in meat broth until cooked and the broth reduced to a jelly
3. When the jelly begins to stick to the bottom of the pan, remove the turnips
4. Pour a glass of white wine into the pan, stir with a spoon to dissolve the reduced broth, pour it over the turnips, and serve

title: Nabos Guisados Inteiros (Whole Braised Turnips)
body:
## Ingredients

- turnips
- water
- butter
- wheat flour
- broth
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel and turn the turnips and boil in water for five minutes
2. Separately, melt two spoons of butter with two of wheat flour
3. Let the flour brown a little, stirring, and add a cup of broth or water, salt, pepper, nutmeg, and a spoon of sugar
4. Boil the turnips in this sauce and serve

title: Nabos Guisados Picados (Shredded Braised Turnips)
body:
## Ingredients

- turnips
- water
- salt
- butter
- nutmeg
- milk
- arrowroot
- egg yolk
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the turnips, cut into thin strips, and boil for five minutes in water and salt
2. Remove with a skimmer and drain
3. Sauté in two spoons of butter with salt, nutmeg, three cups of milk, a spoon of arrowroot, an egg yolk, and a spoon of sugar
4. Finish cooking the turnips in this sauce and serve

title: Nabos Refogados (Sautéed Turnips)
body:
## Ingredients

- turnips
- water
- butter
- salt
- parsley
- onion
- arrowroot
- mustard sauce

## Directions

1. Peel a dozen turnips, cut into slices, and boil in water with a spoon of butter, salt, parsley, and an onion
2. When cooked, remove with a skimmer and drain
3. Strain the broth, reduce it, and add a spoon of arrowroot and another of mustard sauce
4. After boiling, add the turnips, let cook a little more, and serve

title: Neck of Mutton Grilled (Pescoço de carneiro assado na grelha)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton neck
- cold water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- breadcrumbs
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Cut the neck meat into slices and place on the fire with cold water, salt, parsley and onion leaves
2. After boiling for half an hour, remove and wrap with breadcrumbs
3. Cover with a little melted butter, grill, and serve with a spicy sauce

title: Noz de Vitela Frita (Fried Veal Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal loin
- bacon
- broth
- ham
- parsley
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- carrots
- lemon

## Directions

1. Beat the veal loin until flat, wrapped in a strong cloth
2. Boil for five minutes to tenderize
3. Lard it in this state
4. Place in a pan with bacon, broth, a little ham, parsley, onion, salt, pepper, and carrots
5. When cooked, strain the sauce and let it thicken
6. Return the loin to the vessel and let it cook over coals until serving time
7. Before serving, add some slices of lemon

title: Noz de Vitela Recheada (Stuffed Veal Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal loin
- turkey or chicken meat
- bacon slices
- carrots
- white wine

## Directions

1. Beat the loin wrapped in cloth
2. Open it on one side and fill with chopped turkey or chicken meat
3. Sew the hole with thread
4. Place the prepared loin in a pan lined with bacon slices and carrots
5. Moisten with a glass of white wine
6. Cook over moderate heat
7. Serve with fresh vegetables

title: Noz de Vitella Assada no Espeto (Roasted Veal Rump on the Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal rump
- cloth
- salt
- pepper
- fine pork fat
- butter
- paper
- salad or spicy sauce for serving

## Directions

1. Wrap the veal rump in cloth after rubbing with salt, ground pepper, and fine pork fat
2. Place on the spit, covered with paper greased with butter, and roast
3. When roasted, remove the paper and continue roasting to create color
4. Serve with some salad or spicy sauce

title: Noz de Vitella com Gelea (Veal Rump with Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal rump
- carrots
- onions
- parsley
- chervil
- pepper
- nutmeg
- salt
- roasted beef
- veal hand
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Beat and lard the rump, then place to cook with carrots, onions, parsley, chervil, pepper, nutmeg, salt, pieces of roasted beef, a veal hand, and meat broth
2. When the rump is cooked, remove it, strain the sauce, and let it thicken
3. Pour this over the rump and let cool to serve

title: Noz de Vitella Frita (Fried Veal Rump)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal rump
- cloth
- water
- pork fat
- broth
- ham
- parsley
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- carrots

## Directions

1. Flatten the veal rump wrapped in strong cloth by beating
2. Boil for five minutes to firm it, then lard it
3. Place in a pot with pork fat, broth, a little ham, parsley, onion, salt, pepper, and carrots
4. When cooked, strain the sauce and let it thicken
5. Return the rump to the pot and let it cook over coals

title: Olha de Gallinha (Chicken Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- one fat chicken, cut up
- two pigeons
- some mutton ribs
- a piece of pork loin
- onions
- one cabbage, cut up
- one or two lettuce heads
- cassava roots
- sweet potatoes or some pieces of squash
- salt
- parsley
- pepper
- water
- sauce for meats

## Directions

1. Take one fat chicken cut up, two pigeons, some mutton ribs, a piece of pork loin, onions, one cabbage cut up, one or two lettuce heads, cassava roots, sweet potatoes or some pieces of squash, salt, parsley and pepper.
2. Let everything boil with water until cooked, and serve with any sauce for meats: a middle dish.

title: Olhete, Chaguiriça etc., Ensopados (Stewed Small Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- water
- salt
- cassava flour
- hot fat
- wine
- onions
- garlic
- parsley
- bay leaves
- clove
- pepper

## Directions

1. After removing the skin, boil the fish in water and salt
2. Wrap it in cassava flour and pass it through hot fat
3. Add one or two bottles of wine according to the size of the fish, the fish should remain covered
4. Add one or two chopped onions, one garlic clove, parsley, bay leaves, clove, and plenty of pepper
5. Let it boil for half an hour, remove the fish and serve it with its sauce strained and reduced

title: Olhete, Chaguriçá, Canjurupí, Cachorro e Ubarana Cozidos (Boiled Small Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salted water
- parsley
- celery
- onion
- bay leaves
- pepper
- clove
- thyme
- melted butter
- wheat flour
- white wine
- capers

## Directions

1. Clean the fish, cut it into steaks, and boil in salted water with parsley, celery, onion, bay leaves, pepper, clove and thyme
2. When cooked, remove the steaks, drain them
3. Make a sauce of melted butter with wheat flour and white wine, to which are added a chopped onion and one tablespoon of capers
4. Put the steaks in the same sauce and having boiled a little more, serve

title: Omelette com Carapicús ou Cogumelos (Omelette with Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoonful of cow butter
- one chalice of white wine
- mushrooms
- one dozen eggs
- half a chalice of wine
- fine salt
- ground pepper
- two spoonfuls of butter

## Directions

1. Melt one spoonful of cow butter, mix with one chalice of white wine, and boil in this sauce a portion of finely chopped mushrooms
2. Beat one dozen eggs with half a chalice of wine, fine salt, and ground pepper
3. Melt two spoonfuls of butter in a frying pan
4. Pour half the eggs, let fry, and when it begins to stick to the bottom, lift and add one more spoonful of butter
5. When melted, flip the omelette, add the sautéed mushrooms, and pour the rest of the eggs
6. Cover the frying pan with a lid filled with coals
7. When cooked, serve

title: Omelette com Cognac (Omelette with Cognac)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight egg yolks
- one quarter pound of powdered sugar
- a little lemon zest
- nutmeg
- three spoonfuls of milk cream
- egg whites
- two spoonfuls of butter
- powdered sugar
- one glass of cognac

## Directions

1. Beat eight egg yolks with one quarter pound of powdered sugar, a little lemon zest, nutmeg and three spoonfuls of milk cream
2. Add the whites and beat all together
3. Melt two spoonfuls of butter in a saucepan, and when hot, pour the beaten eggs
4. When fried, place on a plate
5. Cover with a light layer of powdered sugar
6. At serving time, pour one glass of cognac over the omelette
7. Set the omelette on fire
8. When burning, serve

title: Omelette com Doces (Omelet with Sweets)
body:
## Ingredients

- ten eggs
- a little butter
- sweets of choice

## Directions

1. Beat ten eggs well.
2. Put a little butter in a frying pan, and when it is melted, pour the eggs into it.
3. When cooked, pour them onto a serving dish so that half the eggs remain in the pan.
4. In this state, add the sweets you wish, and cover them with the other part that remains in the pan, and serve hot.

title: Omelette com Dourado ou Piaba (Omelet with Dourado or Piaba Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- two roe of dourado or piaba fish
- a piece of fresh fish the size of an egg
- six heads of green onions, finely chopped
- a little butter
- parsley
- green onion leaves
- lemon juice
- twelve eggs
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the roe in boiling and slightly salted water.
2. Take a piece of fresh fish the size of an egg, to which six finely chopped heads of green onions are added.
3. Chop together the roe and the fish so as to mix everything very well, and put everything in a casserole with a little butter to sauté until the butter is melted, which is where the delicacy of this dish lies.
4. Take a second portion of butter at will, which is kneaded with parsley and green onion leaves and place this mixture on a long serving dish, on which the omelet is to be placed.
5. Pour a little lemon juice over the mixture and place the dish over coals.
6. Beat twelve eggs and the sautéed roe and fish so that everything is well mixed.
7. Make the omelet in the manner already indicated for other omelets, and place it on the prepared dish.
8. Be careful that neither the eggs nor the fish boil.

title: Omelette de Assucar (Sugar Omelette)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight egg yolks
- one quarter of powdered sugar
- some lemon zest
- three spoonfuls of milk cream
- egg whites
- a little fine salt
- a little melted butter
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Take eight egg yolks and beat them strongly with one quarter of powdered sugar, some lemon zest and three spoonfuls of milk cream
2. Then add the egg whites with a little fine salt
3. Beat well
4. Fry the mixture in a saucepan with a little melted butter
5. When fried, place on a plate
6. Cover with powdered sugar and having passed a hot iron over it, serve

title: Omelette de Vento (Fluffy Omelette)
body:
## Ingredients

- six to eight egg yolks
- four spoonfuls of sugar
- a little ground cinnamon
- egg whites
- fine salt
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Beat six to eight egg yolks with four spoonfuls of sugar and a little ground cinnamon
2. Separately, beat the whites with a little fine salt until stiff
3. Add the yolks to the whites
4. Pour everything onto a plate placed over the fire
5. Cover it with a lid full of coals
6. The fire should be medium so that the eggs rise
7. When cooked, cover with sugar and cinnamon
8. Serve hot

title: Omelette Fofa (Fluffy Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight eggs
- six spoonfuls of clean sugar
- some finely chopped lemon zest
- a piece of butter
- refined sugar

## Directions

1. Break eight eggs and separate the whites from the yolks.
2. Mix six spoonfuls of clean sugar with the yolks and some finely chopped lemon zest, as fine as possible, and mix everything together.
3. At the time of serving, beat the whites and mix them with the yolks.
4. Take a piece of butter, which is melted in a frying pan, and after it is melted, pour in the eggs and turn the omelet.
5. When the butter is absorbed, pour it into a deep serving dish buttered, and place this dish over coals.
6. Sprinkle refined sugar on top and place a cover with coals over the dish, being careful that the omelet does not burn, and serve hot.

title: Omelette Mineira (Miner's Omelette)
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- one spoonful of corn flour strained
- borage leaves
- two spoonfuls of fat

## Directions

1. Beat six eggs with one spoonful of strained corn flour
2. Put some finely chopped borage leaves in this batter
3. Fry two spoonfuls of fat
4. Take one spoonful of this mixture and pour it in the fat
5. Repeat this operation until the beaten eggs are finished
6. Serve these cakes with some sauce

title: Omelette Simples (Simple Omelette)
body:
## Ingredients

- six to eight eggs
- two spoonfuls of milk
- a little fine salt
- ground pepper
- one spoonful of butter

## Directions

1. Beat six to eight eggs, yolks and whites, with two spoonfuls of milk
2. Add a little fine salt and ground pepper
3. Melt one spoonful of butter in a frying pan
4. When hot, pour the eggs
5. When sticking to the frying pan, lift with the point of a knife and add one more spoonful of butter
6. When melted, flip the eggs, fry on the other side, and serve
7. The taste of this omelette can be varied with seasonings such as nutmeg, onion, ginger, cinnamon and sugar, as well as other additions such as grated cheese, potatoes, breadcrumbs, chopped herbs, chopped meat, etc.

title: Orelhas de Porco com Polpa (Pig's Ears with Pulp)
body:
## Ingredients

- pig's ears
- lentils
- guandú
- mangalós
- chickpeas
- water
- onions
- carrots

## Directions

1. Pass the ears through a flame and clean them.
2. Cook them with lentils, guandú, mangalós, and chickpeas in water with onions and carrots.
3. Make a pulp from some of these grains and place it on top.

title: Orelhas de Porco à Sainte-Menehould (Pig's Ears Saint-Menehould)
body:
## Ingredients

- pig's ears
- meat broth
- red wine
- carrots
- onions
- cloves
- bundle of aromatic herbs
- pepper
- salt
- warm butter
- bread crumb
- egg yolk
- piquant sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the pig's ears in meat broth with red wine, carrots, onions with cloves inserted into one of them, add a bundle of aromatic herbs, broken pepper and salt.
2. Once cooked and cold, brush with a little warm butter, place bread crumb on top.
3. Cover with a beaten egg yolk, put another layer of bread crumb on top, and let it brown in the oven.
4. Serve with a piquant sauce.

title: Orelhas de Porco à Sainte-Menehould (Pork Ears Sainte-Menehould-Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork ears
- Meat broth
- Red wine
- Carrots
- Onions
- Cloves
- Aromatic herbs
- Pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cook the pork ears in meat broth with red wine, carrots, and onions, inserting cloves into one of the onions
2. Add a bundle of aromatic herbs, broken pepper and salt

title: Orelhas de Vitela com Inhame (Veal Ears with Yam)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal ears
- water
- salt
- ham
- bread crust
- clove
- nutmeg
- sugar
- butter
- egg yolk
- white wine
- yam roots

## Directions

1. Clean and boil the ears with water and salt
2. Fill them with a mixture of chopped ham, bread crust, clove, nutmeg, sugar, all kneaded together with butter and an egg yolk
3. Fry them in a little butter
4. Add a glass of white wine and yam roots boiled in water and salt

title: Orelhas de Vitela Cozidas (Boiled Veal Ears)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal ears
- meat broth
- white wine
- cabbage leaves
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- marjoram
- onion
- clove
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Scald the ears well, clean and blanch them
2. Place in a pan with meat broth, a glass of white wine, cabbage leaves, salt, pepper, parsley, marjoram, onion and clove
3. After cooking, remove them
4. Pass the sauce through a sieve
5. Thicken with egg yolks
6. Serve with the ears

title: Orelhas de Vitela Refogadas (Braised Veal Ears)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal ears
- meat broth
- herb bouquet
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- butter
- sugar
- vinegar or lemon juice
- raisins

## Directions

1. Clean the ears well
2. Boil them in a pan with meat broth, herb bouquet, salt and pepper
3. Remove the ears after cooking
4. Thicken the strained broth with flour toasted in butter
5. Add a little sugar, vinegar or lemon juice, and some scalded raisins
6. Let it boil a little more with the ears
7. Serve

title: Orelhas e Pés de Porco Assados (Roasted Pig's Ears and Feet)
body:
## Ingredients

- pig's feet
- pig's ears
- dried bread crumbs or dried herbs
- onions
- toasted onions
- mustard sauce

## Directions

1. After boiling, cook the feet for four to five hours.
2. Then split them in two, cover with bread crumb powder, and roast on a grill.
3. Cut the ears into thin strips and sauté them with a portion of toasted onions.
4. At the moment of serving, add a little mustard sauce and place the roasted feet on top.

title: Orelhas e Pés de Porco à Choisy (Pork Ears and Feet Choisy-Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Four pork ears
- Four pork feet
- Water
- Seasonings
- Butter
- Onions
- Spanish sauce
- Mustard sauce

## Directions

1. Clean the ears and feet well; after passing them through a flame, scrape them, wash them many times, and boil in a vessel with plenty of water
2. Cook them with some seasonings and a little butter
3. When cooked, let them cool in their own liquid, drain them and cut into even strips
4. Cut some large onions into strips, place them in a casserole with a little butter, and cook them
5. When the onions are cooked, add three spoonfuls of Spanish sauce, letting it cook slowly by the fire
6. At the moment of serving, place the ears cut into strips in the pan, add a mustard sauce, and serve

title: Orelhas Fritas (Fried Veal Ears)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal ears
- ham slices
- herb bouquet
- salt
- pepper
- carrot
- cabbage leaves
- wine
- water
- egg yolks
- sugar
- lemon
- cooked ham
- eggs
- wheat flour
- raw egg
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil the well-cleaned ears with ham slices, herb bouquet, salt, pepper, a carrot, cabbage leaves, equal parts wine and water
2. Remove them when cooked
3. Strain and thicken the broth
4. Before serving, add two egg yolks, a little sugar, and lemon juice
5. Fill the ears with a stuffing made of cooked and chopped ham, eight cooked and chopped eggs, a little wheat flour, and a raw egg
6. Fry in butter
7. Serve with the prepared sauce

title: Orelhas à Caseira (Homestyle Pork Ears)
body:
## Ingredients

- Wheat flour
- Onions
- Vinegar
- Meat broth
- Salt
- Indian pepper
- Pork ears
- Bread toasts

## Directions

1. Place some wheat flour and onions in a casserole; when cooked and having taken on a golden color, add half a cup of vinegar and a cup of meat broth, salt, and broken Indian pepper
2. Let them boil a few times
3. Cut the ears of a cooked and seasoned pig into long strips
4. Place them in a casserole, pouring the prepared sauce over them
5. Braise everything over very low heat so it never boils
6. Serve on a platter with bread toasts placed on top and around

title: Ostras Cruas (Raw Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- pepper
- lemon

## Directions

1. Take a portion of oysters and clean the outside as much as possible
2. Open them by placing on an iron plate with live coals beneath; as soon as the plate heats, they open by themselves
3. With a strong knife, break the joint and scrape the meat attached to move it to the other shell so all meat remains in one shell
4. Dust with a little ground pepper and add a few drops of lemon to each and serve

title: Ostras Fritas (Fried Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- eggs
- manioc starch
- butter
- fat
- pepper
- parsley

## Directions

1. After boiling the oysters in their own water, dry them one by one with a towel
2. Beat two eggs with half a tablespoon of manioc starch and one tablespoon of melted butter
3. Pass the oysters in this batter, fry in fat and serve sprinkled with pepper and chopped parsley

title: Ostras Guisadas (Stewed Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- wheat flour
- salt
- pepper
- white wine
- carapicús

## Directions

1. Melt two tablespoons of butter, mix in one of wheat flour, little salt and pepper
2. Add half a bottle of white wine
3. When boiled, add a portion of chopped cooked carapicús

title: Ostras Refogadas (Sautéed Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- butter
- onion
- parsley
- pepper
- nutmeg
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Open two or three dozen oysters and place their meat and water in a pan
2. Heat until almost boiling
3. Place in a fireproof dish with two tablespoons of butter, some chopped onion, parsley, pepper and grated nutmeg
4. Heat to melt the butter and add the oysters
5. Cover with a layer of bread crumbs, cover the pot, place coals on top
6. When the bread has a good color, serve

title: Outro Methodo de Torrar o Café (Another Method of Roasting Coffee)
body:
## Ingredients

- coffee
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. The best method of roasting coffee is to roast it without sugar, and after it is roasted and ready, and while still hot, sprinkle it with powdered sugar, which prevents the evaporation of the essential oil that constitutes the aroma of the coffee.

title: Outro Rim de Vitella Frito (Another Fried Veal Kidneys)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal kidneys
- fat
- white wine
- salt
- parsley
- pepper
- bread, grated
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut veal kidneys into thin lengthwise pieces and place them in a pan with melted fat
2. Immediately add a glass of white wine so they do not become fried, a little salt, parsley, and pepper
3. Before serving, add some grated bread and lemon juice

title: Ovos com Amendoada (Eggs with Almond Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of almonds
- a quarter of sugar moistened with a few drops of milk
- a cup of milk
- the breast of a hen, finely chopped
- salt
- lemon zest
- eight eggs
- two spoonfuls of butter

## Directions

1. Crush a quarter of almonds and a quarter of sugar moistened with a few drops of milk.
2. Then add a cup of milk and the breast of a hen finely chopped, salt, lemon zest.
3. Heat, and add eight well-beaten eggs.
4. Mix everything, and melt two spoonfuls of butter in a casserole.
5. Pour this mixture into it, let it fry without stirring, and serve.

title: Ovos com Carne Fria Picadinha (Eggs with Minced Cold Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- parsley
- onions
- chives
- salt
- pepper from India
- garlic clove
- cold meat, minced
- lard or fat
- tomato and onion sauce

## Directions

1. Beat whole eggs well
2. Add finely chopped parsley, onions, and chives, salt, pepper from India, and one clove of garlic; mix well
3. Add a portion of finely chopped cold meat
4. Place in a casserole with melted but not very hot lard
5. Cook over low heat with a lid of coals on top
6. Serve in the same casserole with a sauce of tomatoes and onions on top, or carefully remove and place on a plate

title: Ovos com Queijo (Eggs with Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- equal parts of breadcrumb and grated cheese
- salt
- pepper
- three or four egg yolks
- one or two spoonfuls of milk
- eight eggs
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Mix on a heat-resistant plate equal parts of breadcrumb and grated cheese.
2. Season with salt and pepper, and mix three or four egg yolks and one or two spoonfuls of milk.
3. Place over coals until hot.
4. Break eight eggs over this mixture, and add a portion of grated cheese.
5. Cover with a lid full of coals until the whites are cooked and the cheese is melted, and serve.

title: Ovos com Queijo Fresco (Eggs with Fresh Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of fresh cheese
- fat or oil
- beaten eggs
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Fry slices of fresh cheese in fat, and when well fried, pour beaten eggs on top, and let fry without stirring until the eggs are cooked.
2. Turn the casserole onto a plate, and cover with sugar and cinnamon powder.

title: Ovos com Sopa de Pão (Eggs with Bread Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- breadcrumb
- half a bottle of milk
- salt
- ground cinnamon
- lemon zest
- half a spoonful of butter
- a dozen eggs
- a spoonful of melted butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of breadcrumb in half a bottle of milk with salt, ground cinnamon, lemon zest, and half a spoonful of butter.
2. When the bread is broken down, add a dozen well-beaten eggs, mix everything, and pour into a casserole containing a spoonful of melted butter.
3. Place on the fire or over coals, let boil until cooked.
4. Turn the casserole onto a plate, cover with sugar, and serve.

title: Ovos Cozidos com Casca (Boiled Eggs in Shell)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- water

## Directions

1. Fill a large vessel with water so the eggs can be covered with water at the bottom of the vessel.
2. When the water is boiling, put in the eggs and begin counting from one to two hundred.

title: Ovos Cozidos em Calda (Eggs Cooked in Sugar Syrup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of sugar
- twenty egg yolks
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Make a syrup from one pound of sugar and cook until it reaches the mirror stage.
2. Remove from heat and let it cool.
3. Beat twenty egg yolks, taking care to separate the whites completely as even the smallest particle of white will prevent the yolks from binding with the syrup.
4. Add the beaten yolks to the cooled syrup.
5. Return the vessel to the fire, heat over coals, and stir until it reaches a good consistency.
6. Pour the mixture into cups.
7. Cover with cinnamon.
8. Serve cold.

title: Ovos Cozidos sem Casca (Poached Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Heat salted water in a wide vessel.
2. Break the eggs into the water carefully so they remain whole and keep their shape, staying separate from each other so they do not stick together.
3. Cook while counting to three hundred.
4. Remove with a skimmer.
5. Serve with meat sauce or vegetables.

title: Ovos Dourados (Golden Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of bread
- butter
- six hard-boiled eggs
- one onion
- parsley leaves
- salt
- pepper
- six beaten eggs
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Fry slices of bread in butter.
2. Place the fried bread on a plate that can withstand direct heat from the fire.
3. Shell and chop six hard-boiled eggs.
4. Mix the chopped eggs with one onion, parsley leaves, salt, and pepper.
5. Add the mixture to the plate with the fried bread.
6. Pour six beaten eggs mixed with grated cheese over the top.
7. Place the plate over coals and cover with a lid filled with coals.
8. When cooked, serve.

title: Ovos Dourados Fritos (Fried Golden Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of bread
- milk
- four beaten eggs
- one spoonful of wheat flour
- butter or beef fat
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Soak slices of bread in milk.
2. Remove and let them drain.
3. Pass the bread slices through four beaten eggs mixed with one spoonful of wheat flour.
4. Fry in a saucepan with butter or beef fat.
5. When fried on one side, cover with sugar and cinnamon.
6. Serve.

title: Ovos em Calda (Eggs in Syrup)
body:
## Ingredients

- two dozen eggs
- a little butter
- two pounds sugar

## Directions

1. Beat two dozen eggs well and pour into a casserole with a little butter
2. Fry until the eggs are hard
3. Remove the casserole from the fire and cut the eggs into slices
4. Make a syrup of two pounds of sugar at soft-ball stage
5. Put the slices in the syrup
6. Boil a little more, place on a plate
7. Sprinkle with cinnamon and sugar and serve

title: Ovos em Gelea (Eggs in Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- four whole eggs
- two cups of meat broth
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Beat four whole eggs with two cups of meat broth, salt, and pepper in a bowl of sufficient capacity.
2. Place the bowl in a pot of hot water, taking care that no water enters the bowl.
3. Leave until the contents of the bowl become hard.
4. Let cool a little.
5. Turn the bowl over onto a plate.
6. Serve.
7. Alternatively, divide the mixture into cups and proceed as described, serving with a spicy sauce or cream.

title: Ovos Ensopados (Stewed Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- water
- one spoonful of wheat flour
- one spoonful of butter
- one spoonful of vinegar
- one glass of wine
- salt
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Break eggs into a pot of boiling water, carefully dropping them into the water so they remain whole.
2. Cook until done.
3. Place the cooked eggs on a plate and let them drain.
4. Prepare a sauce with one spoonful of wheat flour, one of butter, one spoonful of vinegar, one glass of wine, salt, a little sugar, and cinnamon.
5. Let the sauce simmer for a few minutes.
6. Pour the sauce over the eggs and serve.

title: Ovos Ensopados com Molho de Cebola (Stewed Eggs with Onion Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- one onion
- one spoonful of butter
- wheat flour
- water
- one spoonful of vinegar
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Boil eggs in their shells until hard.
2. Shell and slice them into rounds.
3. Fry a chopped onion in one spoonful of butter.
4. Cover with wheat flour.
5. Add one glass of water, one spoonful of vinegar, salt, and pepper.
6. Simmer for a while.
7. Pour over the egg slices and serve.

title: Ovos Ensopados com Queijo (Stewed Eggs with Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter of cheese
- one glass of white wine
- one spoonful of butter
- salt
- sorrel
- a pinch of coriander seeds
- six beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Grate one quarter of cheese.
2. Place in a saucepan with one glass of white wine, one spoonful of butter, salt, sorrel, and a pinch of coriander seeds.
3. Place over fire and stir until the cheese dissolves completely.
4. Remove from heat and let cool.
5. Add six beaten eggs.
6. Return to fire and simmer this mixture, stirring until cooked.
7. Place on a plate and serve.

title: Ovos Escaldados com Molho de Tomates (Poached Eggs with Tomato Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- eggs
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Put water to boil in a casserole, and when it is boiling, gently break a portion of eggs so they do not burst.
2. When well poached, remove them with a skimming spoon, and proceed this way until all the eggs you wish are finished.
3. After finished, place the eggs in a serving dish, pouring tomato sauce on top, and send to the table.

title: Ovos Escaldados com Substancia de Carne (Poached Eggs with Meat Essence)
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- eggs
- meat essence

## Directions

1. Proceed as in the preceding article, pouring good meat essence over the eggs.

title: Ovos Escaldados com Sumo (Poached Eggs with Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- sorrel
- one spoonful of butter
- half spoonful of wheat flour
- two egg yolks
- salt
- eight to ten poached eggs

## Directions

1. Pound a portion of sorrel.
2. Squeeze out the juice and strain through cloth.
3. Add one spoonful of butter, half a spoonful of wheat flour, two egg yolks, and a little salt.
4. Simmer for a while, stirring.
5. Add eight to ten poached eggs in salted water.
6. Simmer once more and serve.

title: Ovos Estrelados (Fried Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoonful of butter
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Heat one spoonful of butter with salt in a frying pan.
2. When hot, carefully break eggs into it so they do not break.
3. Fry for a little while.
4. Cover with a little pepper and nutmeg.
5. Serve.
6. These eggs may be prepared in a deep plate that can withstand the heat of the fire.

title: Ovos Estufados (Braised Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley
- onion leaves
- half an onion
- bread crumbs
- milk
- three egg yolks
- butter
- six to eight eggs
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Chop parsley, onion leaves, and half an onion.
2. Mix with bread crumbs softened in milk and mixed with three egg yolks.
3. Knead until it becomes a soft paste.
4. Spread this paste on a plate greased with butter.
5. Place over coals until the paste is almost cooked.
6. Pour six to eight eggs over the top.
7. Sprinkle with salt and ground pepper.
8. Cover with a lid filled with coals.
9. Cook until done and serve.

title: Ovos Estufados com Cebola (Braised Eggs with Onion)
body:
## Ingredients

- six onions
- one spoonful of wheat flour
- one spoonful of milk
- salt
- pepper
- one dozen eggs

## Directions

1. Chop six onions and fry in one spoonful of wheat flour.
2. Add one spoonful of milk, salt, and pepper.
3. Simmer for a while.
4. Mix in one dozen very well beaten eggs.
5. Place over coals, covering with a lid filled with coals.
6. When cooked, turn the saucepan over onto a plate and serve.

title: Ovos Fritos à Mineira (Eggs Fried in the Miner's Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Break six eggs into a bowl.
2. Add a little ground salt and pepper.
3. Beat until frothy.
4. Fry a piece of lard in a saucepan until the fat is hot.
5. Pour in the eggs and fry without stirring.
6. When fried (not too hard), place on a plate and serve.
7. Some cover them with a little sugar and powdered cinnamon.

title: Ovos Fritos à Moda de Hotel (Eggs Fried in Hotel Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- six to eight eggs
- salt
- chive leaves
- pepper
- one spoonful of butter

## Directions

1. Break six to eight eggs into a bowl.
2. Beat with a little ground salt, chopped chive leaves, and a little ground pepper.
3. Melt one spoonful of butter in a frying pan.
4. When melted and still not very hot, pour in the eggs.
5. Continue stirring so they do not stick to the bottom.
6. When fried, serve.

title: Ovos Mexidos com Creme (Scrambled Eggs with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- sweetened milk
- orange blossom water
- six egg yolks
- a little wheat flour
- a quarter pound of refined sugar

## Directions

1. Poach six eggs in milk sweetened and flavored with orange blossom water, and let them drain and cool.
2. Add to the milk that was used to poach them six egg yolks, a little wheat flour, and a quarter pound of refined sugar.
3. Mix, pass through a fine sieve, and put the cream to thicken over a gentle heat.
4. Pour sugar over the eggs, pour the cream on top, and serve.

title: Ovos Mexidos com Hervilhas Novas (Scrambled Eggs with New Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- eggs
- a little butter

## Directions

1. Proceed as already explained for asparagus tips.

title: Ovos Mexidos com Pontas de Espargos (Scrambled Eggs with Asparagus Tips)
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus tips
- eggs
- a little butter

## Directions

1. After boiling a portion of asparagus tips, cut them into small pieces and drain them.
2. Add them to a portion of eggs and beat well.
