3. In this state, place this mixture in a casserole with a little butter, and bring to a gentle heat, stirring always with a spatula until they reach good consistency.

title: Ovos Mexidos com Queijo (Scrambled Eggs with Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve eggs
- four ounces of Flemish cheese or well-cured cheese
- two ounces of butter
- salt
- pepper from India
- a glass of wine

## Directions

1. Break the eggs and beat them well in a casserole
2. Add the butter and grated cheese
3. Place the casserole over a brisk fire and stir continuously until the mixture is sufficiently cooked and still soft
4. Add salt according to the saltiness of the cheese and a portion of pepper from India
5. Serve on a plate previously heated
6. Pour a glass of wine over the top

title: Ovos Mexidos com Substância de Carne (Scrambled Eggs with Meat Essence)
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve eggs
- two ounces of fresh butter
- meat essence

## Directions

1. Take twelve eggs and two ounces of fresh butter
2. Beat them together
3. Add meat essence gradually
4. Add salt and pepper from India
5. Place in a casserole, stirring constantly with a spatula until of good consistency
6. Serve on a plate previously heated over coals

title: Ovos Nevados (Snowy Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- six egg yolks
- one bottle of boiled milk
- a little salt
- a little sugar
- some pieces of cinnamon
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat six egg yolks and mix in one bottle of boiled milk with a little salt
2. Add a little sugar and some pieces of cinnamon
3. Place on the fire and let boil a little
4. Place in a tureen
5. Separately, beat the egg whites until stiff
6. Having placed a large saucepan on the fire with one or two bottles of water or milk, drop a full spoonful of the beaten white, repeating until the white is finished
7. Remove with a skimmer, letting it drain well
8. Pour over the milk and cover with sugar and cinnamon powder
9. Serve

title: Ovos Refogados (Braised Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- salt
- ground pepper
- hot fat or oil
- wheat flour
- butter
- lemon juice
- broth
- bread

## Directions

1. Place a broken egg in a cup, add a little salt and ground pepper, and invert the cup in a frying pan containing hot fat.
2. When the egg is cooked on one side, turn it inside the cup to fry on the other side.
3. Fry a dozen or more eggs in the same way.
4. Serve on a plate, placing on top of each egg a slice of fried bread the same size as the egg, pouring over them a sauce made with a spoonful of wheat flour and another of melted butter, the juice of a lemon, and a cup of broth.
5. After it has boiled, serve.

title: Paca assada no espeto (Paca Roasted on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- one very young and fat paca
- water
- salt
- pepper
- sour orange juice
- manjerona
- garlic
- mint leaves

## Directions

1. When the paca is very young and fat, instead of removing the hide, scald it in very hot water and pluck it like a house pig
2. Soak it for six hours in a brine made with sour orange juice, salt, pepper, manjerona, garlic, and some mint leaves
3. Remove it and put it on a spit, roast it like a pig, and serve

title: Paca assada no forno (Paca Roasted in the Oven)
body:
## Ingredients

- one paca
- palm hearts
- chopped bacon
- salt
- pepper
- cassava flour
- meat broth
- bacon slices
- salad

## Directions

1. Fill the paca with a stuffing made of palm hearts, chopped bacon, salt, little pepper, and cassava flour moistened with meat broth
2. Place it in a pan over bacon slices, and let it cook in the oven for two to three hours, and serve with salad

title: Paca assada à imperial (Paca Roasted Imperial Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- one paca
- salt
- pepper
- onions
- parsley
- manjerona
- two bottles of red wine
- bacon
- mushrooms
- bacon
- palm heart
- pitted olives
- breadcrumbs
- one onion
- salt
- pepper
- fat

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the paca and put it in a brine made with salt, pepper, onions, parsley, manjerona and two bottles of red wine for twelve hours
2. Lard it with bacon and fill it with mushrooms, bacon, palm heart, some pitted olives, breadcrumbs, one onion, salt and pepper, all finely chopped
3. Wrap it in paper greased with fat, put it on a spit and roast it on low heat until cooked
4. Remove the paper, increase the heat, give it a few more turns so the paca browns, and serve with the sauce that remained in the dripping pan

title: Paca cozida no sôro (Paca Cooked in Whey)
body:
## Ingredients

- one paca, cut into pieces
- whey
- salt
- peppers
- one bottle of hot white wine
- two egg yolks
- one onion
- mushrooms or carapicús
- cornstarch
- okra

## Directions

1. Cut the paca into pieces and boil it with whey, salt and peppers
2. When almost cooked, remove it and finish cooking it in a sauce made of one bottle of hot white wine with two beaten egg yolks, one onion and a portion of chopped mushrooms or carapicús, one tablespoon of cornstarch and some okra cut into slices, and serve

title: Paca esfolada e assada no espeto (Paca Skinned and Roasted on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- one paca
- salt
- pepper juice
- lemon juice
- tomato juice
- water
- one clove of garlic
- two onions
- parsley
- fat

## Directions

1. Remove the skin of the paca and rub it with salt, pepper juice, lemon juice and tomato juice
2. Roast it on a spit over moderate heat, basting it with a sauce made with two cups of water, one crushed clove of garlic, two cut onions, a little parsley and two tablespoons of fat, which are boiled for a moment in a pan
3. When roasted, serve with its sauce

title: Paca estufada com arroz e tomates (Paca Braised with Rice and Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- one paca
- boiling water
- cold water
- chicken meat
- mushrooms
- cassava flour
- one onion

## Directions

1. Put a paca in a pan and pour boiling water over it and having placed the pan on the fire, let it boil once, then remove the paca, which is then placed in cold water
2. Separately, chop the meat of two chickens, a portion of mushrooms, another of cassava flour and one onion

title: Paca Estufada com Arroz e Tomates (Stuffed Paca with Rice and Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- one paca
- boiling water
- liver from two chickens
- carapicús
- manioc flour
- one onion
- salt
- comarí peppers
- chopped parsley
- two tablespoons pork fat
- water
- one cup orange wine
- bacon slices
- rice
- tomatoes

## Directions

1. Put a paca in a pot and pour boiling water over it.
2. Place the pot over fire, let it boil once, then remove the paca and place it in cold water.
3. Chop together the liver from two chickens, a portion of carapicús, manioc flour, and one onion.
4. Mix with salt, comarí peppers, and chopped parsley.
5. Fry this mixture in two tablespoons of pork fat, adding a little water, and boil for ten minutes.
6. Stuff the paca with this filling.
7. Place it in a pot over bacon slices with one cup of orange wine.
8. Seal the pot lid and boil over moderate fire for three to four hours.
9. Serve with rice fried with tomatoes.

title: Paca Guisada com Cogumelos ou Carapicús (Paca Stewed with Mushrooms or Carapicús)
body:
## Ingredients

- one paca cut in pieces
- water
- salt
- ginger
- two tablespoons pork fat
- two cups meat broth
- comarí peppers
- one chopped onion
- one clove
- portion of chopped mushrooms
- one teaspoon arrowroot dissolved in two tablespoons lemon juice

## Directions

1. Boil the paca cut in pieces in water with salt and a little ginger.
2. When cooked, remove it and fry in two tablespoons of pork fat.
3. Remove again and add to the fat two cups of meat broth, comarí peppers, one chopped onion, and one clove.
4. Add a portion of chopped mushrooms and one teaspoon of arrowroot dissolved in two tablespoons of lemon juice.
5. Boil everything for a quarter of an hour.
6. Place on a plate, put the paca pieces on top, and serve.

title: Paca Guisada com Gariroba (Paca Stewed with Palmito)
body:
## Ingredients

- bitter palmito from coconut tree
- one paca cut in pieces
- water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- celery root
- three tablespoons pork fat
- one tablespoon wheat flour
- one chopped onion
- one cup white wine

## Directions

1. Boil the bitter coconut palmito, remove it, and cut it into small pieces.
2. Place it with the paca cut in pieces in water with salt, parsley, onion leaves, and celery root.
3. When cooked, remove the palmito and meat.
4. Fry the meat on both sides in three tablespoons of pork fat.
5. Sprinkle it with one tablespoon of wheat flour.
6. Add one chopped onion and one cup of white wine.
7. Place the palmito on the plate and put the paca pieces on top.
8. Pour the sauce over and serve.

title: Paca Guisada com Quiabos (Paca Stewed with Okra)
body:
## Ingredients

- one whole paca
- water
- salt
- peppers
- sprig of mint
- ginger
- two tablespoons pork fat
- one chopped onion
- broth
- juice of one terra orange
- portion of young whole okra

## Directions

1. Boil the whole paca in water with salt, peppers, a sprig of mint, and ginger.
2. When cooked, separate the meat from the bones.
3. Fry the meat in two tablespoons of pork fat with one chopped onion.
4. Add the strained and reduced broth, juice of one terra orange, and a portion of young whole okra.
5. Let it boil for a quarter of an hour and serve.

title: Paca refogada com gilós (Paca Braised with Okra)
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of paca meat
- fat
- two tablespoons of wheat flour
- a dozen tomatoes
- water
- salt
- peppers
- grated ginger
- one chopped onion
- okra

## Directions

1. Braise some pieces of paca meat in fat
2. When fried, remove them and add to the same fat two tablespoons of wheat flour and a dozen tomatoes
3. Stir until they brown, add one bottle of water, salt, peppers, grated ginger, one chopped onion and the paca meat
4. Let it boil until cooked and the broth is reduced to a few tablespoons, and serve with okra blanched in water and salt

title: Paio Caseiro (Homemade Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat, neither too fat nor too lean
- English potatoes
- salt
- Indian pepper, whole
- nutmeg
- cow tripe, well-washed

## Directions

1. After the meat is boiled, chop it finely.
2. Cook the potatoes, selecting the most floury ones, peel and pound in a mortar.
3. Add the chopped meat to the mortar and pound everything together until well mixed.
4. Add salt, whole Indian pepper and nutmeg.
5. Fill some well-washed cow tripe with the mixture.
6. Tie these tripe at intervals with string, hang them in smoke or in the open air.
7. Cook these sausages in a pot or in well-seasoned meat broth, or eat them roasted on a grill, pricking them with a pin so they do not burst.

title: Paio Mineiro (Mineiro Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork loins
- six cloves of garlic
- two tablespoons of Indian pepper powder
- salt
- one bottle of wine
- some chili peppers
- pork tripe
- cornmeal
- cold water

## Directions

1. Cut the loins into small pieces and place in a vessel with six crushed garlic cloves, two tablespoons of Indian pepper powder, plenty of salt, one bottle of wine and some chili peppers.
2. Leave everything mixed for twenty-four hours, stirring occasionally with a wooden spoon.
3. Use pork tripe, and when they are thin, cook two together.
4. Fill the tripe, tie them well at intervals, pass them through boiling water as they are being filled, and place in the smokehouse, away from the fire for twenty days.
5. The tripe should be washed with cornmeal and placed in cold water, which should be changed until the sausages are full, which should be kept in a bag to go to the smokehouse.
6. In the absence of wine to season the meat, replace it with well-salted water.
7. Cold weather is preferable for making sausages.

title: Paladar de Vacca (Beef Palate)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef palate
- water
- salt
- veal udder, finely cooked
- stuffing
- bacon slices
- Italian sauce

## Directions

1. After removing the palate, soak it in water to release the blood
2. Blanch it and remove the skin with a knife
3. Wash in cold water and scrape a second time, cleaning it well and removing the black meat
4. Boil for at least five hours in salted water
5. When tender to the touch, remove from heat
6. Cut into wide strips and arrange on these strips a cooked stuffing, covering it with finely cooked veal udder
7. Add more stuffing and roll these strips, arranging them around a platter
8. Fill the center with stuffing, leaving a hollow in the middle
9. Cover all with bacon slices and place on a vessel with heat below and above or in the oven
10. After cooking about a quarter hour, drain the fat and pour Italian sauce into the hollow left between the stuffing

title: Paladar de Vacca de Grelha (Grilled Beef Palate)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef palate, cooked and cooled
- sweet oil
- salt
- pepper
- garlic
- parsley, chopped
- tomato sauce or Robert sauce

## Directions

1. When cooked and cold, immerse in a sauce of sweet oil, salt, pepper, garlic, and a pinch of chopped parsley
2. After two hours, remove and turn several times to ensure it is well soaked
3. Grill and serve with tomato sauce or Robert sauce

title: Paladar de Vacca Ensopado (Stewed Beef Palate)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef palate
- water

## Directions

1. Wash and scrape the palate
2. Remove the hard black skin after scalding with hot water
3. Wash several times in hot water and finally in cold water
4. Cut into pieces the thickness of three fingers
5. Cook these slowly for six to seven hours
6. Place on a plate and after draining, serve with any sauce

title: Paladar de Vacca à Bechamel (Beef Palate with Béchamel Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef palate
- Béchamel sauce
- ground Indian pepper

## Directions

1. Blanch the palate as explained in recipe 84
2. Cut the palate into small squares and place in a serving platter
3. Cover with Béchamel sauce sprinkled with ground Indian pepper

title: Palmito Cozido (Boiled Heart of Palm)
body:
## Ingredients

- one heart of palm
- water
- salt
- fat
- onion
- tomatoes
- vinegar
- mustard sauce

## Directions

1. Cut one heart of palm into pieces two inches long
2. Boil in salted water
3. Serve with a sauce of fat, chopped onion, tomatoes, vinegar and mustard sauce

title: Palmito Frito com Ovos (Fried Heart of Palm with Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- one heart of palm
- water
- salt
- fat
- salt
- pepper
- eggs

## Directions

1. Cut a heart of palm into pieces and boil in salted water
2. Remove and place in a pot with fat to fry
3. Sprinkle with salt and pepper
4. Break eggs on top and continue to boil until eggs are cooked and serve

title: Palmito Frito com Queijo (Fried Heart of Palm with Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- one heart of palm
- water
- salt
- half pound of grated cheese
- a dozen tomatoes
- two spoonfuls of fat
- a little cilantro

## Directions

1. Cut a heart of palm, boil in salted water and when cooked, dry with a towel
2. On the other hand, fry half pound of grated cheese with a dozen tomatoes in two spoonfuls of fat
3. Add the heart of palm and a little cilantro
4. Boil a bit more and serve

title: Palmito Guisado com Nata (Braised Heart of Palm with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- heart of palm
- cow's butter
- wheat flour
- salt
- cloves
- one cup of milk

## Directions

1. Chop the heart of palm into small pieces and fry in cow's butter
2. Add wheat flour, salt, cloves and one cup of milk
3. Let boil over moderate heat and serve

title: Palmito Refogado (Sautéed Heart of Palm)
body:
## Ingredients

- one heart of palm
- fat
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- water

## Directions

1. Take a heart of palm, chop finely and wash in cold water
2. Place in hot fat
3. Add salt, pepper and parsley
4. Stir, add one cup of water
5. Once boiled, serve

title: Palmito Refogado com Picados (Sautéed Heart of Palm with Diced Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of pork
- two spoonfuls of fat
- one heart of palm
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- tomatoes
- one cup of water

## Directions

1. Cut two pounds of pork into small pieces
2. Fry in two spoonfuls of fat
3. Add one chopped and washed heart of palm, salt, pepper, parsley, tomatoes and one cup of water
4. Let boil for half an hour and serve

title: Pamonias de Milho Verde (Corn Pudding Parcels)
body:
## Ingredients

- corn bagasse
- sugar
- salt
- banana leaves

## Directions

1. Take the bagasse that remained after straining the corn milk
2. Mix each pound with a quarter pound of sugar and a little salt
3. Wrap a piece of the mixture in banana leaf
4. Bake in the oven and serve

title: Panada Côr de Leite (Milk-Colored Panada)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup milk
- 1 tablespoon sugar

## Directions

1. Make as milk panada, but instead of fat, add a cup of milk and a tablespoon of sugar

title: Panada de Manteiga (Butter Panada)
body:
## Ingredients

- one loaf of bread
- one bottle of water
- a little salt
- a little fat or butter

## Directions

1. Boil one loaf of bread in one bottle of water
2. When it begins to dissolve, add a little salt and a little fat or butter

title: Panquecas (Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- four tablespoons wheat flour
- two beaten eggs
- two cups milk
- a little salt
- nutmeg
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Make a mixture of four tablespoons of wheat flour, two beaten eggs, two cups of milk, a little salt and nutmeg
2. Fry in a heated frying pan
3. When well cooked on both sides, cover with sugar and cinnamon powder and serve hot

title: Panquecas de Batatas (Potato Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- a portion of small potatoes
- one spoonful of fine cornmeal
- one spoonful of sugar
- two beaten eggs
- one spoonful of butter
- salt
- half a cup of milk

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of small potatoes, peel and mash them well
2. Mix with one spoonful of fine cornmeal, one of sugar, two beaten eggs, one spoonful of butter, salt, and half a cup of milk
3. Proceed as already stated

title: Panquecas de Batatas (Potato Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- one tablespoon polished cornmeal
- one tablespoon sugar
- two beaten eggs
- one tablespoon butter
- salt
- half cup milk

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of potatoes, peel and mash well
2. Mix with one tablespoon of polished cornmeal, one tablespoon of sugar, two beaten eggs, one tablespoon of butter, salt and half cup of milk
3. Proceed as already described

title: Panquecas Economicas (Economic Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- four spoonfuls of fine cornmeal
- one spoonful of arrowroot
- two well-beaten eggs
- a little salt
- lemon zest
- two cups of milk

## Directions

1. Make a batter with four spoonfuls of fine cornmeal, one spoonful of arrowroot, two well-beaten eggs, a little salt, lemon zest, and two cups of milk
2. Proceed as already stated

title: Panquecas Econômicas (Economical Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- four tablespoons polished cornmeal
- one tablespoon tapioca pearls
- two beaten eggs
- a little salt
- lemon peel
- two cups milk

## Directions

1. Make a mixture of four tablespoons of polished cornmeal with one tablespoon of tapioca pearls, two beaten eggs, a little salt, lemon peel and two cups of milk
2. Proceed as already described

title: Partridge, Quail, etc. Boned and Braised (Perdizes, codornizes etc. desossadas e refogadas)
body:
## Ingredients

- birds roasted on a spit
- onion
- parsley
- one clove of garlic
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- one chalice of white wine
- a pinch of polvilho

## Directions

1. Take the birds roasted on a spit
2. remove their bones and chop the meat with onion, parsley, one clove of garlic, salt and pepper
3. braise this chopped mixture in butter
4. moisten it with one chalice of white wine in which a pinch of polvilho has been dissolved
5. serve on toasted bread

title: Partridge, Quail, etc. Braised in the Fashion (Perdizes, codornizes, etc. refogadas à moda)
body:
## Ingredients

- birds
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- handful of breadcrumbs
- same amount of carapicús
- one onion
- one chalice of wine
- slices of peeled lemon

## Directions

1. Cut the birds in half
2. rub with salt and pepper and fry in butter
3. remove after frying on both sides
4. in the same butter, put a handful of breadcrumbs, the same amount of carapicús, and one chopped onion
5. fry a little while stirring and add one chalice of wine and some slices of peeled lemon
6. give it another boil and serve over the birds

title: Partridge, Quail, etc. Braised with Ora-pro-nobis (Perdizes, codornizes, eto. refogadas com ora-pro-nobis)
body:
## Ingredients

- birds
- fat
- one onion
- one tablespoon of fine fubá
- two cups of water
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- portion of ora-pro-nobis
- juice of one land orange

## Directions

1. Take the birds and cut them into pieces
2. braise them in little fat, turning them on both sides to brown
3. add one chopped onion, one tablespoon of fine fubá, two cups of water, salt, pepper, parsley, a portion of ora-pro-nobis and the juice of one land orange
4. boil for another half hour and serve with fubá angá from the mill

title: Partridge, Quail, etc. Braised with Sapucaia (Perdizes, codornizes, etc. refogadas com sapucaias)
body:
## Ingredients

- birds roasted on a spit
- two cups of wine
- two egg yolks
- a pinch of polvilho
- portion of peeled and cut sapucaia fruits
- one tablespoon of sugar
- juice of one lemon
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cut the roasted birds on a spit into pieces
2. on the other hand make a sauce with two cups of wine, two beaten egg yolks, a pinch of polvilho, a portion of peeled and cut sapucaia fruits, one tablespoon of sugar, the juice of one lemon, little salt and nutmeg
3. put the meat in it
4. let it boil for another quarter of an hour and serve

title: Partridge, Quail, etc. Chopped and Braised (Perdizes, coodornizes, eto. pioadas e refogadas)
body:
## Ingredients

- birds roasted on a spit
- salt
- parsley
- onion
- two peppers
- one tablespoon of butter
- handful of breadcrumbs
- one large peeled cucumber without core
- one cup of wine
- slices of peeled lemon

## Directions

1. Roast the birds on a spit
2. remove the bones and cut the meat into pieces
3. add salt, parsley, chopped onion, two peppers, one tablespoon of butter, a handful of breadcrumbs, one large peeled cucumber without core, and boil everything with one cup of wine
4. add some slices of peeled lemon and serve

title: Partridge, Quail, etc. Cooked with Chuchú (Perdizes, codornizes, etc.cozidas com chuchús)
body:
## Ingredients

- bird
- half a quarter of bacon
- salt
- parsley
- peppers
- marjoram
- onion leaves
- ginger
- half a clove of garlic
- little cilantro

## Directions

1. Boil the bird with half a quarter of bacon, salt, parsley, peppers, marjoram, onion leaves, ginger, half a clove of garlic, and a little cilantro
2. when cooked, remove the bird and reduce

title: Partridge, Quail, etc. Escabeche (Perdizes, codornizes, etc. de escabeche)
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- clove of India
- lemon juice
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Flatten the partridges with a pomatoria
2. soak them with salt, pepper, onion, clove of India and lemon juice
3. after four hours, pass them in melted butter
4. roast on the grill on both sides
5. serve with salad or spicy sauce

title: Partridge, Quail, etc. Roasted and Braised (Perdizes, codornizes, etc. assadas e refogadas)
body:
## Ingredients

- bird offal
- half a quarter of bacon
- salt
- parsley
- pepper
- one onion
- two boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Chop the bird offal with half a quarter of bacon, salt, parsley, pepper, one onion and two boiled eggs
2. fill the bird with this mixture
3. sew the opening shut
4. braise the bird in fat, turning it on all sides
5. then place it on a spit and wrap it in paper greased with fat
6. roast over regular heat and serve with some spicy sauce or salad

title: Partridge, Quail, etc. Roasted in Embers with Manioc (Perdizes, codornizes, etc. assadas no búrralho com mandioca)
body:
## Ingredients

- birds
- salt
- juice of pepper
- strips of bacon
- manioc
- milk
- one tablespoon of sugar
- little cinnamon
- two egg yolks
- one chalice of wine

## Directions

1. Rub the birds with salt and juice of pepper
2. wrap them in fine strips of bacon and put them in the embers with some manioc
3. when cooked remove them
4. unwrap the bacon and serve with the manioc peeled and with a sauce made of boiled milk with one tablespoon of sugar, a little ground cinnamon and incorporated with two egg yolks dissolved in one chalice of wine

title: Partridge, Quail, etc. Roasted in the Oven (Perdizes, codornizes, etc. assadas no forno)
body:
## Ingredients

- bird offal
- one onion
- parsley
- half a quarter of bacon
- salt
- peppers
- two boiled eggs
- half a dozen tomatoes
- strips of bacon
- some tomatoes
- one chalice of wine
- slices of peeled lemon

## Directions

1. Chop the bird offal with one onion, parsley, half a quarter of bacon, salt, peppers, two boiled eggs and half a dozen tomatoes
2. fill the bird with this chopped mixture
3. sew the opening shut
4. put it in a pan lined with strips of bacon, with more some tomatoes, one chalice of wine and slices of peeled lemon
5. cover the pan well and let it boil between coals or in the oven
6. when cooked, serve with its own strained and degreased sauce

title: Partridge, Quail, etc. Roasted on a Spit (Perdiges, codornizes, etc. assadasno espeto)
body:
## Ingredients

- birds
- salt
- chopped peppers
- chopped parsley
- half a clove of garlic
- bacon strips
- hot milk

## Directions

1. Soak the birds with salt, chopped peppers, chopped parsley and half a clove of garlic
2. after they are well impregnated, lard the birds with thin strips of bacon
3. place them on a spit and roast over moderate heat
4. baste with hot milk
5. when cooked, serve with salads, some compote or with vegetables

title: Partridge, Quail, etc. Singed (Perdizes, codornizes, eto. chamuscadas)
body:
## Ingredients

- birds
- bacon
- white wine
- salt
- chopped parsley
- juice of peppers
- juice of garlic

## Directions

1. Pluck and clean the birds and place them on a spit
2. roast a little over a flame
3. then lard with bacon and finish roasting over moderate heat
4. baste with white wine to which have been added a little salt, chopped parsley, juice of peppers and of garlic
5. when cooked, serve with some spicy sauce

title: Partridge, Quail, or Lark Braised (Perdiz, codorniz, cotovia refogadas)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bird
- salt
- pepper
- one pound of fat beef
- parsley
- cilantro
- onion leaves
- one cup water
- one chalice of juice from land orange

## Directions

1. Take the bird, rub it with salt and pepper, and wrap it in strips of bacon
2. place it in a pan with one pound of fat beef, salt, pepper, parsley, cilantro, onion leaves, one cup water, and one chalice of juice from land orange
3. cover the pan and let it boil for one hour over moderate heat
4. then braise one tablespoon of wheat flour in two tablespoons of butter
5. fry the bird in this mixture and immediately add the broth in which the bird was cooked
6. let it cook for another half hour and serve

title: Passarinhos Assados no Forno (Small Birds Roasted in the Oven)
body:
## Ingredients

- one dozen small birds
- two spoonfuls of fat
- one spoonful of fine cornmeal or wheat flour
- one glass of wine
- salt
- peppers
- slices of peeled lemon

## Directions

1. Braise a dozen small birds in two spoonfuls of fat, dust with one spoonful of fine cornmeal or wheat flour, one glass of wine, salt, peppers and some slices of peeled lemon
2. Let them roast over coals until cooked, and serve

title: Passarinhos Fritos (Fried Small Birds)
body:
## Ingredients

- small birds
- salt
- peppers
- egg yolks
- wheat flour
- fat
- compote

## Directions

1. Clean the small birds, season with salt and peppers, flatten them with a spatula, coat them in egg yolks, and wrap them in wheat flour
2. Fry them in plenty of fat
3. When cooked, serve with some compote

title: Passarinhos Fritos com Bananas (Fried Small Birds with Bananas)
body:
## Ingredients

- small birds
- salt
- bananas
- bread crumbs
- sugar
- ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Season the small birds with salt, fry them with peeled bananas split in half
2. When cooked, dust with a portion of grated bread
3. Place them on a plate, cover with a layer of sugar and ground cinnamon, and serve

title: Passarinhos Guisados com Tomates (Small Birds Stewed with Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- two dozen tomatoes
- one cup of water
- one glass of white wine
- salt
- peppers
- two dozen small birds, cleaned

## Directions

1. Boil two dozen tomatoes with one cup of water, one glass of white wine, salt and peppers
2. Add two dozen cleaned small birds
3. Let them simmer for half an hour
4. Serve

title: Passarinhos Guisados com Tomates (Small Birds Stewed with Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- two dozen tomatoes
- one cup of water
- one glass of white wine
- salt
- peppers
- two dozen cleaned small birds
- one spoonful of butter
- one spoonful of wheat flour

## Directions

1. Boil two dozen tomatoes with one cup of water, one glass of white wine, salt and peppers
2. Then add two dozen cleaned small birds and let them boil for half an hour, adding one spoonful of butter and another of wheat flour
3. When cooked, serve

title: Passarinhos Refogados em Molho Branco (Small Birds Braised in White Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- breasts from a dozen small birds
- butter
- wheat flour
- remainder of the small birds
- one glass of white wine
- two cups of water
- half a dozen tomatoes

## Directions

1. Fry the breasts of a dozen small birds in butter
2. On the other hand, melt a little butter, mix one spoonful of wheat flour, the rest of the small birds, one glass of white wine, two cups of water, and half a dozen tomatoes
3. Let it boil for half an hour
4. Then place the fried breasts in this sauce, boil a little more, and serve

title: Passarinhos Refogados em Môlho Branco (Small Birds Braised in White Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- breasts from one dozen small birds
- butter
- wheat flour
- rest of the small birds
- one glass of white wine
- two cups of water
- half dozen tomatoes

## Directions

1. Fry the breasts from one dozen small birds in butter
2. In another pan, melt a little butter and mix one spoon of wheat flour, the rest of the small birds, one glass of white wine, two cups of water and half dozen tomatoes
3. Let it simmer for half an hour
4. Add the fried breasts to this sauce
5. Simmer a little more
6. Serve

title: Passaros Miudos Assados no Espeto (Small Birds Roasted on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- small birds
- salt
- bacon slices
- bread slices

## Directions

1. Pluck and clean the small birds
2. Rub them with salt
3. Wrap each one in a bacon slice
4. Place them on a spit, alternating with bread slices
5. Roast over moderate fire
6. When cooked, serve each bird on one bread slice

title: Passaros Miudos Refogados (Small Birds Braised)
body:
## Ingredients

- small birds
- two spoons of butter
- one peeled and chopped apple
- one onion
- one handful of breadcrumbs
- one glass of white wine
- one glass of water

## Directions

1. After cleaning the small birds, braise them in two spoons of butter
2. When fried, remove them and braise one peeled and chopped apple, one onion and one handful of breadcrumbs in the same butter
3. Moisten everything with one glass of white wine and one glass of water
4. Place the small birds in this sauce
5. Let them simmer for one quarter of an hour
6. Serve each bird placed on one slice of bread fried in butter and pour the sauce over it

title: Pasteis de Capa de Fubá à Brazileira (Brazilian Cornmeal Pastry Crust Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- four spoonfuls of fine cornmeal
- two eggs
- one small spoonful of arrowroot boiled in one cup of water or milk
- a little salt
- filling as in the recipe above

## Directions

1. Proceed exactly as in the recipe above, with the crust made with four spoonfuls of fine cornmeal, two eggs and one small spoonful of arrowroot boiled in one cup of water or milk and a little salt
2. This crust should be more substantial than one made with wheat flour

title: Pasteis de Carne à Brazileira (Brazilian Meat Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of beef or mutton
- half a pound of bacon
- one onion
- salt
- plenty of cumin pepper
- parsley
- one cup of water
- one glass of brandy
- one glass of lemon juice
- olives
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Mince one pound of beef or mutton with half a pound of bacon, one onion, salt, plenty of cumin pepper, and parsley
2. Sauté everything with one cup of water, one glass of brandy and one glass of lemon juice
3. When cooked, extend the dough for empada crust, and cutting into circles with a glass, place in the middle of each one a spoonful of the meat and some olives
4. Fold the edges and place the pastries in a pan with hot fat, leaving to fry until golden
5. Remove, cover with sugar and cinnamon and serve

title: Pasteis de Estudantes (Students' Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of brown sugar
- water
- cinnamon
- ground pepper
- manioc flour
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Boil half a pound of brown sugar with water, cinnamon, and ground pepper until it reaches mirror point
2. Remove from fire and stir in manioc flour until it becomes a medium-firm dough
3. Fill pastries with this dough, fry in fat and cover with sugar and cinnamon, serve

title: Pasteis de Fígado de Ganso (Goose Liver Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- goose
- fubá (corn meal)
- water
- dried cogumelos (mushrooms)
- cogumelos (mushrooms), weight equal to liver
- dried roscas (hard biscuits), ground
- sugar
- nutmeg
- cardamom
- salt
- three tablespoons goose fat
- one cup white wine
- pastry dough

## Directions

1. Place the goose in a cage slightly larger than itself
2. Make balls with one tablespoon of fubá, water, and a little dried mushroom powder
3. Make the goose swallow a portion of these balls three times daily, increasing the dose day by day
4. Give no other food or even water
5. After twenty-four to thirty days, the goose is fattened and the liver is the size of a plate, sometimes weighing four pounds
6. Chop the liver with a weight of mushrooms equal to its own weight
7. Mix in ground dried biscuits, sugar, nutmeg, cardamom, and a little salt
8. Sauté in three tablespoons of goose fat, adding one cup of white wine
9. Let simmer for one hour near the fire
10. Make a pastry crust and fill it with this mixture
11. Finish cooking in the oven
12. Can also be eaten cold

title: Pasteis de Gallinha (Chicken Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- the breast, gizzard, liver of one chicken
- half a quarter of bacon
- two egg yolks
- one onion
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- one cup of white wine

## Directions

1. Mince the breast, gizzard, and liver of one chicken with half a quarter of bacon, two egg yolks, one onion, parsley, salt and pepper
2. Simmer a little, adding one cup of white wine until it reduces
3. Fill the crusts with this mixture and fry the pastries in fat

title: Pasteis de Nata (Cream Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- four cups of milk cream
- ten egg yolks
- one spoonful of sugar
- lemon zest
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Beat four cups of milk cream with ten egg yolks, one spoonful of sugar, and lemon zest
2. Bring to a boil, stir and fill the crusts with this mixture
3. Place in the oven, and when baked, serve covered with sugar and powdered cinnamon

title: Pasteis de Ovos (Egg Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- one dozen eggs
- two spoonfuls of milk
- one pound of sugar in syrup after cooling
- puff pastry dough
- sugar

## Directions

1. Beat one dozen eggs with two spoonfuls of milk
2. Add one pound of sugar in cooled syrup
3. Return to fire, boil a little, stirring, and fill the puff pastry crusts with this mixture
4. Place the pastries in the oven, bake, and serve with a little sugar on top

title: Pasteis de Passarinhos à Brazileira (Brazilian Bird Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- a portion of small birds
- vinegar
- ground pepper
- dough

## Directions

1. Fry a portion of small birds until well toasted
2. Sprinkle with vinegar and ground pepper
3. Roll each bird in a round of dough
4. Join the edges and fry in fat

title: Pasteis, Massa Folhada para Capa (Pastries with Puff Pastry Crust)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of wheat flour
- six egg yolks
- a little salt
- milk
- butter

## Directions

1. Knead one pound of wheat flour with six egg yolks, a little salt and milk
2. Knead well and extend over a board, coat the surface with butter
3. Cut the crusts with a glass and proceed as in the previous article

title: Pastel de Carne de Vitella (Veal Meat Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of veal
- one quarter of bacon
- two onions
- parsley
- salt
- peppers
- one cup of water
- one cup of wine

## Directions

1. Mince one pound of veal with one quarter of bacon, two onions, parsley, salt and peppers
2. Place everything in a pan with one cup of water and one cup of wine, and let simmer a little
3. Fill small pastries with puff pastry crust with this mixture, put them in the oven, bake them; when baked, serve

title: Pastel Picante (Spicy Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of ham
- half a pound of veal
- one quarter of fresh bacon
- two onions
- salt
- parsley
- peppers
- nutmeg
- puff pastry dough
- white wine

## Directions

1. Mince half a pound of ham, half a pound of veal, one quarter of fresh bacon, two onions, salt, parsley, peppers and nutmeg
2. Make some small forms of puff pastry and bake in the oven
3. Place a portion of this minced mixture inside the forms, cover the forms with a crust
4. Simmer a little in white wine, place in the oven, and serve

title: Pato Assado no Espeto com Marmelos (Duck Roasted on a Spit with Quinces)
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- quinces, peeled and halved, seeds removed
- squash pieces, if quinces are unavailable
- vinegar
- parsley leaf
- cloves
- lemon juice
- salt
- garlic
- oiled paper

## Directions

1. Fill the duck with peeled quinces, halved and seeded, or squash pieces briefly stewed in vinegar with a parsley leaf and cloves if quinces are unavailable
2. Rub the duck with lemon juice, salt, and garlic
3. Place on a spit and roast, wrapped in oiled paper
4. Finally, remove the paper and let it take color
5. Serve with its sauce

title: Pato Assado à Moda do Porto (Duck Roasted Porto Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- bacon slices
- equal parts wine and water
- salt
- parsley
- one onion
- cloves
- one bay leaf
- pinch of cumin
- one tablespoon butter
- one tablespoon wheat flour

## Directions

1. Place the duck in a pan belly-side down on bacon slices
2. Cover with equal parts wine and water
3. Add salt, parsley, one onion, cloves, one bay leaf, and a pinch of cumin
4. Let simmer over moderate heat until cooked
5. Strain the broth and add one tablespoon of butter mixed with one tablespoon of wheat flour
6. Let reduce to two cups and serve with the duck

title: Pato Desossado e Assado no Espeto (Boned and Roasted Duck on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- one half pound veal meat
- handful carapicús
- four to six grated potatoes (if carapicús unavailable)
- two egg yolks
- parsley, chopped
- salt
- pepper
- two tablespoons butter
- thin bacon slices

## Directions

1. Open the duck along the back and remove the bones with some meat
2. Chop this meat with one half pound of veal meat
3. Mix in a handful of carapicús, or four to six grated potatoes if unavailable, two egg yolks, chopped parsley, salt, pepper, and two tablespoons of butter
4. Fill the duck with this mixture and sew it closed
5. Roast on a spit, wrapped in thin bacon slices
6. Serve with its own sauce, after removing the fat

title: Pato Ensopado com Carurú (Duck Stewed with Caruru)
body:
## Ingredients

- duck pieces
- three tablespoons fat
- one tablespoon wheat flour
- one bottle water
- one half cup vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- onion leaves
- parsley
- carurú, washed
- angú de fubá de moinho or pirão de farinha de mandioca

## Directions

1. Fry the duck pieces in three tablespoons of fat in which one tablespoon of wheat flour is already frying
2. Turn the duck pieces to brown on all sides
3. Add one bottle of water, one half cup of vinegar, salt, pepper, onion leaves, parsley, and a portion of washed carurú
4. Let simmer for half an hour uncovered over the fire
5. Serve with angú de fubá de moinho or pirão de farinha de mandioca

title: Pato ensopado com carurú (Duck Stewed with Okra)
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of duck
- three spoonfuls of fat
- one spoonful of wheat flour
- one bottle of water
- half a cup of vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- onion leaves
- parsley
- a portion of okra, washed

## Directions

1. Take the pieces of duck and fry them in three spoonfuls of fat in which wheat flour is already frying
2. Turn the pieces of duck to brown on all sides
3. Add one bottle of water, half a cup of vinegar, salt, pepper, onion leaves, parsley and a portion of washed okra
4. Let it boil for half an hour over uncovered fire
5. Serve with cornmeal mush or cassava flour pirão

title: Pato ensopado com nabos (Duck Stewed with Turnips)
body:
## Ingredients

- one duck
- wheat flour
- four spoonfuls of fat
- half a bottle of water
- same amount of white wine
- salt
- nutmeg
- parsley
- marjoram
- a portion of turnips
- two spoonfuls of butter
- one spoonful of wheat flour

## Directions

1. Take the duck, wrap it in wheat flour and fry it in four spoonfuls of fat, turning it on all sides to fry evenly
2. Add half a bottle of water and the same amount of white wine, salt, nutmeg, parsley and marjoram
3. When almost cooked, add a portion of turnips sautéed in two spoonfuls of butter with one spoonful of wheat flour
4. Let it cook over very low fire and serve

title: Pato Ensopado com Nabos (Duck Stewed with Turnips)
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- wheat flour

## Directions

1. Wrap the duck in wheat flour and fry it in fat

title: Pato frito com quibébe (Fried Duck with Squash Purée)
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of duck
- wheat flour
- egg yolks
- butter
- squash or pumpkin
- water
- salt
- fat

## Directions

1. Take the pieces of duck, wrap them in wheat flour, pass them through egg yolks, and wrap them again in wheat flour
2. Fry them in butter
3. Separately, boil a squash or pumpkin with the center removed in water and salt
4. When cooked, remove from water, scrape off the skin, pass the flesh through a sieve, and sauté it with two spoonfuls of fat
5. Place this purée on the plate, put the fried duck pieces on top and serve, pouring over it the butter in which the duck was fried

title: Pato guisado com carapicús (Duck Stewed with Carapicú)
body:
## Ingredients

- duck, cut in pieces
- half a pound of bacon
- water
- salt
- pepper
- marjoram
- portion of carapicú, chosen and blanched
- one spoonful of vinegar
- one spoonful of breadcrumb crumb
- breadcrumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut the duck in pieces and boil them with half a pound of bacon, water, salt, pepper and marjoram until cooked
2. Remove from fire, strain the broth and boil in it a portion of chosen and blanched carapicú, adding one spoonful of vinegar with one spoonful of bread crumb
3. Wrap the meat in breadcrumbs and fry it in plenty of butter
4. When the carapicú is cooked and the broth has reduced, serve on a platter, placing the fried duck pieces on top

title: Pato guisado com pinhões (Duck Stewed with Pine Nuts)
body:
## Ingredients

- one whole duck
- half a pound of bacon
- one pound of salted pork loin
- peppers
- one onion
- parsley
- celery root
- a portion of pine nuts, cooked and shelled
- juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Boil one whole duck with half a pound of bacon, one pound of salted pork loin, peppers, one onion, parsley and celery root
2. When cooked, reduce the broth to three cups of liquid
3. Place the duck in a casserole with this sauce, a portion of cooked and shelled pine nuts and the juice of one lemon
4. Let it boil for half an hour between coals and serve

title: Pato Recheado de Azeitonas e Assado (Duck Stuffed with Olives and Roasted)
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- washed olives
- palm hearts, chopped to same size as olives
- bacon slices
- peppers
- onion leaves
- parsley
- manjerona (marjoram)

## Directions

1. Fill the duck with washed olives and palm hearts chopped to the same size
2. Place in a casserole on bacon slices
3. Add peppers, onion leaves, parsley, and manjerona
4. Put in the oven and roast until cooked
5. Serve with its sauce and salads

title: Pato Refogado com Aboboras (Duck Braised with Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- one young duck
- one cup water
- one tablespoon vinegar
- salt
- one onion, cut
- four to six squash, cut into four to eight pieces

## Directions

1. Remove the meat from the duck bones
2. Pass the meat in fat until it is roasted
3. Add one cup of water, one tablespoon of vinegar, salt, and the cut onion
4. Add four to six squash cut into four to eight pieces, without peeling and without removing the center (the skin should still be as tender as the flesh itself)
5. Cover the pan and finish cooking near the fire
6. Serve

title: Pato Refogado com Cenouras (Duck Braised with Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice
- garlic
- old wine
- butter
- one cup water
- one glass brandy
- one half tablespoon wheat flour
- carrots

## Directions

1. Cut the duck into pieces and soak in salt, pepper, lemon juice, and garlic
2. After three hours in this old wine, fry on both sides in a little butter
3. Add one cup of water, one glass of brandy, and one half tablespoon of wheat flour
4. After one boil, add sliced carrots
5. Let simmer near the fire until everything is cooked and serve

title: Pato Refogado com Tayoba (Duck Braised with Tayoba)
body:
## Ingredients

- one fat duck
- one cup meat broth
- salt
- pepper
- onion leaves
- one tayoba root, boiled in water and salt
- one egg yolk
- one small spoon sugar
- one tablespoon vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the duck into pieces and pass through hot fat
2. When almost cooked, add one cup of meat broth, salt, pepper, onion leaves, and tayoba root that has been boiled in water and salt and cut into slices
3. Let simmer near the fire for one hour
4. Thicken the broth with an egg yolk beaten with one small spoon of sugar and one tablespoon of vinegar
5. Serve with its broth

title: Peito de Carneiro Grelhado (Grilled Lamb Breast)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb breast
- water
- salt
- breadcrumbs
- pepper
- parsley
- picante sauce

## Directions

1. Boil the lamb breast in water and salt until cooked
2. Remove and drain it
3. Wrap in grated breadcrumbs mixed with pepper, salt and chopped parsley
4. Cover with paper and roast on a grill over coals
5. Serve with a picante sauce

title: Peito de Carneiro Guisado (Stewed Lamb Breast)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb breast
- bacon strips
- pepper
- salt
- parsley
- celery root
- water
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- egg yolks
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Place the lamb breast in a pan on strips of bacon with pepper, salt, parsley and a celery root, so that it is covered with water
2. Let it boil until cooked
3. Remove the bones and cover with grated breadcrumbs moistened in butter
4. Roast on the grill and serve with a sauce made from the broth
5. Strain the broth, remove the fat and thicken with two egg yolks
6. Add one spoonful of vinegar

title: Peito de Ganso Frito com Hervilhas (Fried Goose Breast with Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- one goose breast, well fatty
- salt
- pepper
- dried peas or white beans
- water
- goose fat
- one dozen small onions

## Directions

1. Place the goose breast in a casserole with salt and pepper over moderate heat to render the fat first
2. Then increase the heat until the meat is cooked
3. Cook a portion of dried peas or white beans with salt and water separately
4. When cooked, drain and pass through a sieve
5. Sauté this pea purée in goose fat
6. Place the goose breast on a serving dish on top of the purée
7. Cook a dozen small onions in the same goose fat
8. Serve with the onions around the breast

title: Peito de Vacca à Hamburgueza (Beef Brisket Hamburg Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef brisket
- ham slices
- seasonings
- bacon slices
- onions
- carrots
- white wine
- cheese

## Directions

1. Cut the brisket into thick slices.
2. Layer thin slices of ham and seasonings on each slice.
3. Place these slices in a pan lined with bacon slices.
4. Put sliced onions, carrots, and more bacon slices on top.
5. Roast in the oven for two hours.
6. Pour half a bottle of white wine over them and finish cooking over moderate heat.
7. Place on a platter and cover with a layer of sliced cheese before serving.

title: Peito de Vitela Coberto com Geléia (Veal Breast Covered with Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- veal knuckle
- carrots
- salt
- pepper
- herb bouquet
- lemon

## Directions

1. Open the veal breast and fill with the stuffing indicated for braised breast
2. Cook with veal knuckle cut into pieces, carrots, salt, pepper and herb bouquet
3. When cooked, remove and pass the broth through a sieve
4. Remove the fat and let it thicken
5. Return the breast to the vessel and let it boil a little more
6. Place the breast on a platter, add some peeled lemon slices without seeds
7. Pour the broth over it and let it cool
8. Serve

title: Peito de Vitela com Mariscos (Veal Breast with Shellfish)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- water
- salt
- wine
- lemon juice
- breadcrumbs
- nutmeg
- butter
- shellfish

## Directions

1. Boil the breast in water and salt
2. Remove the skin
3. In a casserole, place a cup of wine, lemon juice, breadcrumbs, a little nutmeg and butter
4. Let it boil a little
5. Add the breast meat and the shellfish
6. Serve when cooked

title: Peito de Vitela com Palmito (Veal Breast with Palm Heart)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- lettuce leaves
- salt
- parsley
- palmito
- fat
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Cut the breast meat into equal pieces
2. Scald and cook with lettuce leaves, salt and parsley
3. When cooked, sauté chopped palm heart with a little fat
4. Add meat broth
5. Just before serving, add the meat to the casserole, give it another boil
6. Serve

title: Peito de Vitela com Palmito (Veal Breast with Palm Heart)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast meat
- lettuce leaves
- salt
- parsley
- palm heart, chopped
- a little fat
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Cut the veal breast meat into equal pieces
2. Blanch the meat
3. Cook with some lettuce leaves, salt and parsley
4. Once cooked, brown the chopped palm heart with a little fat
5. Add meat broth to it
6. Just before serving, place the meat in a casserole, give it another boil, and serve

title: Peito de Vitela Confeitada (Stuffed Veal Breast)
body:
## Ingredients

- melted butter
- scalded raisins
- dried biscuits, ground
- sugar
- salt
- one dozen pounded almonds
- eggs

## Directions

1. Make a stuffing with melted butter, scalded raisins, a portion of dried biscuits ground, a little sugar, little salt, one dozen pounded almonds, and some eggs
2. Lift the skin covering the breast
3. Fill the space with this stuffing, securing the skin with a few stitches
4. Once the breast is thus prepared, spit it and roast it over coals, moistening the meat with melted butter

title: Peito de Vitela Estufado (Braised Veal Breast)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- bacon
- veal meat
- salt
- parsley
- pepper
- nutmeg
- wheat flour
- two egg yolks
- bacon strips
- water
- white wine
- carrots
- onion
- garlic clove
- lemon juice
- peas or green beans

## Directions

1. Take the veal breast and split it in half
2. Fill it with a stuffing made of bacon, veal meat, salt, parsley, pepper, nutmeg, a little wheat flour and two egg yolks
3. Close the opening with stitches
4. Place it in the oven, after wrapping it in bacon strips
5. Add a glass of water mixed with another of white wine, carrots, salt, parsley, pepper, onion, and a garlic clove
6. Once cooked, serve with its sauce, after adding lemon juice; it can also be served with fresh peas or green beans

title: Peito de Vitela Refogado (Braised Veal Breast)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- bacon
- flour
- water
- capers
- carapicú
- lemon

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into pieces the size of a walnut
2. Fry a portion of chopped bacon
3. Add a little flour and let it brown
4. Add the veal meat, stir, and add a little water
5. Add some capers, some scalded and washed carapicú, and some peeled lemon slices without seeds
6. Let it boil
7. Serve when cooked

title: Peito do Ganso à Pommerania (Pomeranian Style Goose Breast)
body:
## Ingredients

- goose breasts from young fattened geese
- one ounce salt per breast
- one eighth ounce niter per breast
- one eighth ounce muscovado sugar per breast

## Directions

1. Soak the goose breasts in brine made with one ounce of salt, one-eighth ounce of niter, and one-eighth ounce of muscovado sugar per breast
2. Leave in this brine for eight days
3. Hang alternately in the sun and in smoke
4. Eat cooked or raw, like ham, to which they are very similar, though more tender and much more delicate

title: Peitos de Frango Guisados (Stewed Chicken Breasts)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken breasts
- bacon
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onion leaves
- wine
- meat broth
- egg yolks
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Remove breasts from chickens and cut into strips
2. Braise with chopped bacon, salt, pepper, chopped parsley, and onion leaves
3. When cooked, add a glass of wine and a little meat broth
4. Let boil a little more, remove the meat and strain the broth
5. Remove the fat, thicken the broth with two egg yolks
6. Put the meat back with peeled lemon slices, warm, and serve

title: Peitos de Peru Assados (Roasted Turkey Breasts)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey breasts from two turkeys
- salt pork strips
- salt
- nutmeg
- parsley
- bread crumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut the breasts from two turkeys and remove the meat in slices, along with the skin
2. Wrap each slice in salt pork strips and season with salt, nutmeg, and parsley
3. Roast all in a pan placed in the oven
4. When cooked, coat in bread crumbs and fry in butter until golden
5. Serve

title: Peitos de Peru Guisados (Stewed Turkey Breasts)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey breasts from two turkeys
- salt pork slices
- white wine
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- bay leaves
- vegetables

## Directions

1. Cut the breasts from two turkeys, leaving the bones in
2. Remove the skin and lard with thin salt pork strips
3. Simmer briefly in water
4. Cook in salt pork slices, white wine, salt, pepper, onion, and bay leaves
5. When cooked, remove and strain the sauce
6. Remove the fat and reduce the sauce to one tablespoon
7. Pour the sauce over the breasts and serve with fresh vegetables

title: Peitos de Vitela à Camponeza (Veal Breasts Peasant Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breasts
- water
- ham
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onion leaves
- wheat flour
- broth
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Place the veal breasts in cold water on the fire
2. Give them a boil, then remove and let cool
3. Cut into pieces that are not too large
4. Fry with chopped ham, a little salt, pepper, parsley, onion leaves, half a spoon of wheat flour
5. Add a cup of water or broth and let cook
6. When cooked, remove and pass the sauce through a sieve
7. Add three egg yolks, return to the fire for five to six minutes
8. Serve

title: Peixe Assado no Espeto (Roasted Fish on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- nutmeg
- half a bottle of white wine
- four to five tablespoons of sweet oil
- juice of one lemon
- two tablespoons of capers
- salt
- pepper
- one tablespoon of wheat flour
- one tablespoon of butter

## Directions

1. Thread the fish lengthwise onto a spit, tie the head and tail to keep them secure
2. Roast over a regular fire, moistening from time to time with a sauce made of half a bottle of white wine, four to five tablespoons of sweet oil, and the juice of one lemon
3. When the fish is roasted, remove it from the spit and place it on a plate
4. Serve alongside in a tureen the sauce after adding two tablespoons of capers, salt, pepper, one tablespoon of wheat flour beaten with one tablespoon of fried butter

title: Peixe Cascudo Cozido (Boiled Armored Catfish)
body:
## Ingredients

- five to six armored catfish
- one bottle of milk
- salt
- parsley
- pepper
- onion leaves
- one tablespoon of lard
- one tablespoon of cassava flour
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from five to six catfish
2. Boil one bottle of milk with salt, parsley, pepper and onion leaves
3. Add the fish and cook for a quarter hour
4. Remove the fish, strain the broth, melt one tablespoon of lard and mix with one tablespoon of cassava flour
5. Add the broth incorporated with two egg yolks, boil and serve over the fish

title: Peixe Cascudo Ensopado (Stewed Armored Catfish)
body:
## Ingredients

- four to five armored catfish
- one bottle of water
- one glass of brandy
- salt
- pepper
- clove
- parsley
- celery root
- dozen tomatoes
- one tablespoon of wheat flour

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from four to five catfish
2. Boil one bottle of water with one glass of brandy, salt, pepper, clove, parsley and celery root
3. Add the fish and let boil for a quarter hour
4. Remove the fish and fry a dozen tomatoes with one tablespoon of wheat flour
5. Add the strained fish broth, stir and reduce the broth
6. Pour the sauce over the fish, keep warm near the fire and serve

title: Pepinos Recheados (Stuffed Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- six large cucumbers
- one quarter pound of mutton meat
- one quarter pound of fresh bacon
- one chopped onion
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- two egg yolks
- one spoonful of wheat flour
- vinegar
- two spoonfuls of fat
- one glass of white wine

## Directions

1. Take six large cucumbers and pierce at the base so the seeds come out through the hole
2. Make a hash of one quarter pound of mutton meat, one quarter pound of fresh bacon, one chopped onion, parsley, salt, pepper, two egg yolks, and one spoonful of wheat flour
3. Fill the cucumbers with this stuffing and close the hole again
4. Scald the cucumbers with vinegar
5. Place in a casserole with two spoonfuls of fat and one glass of white wine
6. Put in the oven, let it boil and serve

title: Pepinos Refogados (Sautéed Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen large cucumbers
- wheat flour
- fat
- one glass of vinegar
- one cup of water
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Peel half a dozen large cucumbers
2. Cut into eight parts
3. Remove the seeds
4. Coat the pieces in wheat flour
5. Sauté in fat
6. Add one glass of vinegar, one cup of water, salt and pepper
7. Let boil for half an hour over moderate heat
8. Serve

title: Perdizes, Codornizes, etc. Assadas no Búrralho com Mandioca (Roasted Partridges, Quail, etc. in Coals with Cassava)
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges, quail, or similar birds
- salt
- pepper juice
- thin slices of bacon
- cassava
- milk
- sugar
- cinnamon
- egg yolks
- wine

## Directions

1. Rub the birds with salt and pepper juice
2. Wrap them in thin slices of bacon
3. Place them in the coals with some cassava
4. When cooked, remove them and unwrap the bacon
5. Serve with peeled cassava and a sauce made from milk boiled with one spoon of sugar, a little ground cinnamon, and two egg yolks dissolved in a glass of wine

title: Perdizes, Codornizes, etc. Cozidas com Chuchús (Partridges, Quail, etc. Cooked with Chayotes)
body:
## Ingredients

- partridge, quail, or similar bird
- half a quarter pound of bacon
- salt
- parsley
- peppers
- marjoram
- onion leaves
- ginger
- half a garlic clove
- coriander
- chayotes
- arrowroot
- bitter orange juice

## Directions

1. Boil the bird with half a quarter pound of bacon, salt, parsley, peppers, marjoram, onion leaves, ginger, half a garlic clove, and a little coriander
2. When cooked, remove the bird and strain the broth
3. Return the bird cut into pieces to the pan with a portion of chayotes and a small spoon of arrowroot dissolved in a glass of bitter orange juice
4. Boil for another half hour and serve

title: Perdizes, Codornizes, etc. Ensopadas com Guandús (Partridges, Quail, etc. Stewed with Pigeon Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- cornmeal flour
- fat
- partridge, quail, or similar bird
- cooked pigeon peas
- water
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Brown one spoon of cornmeal flour with two spoons of fat
2. Add the bird cut into pieces and turn on all sides
3. Add a portion of pigeon peas cooked in water and salt
4. Stir and boil for another half hour
5. Add a glass of vinegar at the end and serve

title: Perdizes, Codornizes, etc. Ensopadas com Moranga (Partridges, Quail, etc. Stewed with Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- partridge, quail, or similar bird
- fat
- water
- salt
- parsley
- peppers
- cumin
- onion leaves
- squash
- lemon juice
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Fry the bird in fat, then cut it into pieces
2. Add water, salt, parsley, peppers, cumin, onion leaves, and a squash peeled, with the seeds removed and cut into pieces
3. Boil until the bird is cooked and the broth is reduced
4. Serve after adding lemon juice beaten with an egg yolk

title: Perdizes, Codornizes, etc. Fritas com Capa (Fried Partridges, Quail, etc. with Coating)
body:
## Ingredients

- partridge, quail, or similar bird
- salt
- pepper juice
- garlic
- wheat flour
- egg yolks
- cinnamon
- sugar
- fat
- lemon

## Directions

1. Cut the birds into pieces and rub with salt, pepper juice, and garlic
2. Roll in wheat flour and pass through egg yolks
3. Dust with cinnamon and a little sugar
4. Fry in fat on both sides
5. Serve by placing a slice of peeled and squeezed lemon on each piece and pour lemon juice over all

title: Perdizes, Codornizes, etc. Fritas com Polpa de Beterraba (Fried Partridges, Quail, etc. with Beet Pulp)
body:
## Ingredients

- partridge, quail, or similar bird
- salt
- ground Indian pepper
- fat
- onion
- breadcrumbs
- red beets
- water
- vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Season the bird cut into pieces with salt and ground Indian pepper and fry in plenty of fat with chopped onion and a handful of breadcrumbs
2. Place the meat on a plate with the pulp of red beets cooked in water and salt, grated and mixed with a glass of vinegar
3. If too sour, add one spoon of sugar
4. Pour off the fat with the breadcrumbs and serve

title: Perdizes, Codornizes, etc. Fritas de Escabeche (Fried Partridges, Quail, etc. in Escabeche)
body:
## Ingredients

- partridge, quail, or similar bird
- bitter orange juice
- salt
- peppers
- half a garlic clove
- parsley
- onion leaves
- butter
- onion
- cornmeal or cassava flour

## Directions

1. Cut the birds into pieces and soak in bitter orange juice with salt, peppers, half a garlic clove, parsley, and onion leaves
2. After six hours, dry the pieces, fry them in butter with chopped onion
3. Dust with cornmeal or cassava flour and serve

title: Perdizes, Codornizes, etc. Guisadas com Abóbora d'Água (Partridges, Quail, etc. Stewed with Water Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- partridge, quail, or similar bird
- water
- salt
- fat
- wheat flour
- water squash
- parsley
- onion leaves
- coriander
- arrowroot
- bitter orange juice

## Directions

1. Boil the birds in water and salt
2. When almost cooked, remove them and cut into pieces, then fry in two spoons of fat
3. Brown with wheat flour
4. Add a portion of water squash peeled and cut into small pieces
5. Pour the broth over and add parsley, onion leaves, a little coriander, a small spoon of arrowroot, and the juice of a bitter orange
6. Let boil for half an hour, or preferably until the broth thickens, and serve

title: Perdizes, Codornizes, etc. Guisadas com Caratinga (Partridges, Quail, etc. Stewed with Caratinga)
body:
## Ingredients

- partridge, quail, or similar bird
- caratinga
- water
- salt
- onion
- tomatoes
- fat
- water
- lemon juice
- parsley
- half a garlic clove
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cook a bird with a caratinga in water and salt
2. When the bird is cooked, remove it and let the caratinga continue cooking until tender, which you can tell by piercing it easily with a feather, then remove it
3. Brown chopped onion and a dozen tomatoes in two spoons of fat
4. Add a cup of water, a glass of lemon juice, parsley, half a garlic clove, and half a spoon of sugar
5. Let boil once more and add the bird and caratinga cut into pieces
6. Boil a little more and serve

title: Perna de Carneiro com Carapicú (Lamb Leg with Carapicú)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb leg
- carapicú
- bacon
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- white wine

## Directions

1. Split the leg down to the bone
2. Remove about one pound of meat and chop it well with a handful of chosen carapicú that has been scalded
3. Place this filling inside the leg in place of the bone and sew up the opening
4. Lard with bacon seasoned with salt, pepper and nutmeg
5. Brown in fat and when it takes color add one bowl of white wine
6. Finish cooking over moderate fire
7. Serve with its sauce

title: Perna de Carneiro com Palmito à Goyana (Lamb Leg with Palm Heart Goyanese Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb leg
- salt
- aromatic herbs
- garlic
- onion
- clove
- palm hearts
- water
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Lard the leg and cook it with salt, a bunch of aromatic herbs, garlic, onion and clove
2. When cooked, pass the sauce through a sieve and remove the fat
3. Let it reduce to two or three cups of liquid
4. Place the leg on a platter garnished with palm hearts cooked in water and salt
5. Cover with grated cheese and pour sauce on top
6. Put in the oven to brown and serve thus

title: Perna de Carneiro Cozida (Boiled Lamb Leg)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb leg
- cloth
- twine
- water
- onions
- bay leaves
- parsley
- marjoram
- salt
- pepper
- saltpeter
- vegetables
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Wrap a lamb leg in a well-sealed cloth, tying it firmly with twine
2. Place in a pan that should be on the fire with boiling water
3. Add onions, bay leaves, parsley, marjoram, salt, pepper and a pinch of saltpeter
4. Let it cook for two hours
5. Serve after removing the cloth, with vegetables, potatoes or one of the sauces indicated for meat

title: Perna de Carneiro Cozida com Legumes (Boiled Lamb Leg with Vegetables)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb leg
- water
- peas
- green beans
- squash shoots
- salt
- parsley
- clove
- onion leaves
- bacon or ham

## Directions

1. Beat the leg well after removing the fat and trimming the bone
2. Scald it in hot water, letting it boil five minutes in this water
3. Remove and place in cold water, leaving it until it cools
4. Place again to cook with a portion of fresh peas, beans or squash shoots, salt, parsley, one clove, onion leaves year-round and a piece of lean bacon or ham
5. When all is cooked, separate the broth and evaporate until reduced to two cups of liquid
6. Then return the leg to it, letting it cook over coals and turning it until it begins to stick to the bottom of the pan and is a good color
7. Serve with the vegetables

title: Perna de Carneiro Cozida à Ingleza (Boiled Lamb Leg English Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb leg
- cloth
- water
- salt
- bay leaf
- English preserves or mustard

## Directions

1. Wrap the leg in a well-sealed cloth
2. Place it to cook in water that should already be boiling on the fire with salt and a bay leaf
3. Serve with English preserves or mustard

title: Perna de Carneiro Ficticia (Mock Lamb Leg)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb leg meat
- one pound of ham
- half pound of bacon
- salt
- pepper
- clove
- onion
- parsley
- garlic
- breadcrumbs
- four egg yolks
- cloth
- butter
- water

## Directions

1. Remove all the meat from the leg and chop it with one pound of ham, half pound of bacon, salt, pepper, clove, onion, parsley and garlic (all finely chopped), a handful of breadcrumbs and four egg yolks
2. Place this mixture around the bone on a cloth that is sewn around it, giving it the shape of a leg
3. Put it to cook in a pan in already boiling water
4. When cooked, remove the cloth and fry the meat in a pan with a little butter
5. Keep the pan on coals until the meat takes color, being careful when it sticks not to break its shape
6. Serve on toasted breadcrumbs fried in butter, placing on top its broth after being reduced and strained

title: Perna de Porco do Matto Refogada com Almeirões (Wild Boar Leg Braised with Chicory)
body:
## Ingredients

- one wild boar leg
- fat
- one cup of white wine
- salt
- pepper
- onions
- parsley
- chicory

## Directions

1. Braise the leg in a little fat
2. Add one cup of white wine, salt, pepper, cut onions, and parsley
3. Let braise well covered for two hours
4. Separately, boil chopped chicory in water and salt
5. Remove and braise the chicory with a little fat
6. Add the meat broth to the chicory
7. Let boil a little and serve with the wild boar leg

title: Perna de Porco do Matto Refogada com Carapicús (Wild Boar Leg Braised with Carapicús)
body:
## Ingredients

- one wild boar leg
- fat
- wheat flour
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice
- parsley
- celery
- one sprig of mint
- ginger
- one cup of water
- one cup of white wine
- carapicús

## Directions

1. Cut the leg into pieces
2. Fry in fat and when fried, dust with wheat flour
3. Add salt, pepper, lemon juice, parsley, celery, one sprig of mint, ginger, one cup of water, one cup of white wine, and cleaned and peeled carapicús
4. Let boil for one hour over a moderate fire
5. Serve

title: Perna de Porco do Matto Refogada com Pirão de Pinhões (Wild Boar Leg Braised with Pine Nut Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one wild boar leg
- two cups of meat broth
- fat
- salt
- pepper
- slices of peeled lemon
- pine nuts
- roasted peanuts
- white wine
- cow butter

## Directions

1. Cut the leg into slices and brown them a little on the grill on both sides
2. Place in a casserole with two cups of meat broth, a little fat, salt, pepper, and slices of peeled lemon
3. Let boil until cooked
4. Meanwhile, boil pine nuts and when cooked, peel and crush them to form a mass
5. Add roasted and crushed peanuts to this mass, salt, white wine, and cow butter as needed to form a polpa not too thick
6. Place the polpa over the plate with the leg slices
7. Pour the meat sauce over it and serve

title: Perna de Vacca Cozida (Cooked Beef Leg)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef leg
- water
- salt
- carrots
- onions
- clove
- cabbage leaves
- brandy
- wheat flour
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Take the beef and leave it to boil for five hours over good fire, having added to the water salt, carrots, onions, clove, the chopped leaves of a cabbage, and a glass of brandy
2. When the beef is cooked, remove it from the fire as well as the seasonings, arranging everything symmetrically on the plate
3. Leave the broth to thicken, adding at the end a little wheat flour and vinegar
4. Leave this sauce over the beef and vegetables before serving

title: Perna de Vitela Assada no Seu Caldo (Veal Leg Roasted in Its Own Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal leg
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chervil
- marjoram
- garlic clove
- vinegar
- wheat flour
- bacon slices
- green beans or small roasted potatoes

## Directions

1. Remove the cuts from the leg, lard them, place them in a mixture of salt, pepper, parsley, chervil, marjoram, a crushed garlic clove, a spoon of vinegar, for two hours
2. Then dust with wheat flour and place to roast in the oven, wrapped in very thin bacon slices
3. Once cooked, serve with its own sauce, after removing the fat, garnishing it with green beans or small roasted potatoes

title: Pernil Assado à Mineira (Roasted Ham Mineiro Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- vinegar
- salt
- garlic
- Indian pepper
- thyme
- bay leaf
- parsley
- chives
- water
- olives
- mangalhos

## Directions

1. Soak the ham for twenty-four hours in vinegar, salt, garlic, Indian pepper, thyme, bay leaf, parsley and chives, all finely chopped.
2. After this time, remove the ham, making holes in it into which the seasoning leaves are introduced, placing one or two selected olives in each hole.
3. Place it in a skillet with enough water to cover it completely and let it boil until the water is completely evaporated, making small holes with a stick from time to time to allow blood to escape and water to penetrate.
4. Place it in an oven with the same skillet, well heated, until it takes on a nice color.
5. Serve with boiled and toasted mangalhos in fat or butter.

title: Pernil de Porco do Matto da Assado na Grelha (Wild Boar Leg Grilled)
body:
## Ingredients

- one wild boar leg
- water
- salt
- parsley
- onions
- bay leaves
- pepper
- cassava flour
- fat
- lemon juice
- small potatoes

## Directions

1. Boil in water with salt, parsley, onions, bay leaves, and pepper one wild boar leg
2. When it has boiled two or three times, cut into slices the thickness of two fingers
3. Coat in cassava flour and grill, drizzling with a little fat and lemon juice
4. Serve with small potatoes

title: Peru Assado com Molho de Leite (Turkey Roasted with Milk Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- turkey liver
- calf liver
- bread crumbs
- red wine
- two tablespoons of sugar
- nutmeg
- cardamom
- salt

## Directions

1. Take the turkey and fill the breast with its liver and a piece of calf liver passed through a sieve and mixed with a handful of bread crumbs moistened with red wine
2. Add two tablespoons of sugar, grated nutmeg, cardamom, and a little salt
3. Spit the turkey and roast

title: Peru Assado à Italiana (Turkey Roasted Italian Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- turkey giblets
- livers from three or four chickens
- half a pound of salt pork
- grated cheese
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaves
- white wine
- butter
- paper
- onion leaves
- one glass of wine
- two tablespoons of butter
- one chicara of meat broth
- four egg yolks
- garlic

## Directions

1. Stuff the turkey with minced giblets, livers from three or four chickens, and half a pound of salt pork mixed with grated cheese, little salt, pepper, and bay leaves
2. Moisten everything well with white wine
3. Roast on a spit covered with salt pork slices and paper buttered with butter
4. Meanwhile, take onion leaves, salt them, and scald with hot water
5. Mince them very finely and place in a pan with one glass of wine, two tablespoons of butter, one chicara of meat broth, four egg yolks, salt, pepper, and a clove of garlic
6. Cook for a while
7. When the turkey is roasted, remove the salt pork and paper
8. Serve with this sauce

title: Peru Recheado e Assado no Forno (Stuffed and Roast Turkey in the Oven)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds veal
- dozen Nantes sardines
- half pound bacon
- handful of carapicús
- dozen olives
- peppers
- two onions
- two cloves
- handful of dried rusks
- four tablespoons butter
- four egg yolks
- nutmeg
- cinnamon
- tablespoon sugar
- salt
- bacon slices

## Directions

1. Take two pounds of veal, a dozen Nantes sardines, half a pound of bacon, a handful of carapicús, a dozen olives, some peppers, two onions and two cloves, chop it all very finely and fill the cavity of the turkey's belly with this mixture.
2. On the other hand, knead a handful of dried rusks ground with four tablespoons of butter, four egg yolks, nutmeg, cinnamon, a tablespoon of sugar and a little salt.
3. With this mixture, fill the crop of the turkey.
4. Wrapped in thin bacon slices, place it in a pan to roast in the oven and serve with its sauce.

title: Peru Refogado (Braised Turkey)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- four tablespoons of lard
- one bottle of water
- two tablespoons of wheat flour
- salt
- bay leaves
- pepper
- onion
- four egg yolks
- one glass of wine
- lemon juice
- mangarito root

## Directions

1. Cut the turkey into pieces and braise in four tablespoons of lard
2. Add one bottle of water and two tablespoons of wheat flour
3. Add salt, bay leaves, pepper, and chopped onion
4. Finish cooking the turkey
5. Remove the meat and strain the broth
6. Beat four egg yolks with one glass of wine and a little lemon juice into the broth
7. Pour this sauce over the turkey placed in the center of a quantity of mangarito root well fried in butter
8. Serve

title: Peru Refogado com Beringela Roxa (Braised Turkey with Purple Eggplant)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- one pound of minced salt pork
- parsley
- onion
- pepper
- cloves
- one tablespoon of wheat flour
- one chicara of red wine
- six purple eggplants
- one tablespoon of sugar
- three egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut the turkey into pieces and place in a pan with one pound of minced salt pork, parsley, onion, pepper, and cloves
2. When the salt pork is fried, remove the turkey pieces and the rinds
3. Add one tablespoon of wheat flour to the fat
4. When cooled, add one chicara of red wine
5. Add six purple eggplants that have been parboiled in hot water, one tablespoon of sugar, and three egg yolks
6. Return to the fire
7. When simmering, add the turkey meat and finish cooking near the fire
8. Serve

title: Peru Refogado com Cará (Braised Turkey with Cará Root)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey meat
- lard
- cará root
- one tablespoon of wheat flour
- one bowl of meat broth
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- marjoram
- two egg yolks
- two tablespoons of lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut the turkey meat into pieces after separating the bones
2. Fry briefly in hot lard and remove
3. Add some cará root cut into slices to the same lard and stir
4. Add one tablespoon of wheat flour
5. Add one bowl of meat broth, salt, parsley, onion leaves, and a little marjoram
6. After one boil, add the meat and cook near the fire until everything is well cooked
7. Add the sauce with two egg yolks beaten in two tablespoons of lemon juice
8. Serve

title: Peru Refogado com Castanhas do Para (Braised Turkey with Brazil Nuts)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- melted lard
- hot water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- Brazil nuts

## Directions

1. Take the turkey cut into pieces and braise in melted lard, turning the pieces until they take on good color evenly
2. Add a glass of hot water, salt, minced parsley, onion leaves, and a quantity of Brazil nuts
3. Simmer over moderate fire for two to three hours
4. Serve with the Brazil nuts

title: Perã Ensopado com Ora-pro-nobis (Turkey Stew with Ora-pro-nobis)
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons of butter
- turkey pieces
- one cup of water
- two cups of boiled milk
- one garlic clove
- salt
- nutmeg
- two egg yolks
- one tablespoon of sugar
- a handful of ora-pro-nobis leaves

## Directions

1. Melt two tablespoons of butter, and when well hot, add the turkey pieces, turning them to fry on both sides
2. Remove from heat and let cool to then add one cup of water with two cups of boiled milk, one garlic clove, salt, nutmeg and two egg yolks
3. Return everything to heat and after boiling for half an hour over moderate heat, add one tablespoon of sugar and a handful of ora-pro-nobis leaves
4. Boil a little more and serve

title: Perã Estufado à Moda (Turkey Braised in the Manner)
body:
## Ingredients

- one turkey
- one glass of white wine
- two tablespoons of butter

## Directions

1. Take the cleaned turkey and roast it on the spit, moistening it with a glass of white wine mixed with two tablespoons of butter
2. When half cooked, remove it from the spit and place it in a pan with the sauce that dripped
3. Roast it, turning it from time to time
4. When cooked, serve with salads

title: Perã Frito com Beterraba (Turkey Fried with Beets)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey pieces
- bread crumbs
- butter
- one or more beet roots
- water
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Take the turkey pieces, coat them in bread crumbs and fry in plenty of butter
2. On the other hand take one or more beet roots which boil in hot salted water
