3. Then peel and grate them, refrying this mixture with a little butter and one tablespoon of vinegar
4. Pour this over the plate with the turkey pieces

title: Perú Assado com Môlho de Leite (Roast Turkey with Milk Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- turkey liver
- veal liver
- handful of grated bread
- red wine
- two tablespoons sugar
- nutmeg
- cardamom
- salt
- paper
- butter
- two cups milk
- two egg yolks
- wheat flour

## Directions

1. Take the turkey and fill the breast with its liver and a piece of veal liver passed through a sieve, mixed with a handful of grated bread moistened with red wine, adding two tablespoons of sugar, grated nutmeg, cardamom and a little salt.
2. Spit it and roast it, covered with paper brushed with butter.
3. When cooked, remove the paper.
4. Make a sauce with two cups of milk, two egg yolks, a little butter, salt and a tablespoon of wheat flour.
5. Pour this sauce by spoonfuls over the turkey, continuing to roast it until it takes on a good color.
6. Serve with its sauce.

title: Perú Assado com Pinhões (Roast Turkey with Pine Nuts)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- salt
- garlic
- onion
- lemon juice
- cooked and peeled pine nuts
- cooked eggs
- half dozen bananas
- lemon slices
- bacon

## Directions

1. Soak the turkey, brushed with salt, garlic, onion and lemon juice, for four hours.
2. Then fill it with cooked and peeled pine nuts, cut cooked eggs, half a dozen sliced bananas and peeled lemon slices without seeds.
3. Place it in a pan lined with bacon and roast it.
4. When cooked, serve it with its own sauce after removing the fat.

title: Perú Assado com Sardinhas (Roast Turkey with Sardines)
body:
## Ingredients

- two chicken breasts
- dozen sardines
- turkey meat
- pine nuts
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- two onions
- marjoram
- bacon
- paper

## Directions

1. Cut two chicken breasts into long strips, as well as a dozen sardines split into four strips each.
2. Mix with chopped turkey meat, a portion of split pine nuts, salt, pepper, parsley, two chopped onions and a little marjoram.
3. Take the turkey, open it along the back, remove the bones and some meat to chop, and fill it with the mixture.
4. Cook it and roast it on the spit, covered with bacon slices and buttered paper.
5. When cooked, serve it with its own sauce.

title: Perú Assado Imitando Caça (Roast Turkey Imitating Game)
body:
## Ingredients

- young turkey
- juniper berries
- cumin seeds
- strong vinegar
- cloves
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaves
- peeled apples
- parsley
- marjoram
- bacon

## Directions

1. Take a young turkey and fill it with juniper berries and cumin seeds.
2. Soak it in strong vinegar with cloves, salt, pepper and bay leaves.
3. After twelve hours, remove the juniper berries and cumin seeds from the soaking liquid and replace them with peeled apples without seeds and split, with a few sprigs of parsley and marjoram.
4. Lard the breast with fine strips of bacon and roast it in the oven in a pan lined with bacon slices.
5. When roasted, serve it with its sauce.

title: Perú Assado no Espeto (Roast Turkey on the Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- fine bacon
- salt
- parsley juice
- onion
- paper
- butter
- wine

## Directions

1. Take the turkey, lard it with fine bacon, rub it with salt, parsley juice and onion.
2. Wrap it in paper and roast it on the spit, basting it from time to time with a sauce made of two tablespoons of butter and a cup of wine.
3. When almost cooked, remove the paper, allow it to brown, and serve.

title: Perú Assado no Espeto à Fluminense (Roast Turkey on the Spit Fluminense-Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- salt
- ground pepper
- onion
- parsley
- lemon juice
- giblets from four chickens
- carapicús
- half pound bacon
- three egg yolks
- nutmeg
- bacon slices
- grated bread

## Directions

1. Sprinkle the turkey with salt, ground pepper, onion and finely chopped parsley.
2. Wrap it in cloth and moisten it with lemon juice.
3. After twenty-four hours, remove the cloth and fill it with the giblets from four chickens, a handful of carapicús, one onion, half a pound of bacon, all finely chopped.
4. Add three egg yolks, salt and nutmeg, and fill the turkey with the mixture.
5. Spit it and roast it, wrapped in very fine bacon slices.
6. When cooked, remove the rest of the bacon, wrap the turkey in grated bread and continue roasting it until it takes on sufficient color.
7. Serve with its sauce after removing the fat.

title: Perú Assado no Espeto à Moda Allemã (Roast Turkey on the Spit German-Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- bacon
- grated bread

## Directions

1. Brush the turkey with salt and pepper and wrap it in a cloth moistened with vinegar.
2. The next day, place it on the spit, wrapped in fine bacon slices, and roast it.
3. When cooked, remove the bacon, wrap the turkey in grated bread, moistening it with the sauce that dripped down.
4. Allow it to take color and serve.

title: Perú Assado no Forno com Môlho (Roast Turkey in the Oven with Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- bacon strips
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- garlic
- onion
- cloves
- cup of white wine
- four cups water

## Directions

1. Lard the breast of the turkey with strips of bacon seasoned with salt, pepper, chopped parsley and garlic.
2. Place it in a pan over bacon slices with a cut onion, salt, cloves, a cup of white wine and four cups of water.
3. Turn it from time to time after placing it in the oven.
4. When cooked, serve with its own strained sauce, or with beans, peas, spinach etc.

title: Perú de Escabeche Assado no Espeto (Turkey Escabeche Roast on the Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- whey
- salt
- sugar
- cloves
- garlic
- bacon
- grated bread
- cream

## Directions

1. Soak the turkey for twenty-four hours in whey with salt, a little sugar, cloves and a clove of garlic.
2. Wrap it in bacon slices and spit it to roast.
3. When almost cooked, remove the bacon, wrap it in grated bread and finish roasting it until it takes on a good color, basting it with cream.
4. Serve at the table.

title: Perú Ensopado com Chuchú (Turkey Stew with Chuchú)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftovers of roasted turkey
- two tablespoons of fat
- a portion of peeled and cut chuchús
- salt
- some peppers
- parsley
- onion
- one bowl of meat broth
- one tablespoon of cornmeal

## Directions

1. Take the leftovers of roasted turkey, cut them into pieces and add them with two tablespoons of fat, a portion of peeled and cut chuchús, salt, some peppers, parsley, onion, a bowl of meat broth and one tablespoon of cornmeal
2. Leave to boil for one hour over moderate heat
3. Serve with cornmeal porridge

title: Perú Ensopado com Quiabos (Turkey Stew with Okra)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of bacon
- two tablespoons of wheat flour
- turkey wings, legs and neck
- one bowl of water
- one glass of white wine
- salt
- parsley
- pepper
- clove
- some chopped carrots
- a portion of okra
- half a dozen tomatoes

## Directions

1. Fry half a pound of bacon and after melting remove the cracklings
2. Add two tablespoons of wheat flour to the hot fat, stir and then add the turkey wings, legs and neck
3. Let them fry a little, turning the pieces and add one bowl of water, one glass of white wine, salt, parsley, pepper, clove, some chopped carrots, a portion of okra and half a dozen tomatoes
4. Leave to boil for one hour over very moderate heat until the broth is almost dry
5. Serve

title: Perú Ensopado de Môlho Pardo (Turkey Stew with Brown Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey blood
- one cup of vinegar
- turkey wings, giblets, legs and neck
- one pound of veal cut into pieces
- two tablespoons of fat
- one bowl of meat broth or water
- a handful of raisins
- some peeled and cut apples
- salt
- sugar
- nutmeg
- clove
- one cut onion
- one tablespoon of wheat flour

## Directions

1. When killing the turkey, collect its blood in a bowl containing one cup of vinegar
2. After cleaning the turkey, take the wings, giblets, legs, neck, one pound of cut veal and fry everything with two tablespoons of fat
3. In another pan over heat place one containing the blood and one bowl of meat broth or water, a handful of raisins, some peeled and cut apples, salt, sugar, nutmeg, clove, one cut onion and one tablespoon of wheat flour
4. While boiling, add to this sauce the turkey and veal meat already fried
5. Leave to boil for two hours
6. Serve with cooked macaroni or wheat flour balls mixed with eggs

title: Perú Estufado com Cogumelos (Turkey Braised with Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey pieces
- two handfuls of mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- marjoram
- parsley
- two glasses of wine
- two tablespoons of fat
- two cups of meat broth or water
- one tablespoon of vinegar

## Directions

1. Take the turkey pieces, two handfuls of mushrooms, salt, pepper, marjoram, parsley, two glasses of wine, two tablespoons of fat and two cups of meat broth or water
2. Put everything in a pan and seal the lid
3. Place over moderate heat and leave to boil for three to four hours
4. Then add one tablespoon of vinegar and serve

title: Perú Estufado com Gerumbeba (Turkey Braised with Gerumbeba Fruit)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey legs, wings and neck
- one quarter pound of chopped bacon
- a dozen fruits of figueira do inferno singed and peeled
- salt
- clove
- onion
- four glasses of white wine

## Directions

1. Cut the turkey legs, wings and neck and place in a pan with one quarter pound of chopped bacon, a dozen fruits of figueira do inferno singed and peeled, salt, clove, onion and four glasses of white wine
2. Seal the lid
3. Leave to boil over moderate heat or in the oven for two to three hours
4. When cooked, place the meat on a plate, the fruits around it with some peeled lemon slices on top, and serve

title: Perú Estufado com Grelos de Abobora (Turkey Braised with Squash Sprouts)
body:
## Ingredients

- one turkey
- wheat flour
- half a pound of chopped bacon
- salt
- nutmeg
- one onion studded with cloves
- a portion of peeled and scalded squash sprouts
- four cups of meat broth or water
- one egg yolk
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Take the turkey, cut it into pieces, coat them in wheat flour and place in a pan with half a pound of chopped bacon, salt, nutmeg, one onion studded with cloves, a portion of peeled and scalded squash sprouts and four cups of meat broth or water
2. Seal the lid carefully
3. Leave to boil for two hours over coals or near the fire
4. When cooked, place the sprouts on the plate covering them with a thin layer of bread crumbs
5. Place the turkey pieces on top and pour over all the strained and thickened sauce with one egg yolk
6. Serve

title: Perú Feito com Molho de Mostarda e Leite (Turkey with Mustard and Milk Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey pieces
- wheat flour
- butter
- milk
- cornstarch
- sugar
- English mustard powder
- white wine

## Directions

1. Fry the turkey pieces, coated in wheat flour, in butter on both sides
2. When well cooked and browned, serve with the following sauce: boil two cups of milk with a pinch of cornstarch and two spoons of sugar
3. Remove from heat and mix in two spoons of English mustard powder and one glass of white wine

title: Perú Frito com Alcachofras (Turkey Fried with Artichokes)
body:
## Ingredients

- one turkey
- salt
- chopped parsley
- onion
- garlic
- comari pepper
- three tablespoons of fat

## Directions

1. Cut the turkey into pieces and soak with salt, chopped parsley, onion, garlic and comari pepper for four to six hours
2. Place three tablespoons of fat on the heat and when well hot add the turkey pieces
3. Fry on both sides and place on a plate, garnishing them around the bottom with artichokes, scattering chopped parsley over all and pouring hot fat on top

title: Perú Frito com Batatinhas (Fried Turkey with Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey legs
- potatoes, peeled
- butter
- parsley, chopped
- lemon, peeled and sliced

## Directions

1. Take the turkey legs and a portion of peeled potatoes and fry them in butter over moderate heat
2. When cooked, remove them with a skimmer
3. Place on a plate, covering them with a portion of chopped parsley and some slices of peeled lemon

title: Perú Frito com Batatinhas (Turkey Fried with Small Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey pieces
- a portion of peeled small potatoes
- butter
- chopped parsley
- peeled lemon slices

## Directions

1. Take the turkey pieces and a portion of peeled small potatoes which fry in butter over moderate heat
2. When cooked, remove with a skimmer
3. Place on the plate covering them with a portion of chopped parsley and some peeled lemon slices

title: Perú Frito com Beterraba (Fried Turkey with Beets)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey pieces
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- beets
- water
- salt
- vinegar
- bread
- onion

## Directions

1. Take the turkey pieces, coat them in breadcrumbs, and fry them in plenty of butter
2. On the other hand, take one or more beet roots, which are boiled in water and salt
3. Then they are peeled and grated, sautéing this mass with a little butter and a spoon of vinegar
4. Pour this onto the plate with the turkey pieces on top, covering everything with small pieces of bread and chopped onion, both fried in butter

title: Perú Frito com Palmitos (Fried Turkey with Palm Hearts)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- salt
- butter
- onion, chopped
- palm heart
- water
- breadcrumbs
- ground pepper

## Directions

1. Take the turkey, cut it into pieces, salt them, and fry them in butter with a chopped onion
2. Place on a plate over a palm heart cut into long pieces, cooked in water and salt, and covered with a layer of breadcrumbs
3. Sprinkle everything with ground pepper and pour over it all the butter in which the turkey was fried, and serve

title: Perú Frito Dourado (Golden Fried Turkey)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey pieces
- wheat flour
- butter
- egg yolks
- white wine
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Coat the turkey pieces in wheat flour and fry them on both sides in a little butter
2. When cooked, pour over them by the spoon a mixture of four egg yolks, one spoon of wheat flour, one glass of wine with grated nutmeg
3. After a little time, serve

title: Perú Frito à Parmezão (Turkey Fried Parmesan Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey pieces
- salt
- ground pepper
- bread crumbs
- egg yolks
- butter
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Take the turkey pieces, season with salt and ground pepper, coat in bread crumbs, then pass through egg yolks and fry in a little butter
2. When cooked, place on a plate and cover with a layer of grated cheese
3. Place in the oven and let take on good color and serve

title: Perú Guisado com Chicórias (Stewed Turkey with Chicory)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- cumin
- breadcrumbs
- eggs
- butter
- white wine

## Directions

1. Boil the turkey cut into pieces in water, salt, parsley, onion leaves and a pinch of cumin
2. When cooked, remove the meat, coat it in breadcrumbs and pass it through eggs, then fry it in butter
3. Reduce the broth to one cup, strain it and mix it with another cup of white wine
4. Pour it over the meat, placed over sautéed chicory

title: Perú Guisado com Giló (Stewed Turkey with Giló)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey pieces
- water
- salt
- cornstarch
- white wine
- cloves
- nutmeg
- giló

## Directions

1. Boil the turkey pieces in water and salt until cooked
2. Remove the pieces and separate the meat from the bones, cutting it into smaller pieces
3. Thicken the broth, and when little remains, add to it one spoon of cornstarch, dissolved in one cup of white wine, two cloves, a little grated nutmeg
4. Let it boil a little and place on a plate, garnishing it around with gilos boiled in water and salt, and serve

title: Perú Guisado com Tayoba (Stewed Turkey with Tayoba)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- wheat flour
- butter
- tayoba
- egg yolks
- vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take the turkey, cut it into pieces, and boil these in water, salt, parsley and onion leaves
2. When almost cooked, remove them and place in the same water to cook a tayoba root, cut into pieces
3. Coat the meat in wheat flour, frying it in butter on both sides
4. When the tayoba is cooked, pour it around the meat, pouring over it the reduced and thickened broth with two egg yolks, dissolved in one spoon of vinegar and one spoon of sugar, and serve thus

title: Perú Guisado no Leite (Turkey Stewed in Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- milk
- salt
- onion leaves
- parsley
- butter
- bread
- cheese, grated
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut the turkey into pieces and boil them in milk, salt, onion leaves and parsley
2. When cooked, remove them and fry them in butter, arranging them on slices of fried bread and then moistened with milk
3. Cover everything with a layer of grated cheese, sugar and cinnamon and take the plate to the oven, where you let it cook until it takes on a good color, and serve

title: Perú Lardeado e Assado no Espeto (Larded and Roast Turkey on the Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- young turkey
- fine bacon
- vinegar
- cloves
- bay leaves
- onion
- pepper
- salt
- cup of full-fat milk

## Directions

1. Take a young turkey and lard the breast with fine bacon.
2. Soak it in vinegar with cloves, bay leaves, onion, pepper and salt.
3. After four hours, spit it and roast it, basting it with a cup of full-fat milk and then with the sauce that dripped down.
4. Serve with salads.

title: Perú Recheado de Carne (Turkey Stuffed with Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of veal
- a dozen Nantes sardines
- half a pound of bacon
- a handful of carapicús
- a dozen olives
- some peppers
- two onions
- two cloves
- a handful of dried rusks ground
- four tablespoons of butter
- four egg yolks
- nutmeg
- cinnamon
- one tablespoon of sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. Mince everything very finely and fill the cavity of the turkey's belly with this mixture
2. Knead a handful of dried rusks ground with four tablespoons of butter, four egg yolks, nutmeg, cinnamon, one tablespoon of sugar and a little salt
3. Fill the turkey's crop with this mixture
4. Wrap in thin slices of bacon
5. Place in a pan to roast in the oven
6. Serve with its sauce

title: Perú Recheado de Fructas e Assado (Turkey Stuffed with Fruit and Roasted)
body:
## Ingredients

- six quinces
- a dozen chuchús peeled and cut into small pieces
- a dozen plums
- a handful of scalded raisins
- a handful of bread crumb softened in white wine
- two tablespoons of sugar
- cinnamon
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Take six quinces, a dozen peeled and cut chuchús, a dozen plums, a handful of scalded raisins and a handful of bread crumb softened in as much white wine as needed, two tablespoons of sugar, cinnamon and nutmeg
2. Mix everything and fill the turkey with the mixture
3. Place it to roast in the oven in a pan over slices of bacon
4. When cooked, serve

title: Perú Recheado e Assado no Espeto (Stuffed and Roast Turkey on the Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- pound of cooked ham
- pound of veal
- half pound bacon
- four cooked eggs
- parsley
- two onions
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- handful of bread crumb
- white wine
- two tablespoons butter
- dozen ground peanuts
- quarter measure of raisins
- two tablespoons sugar
- nutmeg
- cardamom
- paper

## Directions

1. Chop a pound of cooked ham, a pound of veal, half a pound of bacon, four cooked eggs, parsley, two onions, salt, pepper and cloves, and fill the cavity of the turkey with this mixture.
2. On the other hand, knead a handful of bread crumb softened in white wine, two tablespoons of butter, a dozen ground peanuts, quarter measure of raisins scalded in hot water, two tablespoons of sugar, a little salt, nutmeg and cardamom.
3. Fill the crop of the turkey with this mixture.
4. Lard the breast with very thin strips of bacon, place the turkey on the spit and roast it wrapped in buttered paper, basting it with a cup of wine mixed with four tablespoons of butter.
5. When roasted, serve.

title: Perú Refogado com Mangaritos (Sautéed Turkey with Mangaritos)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey pieces
- water
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- parsley
- onion
- mangaritos
- lard
- wheat flour
- butter
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take the turkey pieces and cook them in water, salt, pepper, cloves, parsley and onion with a portion of mangaritos, and fry everything in four spoons of lard (if the mangaritos are large, cut them into slices)
2. Pass the broth through a cloth, and mix it with one spoon of wheat flour
3. Fry it in a little butter and lemon juice; reduce it to few cups, and pour it over the meat and the fried mangaritos, placed on a plate, to then be taken to the table

title: Pescada Assada na Grelha (Grilled Hake)
body:
## Ingredients

- hake
- two egg yolks
- one tablespoon of melted butter
- salt
- breadcrumbs
- butter for humidifying
- parsley
- chervil
- vinegar
- wheat flour

## Directions

1. Wash and clean perfectly a good fish, open the belly, and remove the skin starting from the tail to the head
2. Beat two egg yolks with one tablespoon of melted butter, and coat the fish sprinkled with salt with this mixture
3. Wrap it then in breadcrumbs, humidifying with butter, and covering again with a new layer of breadcrumbs
4. Grill it
5. Chop a little parsley and chervil and add one cup of vinegar, let it soak for a quarter of an hour; then strain, squeeze and mix two tablespoons of melted butter and one tablespoon of wheat flour
6. Heat it a little over the fire and serve with the fish

title: Pescada Estufada (Braised Hake)
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoons of butter
- parsley
- chopped onion leaves
- two or three fish
- wheat flour
- salt
- peppers
- coals
- one tablespoon of wheat flour
- two egg yolks
- one glass of wine

## Directions

1. Spread two tablespoons of butter on a dish that can withstand the heat of the fire
2. Sprinkle with chopped parsley and onion leaves
3. Place two or three fish wrapped in wheat flour, salt and peppers on top, and cook them over coals
4. When almost cooked, remove them, strain the butter into a casserole, mix it with one tablespoon of wheat flour, two egg yolks and one glass of wine
5. Boil a little more, pour over the fish and serve

title: Pescada Frita (Fried Hake)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh hake
- salt
- ground peppers
- cassava flour or breadcrumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. This fish should only be fried if very fresh
2. Clean it, remove the skin, sprinkle with salt and ground peppers and wrap in cassava flour or breadcrumbs
3. Put it in the frying pan with hot butter; when cooked serve it

title: Pescoço de Carneiro Assado na Grelha (Grilled Lamb Neck)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb neck meat
- Cold water
- Salt
- Parsley
- Onion leaves
- Bread crumbs
- Melted butter
- Spicy sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the neck meat into slices
2. Place over fire with cold water, salt, parsley, and onion leaves
3. Boil for half an hour
4. Remove and wrap with bread crumbs
5. Cover with a little melted butter
6. Roast on the grill
7. Serve with spicy sauce

title: Pescoço de Carneiro Ensopado (Stewed Lamb Neck)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb neck meat
- Melted fat
- Bowl of meat broth
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Onion leaves
- Potatoes
- Cará root
- Peas
- Beans or mangaritos

## Directions

1. Braise the neck meat in a little melted fat
2. Add a bowl of meat broth, salt, pepper, parsley, and onion leaves
3. Cook until done
4. Serve with stewed English potatoes, cará root, braised peas, roasted beans or mangaritos

title: Picadinho de Vitella Assado (Minced Roasted Veal)
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted veal meat
- meat broth
- raisins
- lemon slices
- dried breadcrumbs or crackers
- sugar
- one glass wine

## Directions

1. Cut the roasted meat into small pieces
2. Add the substance that comes with the meat and meat broth
3. Place over fire with raisins, lemon slices, ground dried breadcrumbs or crackers, a little sugar, and one glass wine
4. Give it another boil and serve

title: Picado de Vitela (Veal Hash)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal meat
- bacon
- ham or salted pork meat
- seasonings
- egg yolks
- bacon slices
- wine
- potatoes
- lemon juice or vinegar

## Directions

1. Take veal meat, bacon, a piece of ham or salted pork meat, seasonings, some egg yolks
2. Place this mixture in a casserole lined with bacon slices, also putting some on top
3. Moisten with a glass of wine and roast in the oven or over coals
4. Serve with potatoes cooked in butter before serving; a little lemon juice or vinegar can be added

title: Pike, Trahira, etc. Roasted on a Spit (Lucio, trahira, etc. assados no espeto)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish
- Fine strips of bacon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Half bottle of white wine
- Four to five spoons of sweet olive oil
- Lemon juice
- Two spoons of capers
- One spoon of wheat flour
- One spoon of fried butter

## Directions

1. Remove scales and clean the fish
2. Lard the loins with fine strips of bacon well seasoned with salt, pepper, and nutmeg
3. Insert on the spit lengthwise
4. Tie the head and tail securely
5. Roast over regular fire, occasionally dampening with a sauce made of half a bottle of white wine, four to five spoons of sweet olive oil, and lemon juice
6. When roasted, remove from spit and place on a plate
7. Serve with the sauce in a tureen, after adding two spoons of capers, salt, pepper, one spoon of wheat flour beaten with one spoon of fried butter

title: Pike, Trahira, etc. Roasted on the Grill (Lucio, trahira, etc. assados na grelha)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish cleaned
- Butter

## Directions

1. Take the cleaned fish and wrap in well-buttered paper
2. Roast on the grill
3. When roasted, remove the paper

title: Pike, Trahira, etc. Sautéed (Lucio, trahira, etc. refogados)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish cleaned
- Butter
- One glass of white wine
- Same amount of water
- Salt
- Parsley
- Onion
- Pepper
- Manioc flour for pirão
- Fish broth

## Directions

1. Take the cleaned fish and pass in butter, turning on all sides
2. Add one glass of white wine and the same amount of water, salt, parsley, onion, and pepper
3. Let boil until cooked
4. Serve dry and hot with pirão made of manioc flour and the fish broth

title: Pike, Trahira, Tubarana, or Piranha Stewed (Lucio, trahira, tubarana, pyranha ensopados)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish scaled and cleaned
- Red wine
- Cloves
- Bay leaves
- Onions
- Parsley
- Serpol
- Salt
- Pepper
- One spoon of butter
- Vinegar
- Sweet olive oil

## Directions

1. Boil the scaled and cleaned fish in enough red wine with cloves, bay leaves, onions, parsley, serpol, salt and pepper
2. When colored, add one spoon of butter
3. Let boil a little more and serve with the sauce
4. When wanting to eat it cold, make a sauce with vinegar, sweet olive oil, salt and pepper, and omit the fish sauce

title: Pombas Torquazes Assadas na Panela (Roasted Pigeons in a Pan)
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- fat
- salt
- parsley
- bread crumbs
- borage leaves
- vinegar
- olive oil

## Directions

1. Braise the pigeons in fat and turn them to fry on all sides
2. Sprinkle with salt, parsley, a handful of bread crumbs, and cover the pan
3. Let it finish boiling over coals and serve with a sauce made of chopped borage leaves, salt, vinegar and olive oil

title: Pombas Torquazes Assados e Recheados (Roasted Stuffed Pigeons)
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- pigeon livers
- bacon
- chicken livers from two chickens
- olives without pits
- peppers
- half a garlic clove
- banana leaves

## Directions

1. Stuff the pigeons with their livers chopped and mixed with half a quarter of bacon, the livers of two chickens, some pitted olives, peppers and half a garlic clove, all chopped
2. Lard the breast with thin strips of bacon
3. Wrap in banana leaves and roast over moderate fire
4. When cooked, serve with some spicy sauce

title: Pombas Torquazes Conservados (Preserved Pigeons)
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- salt
- pepper juice
- water
- fat

## Directions

1. Take clean pigeons and rub them with salt and pepper juice, leaving them in these seasonings for three hours to be impregnated
2. Boil them for a quarter hour in water
3. Remove, let cool, and dry them
4. Place them in a barrel with plenty of fat and let them boil until nearly cooked
5. Remove them and pour the melted fat over them, ensuring the pigeons are covered by it
6. Seal the barrel and keep it in this state until needed

title: Pombas Torquazes Fritas com Fatias (Roasted Pigeons Fried with Bread Slices)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat from pigeons, doves, etc.
- half a quarter of bacon
- a handful of carapicús
- a handful of cassava flour
- one onion
- salt
- parsley
- pepper
- one cup of white wine
- bread slices
- butter for frying

## Directions

1. Remove all meat from the birds and chop it with half a quarter of bacon, a handful of carapicús, another of cassava flour, one onion, salt, parsley and pepper
2. Mix everything and boil in one cup of white wine
3. When cooked, serve over slices of bread fried in butter

title: Pombas Torquazes Fritas com Grelos de Abobora (Fried Pigeons with Squash Shoots)
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- bread crumbs
- fat
- salt
- pepper
- squash shoots
- water

## Directions

1. Cut the pigeons into four pieces, coat them in bread crumbs, and fry in plenty of fat
2. Sprinkle with salt and ground pepper
3. When cooked, remove them and braise some squash shoots that have been peeled and boiled in water and salt in the same fat
4. Place the shoots on a plate with the fat and put the pigeons on top, then serve

title: Pombas Torquazes Fritas com Ovos (Fried Pigeons with Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- salt
- wheat flour
- fat
- one dozen beaten eggs
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Split the pigeons in half, season with salt, coat in wheat flour
2. Fry in a little fat on both sides
3. When cooked, pour one dozen beaten eggs on top and leave on the fire until the eggs are also cooked
4. Place on a plate, inverting the pan so the pigeons are on top
5. Cover with sugar and cinnamon, and serve

title: Pombas Torquazes Guisados com Carapicús (Pigeons Stewed with Carapicús)
body:
## Ingredients

- four pigeons
- water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- coriander
- peppers
- three spoonfuls of fat
- carapicús
- six tomatoes
- one glass of vinegar
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil four pigeons in water, salt, parsley, onion leaves, coriander and some peppers
2. When cooked, cut into four pieces and fry in three spoonfuls of fat
3. Add some chopped carapicús and half a dozen tomatoes
4. After stirring, add one glass of vinegar and the broth reduced to three cups and mixed with two egg yolks
5. Let it boil for another quarter hour over coals and serve

title: Pombas Torquazes Refogadas com Palmito (Roasted Pigeons Braised with Palm Heart)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pigeons
- two spoonfuls of fat
- salt
- bread crusts, grated
- parsley leaves
- one glass of white wine
- one palm heart
- water

## Directions

1. Braise two pigeons in two spoonfuls of fat, adding a little salt
2. Split them in half and add some grated bread crusts, parsley leaves, and one glass of white wine, letting them boil for a quarter hour over coals
3. When cooked, add one chopped palm heart that has been boiled in water and salt, and one cup of water
4. Let it boil a bit more and serve

title: Pombas Torquazes Refogadas com Quibebe (Roasted Pigeons Braised with Squash Puree)
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- salt
- fat
- wheat flour
- one cup of milk
- one squash
- water
- one cup of cream
- two spoonfuls of sugar
- eight spoonfuls of butter

## Directions

1. Split the pigeons in half, salt them, and fry them in fat on both sides
2. Dust them with wheat flour, add one cup of milk, and let them boil over coals
3. On the other side, cook a peeled, halved, and seeded squash in water and salt
4. Remove the pieces and mash them to make a pulp
5. Mix in one cup of cream, two spoonfuls of sugar and eight spoonfuls of butter
6. Place on a plate, put the pigeons with the sauce on top, and serve

title: Pombas Torquazes, Rolas, etc. Assadas em Papel (Torcaz Pigeons, Wood Pigeons, etc. Roasted in Paper)
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- giblets
- cassava flour
- butter
- bitter orange juice
- sugar
- salt
- cinnamon
- thin bacon slices
- paper

## Directions

1. Open the birds along the back and remove the bones
2. Make a forcemeat with the giblets, cassava flour, butter, bitter orange juice, one spoon of sugar, salt, and ground cinnamon
3. Stuff the birds and sew the opening
4. Wrap each bird in a thin bacon slice and then in paper
5. Roast in the oven
6. When cooked, remove the paper and bacon and serve with some spicy sauce

title: Pombas Torquazes, Rolas, etc. Assadas no Espeto (Spitted Torcaz Pigeons, Wood Pigeons, etc.)
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- vinegar
- melted butter
- salt
- pepper
- pigeon livers
- sugar
- nutmeg
- bitter orange juice

## Directions

1. Clean the birds, tuck their feet inside, and spit them
2. Roast, basting with a sauce made of vinegar, melted butter, salt, and pepper
3. When roasted, take the sauce and add the pounded livers of the birds, a little sugar, nutmeg, and one spoon of bitter orange juice
4. Boil a little and serve with the pigeons

title: Pombas Torquazes, Rolas, etc. Fritas com Fatias (Fried Torcaz Pigeons, Wood Pigeons, etc. with Slices)
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeon meat
- quarter pound of bacon
- carapicú (a type of plant)
- cassava flour
- onion
- salt
- parsley
- pepper
- white wine
- bread
- butter

## Directions

1. Remove all meat from the birds and chop with quarter pound of bacon, a handful of carapicú, another of cassava flour, onion, salt, parsley, and pepper
2. Mix everything and boil in a cup of white wine
3. When cooked, serve on slices of bread fried in butter

title: Pombas, Rolas, Torquazes, etc. Picadas com Torradas (Chopped Pigeons, Wood Pigeons, etc. with Toast)
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeon meat and giblets
- parsley
- onion
- pepper
- cinnamon
- sugar
- bacon slices
- bread
- butter

## Directions

1. Remove the meat and giblets from the birds and chop very well with parsley, onion, pepper, cinnamon, and sugar
2. Line a pan with bacon slices, place the chopped mixture on buttered bread slices
3. Cover the pan and cook with coals below and above
4. Serve

title: Pombos Assados na Grelha (Grilled Squabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- salt
- pepper
- one half cup meat broth
- three tablespoons vinegar
- two pinches chopped onion
- the same amount of parsley
- one teaspoon mustard powder

## Directions

1. Clean the squabs and cut them in half along the spine
2. Flatten them by beating with a piece of wood
3. Roast them in hot butter
4. Cover both sides with a good layer of breadcrumbs, seasoning with salt and pepper
5. Grill on both sides
6. Serve with the following sauce: boil one half cup meat broth with three tablespoons vinegar, two pinches chopped onion and the same amount of parsley, salt, pepper and one teaspoon mustard powder
7. For this preparation, the youngest squabs should be sought

title: Pombos Assados no Espeto (Squabs Roasted on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs
- bacon strips

## Directions

1. Take the squabs and wrap each one in a strip of bacon
2. Roast on a spit over moderate fire for about half an hour
3. Serve with its sauce
4. Do not allow the squabs to dry while roasting, otherwise they will become insipid
5. Only roast squabs that can already fly

title: Pombos com Creme de Limão (Pigeons with Lemon Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- four pigeons
- lemon juice
- salt
- two tablespoons of butter
- one glass of wine
- cinnamon
- nutmeg
- one cup of cream
- four tablespoons of sugar
- juice of one sweet orange
- one glass of white wine

## Directions

1. Take four pigeons and rub them with lemon juice and a little salt
2. Put them to cook with two tablespoons of butter, one glass of wine, cinnamon, and nutmeg
3. When cooked, remove them and mix with one cup of cream, four tablespoons of sugar, the juice of one sweet orange, and one glass of white wine
4. Let the mixture boil once and serve with the pigeons

title: Pombos com Creme de Limão (Squabs with Lemon Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- four squabs
- lemon juice
- salt
- two tablespoons butter
- one glass wine
- cinnamon
- nutmeg
- one cup cream
- four tablespoons sugar
- one sweet orange
- one glass white wine

## Directions

1. Take four squabs and rub with lemon juice and a little salt
2. Cook with two tablespoons butter, one glass of wine, cinnamon and nutmeg
3. When cooked, remove them and mix with one cup of cream, four tablespoons sugar, the juice of one sweet orange and one glass of white wine
4. Allow the mixture to boil once and serve with the squabs

title: Pombos Estufados com Chicóreas (Squabs Braised with Chicory)
body:
## Ingredients

- mature squabs
- bacon
- one glass water
- salt
- nutmeg
- parsley
- chopped onion
- a little chopped mushrooms
- braised chicory

## Directions

1. Take mature squabs and place them in a pot lined with bacon
2. Add one glass of water, salt, nutmeg, parsley, chopped onion and a little chopped mushrooms
3. When cooked, serve over braised chicory

title: Pombos Estufados com Espargos ou com Palmitos (Squabs Braised with Asparagus or with Palm Hearts)
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs
- salt
- chopped parsley
- pepper
- onion
- cloves
- sweet oil
- four tablespoons butter kneaded with two tablespoons wheat flour
- one cup meat broth
- a portion of asparagus or palm hearts
- breadcrumbs
- butter for frying

## Directions

1. Soak the squabs for twenty-four hours in salt, chopped parsley, pepper, onion, cloves and sweet oil
2. Place them in a pot with four tablespoons butter kneaded with two tablespoons wheat flour and one cup of meat broth
3. Keep them well covered and allow to boil for one hour over moderate fire
4. Meanwhile, boil in water and salt a portion of asparagus or palm hearts, tied in bundles
5. When cooked, place them untied on the plate, covered with breadcrumbs fried in butter
6. Place the squabs on top and pour over everything the sauce from the squabs

title: Pombos Estufados em Quiabos (Squabs Braised with Okra)
body:
## Ingredients

- two large squabs
- salt
- pepper
- breadcrumbs
- bacon strips
- one cup water
- one onion
- chopped parsley
- one garlic clove
- one tablespoon wheat flour
- one cup water
- two dozen okra

## Directions

1. Rub two large squabs with salt and pepper
2. Wrap them in breadcrumbs and then in strips of bacon
3. Place in a pot with one cup of water, sliced onion, chopped parsley and one garlic clove
4. Allow to cook
5. Remove the squabs, strain their broth and remove the fat
6. Add one tablespoon wheat flour and one cup of water
7. Add two dozen okra cut into rounds
8. Allow to cook and reduce
9. Finally pour over the squabs

title: Pombos Fritos com Capa (Squabs Fried with Coating)
body:
## Ingredients

- squab halves
- lemon juice
- salt
- garlic juice
- wheat flour
- egg yolks
- sauce or salad

## Directions

1. Take the halves of squabs and flatten them with a palmatoria
2. Soak for three to four hours in lemon juice, salt and garlic juice
3. Wrap in wheat flour
4. Pass through egg yolks
5. Cover again with wheat flour
6. Fry on both sides in fat
7. Serve with some sauce or salad

title: Pombos Fritos Dourados (Golden Fried Squabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs
- breadcrumbs
- salt
- chopped parsley
- pepper
- butter
- six to eight eggs

## Directions

1. Cut squabs in half and flatten with a palmatoria
2. Wrap them in a coating of breadcrumbs with salt, chopped parsley and pepper
3. Fry on both sides in butter
4. When fried and of good color, place on a plate and pour over them six to eight beaten eggs
5. Place in the oven and allow to take good color, then serve

title: Pombos Fritos à Parmêzão (Squabs Fried with Parmesan)
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice
- wheat flour
- butter
- grated cheese
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Flatten the squabs, cut in half, with a palmatoria
2. Rub with salt, pepper and lemon juice
3. Coat in wheat flour and fry in butter
4. Grill them
5. When grilled, place in a platter and cover with grated cheese, sugar and cinnamon
6. Place in the oven and allow to roast until of good color, then serve

title: Pombos Guisados com Beterrabas (Squabs Stewed with Beets)
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs
- water
- salt
- fresh beets
- wheat flour
- butter
- pepper
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil the squabs in water and salt with some fresh beets
2. When cooked, remove the squabs
3. Coat them in wheat flour and fry in butter
4. Chop the beets and allow to cool
5. Mix with salt, pepper and vinegar
6. Place in the platter and put the fried squabs on top
7. Pour fried butter over everything
8. Serve cold

title: Pombos Guisados com Doce de Maracujás (Squabs Stewed with Passion Fruit Sweet)
body:
## Ingredients

- young squabs
- butter
- a portion of passion fruit pulp
- two tablespoons sugar
- some cloves
- cinnamon
- one glass white wine

## Directions

1. Take young squabs and fry them in butter
2. Place them in a pot with a portion of passion fruit pulp, two tablespoons sugar, some cloves, cinnamon and one glass of white wine
3. Allow to boil over very low fire
4. When cooked, serve the passion fruit sweet with the squabs on top

title: Pombos Guisados com Favas Novas (Pigeons Stewed with New Broad Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- three pigeons
- a portion of new broad beans
- water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- two tablespoons of fat
- breadcrumbs
- two tablespoons of wheat flour
- one chopped onion

## Directions

1. Put three pigeons to boil with a portion of new broad beans in water, salt, parsley and onion leaves
2. When cooked, remove the pigeons and cut them in half
3. Fry them on both sides in two tablespoons of fat until they take on good color
4. Coat them in breadcrumbs and fry them again
5. In the same butter, fry two tablespoons of wheat flour and one chopped onion until the flour is toasted
6. Mix in the broad beans and let everything boil until the broth from the beans is reduced
7. Serve together with the pigeons

title: Pombos Guisados com Favas Novas (Squabs Stewed with Fresh Broad Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- three squabs
- a portion of fresh broad beans
- water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves

## Directions

1. Place three squabs to boil with a portion of fresh broad beans in water, salt, parsley and onion leaves
2. When cooked, remove the squabs, cut them in half and fry them

title: Pombos Guisados com Mangaritos (Pigeons Stewed with Mangaritos)
body:
## Ingredients

- four large pigeons
- water
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onion
- a portion of mangaritos
- butter
- one chopped onion
- one tablespoon of wheat flour

## Directions

1. Boil four large pigeons with water, salt, pepper, parsley, onion and a portion of mangaritos
2. When cooked, remove them and divide into two parts
3. Fry them on both sides in butter and remove
4. In the same butter, fry mangaritos cut into slices; after they are well toasted, remove them from the heat
5. In this same butter, fry one chopped onion and one tablespoon of wheat flour, adding to it one large cup of the broth from the pigeons
6. Let it boil for a little while and add the pieces of pigeons and the mangaritos
7. Give it one more boil and serve

title: Pombos Guisados com Palmitos (Pigeons Stewed with Palm Hearts)
body:
## Ingredients

- four young pigeons
- water
- parsley
- onion leaves
- salt
- pepper
- a portion of mushrooms
- one palm heart cut into pieces
- wheat flour
- one cup of broth
- two egg yolks
- three tablespoons of cream

## Directions

1. Cook four young pigeons in water containing parsley, onion leaves, salt, pepper, a portion of mushrooms, and one palm heart cut into pieces
2. After everything boils for a quarter of an hour, transfer to a casserole after dusting with wheat flour and moistening only with one cup of broth
3. Let it boil over coals for a quarter of an hour
4. Serve on the plate, pouring the sauce over it, to which are added at this time two egg yolks beaten with three tablespoons of cream

title: Pombos Recheados e Ensopados (Stuffed and Stewed Squabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of beef or pork liver, finely chopped
- the same amount of finely chopped mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- water
- a portion of carapicús
- a pinch of cornstarch
- one tablespoon vinegar

## Directions

1. Mix a piece of finely chopped beef or pork liver with the same amount of finely chopped mushrooms, salt and pepper
2. Stuff the squabs with this mixture, selecting ones no more than ten to fifteen days old
3. Boil with water and a portion of carapicús
4. Serve the carapicús afterwards with the squabs
5. Pour over them the reduced sauce incorporated with a pinch of cornstarch and mixed with one tablespoon of vinegar

title: Pombos Refogados com Abóbora (Braised Pigeons with Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- small pigeons
- small squashes with still soft skin
- hot water
- bacon piece, chopped
- one tablespoon butter
- salt
- pepper
- half a dozen tomatoes

## Directions

1. Take small pigeons and small squashes with still soft skin and scald them in hot water, leaving them until the water cools
2. Melt a chopped piece of bacon
3. When well heated, add the halved small pigeons and whole squashes, always turning them
4. Add one cup of water, salt, pepper, half a dozen tomatoes
5. Let them dry near the fire
6. After half an hour, serve them

title: Pombos Refogados com Arroz (Braised Pigeons with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- four pigeons, plucked and cleaned
- four tablespoons fat
- chosen and washed rice
- one bottle of meat broth
- salt
- grated nutmeg
- one clove
- one cut onion

## Directions

1. Fry the four pigeons in four tablespoons of fat until roasted, turning them until they take on good color
2. Remove them and heat the fat well
3. Add a portion of chosen and washed rice and stir
4. When the rice is hot and evenly greased, add one bottle of meat broth, salt, grated nutmeg, one clove, one cut onion, and the pigeons
5. Let them dry near the fire, and when cooked, serve them

title: Pombos Refogados com Carapicús (Braised Pigeons with Carapicú)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound of bacon, chopped into small pieces
- one tablespoon butter
- four pigeons
- handful of carapicús cut into pieces
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onion leaves
- one cup of meat broth
- one tablespoon wheat flour
- one tablespoon vinegar

## Directions

1. Fry a quarter pound of chopped bacon in one tablespoon of butter
2. When half fried, add four pigeons to the same fat, turning them until of good color on all sides
3. Add a handful of carapicús cut into pieces, salt, pepper, parsley, onion leaves, and one cup of meat broth
4. Let everything boil well over moderate fire
5. When the pigeons are cooked, place them in a serving dish
6. Arrange the innards with the carapicús around them and on top the sauce after adding one tablespoon of wheat flour mixed in vinegar

title: Pombos Refogados com Chuchús (Braised Pigeons with Chayote)
body:
## Ingredients

- small pigeons
- butter
- chuchús, peeled and split and blanched
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- two glasses of white wine

## Directions

1. Open the small pigeons in the middle and fry them (always turning them) in hot butter over the fire
2. When they have taken on good color, add a portion of chuchús peeled, split, and blanched
3. Add salt, parsley, onion leaves, and two glasses of white wine
4. Cover the pan and let it cook for half an hour over moderate fire
5. Serve afterwards

title: Pombos Refogados com Hervilhas (Braised Pigeons with Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- four young pigeons
- two tablespoons butter
- portion of fresh peas
- two cups of water
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- nutmeg
- two egg yolks
- one glass of white wine
- pinch of sugar

## Directions

1. Take four young pigeons and braise them in two tablespoons of butter
2. When they have taken color, remove them and cut them in half
3. Place a portion of fresh peas in the same fat and then two cups of water
4. Add the pieces of pigeons, salt, pepper, parsley, and nutmeg
5. Let them cook over live coals
6. When serving, add two egg yolks mixed in one glass of white wine and a pinch of sugar

title: Pombos Refogados com Hervilhas (Braised Squabs with Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- four young squabs
- two tablespoons butter
- a portion of fresh peas
- two cups water
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- nutmeg
- two egg yolks
- one glass white wine
- a pinch of sugar

## Directions

1. Brown the squabs in butter
2. Once they have taken color, remove and cut them in half
3. In the same fat, add a portion of fresh peas
4. Add two cups of water, the pieces of squab, salt, pepper, parsley, and nutmeg
5. Allow to cook over live coals
6. When serving, add two egg yolks dissolved in a glass of white wine and a pinch of sugar

title: Pombos Refogados Instantaneamente (Quickly Braised Squabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- two squabs
- one quarter butter
- one half of chopped bacon
- the same amount of carapicús
- one tablespoon wheat flour
- one cup meat broth
- salt
- parsley
- one lemon

## Directions

1. Cut two squabs into four pieces each
2. Fry one quarter butter with one half chopped bacon and the same amount of carapicús
3. Immediately add the pieces of squab
4. Turn them to brown on all sides
5. Allow to fry for ten to fifteen minutes
6. Remove the squabs and add one tablespoon wheat flour to the butter, stirring
7. Add one cup of meat broth, salt and parsley
8. Return the squabs to the sauce for a moment
9. Serve after squeezing lemon juice over them

title: Porco do Matto com Ameixas (Wild Boar with Prunes)
body:
## Ingredients

- wild boar meat
- water
- salt
- butter
- wheat flour
- rapadura (brown sugar)
- two dozen prunes
- cinnamon
- lemon peel
- wine
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Cook the meat and scald in salted water
2. Fry afterwards in butter with a little wheat flour
3. Add a little rapadura, two dozen prunes, cinnamon, lemon peel, wine, and a little meat broth
4. Let boil and strain through a sieve
5. Place the meat in this sauce
6. Give it one more boil and serve

title: Porco do Matto Ensopado (Stewed Wild Boar)
body:
## Ingredients

- wild boar meat
- water
- salt
- toasted wheat flour
- onions
- juniper berries
- cloves
- vinegar
- sugar
- rosemary (if fresh juniper berries are unavailable)

## Directions

1. Cut meat into pieces and soak for twelve hours
2. Scald and place over the fire with water and salt
3. Remove the scum
4. When cooked, add plenty of toasted wheat flour, plenty of onions, juniper berries, cloves, salt, vinegar, and sugar
5. Let boil with the meat
6. When serving, remove the meat and strain the sauce
7. Serve
8. If fresh juniper berries are unavailable, add some rosemary leaves

title: Porco Ensopado com Cará do Ar (Pork Stewed with Air Yam)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat
- fat
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- hot water
- air yams
- six tomatoes
- six camari peppers
- two eggs
- two spoonfuls of vinegar

## Directions

1. Take some pieces of pork meat, or also the loin cut into pieces, braise with a little fat, salt, parsley, and onion leaves, stirring.
2. Add a large bowl of water, some peeled air yams cut into pieces, half a dozen tomatoes, six camari peppers.
3. Leave on the fire until everything is cooked.
4. Add two beaten eggs with two spoonfuls of vinegar, and when it has boiled, serve.

title: Porco Ensopado com Nata (Pork Stewed with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat
- hot fat
- milk
- salt
- parsley leaf
- onion leaf
- cinnamon
- nutmeg
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut the pork meat into thin slices.
2. Pass these in hot fat, put them in a pan with two cups of milk, salt, parsley leaf, onion leaf, cinnamon and nutmeg.
3. Let boil for half an hour over a moderate fire, and when the broth has thickened with two egg yolks, serve.

title: Porco Estufado com Choucroute (Pork Braised with Sauerkraut)
body:
## Ingredients

- fermented cabbage or finely chopped cabbage
- vinegar
- pork meat
- fat
- white wine
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- fried or cooked potatoes

## Directions

1. Take a portion of fermented cabbage, or in its absence, finely chopped cabbage, scalded with vinegar.
2. After having fried some pieces of pork meat in two or three spoonfuls of fat, remove them and put the cabbage in the same fat, stir until it is well saturated with fat.
3. At this point, add the meat, two cups of white wine, salt, pepper, a cut onion.
4. Leave by the fire until they are well cooked, and serve with fried or cooked potatoes.

title: Porco Guisado com Abóbora (Pork Stewed with Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat
- squash
- salt
- peppers
- parsley
- onions
- cream

## Directions

1. Boil a piece of pork meat and a peeled squash divided into pieces and with the seeds removed, with salt, peppers, parsley and onions.
2. When cooked, remove the meat and the pieces of squash, which are well pounded and passed through a sieve to form a pulp, to which half a cup of cream is added.
3. Cut the meat into small pieces, which are fried in little fat and a chopped onion; when the meat is well browned, pour it with the fat and onion over the squash pulp.

title: Porco Imitando Caça (Pork Imitating Game)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork leg, ribs or loin
- vinegar
- thyme
- marjoram
- bay leaf
- sage
- coriander
- salt
- juniper berries
- clove
- pepper
- onion
- parsley
- mint sprigs
- walnut hulls

## Directions

1. Take either the leg, ribs or loin of pork, place them to soak in escabeche for twenty-four hours, in vinegar, thyme, marjoram, bay leaf, sage, coriander, salt, juniper berries, clove, pepper, onion, parsley, some sprigs of mint, and the hulls of walnuts.
2. Leave all in escabeche for eight days; the pork meat thus prepared will have the taste and appearance of wild boar.
3. For this it is necessary that the pork be young and lean.

title: Porco Refogado com Palmitos (Pork Braised with Palm Hearts)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat
- palm heart
- fat
- hot water
- salt
- onion
- six camari peppers

## Directions

1. Cut a piece of pork meat into small parts, which are braised with a finely chopped palm heart and scalded in very hot water, with everything well saturated with fat.
2. When the palm heart is in circumstances to stick to the bottom of the pan, add a bowl of hot water, salt, an onion, half a dozen camari peppers.
3. Let it boil until the water dries up and serve.

title: Porco Refogado com Quiabos à Mineira (Pork Braised with Okra, Minas Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat
- fat
- hot water
- salt
- parsley
- onion
- garlic clove
- cornmeal
- okra

## Directions

1. Take a piece of pork meat, cut it into very small parts, which are scalded, left to cool, and then fried with little fat until they become browned.
2. At this point add two cups of water, salt, parsley, onion, a clove of garlic, a spoonful of cornmeal, and a portion of okra cut into thin slices.
3. Let boil for half an hour, and when the sauce is almost dry and the meat is cooked, serve.

title: Posta de Carne Aferventada e Guarnecida de Raizes Cobertas de Substancia de Carne (Beef Steak Simmered and Garnished with Roots Covered in Meat Substance)
body:
## Ingredients

- good piece of beef from the chuck covered with fat
- water
- salt
- cloves
- roots
- small onions
- cabbage
- meat broth substance

## Directions

1. Take a good piece of beef from the chuck covered with fat
2. Remove the bone and tie it, then place it in a large pot
3. Break the bone and place it inside
4. Fill three-quarters of the pot with cold water and let it cook moderately
5. Skim slowly and add cold water from time to time
6. When well skimmed, add a little salt and two cloves, taking care that everything boils very slowly for five hours
7. When adding salt, also add the roots to be cooked
8. Test with a knife: if it enters easily and exits the same way, the beef is cooked
9. Remove, let it drain, and brush two or three times with meat broth substance
10. Place on a platter with small onions and cabbage around it, all small and covered with meat broth substance

title: Posta de Carne de Vacca à Moda (Beef Brisket in Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef brisket
- bacon
- salt
- seasonings
- calf meat
- carrots
- onions
- green herbs
- white wine
- cognac
- beef broth
- pepper
- roasted onion

## Directions

1. Fill the brisket with thick pieces of bacon previously salted.
2. Add the appropriate seasonings and tie it.
3. Place it in a casserole.
4. Add a piece of calf meat, two carrots, four onions, a bunch of green herbs, a glass of white wine, a goblet of cognac, two glasses of beef broth, salt, pepper, and a bit of roasted onion.
5. Bring to a boil over high heat.
6. Baste the meat with the sauce in the casserole and cook for five hours over low heat, always basting.
7. Drain and untie the meat, placing it on a platter surrounded by the calf meat cut into pieces.
8. Add the carrots and onions around it.
9. Pass the broth from the casserole through a silk sieve and return it to the fire until it reduces to half.
10. Use this to baste the meat, and serve the rest in a tureen.

title: Preparação das Sardinhas de Nantes ou de Outro Qualquer Peixe Conservado em Azeite Doce (Preparation of Nantes Sardines or Any Other Fish Preserved in Sweet Oil)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish
- Sea water or well salted water
- Cloves
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Bay leaves
- Good sweet oil

## Directions

1. Clean the fish by removing the scales
2. Scald with sea water or well salted water
3. Let cool in the same water
4. Put in a pan with cloves, pepper, nutmeg and bay leaves
5. Pour good sweet oil over it, with the fish completely covered
6. Keep for later use
7. Large fish may be preserved this way, but they should be cut into steaks before being prepared

title: Presunto Assado (Roasted Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ham
- water
- butter
- hot sauce or pea, bean, or fava bean pulp

## Directions

1. Boil a ham in water until well cooked
2. After removing the skin, fry it in cow butter until it has a good color
3. Serve it with a spicy sauce or pea, bean, or fava bean pulp

title: Presunto de Escabeche (Pickled Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ham leg
- salt
- four ounces of saltpeter
- serpol
- bay leaf
- Indian cloves
- two bottles of white wine

## Directions

1. Remove the hip bone, half of the thigh bone, and place the ham leg in a suitable vessel
2. Season it with salt, four ounces of saltpeter, add serpol, bay leaf, Indian cloves, and two bottles of white wine
3. Turn it on all sides, but only once a day, keeping it there for seven to eight days before cooking it
4. Remove the skin, then wrap it in three layers of paper anointed with sweet oil or butter
5. Cook it either on the spit or in the oven, requiring three and a half hours to cook
6. Serve it with a Robert sauce or Spanish sauce with Madeira wine

title: Presunto de Porco do Matto Cozido (Cooked Wild Boar Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter of wild boar
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- marjoram
- lemon juice
- ginger
- water
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Place a quarter of the wild boar in a brine made of salt, pepper, cut onion, marjoram, lemon juice, ginger, and a little water
2. Leave in this brine for three days, turning every twelve hours
3. Wrap in cloth and cook in the same brine for five to six hours with some potatoes over a very active fire
4. When cooked, let cool, remove the cloth, and serve cold

title: Presunto de Porco Enfumaçado (Smoked Pork Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- a pork leg from wild boar
- salt (substantial amount)
- peppers
- onions
- cloves
- red wine

## Directions

1. Trim a pork leg from wild boar and place it in a brine with substantial salt, peppers, onions, cloves and red wine
2. Leave it in this brine for ten to fifteen days, having placed a piece of board over the quarter and a large weight on top
3. After this time, remove it, dry it well, and expose it to the sun for one day
4. Wrap it in cloth, hang it in smoke for fifteen to twenty days
5. After this time, it serves both to eat it cold and raw, as well as to eat it cooked or roasted

title: Presunto de Porco Enfumaçado (Smoked Wild Boar Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- one wild boar leg
- salt
- pepper
- onions
- cloves
- red wine

## Directions

1. Trim a wild boar leg and place in a brine with plenty of salt, peppers, onions, cloves, and red wine
2. Leave in this brine for ten to fifteen days with a board placed over the quarter and a large weight on top
3. Remove, dry well, and expose to the sun for one day
4. Wrap in cloth and hang in smoke for fifteen to twenty days
5. After this time, it can be eaten cold and raw, or cooked or roasted

title: Presunto Encapotado (Ham in Bread Crust)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ham leg
- salt
- pepper
- onions
- breadcrumbs
- cinnamon
- sugar
- raisins
- ham cooking broth

## Directions

1. Salt the ham leg and leave it for some days
2. After this, boil it with a little pepper and onions until cooked
3. Remove the skin and cover it with a paste made of grated breadcrumbs, cinnamon, sugar, and raisins moistened with the broth in which the ham was cooked
4. Place it thus prepared in a pan and leave it to roast in the oven
5. Serve it

title: Presunto Fresco de Espeto (Fresh Ham on the Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ham
- wine with garlic
- salt
- pepper
- sweet oil
- sage branch
- half bottle of white wine

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the ham, leaving all the fat
2. Place it in garlic wine for three days with salt, pepper, sweet oil, a sage branch, and half a bottle of white wine
3. Put it on the spit and roast it for two hours, basting it with the garlic wine sauce
4. After cooking, serve it with a sauce made from the juice that dripped, adding chopped scallions and half of the garlic wine where the ham was

title: Presunto Gelado (Glazed Cold Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ham leg
- water
- salt
- four carrots
- four onions
- bay leaves
- serpol
- marjoram
- Indian cloves
- valerian
- blessed herb
- meat stock juice
- watercress
- escarole leaves

## Directions

1. Take the ham leg, remove the hip bone, and give it a strong blow with a large knife on the thigh bone to break it without damaging the meat, carefully removing the broken part of the bone
2. Lightly roast the surface of the skin and soak it in water to remove some salt, which requires forty-eight hours; if the ham is more than one year old, change the water many times
3. Wash it and leave it to drain, placing it in a thick cloth and tying it well
4. Five hours before serving, place it in a large vessel filled with cold water, add four carrots, four onions, some bay leaves, serpol, marjoram, Indian cloves, a little valerian, and blessed herb
5. Leave it to boil over a lively fire, skimming, cover the vessel, and let it cook slowly
6. Half an hour before serving, remove the cloth and let it drain
7. Trim the shank and decorate it; then place it to dry in a mild oven, having covered the ham two or three times with meat stock juice
8. Serve it on a platter on which some watercress branches and escarole leaves (white chicory) have been previously placed

title: Presunto Servido Frio (Cold Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- serpol
- bay leaf
- garlic
- twelve onions
- six cloves
- carrots
- parsley
- celery
- one ounce of saltpeter
- water or one bottle of white wine
- dried breadcrumbs or bread crusts
- slices of tongue or pickled cucumber preserves
- gelatin

## Directions

1. Soak the ham for twenty-four hours, tied in cloth
2. Place it in a vessel with serpol, bay leaf, garlic, twelve onions, six cloves, carrots, parsley, celery, and one ounce of saltpeter
3. Cover completely with water or one bottle of white wine
4. Test with a stick for doneness; if it enters easily, the ham is cooked
5. Remove the ham from the fire and let it cool in the same pot until lukewarm
6. Remove it at this point and lift the skin without removing the fat
7. Sprinkle dried breadcrumbs or bread crusts on top
8. Decorate with slices of tongue or pickled cucumber preserves
9. Place gelatin around it

title: Presunto Servido Frio (Cold Served Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ham
- cloth
- serpol
- bay leaf
- garlic
- twelve onions
- six Indian cloves
- carrots
- parsley
- celery
- one ounce of saltpeter
- water or one bottle of white wine

## Directions

1. Soak the ham for twenty-four hours, tied in a cloth
2. Place it in a vessel with serpol, bay leaf, garlic, twelve onions, six Indian cloves, carrots, parsley, celery, and one ounce of saltpeter
3. Moisten it with water or with a bottle of white wine, in such a way that it is well covered
4. To know if the ham is well cooked, test it with a stick; if it enters easily, it is cooked
5. Remove the ham from the heat and let it cool in the same pan until lukewarm
6. Remove it at this point and lift it up

title: Presunto Servido Quente (Hot Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- spinach
- sliced bread
- Spanish sauce

## Directions

1. After removing salt and cooking the ham as previously explained, place it for a short time in a mild oven to dry the surface, having previously rounded it
2. Serve on a platter with sautéed spinach at the bottom, surrounded by toasted bread slices
3. Top with Spanish sauce
4. Alternatively, serve it over a stew of carrots, asparagus tips, guandú, peas, cabbage, broad beans, mangalós, fern sprouts, lentils, or chickpeas

title: Presunto à Francesa (Ham French Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork leg
- salt
- thyme
- bay leaf
- marjoram
- marjoram
- segorelha
- juniper seeds
- water
- Lisbon wine

## Directions

1. Place a pork leg in a brine composed of salt, thyme, bay leaf, marjoram, marjoram, segorelha, juniper seeds, all moistened with half water and half Lisbon wine.
2. After leaving the ham to soak for fifteen days, drain it and expose it to smoke.
3. Hams prepared in this manner can last a long time, but this result will be achieved with more certainty if rubbed with Lisbon wine, sprinkling them with ash.
4. One must be careful that the leg is entirely covered by the brine.

title: Presunto à Francesa Cozido (Ham French Style, Cooked)
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- cold water
- cloth
- water
- white wine
- onion
- roots
- seasoning herbs

## Directions

1. When you wish to cook them, place them in cold water for three to four days to draw out the salt, and after wrapping them in a cloth, place them in a vessel with equal parts water and white wine, onion, some roots and a bundle of seasoning herbs.
2. Must leave them to cook over a moderate fire for five to six hours and let them cool in the same vessel.
3. Remove the skin carefully, leaving the fat.
4. Cover with finely chopped parsley, bread crumb fried in butter and pass a hot iron over them.

title: Presunto à Ingleza (English-Style Glazed Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ham
- wheat flour
- Madeira wine
- ham stock
- decorative paper

## Directions

1. After removing the salt as previously explained, leave it to cook until the water reduces to three-quarters
2. After cooling in the same vessel, remove the skin and wrap it with a layer of wheat flour paste prepared as for pastries
3. Place it in a frying pan and put it in the oven until almost cooked
4. Remove it from the oven and introduce a cup of Madeira wine through a hole made in the paste and return it to the oven for half an hour
5. Decorate the shank with cut paper and serve with a sauce made from the ham stock diluted in a little Madeira wine

title: Presunto à Ingleza (English-Style Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- ten pounds of salt
- half pound of ground Indian pepper
- four ounces of saltpeter
- water
- salt
- serpol
- bay leaf
- two ounces of ginger
- four ounces of Indian pepper in grain
- four ounces of juniper berries in grain
- marjoram
- sage
- oregano
- four pounds of molasses or muscovado sugar
- wine mixed with vinegar

## Directions

1. After cutting two hams, round them and cut the trimmings around them
2. Prick the skin with the tip of a knife so that the seasoning can penetrate them
3. Place ten pounds of salt, half pound of ground Indian pepper, and four ounces of saltpeter on a table
4. After rubbing them with this mixture, place them in a small tub, putting the rest of the salt on top, and leave them there for seven days
5. After this time, remove them, bring the brine to a boil, composed of water and the blood that came from the ham, skim well, and add serpol, bay leaf, two ounces of ginger, four ounces of Indian pepper in grain, the same amount of juniper berries in grain, marjoram, sage, oregano, and four pounds of molasses or muscovado sugar
6. Place the hams back in the tub, and when the brine is cold, pour it over them, and if it is not sufficient to cover the hams, add water and salt
7. Place a board with any weight on top to keep them in this brine for three weeks
8. After this time, remove them and place them in a press for twelve hours, and after draining, hang them in smoke
9. When they have taken on a beautiful color, rub them with wine mixed with vinegar to protect them from flies, then dry them for preservation

title: Presunto à Ingleza (Ham English Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pork legs
- ten pounds of salt
- half pound of ground pepper from India
- four ounces of saltpeter

## Directions

1. The following recipe is for preparing two hams.
2. After cutting two legs, round them off, cutting away the scraps around them; prick the skin a little with the point of a knife so that the seasoning can penetrate them.
3. Place on a table ten pounds of salt, half a pound of ground pepper from India, and four ounces of saltpeter.
4. After rubbing them with this mixture, place them in a small tub, putting the rest of the salt on top, and leave them there for seven days.
5. After this time, remove them and bring the brine to a boil.

title: Presunto à Mineira (Ham Miner's Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- one ham leg
- water
- salt
- scraped rapadura or muscovado sugar
- equal parts saltpeter
- wine and vinegar in equal parts

## Directions

1. Take a ham leg, and after cutting and trimming it well, place it in a deep vessel covered with fresh water, keeping it there for three to four days
2. At the end of this time, remove it from the water, drain it, and cover it with a sufficient quantity of salt, where it stays for another three days
3. Remove it from there again, place it on a board, rub it with scraped rapadura or muscovado sugar mixed with equal parts of saltpeter, taking care to prick it with the tip of a knife so that the rapadura and saltpeter can be introduced
4. After finishing this process, preserve it in a vessel for another four days
5. From there, place it on a board, putting another board on top with a weight to give it a flattened shape and to make any remaining liquid drain out
6. The next day, place it inside a sack and hang it in smoke for three weeks
7. After this time, rub it with wine and vinegar in equal parts and store it in a dry place

title: Preá, Esquilo e Gambá Assados no Espeto (Paca, Squirrel and Opossum Roasted on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- one paca, squirrel or opossum
- brandy
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- cloves
- marjoram
- paper
- butter

## Directions

1. Take one of these animals and soak it in brandy with salt, pepper, nutmeg, cloves and marjoram
2. After six hours, thread it on a spit, wrapped in paper greased with butter, and roast it over a gentle fire
3. When cooked, remove the paper and serve with some salad

title: Preá, Esquilo e Gambá Assados no Espeto com Beterraba (Paca, Squirrel and Opossum Roasted on a Spit with Beet Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- one paca, squirrel or opossum
- salt
- pepper from peppers and tomatoes
- egg yolks
- breadcrumbs
- butter, melted
- beets
- vinegar
- fat, melted
- comarí peppers

## Directions

1. Take one of these animals; after removing the head, rub the meat with salt, pepper juice and tomatoes
2. Thread it on a spit and roast it; when almost cooked, brush it with egg yolks, coat it in breadcrumbs, and finish roasting it, moistening with melted butter until it takes on a good color
3. Serve with beet salad prepared as follows: cook beets in salted water, peel and cut them into thin slices; mix one cup of vinegar, three spoons of melted fat, a little salt and some comarí peppers

title: Preá, Esquilo e Gambá Assados no Forno (Paca, Squirrel and Opossum Roasted in the Oven)
body:
## Ingredients

- a slice of bacon for the bottom of the pan
- one paca, squirrel or opossum, cleaned and without head
- three onion heads, whole
- chopped parsley
- salt
- pepper
- another slice of bacon on top
- white wine, two glasses
- one egg yolk
- one spoon of sugar

## Directions

1. Line the bottom of a pan with a slice of bacon, and place either the paca, squirrel or opossum, cleaned and without head, with three whole onion heads, chopped parsley, salt and pepper
2. Cover it with another slice of bacon and roast in the oven or over coals
3. When cooked, remove it and mix into the sauce two glasses of white wine, one egg yolk and one spoon of sugar, bring to a boil, strain the sauce and serve with the roast

title: Preá, Esquilo e Gambá Fritos com Mangaritos (Paca, Squirrel and Opossum Fried with Mangarito)
body:
## Ingredients

- the game, cut in quarters
- ground salt
- parsley
- chopped onion
- juice of comarí peppers
- egg yolks
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- mangarito, peeled and cut into thin slices

## Directions

1. Take the game and cut it in quarters; season it with ground salt, parsley, chopped onion and juice of comarí peppers
2. Pass them through egg yolks and coat them in breadcrumbs; sauté in butter with a portion of peeled and thinly sliced mangarito, and when fried, serve

title: Preá, Esquilo e Gambá Fritos na Grelha (Paca, Squirrel and Opossum Fried on the Grill)
body:
## Ingredients

- game meat, cut in small pieces
- lemon juice
- salt
- parsley
- peppers
- garlic clove
- onions
- marjoram
- fat for frying
- egg yolks
- wheat flour
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Cut the game meat into small pieces, soaking them in lemon juice, salt, parsley, peppers, a clove of garlic, onions and marjoram; after six hours, fry them in fat, and when fried, brush them with egg yolks, coating them afterwards in wheat flour, roasting them thus prepared on the grill, and dripping melted butter; when fried, serve

title: Preá, Esquilo e Gambá Guisados com Quiabos (Paca, Squirrel and Opossum Stewed with Okra)
body:
## Ingredients

- two chopped onions
- three spoons of fat
- one bottle of water
- salt
- chopped parsley
- peppers
- one egg yolk
- one glass of sour orange juice
- two dozen okra, cut into slices
- the game, cut in quarters

## Directions

1. Sauté two chopped onions with three spoons of fat, add one bottle of water, salt, chopped parsley, peppers, one egg yolk, one glass of sour orange juice and two dozen okra cut into slices
2. Let it boil for a moment and pour in this sauce the game cut in quarters
3. Cook over moderate heat for an hour and serve

title: Preá, Esquilo e Gambá Guisados com Tomates (Paca, Squirrel and Opossum Stewed with Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of the game
- water
- salt
- peppers
- parsley
- marjoram
- onion leaves
- ginger
- two dozen tomatoes
- two spoons of fat
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil the pieces of game in water, salt, peppers, parsley, marjoram, onion leaves and ginger
2. When cooked, remove them
3. On the other hand, sauté two dozen tomatoes with two spoons of fat; add the pieces of game cut in quarters, and add the strained broth reduced by half and incorporated with two egg yolks
4. Let everything boil for a quarter of an hour and serve

title: Preá, Esquilo e Gambá Refogados com Guandús (Paca, Squirrel and Opossum Sautéed with Pigeon Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- one paca, squirrel or opossum, cut in quarters
- sour orange juice
- comarí peppers
- salt
- cornmeal
- one cup of water
- one glass of brandy
- half a dozen tomatoes
- pigeon peas
- parsley leaves
- onions

## Directions

1. Cut one of these animals in quarters and moisten them with sour orange juice, comarí peppers and salt
2. Coat them in cornmeal and fry on both sides
3. Add one cup of water, one glass of brandy and half a dozen tomatoes, letting it boil for an hour until cooked
4. On the other hand, boil a portion of pigeon peas in water with salt, parsley leaves and onions
5. When cooked and the water reduced, add them to the meat with the sauce
6. Let it boil a little more and serve

title: Preá, Esquilo e Gambá Refogados com Renovos de Samambaia (Paca, Squirrel and Opossum Sautéed with Fern Fronds)
body:
## Ingredients

- one paca, squirrel or opossum
- fat for sautéing
- breadcrumbs
- parsley
- onion, chopped
- salt
- pepper
- one cup of water
- one spoon of vinegar
- fern fronds

## Directions

1. Take a paca, squirrel or opossum, remove the odor, clean it and wash it with hot water
2. Cut off the head and sauté it in fat
3. Turn it to fry equally on all sides, and dust it with breadcrumbs, adding parsley, chopped onion, salt and pepper
4. Add one cup of water and one spoon of vinegar
5. Let it boil for half an hour, and pour it over fern fronds cooked in salted water, arranged on a plate, and serve with its sauce

title: Pudim de Arroz (Rice Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a plate of rice
- one bottle of milk
- one pound of sugar beaten with five egg yolks
- a little salt
- clove
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Boil half a plate of rice with one bottle of milk until dry
2. Add one pound of sugar beaten with five egg yolks
3. Mix well, add a little salt, clove, and nutmeg
4. Pour this dough into a pudding mold, bake in the oven and serve with proper sauce

title: Pudim de Arroz (Rice Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bottle of milk
- one pound of refined sugar
- twelve ounces of rice
- a little butter
- zest of one lemon
- four whole eggs
- five egg yolks
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Bring a bottle of milk to a boil with one pound of refined sugar and twelve ounces of rice.
2. When the rice is well cooked, add a little butter and let cool.
3. Mince the lemon zest finely, which is mixed with the milk; add four whole eggs and five egg yolks and mix well.
4. Butter a mold and sprinkle with grated breadcrumbs; shake the mold to remove excess breadcrumbs, and pour the mixture in, placing the mold near the fire and covering it with a lid full of hot coals.
5. At the time of serving, turn the mold onto a dish and serve hot or cold.

title: Pudim de Arroz com Queijo (Rice Pudding with Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a plate of rice
- one bottle of milk
- half a pound of grated cheese
- half a pound of sugar
- a little salt
- nutmeg
- eight egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil half a plate of rice with one bottle of milk
2. When cooked and cool, add half a pound of grated cheese, half a pound of sugar, a little salt, nutmeg and eight egg yolks
3. Mix everything well, place the dough in a mold greased with butter, bake in hot ashes covering with a lid full of live coals
4. When cooked, serve with proper sauce

title: Pudim de Arroz com Queijo (Rice Pudding with Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a measure of rice
- one bottle of milk
- half a pound of grated cheese
- half a pound of sugar
- a little salt
- nutmeg
- eight egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil half a measure of rice with one bottle of milk
2. When cooked and cooled, add half a pound of grated cheese, half a pound of sugar, a little salt, nutmeg and eight egg yolks
3. Mix everything well
4. Place the mixture in a buttered mold
5. Bake in the oven, covered with a lid full of hot coals
6. When cooked, serve with an appropriate sauce
