title: Pudim de Batatas Doces (Sweet Potato Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of cooked and peeled sweet potatoes
- five ounces of butter
- ten egg yolks
- three ounces of sugar
- a little salt
- lemon zest
- ground cinnamon
- ten egg whites

## Directions

1. Grate one pound of cooked and peeled sweet potatoes
2. Add five ounces of butter, ten egg yolks, three ounces of sugar, a little salt, lemon zest and ground cinnamon
3. Mix well
4. Add ten beaten egg whites
5. Mix everything together
6. Place in a buttered mold
7. Bake in the oven and serve

title: Pudim de Batatinhas (Potato Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of small potatoes
- half a pound of fine cornmeal
- eight egg yolks
- half a pound of butter
- one pound of sugar
- salt
- chopped lemon zest

## Directions

1. Peel and grate half a pound of small potatoes, mix with half a pound of fine cornmeal, eight egg yolks, half a pound of butter, one pound of sugar, salt and chopped lemon zest
2. Place the dough in a beef bladder and boil in a pot with water

title: Pudim de Batatinhas (Potato Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of potatoes
- half a pound of fine cornmeal
- eight egg yolks
- half a pound of butter
- one pound of sugar
- salt
- lemon zest, chopped

## Directions

1. Peel and grate half a pound of potatoes
2. Mix with half a pound of fine cornmeal, eight egg yolks, half a pound of butter, one pound of sugar, salt and chopped lemon zest
3. Place the mixture in a beef bladder
4. Boil in a pot with water
5. Remove the bladder and serve

title: Pudim de Cará (Yam Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- one yam
- two pounds of yam pulp
- one pound of rice flour
- one and a half pounds of sugar
- half a pound of butter
- one dozen egg yolks
- salt
- nutmeg
- cinnamon
- a little yeast

## Directions

1. Boil a yam until cooked
2. Peel and grate it
3. Mix two pounds of this pulp with one pound of rice flour, one and a half pounds of sugar, half a pound of butter, one dozen egg yolks, salt, nutmeg and cinnamon
4. Mix with a little yeast
5. Place in a warm location for four hours
6. Place in a buttered mold
7. Bake in the oven and serve

title: Pudim de Fubá Mimoso (Fine Cornmeal Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter of butter
- one pound of fine cornmeal
- one spoon of polvilho
- four eggs
- one quarter of sugar
- a portion of scalded raisins
- lemon zest
- salt
- nutmeg
- hot milk as needed

## Directions

1. Mix one quarter of butter with one pound of fine cornmeal, one spoon of polvilho, four eggs, one quarter of sugar
2. Add a portion of scalded raisins, lemon zest, salt and nutmeg
3. Add hot milk to form a thick mixture
4. Tie in a napkin
5. Place in a pot with boiling water
6. Boil until cooked
7. Serve with an appropriate sauce

title: Pudim de Fígado de Vitela (Veal Liver Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal liver
- half pound butter
- half pound fresh bacon, chopped finely
- onions, chopped
- salt
- pepper
- six beaten eggs
- four ounces scalded raisins
- two spoons wheat flour
- four spoons sugar
- one glass white wine
- bacon slices

## Directions

1. Cut the liver into small pieces, pass them through a coarse sieve
2. Mix with half pound butter, half pound fresh chopped bacon, chopped onions, salt, pepper, six beaten eggs, four ounces scalded raisins, two spoons of wheat flour, four spoons of sugar, one glass of white wine
3. Pour this mixture into a pan lined with bacon slices
4. Cook its contents in the oven or over coals, also covering the lid with coals
5. Serve with some sauce for pudding

title: Pudim de Gemada (Egg Yolk Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- five egg yolks
- five spoons of wheat flour
- half a bottle of milk
- salt
- two spoons of butter
- one quarter of peeled and crushed almonds
- one quarter of sugar
- one spoon of orange flower water

## Directions

1. Beat five egg yolks with five spoons of wheat flour, half a bottle of milk, salt and two spoons of butter
2. Boil everything, stirring continuously
3. When cooked, add one quarter of peeled and crushed almonds mixed with one quarter of sugar and one spoon of orange flower water
4. Mix everything together
5. Place in a buttered mold
6. Bake in the oven
7. Serve covered with sugar and cinnamon

title: Pudim de Leite (Milk Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bottle of milk
- three spoons of polvilho
- half a pound of sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon zest
- nutmeg
- one dozen egg yolks
- four egg whites

## Directions

1. Boil one bottle of milk with three spoons of polvilho, half a pound of sugar, cinnamon, lemon zest and nutmeg
2. When it has boiled a little, add one dozen egg yolks and four well-beaten egg whites until very firm
3. Mix everything well
4. Place the mixture in a mold
5. Bake in the oven and serve

title: Pudim de Marreco (Duck Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- one duck
- one chopped onion
- salt
- two spoons of fat
- two pounds of wheat flour
- four eggs
- one spoon of butter
- a portion of peeled raisins
- milk as needed

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from a duck
2. Fry the meat with one chopped onion and salt in two spoons of fat
3. Make a dough with two pounds of wheat flour, four eggs, one spoon of butter, a portion of peeled raisins and enough milk to form a thick mixture
4. Mix well
5. Spread on a board
6. Place the duck meat on this dough
7. Fold the edges together
8. Place in a napkin
9. Put in a pot with plenty of water
10. When cooked, serve with an appropriate sauce

title: Pudim de Nata (Cream Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve egg yolks
- one pound of ground crackers
- nutmeg
- one bottle of milk
- salt
- cinnamon
- a portion of raisins

## Directions

1. Beat twelve egg yolks with one pound of ground crackers, nutmeg, one bottle of milk, salt, cinnamon and a portion of raisins
2. Boil everything
3. When cooked and thick, place in a buttered mold
4. Bake in the oven
5. Serve with an appropriate sauce

title: Pudim de Orelhas de Porco (Pig's Ears Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- fifteen to twenty pig's ears
- pork skin
- salted water
- juniper berries
- coriander
- bay leaf
- clove
- garlic
- thyme
- marjoram
- sage
- four drams of saltpeter
- white wine
- brandy
- smoked sausage
- meat jelly

## Directions

1. Take fifteen to twenty pig's ears, singe and clean them well.
2. Split them in half, placing them in a clay vessel with some pork skin, and pour salted water over them.
3. Add juniper berries, coriander, bay leaf, clove, garlic, thyme, marjoram, sage, four drams of saltpeter.
4. Cover this terrine with a white cloth, placing a lid on top that closes it as well as possible.
5. Leave them soaking for ten to twelve days.
6. Then, draining them, place them to cook in a pan with water, a bottle of white wine and a cup of brandy.
7. Cook them thus for five hours, and after being well cooked, leave them in their broth until they become almost cold.
8. Drain them and arrange the ears in layers in a mold with some slices of smoked sausage.
9. Fill the mold, placing a lid on top that covers it perfectly, and over it a great weight to press well.
10. Leave everything to cool; in this state, remove from the mold, and serve with meat jelly.

title: Pudim de Pão (Bread Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of bread crumb
- hot milk
- one spoon of bread
- one dozen egg yolks
- three spoons of butter
- cinnamon
- nutmeg
- cloves
- a little salt
- half a pound of sugar
- four egg whites
- one glass of Madeira wine

## Directions

1. Soak half a pound of bread crumb in hot milk
2. When well soaked, remove and mash with a spoon
3. Mix with one dozen egg yolks, three spoons of butter, cinnamon, nutmeg, cloves, a little salt and half a pound of sugar
4. Add four egg whites beaten with one glass of Madeira wine
5. Place this mixture in a napkin
6. Put in a pot with water to boil
7. Serve with an appropriate sauce
8. Alternatively, it can be placed in a buttered mold and baked in the oven for one hour

title: Pudim de Rim de Vitela (Veal Kidney Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- two veal kidneys
- half a pound of ground crackers
- one quarter of sugar
- six beaten eggs
- a little salt
- one cup of milk

## Directions

1. Roast two veal kidneys
2. When cold, chop them very finely
3. Mix with half a pound of ground crackers, one quarter of sugar, six beaten eggs, a little salt and one cup of milk
4. Boil everything well in a saucepan
5. Place in a buttered mold
6. Bake in the oven and serve hot

title: Pudim London (London Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of polvilho
- one pound of butter
- eight egg yolks
- lemon zest
- salt
- nutmeg
- a portion of raisins

## Directions

1. Mix half a pound of polvilho with one pound of butter, eight egg yolks, lemon zest, salt, nutmeg and a portion of raisins
2. Mix very well
3. Place in a buttered mold
4. Place the mold in a pot to boil
5. Boil for two hours
6. Serve with an appropriate sauce

title: Pudim Mimoso (Delicate Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight ounces of sugar
- eight ounces of butter
- eight ounces of polvilho
- eight egg yolks
- a little salt
- vanilla
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Mix eight ounces of sugar, eight ounces of butter, eight ounces of polvilho and eight egg yolks
2. Add a little salt, vanilla and nutmeg
3. Mix well for one hour
4. Place in a buttered mold
5. Bake in the oven for half an hour

title: Pudim Polaco (Polish Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight spoons of flour
- eight eggs
- one spoon of marrow
- salt
- a portion of raisins
- milk as needed

## Directions

1. Mix eight spoons of flour with eight eggs, one spoon of marrow, salt and a portion of raisins
2. Add enough milk to form a mixture of good consistency
3. Place this mixture on a cloth
4. Tie it
5. Put in a pot with water to cook over high heat
6. Serve with a sauce for puddings

title: Pudim à Moda de Paris (Pudding Paris Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of beef marrow
- one quarter of bacon
- half a pound of rice powder
- seven egg yolks
- two whole eggs
- half a cup of whole milk
- one glass of vanilla liqueur
- salt
- nutmeg
- half a dozen peeled and minced apples
- one dozen peeled and minced almonds
- two pounds of wheat flour
- one eighth of salt
- two spoonfuls of water
- four eggs
- one quarter of beef kidney fat
- four egg yolks
- one spoonful of arrowroot
- salt
- two spoonfuls of sugar
- one cup of white wine

## Directions

1. Knead one pound of beef marrow with one quarter of bacon, half a pound of rice powder, seven egg yolks, two whole eggs, half a cup of whole milk, one glass of vanilla liqueur, salt, nutmeg, half a dozen peeled and minced apples, and one dozen peeled and minced almonds
2. On the other hand, knead two pounds of wheat flour, one eighth of salt, two spoonfuls of water, four eggs, and one quarter of beef kidney fat
3. Extend this dough over a cloth
4. Combine the above mixture, join the ends of the cloth, tie them, and place the dough to boil in a pot with plenty of water
5. Boil for one hour and remove the cloth carefully so as not to break the dough, serve with a sauce made of four beaten egg yolks with one spoonful of arrowroot, salt, two spoonfuls of sugar, one cup of white wine, bringing everything to a boil a little, and serve

title: Pá de Carneiro Assada (Roasted Lamb Shoulder)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb shoulder
- water
- salt
- onion
- garlic clove
- parsley
- pepper
- nutmeg
- butter
- dried breadcrumbs
- picante sauce

## Directions

1. Boil a shoulder in water and salt until cooked
2. Cover it with one onion, one garlic clove, finely chopped parsley, pepper and ground nutmeg
3. Pour some spoonfuls of melted butter on top
4. Wrap with dried breadcrumbs pressed and placed in a pan
5. Take to the oven where it is left to roast until it takes on a good color
6. Serve with a picante sauce

title: Pá de Carneiro com Cominhos (Lamb Shoulder with Cumin)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb shoulder
- water
- salt
- half an ounce of cumin
- breadcrumbs
- vegetables

## Directions

1. Take a lamb shoulder and cut the meat into pieces
2. Scald these pieces in hot water and place them on the fire with cold water
3. Remove the foam and add some salt, half an ounce of cumin tied in a little bag and breadcrumbs
4. When cooked, serve with vegetables

title: Pá de Carneiro Estufada (Braised Lamb Shoulder)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb shoulder
- bacon strips
- ham
- carapicú
- onions
- parsley
- salt
- water or meat broth
- cornstarch (polvilho)
- vinegar
- chicory or cabbage

## Directions

1. Place the shoulder in a pan on strips of bacon with ham cut into small pieces, a handful of carapicú, sliced onions, parsley and salt
2. Add a bowl of water or meat broth and let it boil
3. When cooked, remove the fat from the broth as well as the parsley and bacon
4. Thicken with half a spoonful of cornstarch
5. Add one spoonful of vinegar and serve with chicory or sautéed cabbage

title: Pá de Carneiro Estufada (Braised Mutton Shoulder)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton shoulder
- fine strips of bacon
- strips of ham
- salt
- parsley
- bay leaves
- marjoram
- meat broth
- lemon juice
- peas or squash shoots

## Directions

1. Scald the mutton shoulder
2. Lard it with fine strips of bacon and ham
3. Put it in a pan with salt, parsley, bay leaves, marjoram, one cup of broth, and one spoon of lemon juice
4. Let it boil over coals for two hours
5. Strain the broth, remove the fat, and reduce it to caramel point
6. Pour this over the shoulder
7. Serve with fresh peas or squash shoots

title: Pá de Carneiro Guisado com Capa (Stuffed Lamb Shoulder Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb shoulder
- salted pork meat
- carapicú
- onion
- parsley
- pepper
- clove
- cloth
- egg yolks
- terra orange juice

## Directions

1. Remove the bone from the shoulder by cutting the meat lengthwise
2. Cover it with a mixture made of salted pork meat, carapicú, onion and parsley (all finely chopped), pepper and one clove
3. Wrap in cloth securing it with ties
4. When cooked, incorporate the rest of the broth with two egg yolks
5. Add a little juice of terra orange and serve with the meat after removing the cloth

title: Pá de Cordeiro Assado (Roasted Lamb Shoulder)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb shoulder
- carapicú filling
- bacon strips
- salt
- clove
- onion
- twine

## Directions

1. Open the shoulder lengthwise
2. Remove the bone
3. Fill inside with carapicú
4. Lard the outside with thin bacon strips
5. Add salt, clove, and onion
6. Tie with twine, giving it a round shape
7. Roast in a pan over coals or in the oven
8. Serve with its own sauce

title: Pá de Cordeiro Assado (Roasted Lamb Shoulder)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb shoulder
- carapicús
- thin strips of bacon
- salt
- clove
- onion
- twine

## Directions

1. Take the lamb shoulder, open it lengthwise, remove the bone, and fill it inside with carapicús
2. Lard it on the outside with thin strips of bacon
3. Add salt, clove, and onion
4. Tie it with twine, giving it a round shape
5. Roast it in a pan over coals or in the oven
6. Serve with its own sauce

title: Pá de Cordeiro com Chuchús (Lamb Shoulder with Chayote)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb shoulder
- bacon
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- water
- bacon pieces
- onions
- cabbage leaf
- parsley
- bay leaf
- chayote

## Directions

1. Remove the bone from the shoulder
2. Lard the meat with bacon seasoned with pepper, salt, and nutmeg
3. Roll the shoulder to give it an elongated shape
4. Cook it in water with small pieces of bacon, onions, a cabbage leaf, parsley, and bay leaf
5. Once cooked, remove the meat and strain the broth
6. Remove the fat and reduce it until only a few spoonfuls remain
7. Fry in it a portion of peeled chayote, cut into cubes and scalded in hot water
8. Serve with the shoulder

title: Pá de Cordeiro com Chuchús (Lamb Shoulder with Chayotes)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb shoulder
- bacon
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- water

## Directions

1. Remove the bone from the shoulder
2. Lard the meat with bacon seasoned with pepper, salt, and nutmeg
3. Roll the shoulder to form an elongated shape
4. Cook in water

title: Pá de Leitão à Moda Tártara (Suckling Pig Shoulder Tartar Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- suckling pig shoulder
- salt
- broken pepper
- sweet oil
- lemon juice
- tartar sauce

## Directions

1. Soak the shoulder in salt, broken pepper, sweet oil and lemon juice.
2. After two hours, roast it over a lively fire.
3. At the moment of serving, place tartar sauce on top.

title: Pá de Veado Enrolada (Rolled Venison Shoulder)
body:
## Ingredients

- two venison shoulders
- some meat
- bacon
- onions
- parsley
- salt
- pimento peppers
- two whole eggs
- portion of bread crumbs
- broth
- butter
- two onions
- parsley
- marjoram
- ginger
- one bottle of wine

## Directions

1. Take the two venison shoulders, remove the bones and some meat that is chopped with an equal amount of bacon, some onions, parsley, salt and pimento peppers, and knead with two whole eggs and a portion of bread crumbs moistened with broth
2. Spread this mixture over the inside of the beaten shoulder and roll it up with the mixture inside
3. Tie the rolls with a string to keep them in this form
4. Braise in hot butter
5. Add two onions, parsley, marjoram, ginger and one bottle of wine, letting everything boil for two hours until well cooked, being careful to keep the pot well covered
6. Place on a plate and cover with the strained and reduced sauce and serve

title: Pá de Veado Ensopado com Castanhas do Pará (Venison Shoulder Stewed with Brazil Nuts)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat from two venison shoulders
- fat
- one tablespoon of wheat flour
- one bowl of water
- salt
- some pimento peppers
- one onion
- parsley
- marjoram
- portion of Brazil nuts
- one egg yolk
- one glass of sour orange juice

## Directions

1. Cut the meat from the two venison shoulders and then cut it into small pieces and braise in hot fat
2. Stir and add one tablespoon of wheat flour, one bowl of water, salt, some pimento peppers, one onion cut up, parsley, marjoram and a portion of Brazil nuts
3. Let cook over moderate heat until everything is cooked
4. Remove the meat and nuts and reduce the broth, incorporating it with one egg yolk and one glass of sour orange juice and serve

title: Pá de Veado Guisada com Carurú (Venison Shoulder Stewed with Okra)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison shoulder
- water
- salt
- pimento peppers
- parsley
- onion leaves
- bay leaf
- ginger
- two tablespoons of butter
- one onion
- meat broth
- one tablespoon of arrowroot
- twelve tomatoes
- portion of okra
- cornmeal porridge

## Directions

1. Boil the venison shoulder in water, salt, pimento peppers, parsley, onion leaves, bay leaf and ginger
2. When cooked, separate the meat from the bones and cut it into pieces
3. On the other hand, melt two tablespoons of butter, add one chopped onion, the meat broth, one tablespoon of arrowroot, twelve tomatoes and a portion of okra
4. Let reduce and when cooked, add the meat and serve with cornmeal porridge

title: Pá de Veado Guisada com Espargos (Braised Venison Shoulder with Asparagus)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison shoulder
- water
- salt
- onion
- ginger
- peppers
- parsley
- asparagus shoots
- breadcrumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil the venison shoulder in water with salt, onion, ginger, peppers, parsley and a portion of asparagus shoots tied in a bundle
2. When the shoulder is cooked, separate the meat from the bones and coat it in breadcrumbs
3. Fry it in butter
4. Place the asparagus shoots on a plate with the fried meat on top
5. Pour the strained and reduced broth over all and serve

title: Pá de Veado Guisada com Espargos (Venison Shoulder Stewed with Asparagus)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison shoulder
- water
- salt
- onion
- ginger
- pimento peppers
- parsley
- portion of asparagus sprouts
- breadcrumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil the venison shoulder in water, salt, onion, ginger, pimento peppers, parsley and a portion of asparagus sprouts tied in a bundle
2. When the shoulder is cooked, separate the meat from the bones and wrap it in breadcrumbs
3. Fry it in butter
4. Place the asparagus sprouts on a plate with the fried meat on top
5. Pour over the whole the strained and reduced broth and serve

title: Pá de Veado Refogado com Beterraba Rouxa (Venison Shoulder Braised with Red Beets)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison shoulder
- salt
- lemon juice
- portion of red beets
- one bottle of red wine
- nutmeg
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Anoint the shoulder with salt and lemon juice and roast it a little on the spit over flame to become toasted externally
2. Then remove it and put it in a pot with a portion of red beets, peeled and cut into small pieces, one bottle of red wine, grated nutmeg and the juice of one lemon
3. Let boil over moderate heat for two hours and serve with the beets

title: Pá de Vitela Assada (Roasted Veal Shoulder)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal shoulder
- butter
- onion leaves
- salt
- pepper
- meat broth
- lemon

## Directions

1. Place the veal shoulder in a pan with a little butter, salt, onion leaves, pepper and two cups of meat broth
2. Roast over coals or in the oven
3. Serve with the proper sauce, adding some peeled lemon slices without seeds before serving

title: Pá de Vitela Assada no Espeto (Veal Shoulder Roasted on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal shoulder
- salt
- garlic
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Lift the skin covering the shoulder
2. Insert a small tube under it to lift it
3. Put it on the spit
4. Season with salt, garlic and vinegar
5. Roast over a brisk fire for the first minutes to harden it, then finish roasting over coals
6. Frequently moisten it with its own sauce
7. Serve with salad

title: Pá de Vitela à Italiana (Veal Shoulder Italian Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal shoulder
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- parsley
- onion
- beef butter
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Lift carefully the skin covering the shoulder, leaving it attached at the bone end
2. Lard it, seasoning with salt, pepper, nutmeg, chopped parsley and onion
3. Cover it again with its skin, securing with stitches
4. Roast in the oven with a little beef butter
5. When cooked, place on a platter, pour a little butter over it, and cover with a layer of grated cheese
6. Serve with its own sauce

title: Pão de Miúdos (Offal Loaf)
body:
## Ingredients

- half of a pig's head
- two pig's feet
- heart
- some pieces of lard
- pig's tongue
- water
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- sweet oil or vinegar

## Directions

1. Let half the head, two feet, the heart, pieces of lard and the tongue of the pig boil in water and salt until cooked.
2. Remove the skin from the head and feet.
3. Line a cloth with these and the other skins, placing in it the rest of the meat chopped, the heart, the tongue cut into strips, with salt, pepper and cloves.
4. Tie the cloth well and let it cook for half an hour.
5. Let it cool, keeping everything for some time between two boards with a weight on top.
6. Remove the cloth and serve with a cold sauce of sweet oil or vinegar.

title: Pão Dourado (Gilded Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- milk
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Soak bread sliced in milk and drain the slices
2. Pass these slices in egg yolks

title: Pão Dourado (Golden Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread sliced into slices
- milk
- egg yolks from beaten eggs
- sugar syrup at mirror point
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Soak bread sliced into slices in milk, drain them
2. Pass these slices through beaten egg yolks and place them in a pan with sugar syrup at mirror point
3. Remove them, place on plates, cover with sugar and cinnamon, and serve
4. Alternatively, soak the bread in white wine instead of milk

title: Pés de Porco com Ovos (Pig's Feet with Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- pig's feet
- water
- salt
- butter
- garlic
- Indian pepper
- parsley
- onion
- marjoram
- twelve eggs
- wheat flour

## Directions

1. Clean the feet and cook them with water and salt until the meat separates from the bones.
2. Drain them in a colander and remove all the bones.
3. Place the meat in a pot with a little butter, salt, garlic, Indian pepper all well ground, parsley, onion and marjoram, and let it sauté.
4. Beat twelve eggs with the whites, add two tablespoons of wheat flour, mix this meat with eggs.
5. Place a tablespoon of butter in a pot and put it over low heat with a lid that has coals on top.
6. Cook slowly and after cooking, serve.

title: Pés de Porco com Tubaras (Pig's Feet with Tubaras)
body:
## Ingredients

- pig's feet
- tubaras (a type of tuber)
- sausage meat
- caul fat

## Directions

1. After the pig's feet have been prepared and cooked as previously explained, remove all the bones.
2. On the other hand, chop the skin of the tubaras and pound it with a little sausage meat.
3. Stuff the pig's feet with this filling, adding to them some slices of tubaras, and wrapping everything in pieces of caul fat.
4. Place on a moderate fire to roast.

title: Pés de Porco com Tubaras (Pig's Feet with Tubaras)
body:
## Ingredients

- pig's feet
- tubara skins
- sausage meat
- tubara slices
- caul fat

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook the feet as previously explained, then remove all the bones.
2. Chop the skin of the tubaras and pound with a little sausage meat.
3. Garnish the feet with this filling, adding some slices of tubaras, and wrap everything in pieces of caul fat.
4. Roast over moderate heat.

title: Pés de Porco à Sainte-Menehould (Pig's Feet Saint-Menehould)
body:
## Ingredients

- pig's feet
- white cloth strips
- thyme
- bay leaf
- carrots
- onions
- cloves
- parsley
- chives
- some brine
- white wine
- dried bread crumbs
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wrap the feet in strips of white cloth so they do not fall apart and place in a pot with thyme, bay leaf, carrots, onions, cloves, parsley, chives, some brine and white wine.
2. After cooking for twenty-four hours, let them cool in their sauce.
3. When cold, cover with powder of dried bread crumbs, placing them alternately and repeated several times in powder of dried bread crumbs with salt and pepper.
4. Roast on low heat and serve when they have taken on color.

title: Pés de Vitela Afogados (Veal Feet Braised)
body:
## Ingredients

- four veal feet
- water
- salt
- wheat flour
- butter
- raisins
- almonds, peeled and cut lengthwise
- lemon slices, peeled and without seeds

## Directions

1. Clean the four veal feet well, blanch them, soaking them for some time
2. Then boil them in water and salt
3. Remove all the bones, strain the broth
4. Brown a little wheat flour in butter until it colors
5. Add the broth to it, some raisins, almonds peeled and cut lengthwise, some lemon slices peeled and without seeds
6. Let the veal feet meat finish cooking in this sauce and serve

title: Pés de Vitela Recheados (Stuffed Veal Feet)
body:
## Ingredients

- four veal feet
- water
- salt
- ham, chopped
- onions
- parsley
- pepper
- egg yolks
- bread crumb soaked in milk
- white wine
- cloves
- nutmeg
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cook the four veal feet in water and salt
2. Remove the bones without tearing the skin
3. Fill the space with ham chopped and mixed with onions, parsley, salt, pepper, egg yolks, bread crumb softened in milk
4. Thus prepared, place them to cook over moderate heat in white wine and some cloves of India, nutmeg, and a spoon of sugar

title: Pés de Vitela Recheados Fritos (Fried Stuffed Veal Feet)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal feet
- butter
- small potatoes

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook the veal feet as described above
2. Fry them in butter with some small potatoes

title: Quadrado de Carneiro com Giló (Lamb Square with Giló)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb square
- Lean bacon lard
- Butter-greased paper
- Giló
- Water
- Salt
- Sauce from roasting

## Directions

1. Beat the lamb square and lard it with lean bacon
2. Spit it and cover with butter-greased paper
3. Roast it
4. When nearly roasted, remove the paper to take on colour
5. Place on a plate
6. Arrange a dozen gilós around it, cooked in water and salt
7. Top with the roasting sauce

title: Quadrado de Carneiro com Quiabos (Lamb Square with Okra)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb squares
- Cold water
- Salt
- Parsley
- Onion leaves
- Vinegar
- Sugar
- Minas cornmeal mush

## Directions

1. Remove excess fat from the lamb squares
2. Roast them a little on the spit, turning several times
3. Remove and cook with cold water, salt, parsley, and onion leaves
4. When nearly cooked, add a spoonful of vinegar
5. If the sauce becomes too acidic, add a spoonful of sugar
6. After boiling a little more, serve with Minas cornmeal mush

title: Quadrado de Carneiro Cozido com Ameixas (Lamb Square Cooked with Plums)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb square
- Hot water
- Cold water
- Salt
- Parsley
- Bay leaves
- Thyme
- Half pound of plums
- Quince pieces
- Four spoonfuls of sugar
- Chalice of white wine
- Lemon juice
- Four egg yolks

## Directions

1. Beat the lamb square lightly and scald it in hot water
2. Place in cold water with salt, parsley, bay leaves, and thyme in a vessel over fire
3. Let boil until nearly cooked and broth nearly dry
4. Add half pound of plums, or in their absence, peeled quince pieces without seeds, four spoonfuls of sugar, a chalice of white wine, and a little lemon juice
5. Boil for half an hour
6. Thicken the broth with four egg yolks
7. Serve

title: Quarto de Carneiro Assado (Roasted Lamb Quarter)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb quarter
- hot water
- salt
- ground pepper
- chopped parsley
- strips of bacon
- wheat flour
- butter
- egg yolk
- paper
- fresh vegetables

## Directions

1. After scalding the lamb quarter in hot water, lard it
2. Season with salt, ground pepper, and chopped parsley on the outside with strips of bacon
3. Cover both sides with a paste made of wheat flour with butter and an egg yolk
4. Wrap the entire quarter with paper untied in butter
5. Spit roast it
6. Serve with fresh vegetables

title: Quarto de Carneiro Assado no Espeto (Lamb Quarter Roasted on Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb quarter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter

## Directions

1. Remove the kidney from the quarter
2. Beat well
3. Wash in many cold waters
4. Rub finally with salt and pepper
5. Spit it to be roasted, moistening it with butter

title: Quarto de Carneiro Assado, Frio com Gelea (Cold Roasted Lamb Quarter with Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb quarter
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Parsley
- Pepper
- Onion
- Bay leaves
- Cloves
- Veal feet
- Butter
- Jelly

## Directions

1. Beat the lamb quarter well
2. Soak for one day in vinegar with salt, parsley, pepper, onion, bay leaves, and cloves
3. Cook with four veal feet
4. When nearly completely cooked, remove and fry in a pan with butter
5. After browning well on both sides, let cool
6. Thicken the broth until it reaches jelly point
7. Strain and when cold, garnish the lamb quarter with slices of jelly

title: Quarto de Carneiro Estufado (Braised Lamb Quarter)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb quarter
- Butter
- Hot water
- Salt
- Burnt sugar
- Toasted wheat flour
- Vinegar
- Lemon slices
- Capers
- Chopped sardines
- Onion
- Bay leaves
- Pepper
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Fry the lamb quarter in butter until browned
2. Add hot water and salt
3. Remove the quarter and remove the fat from the sauce
4. Add a little burnt sugar, a spoonful of toasted wheat flour, vinegar, lemon slices, capers, chopped sardines, a sliced onion, bay leaves, pepper, and sugar
5. When all is boiling, return the quarter and finish cooking over moderate fire
6. The vessel should be well covered

title: Quarto de Carneiro Guisado em Agua (Lamb Quarter Stewed in Water)
body:
## Ingredients

- Whole lamb quarter
- Onion
- Garlic clove
- Carrots
- Cabbage leaves
- Bay leaf
- Cloves
- Parsley
- Chervil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Carapicús
- Pickled cucumbers

## Directions

1. Cut a piece from the side of the foot with bone so it fits in the pan
2. After beating and larding, place to boil in water that completely covers it
3. Add onion, a clove of garlic, carrots, cabbage leaves, a bay leaf, two cloves, parsley, chervil, salt, pepper, and a handful of carapicús
4. Always remove the scum and let boil
5. When cooked and water nearly dry, strain the broth and let continue to cook
6. Remove the broth, leaving only what is necessary for the sauce
7. Remove the fat and add some small pickled cucumbers
8. Serve with the meat

title: Quarto de Carneiro ou Cabrito Assado Coberto (Covered Roasted Lamb or Kid Quarter)
body:
## Ingredients

- fat lamb or kid quarter
- salt
- bread crumbs
- salad

## Directions

1. If the quarter is very fat, do not lard it
2. Spit roast it covered with paper untied, seasoned only with salt
3. Once cooked, remove the paper
4. Moisten the quarter well with the sauce that dripped
5. Cover it with bread crumbs
6. Give it a few more turns on the spit so this coating takes on a good color
7. Serve with some salad

title: Quarto de Carneiro ou Cabrito Assado com Abóbora d'Água (Roasted Lamb or Kid Quarter with Water Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb quarter
- sardines
- cloth
- vinegar
- bay leaves
- ginger
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- garlic
- bacon slices
- meat broth
- wine
- water squash
- fat

## Directions

1. Take the lamb quarter, remove the bone, lard the meat, fill the space with sardines
2. Wrap the quarter in cloth and soak it for six hours in vinegar, bay leaves, ginger, salt, pepper, onion, and garlic
3. Put it in a pan lined with bacon slices, two bowls of broth, and one of wine
4. Let it cook in the oven
5. Once cooked, separate it and strain the broth
6. Remove the fat and reduce it to two cups
7. Take a fresh water squash, peel it, remove the seeds, and cut it into cubes
8. Fry the cubes in a little fat and salt
9. Add a little water and let it cook over coals until dry
10. Serve with the quarter, pouring the sauce on it at the table

title: Quarto de Carneiro Refogado (Braised Lamb Quarter)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb quarter
- Bacon lard with seasonings
- Salt
- Pepper
- Garlic
- Parsley
- Bay leaves
- Bacon slices
- Carapicú
- Wine
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut the beaten lamb quarter into large slices three fingers thick
2. Lard these with bacon seasoned with salt, pepper, garlic, parsley, and bay leaves
3. Place in a well-sealed and sealed casserole over bacon slices with a handful of carapicú
4. Put in oven or over coals with fire on top for one hour
5. Add a glass of wine and a spoonful of lemon juice
6. Let boil for another hour
7. Remove the fat and serve with its own sauce

title: Quarto de Carneiro à Similhança de Quarto de Caça (Lamb Quarter Resembling Game Quarter)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb quarter
- Salt
- Garlic
- Cetola
- Butter
- Meat drippings

## Directions

1. Beat the lamb quarter well
2. Marinate for one day in a sauce made of salt, garlic, and chopped cetola
3. Remove the thin skin
4. Lard lightly
5. Place on spit
6. Let roast slowly, moistening at first with melted butter, and afterwards with the juice that collected on the plate

title: Quarto de Cordeiro ou do Cabrito Imitando Caça (Lamb or Kid Quarter Imitating Game)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb or kid quarter
- thin strips of bacon
- cloth
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaves
- clove
- grated ginger
- paper
- butter
- wine
- fat
- small potatoes
- wheat flour
- milk
- eggs

## Directions

1. Take the lamb or kid quarter, lard it on the outside with thin strips of bacon
2. Wrap it tightly in cloth and soak for twenty-four hours in vinegar seasoned with salt, pepper, bay leaves, clove, and grated ginger
3. Spit roast it covered with buttered paper, or braise it in a tightly covered pan with two spoonfuls of fat and three glasses of red wine
4. Serve either way with its own sauce, first removing the coating, with small potatoes or cakes made of wheat flour, milk, and eggs

title: Quarto de Cordeiro à Allemã (Lamb Quarter German Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb quarter
- hot water
- fine salt
- ground pepper
- chopped parsley
- fine strips of bacon
- wheat flour
- egg yolks
- cow butter

## Directions

1. Scald the lamb quarter in hot water and sprinkle with fine salt, ground pepper, and chopped parsley
2. Cover with fine strips of bacon
3. Roll it in wheat flour and cover with beaten egg yolks
4. Put it in a large casserole with cow butter
5. Let it cook over gentle heat for half an hour
6. Half an hour before serving, add a layer of bread crumbs
7. Let it roast for another half hour over a lively fire

title: Quarto de Veado com Capa Assado no Forno (Venison Haunch with Crust Roasted in the Oven)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison haunch
- salt
- ground pepper
- eight eggs
- one spoonful of salt
- one pound of wheat flour
- nutmeg
- fat

## Directions

1. Take the venison haunch, beat it on both sides
2. Sprinkle it on all sides with salt and ground pepper
3. Leave it thus for twenty-four hours
4. Make a dough with eight eggs, one spoonful of salt, one pound of wheat flour and a little nutmeg
5. When the dough is ready, wrap the haunch with this dough and grease it with fat
6. Place the haunch in a hot oven to roast
7. In two to three hours it will be cooked and browned, then serve it

title: Quarto de Veado de Escabeche e Assado no Espeto (Marinated Venison Haunch Roasted on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison haunch
- lard
- strong vinegar
- onions
- garlic cloves
- parsley
- bay leaves
- cloves
- green peppers
- salt
- ginger
- butter
- white wine

## Directions

1. Trim the venison haunch and lard the exterior with bacon
2. Soak it in strong vinegar with onions, two cloves of garlic, parsley, bay leaves, cloves, some green peppers, salt and ginger
3. Leave it for four to six days
4. Thread it on a spit and roast it, basting with the drippings from the haunch to which two spoonfuls of butter and a glass of white wine have been added

title: Quarto de Veado Estufado com Carás (Venison Haunch Braised with Taro)
body:
## Ingredients

- venison haunch
- breadcrumbs
- bacon
- water
- white wine
- salt
- peppers
- grated ginger
- onion
- taro
- egg yolks
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Brown the venison haunch, coat it in breadcrumbs and cover it with a slice of bacon
2. Place it in a pot with a bottle of water, another of white wine, salt, peppers, grated ginger, a cut onion and taro cooked, peeled and cut into slices
3. Cover the pot and leave it to boil near the fire until the meat is cooked
4. Remove it and place it on a plate with the taro around it
5. Thicken the broth with two beaten egg yolks mixed in a cup of vinegar and serve

title: Quarto de Vitela Assado no Espeto (Veal Quarter Roasted on the Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal quarter
- water
- salt
- pepper
- garlic clove
- parsley
- thyme
- chive leaves
- marjoram
- lemon juice
- paper
- butter
- meat sauce

## Directions

1. Boil the veal quarter for five minutes, remove it from water and let it cool
2. Lard it gently
3. Soak it for six hours in salt, pepper, a garlic clove, parsley, thyme, chive leaves, marjoram, all chopped, and a little lemon juice
4. Spit it covering it with paper greased with butter
5. Once roasted, remove the paper and serve with some meat sauce or any other sauce

title: Quarto de Vitela com Gelea (Veal Quarter with Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal quarter
- white wine
- water
- salt
- four veal feet, cut
- pepper
- three cloves
- nutmeg
- wine
- lemon
- bundle of herbs

## Directions

1. Beat the veal quarter well, soak it in white wine for one day
2. The next day, boil it in water and salt with four cut veal feet, pepper, three cloves of India, nutmeg, a little wine, a split lemon and a bundle of herbs
3. Once cooked, remove it, placing some drops of the broth on a plate and observe if these, once cold, have the point for aspic; otherwise let them boil until this point
4. Then strain the broth, remove the fat, put the quarter back for a few minutes
5. Then remove it and let everything cool
6. The next day, cut the aspic into thin slices and cover the quarter with these

title: Quarto de Vitela Frio Assado com Gelea (Cold Veal Quarter Roasted with Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal quarter
- aspic from the broth

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook the veal quarter as already explained, but do not let the quarter finish cooking
2. Remove it before, spit it and finish roasting it over flames
3. Remove it and let it cool, garnishing it the next day with the aspic from the sauce

title: Quarto de Vitela Panado (Breaded Veal Quarter)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal quarter
- dried biscuits, ground
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil the quarter for five minutes, remove it and let it cool to cover it with ground dried biscuits
2. Spit it and roast it, occasionally spooning melted butter over it so it does not dry
3. Serve with Robert sauce or tomato sauce, or also with salads, potatoes, sardines, or lettuces

title: Quarto de Vitela à Caçador (Veal Quarter Hunter's Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal quarter
- vinegar
- salt
- saltpetre
- garlic
- pepper
- onion
- bundle of aromatic herbs

## Directions

1. Take the veal quarter, soak it in vinegar with salt, a little saltpetre, garlic, pepper, onion, a bundle of aromatic herbs
2. Remove the film, lard it and roast on the spit

title: Quarto de Vitella Recheada (Stuffed Quarter of Veal)
body:
## Ingredients

- one leg of veal
- veal meat
- pigeons
- wheat flour
- eggs
- parsley
- onion
- two cloves

## Directions

1. Take a leg of veal and remove the bones without opening the meat, so that it forms a sack
2. Fill this cavity with a mixture made of chopped veal and pigeon meat, wheat flour, eggs, parsley, onion and two cloves
3. Place the prepared leg in a pan over lardons of salt pork
4. Roast it over coals in the oven, turning it from time to time
5. Serve with fresh peas, beans or salads

title: Queijo Assado (Roasted Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of half-cured cheese
- lard or fat
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Thread some slices of half-cured cheese on a spit of bread
2. Coat with a little lard
3. Roast over coals on both sides
4. Serve hot, sprinkling with sugar and cinnamon
5. It can also be roasted on a grill

title: Queijo de Fígado de Porco (Pork Liver Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of pork liver
- two pounds of lard
- half pound of lard
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- nutmeg
- cilantro
- serpol
- bay leaf
- chives
- sage
- caul or thin lard slices
- gelatin

## Directions

1. Mince three pounds of pork liver, two pounds of lard, and half pound of lard together
2. Season with salt, pepper, cloves, nutmeg, cilantro, serpol, bay leaf, finely chopped chives, and sage
3. Line a casserole with caul or very thin lard slices so the liver does not stick
4. Place a layer of filling at the bottom, then seasoned lard slices, then another layer of filling, alternating until the casserole is full
5. The last layer should be lard slices
6. Cook in the oven for two hours
7. Let the cheese cool, then remove from the casserole by placing it briefly in boiling water
8. Eat cold, though hot is also very pleasant

title: Queijo de Porco (Pork Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pig's head
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaf
- marjoram
- cloves
- cilantro
- half nutmeg
- chives
- two heads of garlic
- ham slices
- vinegar
- white wine
- roots
- bundle of aromatic herbs

## Directions

1. Scald a pig's head in water with salt, then remove all bones
2. Cut the meat, ears, fat, and tongue into very thin slices, being careful not to damage the skin
3. Season with salt, pepper, bay leaf, marjoram, cloves, cilantro, half nutmeg, finely chopped chives, and two heads of finely chopped garlic
4. Place the cleaned head skin in a round casserole and arrange the meat and fat slices on it
5. Add some ham slices, vinegar, and salt
6. Place another equal layer on top and continue alternating, filling the head skin
7. Once filled, sew the skin closed with strong thread and wrap it in white cloth, tied with twine
8. Place in a pot with white wine, roots, a bundle of aromatic herbs, pepper, and salt
9. Let it cook for seven hours
10. When cooked, remove the cloth and place in a vessel that can contain it perfectly
11. Place a board on top that fits inside the vessel, and put a large weight on the board so it takes the shape of the vessel
12. Eat cold

title: Queijo de Porco à Inglesa (English Pork Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pig's head
- salt
- saltpeter powder
- pepper
- cilantro
- nutmeg
- serpol
- bay leaf
- sage
- parsley
- chives
- lemon zest and juice
- white wine
- onions
- carrots
- four cloves
- bundle of aromatic herbs
- chicken breast strips

## Directions

1. Remove bones from a pig's head and cut thin strips from the head and ears, removing the skin in large strips
2. Roll and tie the strips together
3. Place them in escabeche for at least three days in a terrine with salt, a little saltpeter powder, pepper, cilantro, nutmeg, a little serpol, bay leaf, ground sage, parsley, chopped chives, lemon zest and juice
4. Remove from the vinegar, gather all pieces in cloth and tie
5. Cook in water, white wine, onions, carrots, four cloves, a bundle of aromatic herbs, salt, and the head bones, ensuring enough water for the cloth to float
6. Heat a mold in a casserole with the skins that have soaked, and arrange the meats and ears, tongue in alternating layers with a filling made from meat scraps and some chicken breast strips
7. Cover with a tight-fitting lid and place a weight of one arroba on top while hot to make the cheese compact
8. When cold, remove from the mold by placing it briefly in boiling water
9. Eat cold
10. Use the broth to make an excellent soup

title: Queijo Frito (Fried Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- cheese, sliced
- egg yolk
- wheat flour
- lard or fat
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut the cheese into slices
2. Dip in egg yolk
3. Wrap in wheat flour
4. Fry in lard on both sides
5. Serve sprinkled with sugar and cinnamon

title: Quiabos Cozidos (Cooked Okra)
body:
## Ingredients

- three to four dozen fresh whole okras
- water
- salt
- fat
- vinegar
- plenty of comaré peppers

## Directions

1. Take three to four dozen fresh whole okras
2. Boil in water and salt
3. After boiling three to four times, remove them
4. Place on a plate
5. Serve with a sauce of fat, vinegar and plenty of comaré peppers on top

title: Quiabos Guisados com Carne (Okra Stewed with Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- okras
- fat
- chopped meat
- salt
- comaré peppers
- water

## Directions

1. Cut okras into rounds
2. Sauté in hot fat
3. Add chopped meat, salt, comaré peppers, and a little water
4. Let boil
5. Serve with angú

title: Quitute de Carne de Veado (Venison Delicacy)
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted venison meat
- two spoonfuls of wheat flour
- one spoonful of sugar
- three spoonfuls of butter
- white wine
- peeled lemon slices
- scalded raisins
- toasted and chopped peanuts

## Directions

1. Take the roasted venison meat, separate it from the bones and cut it into pieces
2. Make a sauce with two spoonfuls of wheat flour and one spoonful of sugar, which are fried in three spoonfuls of butter until they take on a good colour
3. Add a bottle of white wine, the slices of a peeled lemon, a portion of scalded raisins and an equal amount of toasted and chopped peanuts
4. Place the meat in a casserole, boil it for one hour and serve

title: Quitute de Frango Assado (Roasted Chicken Delicacy)
body:
## Ingredients

- one roasted chicken
- one pound of ham
- meat broth
- red wine
- salt
- one onion
- lemon juice
- three egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut the roasted chicken into pieces with one pound of ham
2. Place in a casserole over the fire with one cup of meat broth, another of red wine, little salt, one cut onion, a little lemon juice, and three egg yolks
3. Let it boil for half an hour over moderate heat and serve

title: Quitute de Fígado de Porco (Pork Liver Delicacy)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork liver
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- minced garlic
- chives
- sage
- wheat flour
- white wine
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut liver into equal slices
2. Melt a little butter in a frying pan and place the liver slices in it
3. Season with pepper and salt, adding minced garlic, chives, and sage
4. When cooked on both sides, drain the butter
5. Add one spoonful of wheat flour diluted in half a cup of white wine, being careful not to let it boil
6. When the sauce is thick, add the juice of one lemon and serve

title: Quitute de Ganso (Goose Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roasted goose
- one quince, peeled and cut
- one onion, cut
- mangaritos or potatoes, fried in butter and sliced
- one cup water
- one cup red wine
- one egg yolk
- one tablespoon wheat flour
- one half cup vinegar
- one cup red wine

## Directions

1. Cut the leftover roasted goose into pieces
2. Place on the fire with the peeled and cut quince, cut onion, fried potatoes or mangaritos sliced, one cup of water and one cup of red wine
3. Let simmer a little
4. At the end, add an egg yolk mixed with one tablespoon of wheat flour, dissolved in one half cup of vinegar and one cup of red wine
5. Give it another boil and serve

title: Quitute de Lebre (Hare Civet)
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- four ounces of bacon cut into pieces
- four ounces of butter
- one spoonful of wheat flour
- one glass of red wine
- one glass of meat broth or water
- salt
- pepper
- seasoning herbs
- some juniper berries
- twelve small onions
- sugar
- hare liver
- hare blood

## Directions

1. Ordinarily the legs are roasted on a spit, and the civet is made from the rest.
2. Cut the hare into pieces, splitting the head as well; set aside the blood and liver.
3. Fry four ounces of bacon cut into pieces with four ounces of butter in a casserole until everything is well browned.
4. Add the pieces of hare to take on color, sprinkling them with one spoonful of wheat flour, and after mixing, add one glass of red wine, another glass of meat broth or water, little salt, pepper, seasonings, a bunch of aromatic herbs, and some juniper berries.
5. When the hare is half-cooked, add twelve small onions, which will be browned in butter with a little sugar.
6. Let them cook; five minutes before serving, add the liver, which is then removed to make a sauce for roasted meat.
7. Add the blood to thicken the sauce, and after it takes a little more heat, serve.

title: Quitute de Perú Assado (Roasted Turkey Dish)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- wheat flour
- carapicú
- parsley
- onion, chopped
- white wine
- meat broth
- roasted turkey pieces

## Directions

1. Melt two spoons of butter, mix in one spoon of wheat flour, a handful of carapicú, parsley and a chopped onion
2. Add to them one cup of white wine and two of meat broth
3. Let it boil and pour into this sauce the pieces of roasted turkey and, having boiled a little, serve

title: Rabo de Porco com Polpa de Guandú (Pig's Tail with Pigeon Pea Puree)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pig's tails
- Salt for brine
- Pigeon peas
- Carrots
- Onions
- Herbs for flavor
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Place the pig's tails in brine for eight days
2. Place them in a vessel with pigeon peas, carrots, onions, a bundle of aromatic herbs, water and salt
3. If the tails have not been brined, make a puree of the pigeon peas, reducing it on the fire so it is not too thin
4. Place the tails over the puree and serve
5. As an alternative to pigeon pea puree, one may use lentils, chickpeas, mangalós or beans

title: Rabo de Porco com Polpa de Guandú (Pig's Tail with Pigeon Pea)
body:
## Ingredients

- pig's tails
- guandú
- carrots
- onions
- bundle of aromatic herbs
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Place pig's tails in brine for eight days
2. Place them in a vessel with guandú, carrots, onions, a bundle of aromatic herbs, water, and salt

title: Rabo de Porco Dourado (Golden Pig's Tail)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eight pig's tails
- Egg yolks
- Breadcrumbs or bread powder
- Oil or fat for frying
- Fresh parsley

## Directions

1. Cook the pig's tails in a frying pan
2. Allow them to cool after cooking
3. Cover them with beaten egg yolks and breadcrumbs or bread powder
4. Fry them until golden
5. Place on a plate with a sprig of fried parsley

title: Rabo de Vacca Assado (Roasted Beef Tail)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tail
- cold water
- bacon pieces
- veal meat slices
- beef meat slices
- thin bacon strips
- three carrots
- three onions
- cloves
- thyme
- bay leaf
- nutmeg
- meat broth
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Take a beef tail and cut it at each joint
2. Place in cold water for two hours
3. Give it a boil for half an hour
4. Cool in cold water, drain, and dry with a cloth
5. In a casserole, melt bacon pieces and add slices of veal and beef meat, covering with very thin bacon strips
6. Add three carrots, three onions with two cloves inserted in each, thyme, bay leaf, and a little nutmeg
7. Moisten with meat broth and add the tail pieces
8. Boil, gradually reducing the heat for three or four hours
9. Remove and arrange in the center of a platter, surrounding with carrots covered with the meat substance and lettuce leaves

title: Rabo de Vacca com Polpa de Lentilhas (Beef Tail with Lentil Puree)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tail
- lentil puree

## Directions

1. Cook the tail as in recipe 88
2. After draining, place on a platter covered with lentil puree

title: Rabos de Carneiro Assados (Roasted Lamb Tails)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb tails
- Hot water
- Beaten eggs
- Bread crumbs
- Melted butter
- Chuchús

## Directions

1. Scald the tails in hot water
2. Wrap them in beaten eggs and bread crumbs
3. Roast on the grill
4. Drizzle melted butter over them
5. Serve with stewed chuchús

title: Rabos de Carneiro Cozidos (Cooked Lamb Tails)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb tails
- Hot water
- Bacon skin
- Onion
- Parsley
- Salt
- Fubá mimoso
- Okra
- Minas cornmeal mush

## Directions

1. Scald the tails in hot water
2. Boil again in hot water with a piece of bacon skin, onion, parsley, and salt
3. When cooked and broth well reduced, add a spoonful of fubá mimoso and a dozen okras cut into slices
4. Let everything boil for half an hour
5. Serve with Minas cornmeal mush

title: Rabos de Carneiro Fritos (Fried Lamb Tails)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb tails
- Hot water
- Salt
- Ground pepper
- Eggs
- Chopped onions
- Fat

## Directions

1. Scald the tails in hot water
2. Cut into pieces
3. Season with salt and ground pepper
4. Wrap in beaten eggs and chopped onions
5. Fry in fat
6. Drizzle beaten eggs on top
7. Let them fry as well
8. Serve

title: Rabos de Carneiro Fritos (Fried Lamb Tails)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb tails
- salt
- ground pepper
- eggs
- minced onions
- fat for frying

## Directions

1. Scald the tails in hot water
2. Cut them into pieces
3. Season with salt and ground pepper
4. Wrap them in eggs and minced onions
5. Fry in fat
6. Pour beaten eggs over them and let them fry as well
7. Serve

title: Raia Manteiga, Lixa etc., Cozida (Boiled Ray)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ray fish
- Salted water
- Parsley
- One onion
- Pepper
- One glass of vinegar
- Melted butter
- Wheat flour
- One teacup white wine

## Directions

1. Boil the fish in salted water with parsley, one onion, pepper and one glass of vinegar
2. When cooked, remove the skin
3. Place on a plate
4. Serve with a sauce made of melted butter, wheat flour and one teacup of white wine

title: Raia Manteiga, Lixa etc., Ensopada (Ray in Milk Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ray fish
- Milk
- One spoon butter
- Half spoon wheat flour
- Parsley
- One onion
- Cloves
- Salt
- Peppers
- Butter
- Tapioca flour or breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the fish
2. Cook in milk with one spoon of butter, half spoon of wheat flour, parsley, one onion, cloves, salt and peppers
3. When cooked, remove and drain, then let cool
4. Coat the fish with butter and wrap in tapioca flour or breadcrumbs
5. Fry on both sides
6. Reduce the strained broth and serve with the fish

title: Raia Manteiga, Lixa etc., Frita (Fried Ray)
body:
## Ingredients

- Very young rays
- Wheat flour
- Butter

## Directions

1. Take very young rays and clean them
2. Cut lengthwise
3. Remove the tail and head
4. Dust with wheat flour
5. Fry in butter
6. Serve with the butter poured on top

title: Raia Manteiga, Lixa etc., Guisadas (Stewed Ray)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ray fish
- Water
- Salt
- Parsley
- Onions
- Cloves
- Peppers
- Fat
- Tapioca flour
- Sour orange juice
- Broth

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the fish
2. Boil with water, salt, parsley, onions, cloves and peppers
3. When cooked, remove and sauté in fat
4. Dust with tapioca flour and moisten with sour orange juice
5. Add the strained and reduced broth
6. Let boil a little more and serve

title: Repolho com Leite (Cabbage with Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- one head of cabbage
- water
- salt
- butter
- pepper
- wheat flour
- milk

## Directions

1. Take a head of cabbage, remove leaves and wash well
2. Give it a few boils in salted water and when almost cooked, let cool and drain
3. Place in a casserole butter, salt and Indian pepper
4. Heat the fire, stir well
5. Add two pinches of wheat flour and gradually add a little rich milk
6. Reduce and serve

title: Repolho com Leite (Cabbage with Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- repolho (cabbage)
- agua (water)
- sal (salt)
- manteiga (butter)
- farinha de trigo (wheat flour)
- leite (milk)

## Directions

1. Boil a cabbage in water and salt for a quarter of an hour, remove it and place it in cold water, and then chop it very finely
2. Separately, mix a spoon of butter with a spoon of wheat flour, add the cabbage, a cup of milk, salt, cook close to the fire and serve

title: Repolho Cozido (Boiled Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabeça de repolho (head of cabbage)
- carne de porco salgada (salted pork)
- linguiças or presunto (sausages or ham)
- cenouras (carrots)
- cebola (onion)
- raiz de aipo (celery root)
- salsa (parsley)
- pimenta (pepper)
- noz moscada (nutmeg)
- agua (water)

## Directions

1. Cut a head of cabbage into four pieces and blanch it
2. Place it in a pot with a piece of salted pork, sausages or ham, some carrots, an onion, a celery root, parsley, a little pepper, nutmeg and enough water
3. Let it boil for an hour, and then place the cabbage on a plate, the meat on top with its broth, and serve

title: Repolho Cozido Recheado (Stuffed Boiled Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabeça de repolho (head of cabbage)
- cebola (onion)
- pimenta (pepper)
- noz moscada (nutmeg)
- carne de porco gorda (fatty pork)
- toucinho (bacon)
- salsa (parsley)
- vinagre or sumo de limão (vinegar or lemon juice)
- caldo de carne (meat broth)

## Directions

1. Blanch a head of cabbage, open it a little, remove the core without breaking it, and fill this space with an onion, pepper, nutmeg and a piece of fatty pork
2. Then place the cabbage in a pot lined with bacon strips, two onions, the chopped cabbage core, parsley, a cup of vinegar or lemon juice and enough meat broth
3. Let it boil over moderate heat, and serve it with its own sauce

title: Repolho Fermentado (Fermented Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- repolho (cabbage)
- barril de vinagre or vinho (barrel that was for vinegar or wine)
- sal (salt)
- bagas de zimbro (juniper berries)
- gengibre (ginger)

## Directions

1. Chop a cabbage and place it in a barrel that was for vinegar or wine, in layers three fingers thick, and on top of this a layer of salt, with different spices, such as juniper berries and ginger
2. Cover it then with a lid that fits snugly in the barrel, and place a heavy weight on top so that the cabbage is well compressed, and place the barrel in a cool place
3. After eight to ten days, it will be ready for use

title: Repolho Fermentado com Pressa (Fermented Cabbage in Haste)
body:
## Ingredients

- repolho (cabbage)
- sal (salt)
- vinagre (vinegar)

## Directions

1. Chop and season a cabbage with salt, and place it in a pan with a cup of vinegar
2. Compress it with a weight and leave it for twenty-four hours
3. Then braise it as already explained for fermented cabbage

title: Repolho Fermentado com Pressa (Hastily Fermented Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- one head of cabbage
- salt
- one cup of vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop and season a cabbage with salt
2. Place it in a pot with one cup of vinegar
3. Compress with a weight and leave for twenty-four hours
4. Afterwards, sauté it as already explained for fermented cabbage

title: Repolho Fermentado Refogado (Braised Fermented Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- repolho fermentado (fermented cabbage)
- agua (water)
- toucinho enfumatado (smoked bacon)
- linguiça (sausage)
- vinho branco (white wine)
- pimenta (pepper)

## Directions

1. Place a portion of fermented cabbage soaked in cold water
2. After an hour, drain it and place it in a pan with a pound of smoked bacon cut into pieces, a sausage, a cup of white wine, two cups of water, and as much pepper as is needed
3. Let it boil over moderate heat and serve

title: Repolho Fermentado à Allemã (Fermented Cabbage German Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- gordura (fat)
- farinha de trigo (wheat flour)
- cebola (onion)
- caldo de carne (meat broth)
- repolho fermentado (fermented cabbage)

## Directions

1. Melt four spoons of fat, add a spoon of wheat flour, a diced onion, half a bottle of meat broth, and a portion of washed fermented cabbage
2. Let it boil well and serve

title: Repolho Refogado (Braised Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- folhas de repolho (cabbage leaves)
- sal (salt)
- pimenta (pepper)
- sumo de limão (lemon juice)
- gordura (fat)

## Directions

1. Take a portion of cabbage leaves and chop them very finely
2. Blanch them, and with a pan of hot fat on the fire, add the drained leaves
3. Add salt, pepper and a spoon of lemon juice
4. Boil for half an hour and serve

title: Repolho Roxo Refogado (Braised Red Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- repolho roxo (red cabbage)
- cebola (onion)
- salsa (parsley)
- manteiga (butter)
- sal (salt)
- pimenta (pepper)
- assucar (sugar)
- vinho tinto (red wine)

## Directions

1. Chop a red cabbage finely
2. Blanch it and let it cool
3. Place two finely diced onions, parsley, two spoons of butter, salt, pepper and a spoon of sugar in a casserole
4. Let it boil and serve, placing on top a cup of red wine

title: Rim de Vacca (Beef Kidneys)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef kidney
- water
- salt
- butter
- wheat flour
- white wine
- chopped parsley
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Split the kidney and remove the hard nerve cord, then cut into small pieces
2. Place for three minutes in hot salted water, away from the fire
3. After draining, dry it
4. Melt a spoonful of butter in a casserole, add the kidney and let it fry for five minutes
5. Remove from fire and add a spoonful of wheat flour
6. Stir and add more a glass of white wine, chopped parsley, salt and pepper
7. Let it boil once more and serve

title: Rim de Vacca Ensopado com Vinho (Beef Kidneys Braised with Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- wheat flour
- beef kidney, sliced thin
- seasonings
- bouquet of aromatic herbs
- wine

## Directions

1. Place a portion of butter in a casserole and after melting, add wheat flour, increasing the fire until it becomes the color of brass
2. Add the kidney cut into fine slices with seasonings and a bouquet of aromatic herbs
3. Add wine and let it boil once, thickening it with a little wheat flour
4. Let it cook slowly until its broth is reduced by half, then serve

title: Rim de Vacca à Caseira (Beef Kidneys Home-Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef kidneys, sliced thin
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- small onions
- garlic clove
- beef or chicken broth
- egg yolks
- sugar
- milk
- vinegar
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cut the kidneys into fine slices and pass through the fire in a little butter
2. Add salt, pepper, parsley, small onions and a garlic clove, all finely chopped
3. When cooked, add a portion of beef or cooked chicken broth and a sauce made with egg yolks, sugar and milk, thickened on the fire without boiling
4. Finally add a little vinegar and do not let boil further so they do not become tough
5. Or some mushrooms can be added to the kidneys at the time they are placed on the fire

title: Rim de Vitella Frito (Fried Veal Kidneys)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal kidneys
- water
- butter
- onion, chopped
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and blanch the kidneys, leaving them in water until they cool
2. Cut them into rounds and fry in butter with a chopped onion, a little salt and pepper

title: Rins de Carneiro Assados (Roasted Lamb Kidneys)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb kidneys
- salt
- pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Remove fat from kidneys and scald in hot water
2. Split in two halves
3. Dust with salt and pepper
4. Place on grill and roast, dripping a little butter on them
5. Turn to roast the other side
6. Serve with salad

title: Rins de Carneiro com Talos de Bananeira (Lamb Kidneys with Banana Plant Shoots)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb kidneys
- one tablespoon cornmeal
- salt
- chopped parsley
- fat
- banana plant shoot tips, peeled and scalded
- two cups meat broth or water
- okra, optional

## Directions

1. Dice and braise kidneys with one tablespoon cornmeal, salt, chopped parsley, and fat
2. Add two banana plant shoot tips that have been peeled and scalded twice in hot water, removing the coarse outer leaves
3. Add two cups meat broth or water
4. Cook over coals until done
5. Serve with Minas cornmeal angú
6. Optionally add half a dozen okras cut into slices for added flavor

title: Rins de Carneiro Estufados (Braised Lamb Kidneys)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb kidneys
- fine breadcrumbs
- clove
- nutmeg
- one cup Champagne wine or other white wine

## Directions

1. Remove fat from kidneys and scald in hot water
2. Slice thinly
3. Fry the slices with breadcrumbs, clove, and nutmeg
4. After stirring a little, add one cup Champagne wine or other white wine
5. Boil over coals for half an hour
6. Serve

title: Rins de Carneiro Fritos (Fried Lamb Kidneys)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb kidneys
- butter
- salt
- one minced onion
- breadcrumbs
- mustard powder

## Directions

1. Remove fat from kidneys and scald in hot water
2. Split down the middle
3. Fry in butter over high heat, seasoning with salt and minced onion
4. Before serving, sprinkle with breadcrumbs and mustard powder

title: Rins de Porco Refogados com Leite (Pig's Kidneys Braised in Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pig's kidneys
- Salt
- Onion leaves
- Two cups of milk
- Two egg yolks
- One spoonful of sugar
- One glass of white wine

## Directions

1. Cut the pig's kidneys into thin slices
2. Add salt and onion leaves with two cups of milk
3. Once cooked, add two beaten egg yolks mixed with a spoonful of sugar and a glass of white wine
4. Bring to a boil once more and serve

title: Rins de Vitella a Pasteleta (Veal Kidneys as a Pasteleta)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal kidney
- veal meat
- water
- salt
- eggs
- dried breadcrumbs, ground
- pepper, ground
- parsley
- bread slices
- milk
- butter
- sugar
- cinnamon, ground

## Directions

1. Boil the kidney and meat in salted water and let cool
2. Chop finely and add eggs, ground dried breadcrumbs, ground pepper, salt and parsley
3. Spread this filling on bread slices moistened in milk
4. Fry these slices on both sides in a casserole with butter and serve covered with sugar and ground cinnamon

title: Rins de Vitella com Vinho Madeira (Veal Kidneys with Madeira Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal kidneys
- water
- butter
- wheat flour
- Madeira wine
- salt
- nutmeg
- sugar
- brazas

## Directions

1. Clean and blanch the kidneys, leaving them in water until they cool
2. Cut them into small slices and fry them in butter, then add a spoonful of wheat flour
3. Mix everything very well, adding a cup of Madeira wine, little salt, nutmeg and a little sugar
4. Let cook covered and boil over hot coals, then serve

title: Rins de Vitella à Parmesão (Veal Kidneys Parmesan-style)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal kidneys
- white wine
- salt
- pepper
- herbs
- butter
- grated cheese
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut and blanch the kidneys, then cook them in a little white wine with salt, pepper, a bundle of herbs and a little butter until the liquid is almost reduced
2. Place on a plate covered with a layer of grated cheese, also sprinkling some sugar mixed with cinnamon on top, and finally add a little melted butter

title: Rins Refogados em Vinho (Kidneys Braised in Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pig's kidneys
- Fat for frying
- Wheat flour
- Two glasses of white wine
- One spoonful of vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper
- One onion, chopped

## Directions

1. Cut the kidneys into small pieces
2. Fry them in fat for some time
3. Mix in a spoonful of wheat flour and stir
4. Add two glasses of white wine, a spoonful of vinegar, salt, pepper, and a chopped onion
5. Allow to boil over coals for half an hour and serve

title: Roballo, Garopa etc., com Geléia (Sea Bass with Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two or three pounds of beef
- Half pound of bacon
- Water
- Salt
- Parsley
- Two onions
- Cloves
- Peppers
- Two spoons of wheat flour
- One bottle of Champagne or good white wine
- Capers

## Directions

1. Boil two or three pounds of beef and half pound of bacon in water with salt, parsley, two onions, cloves and peppers
2. When well boiled, strain
3. Add two spoons of wheat flour, one bottle of Champagne or good white wine and a portion of capers
4. Place the fish over low heat for half an hour
5. Serve with its own broth reduced appropriately

title: Roballo, Garopa etc., com Molho de Vinagre (Sea Bass with Vinegar Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sea bass
- Ten egg yolks
- Half bottle of water
- One teacup of vinegar
- Salt
- Grated nutmeg
- Two spoons of butter
- One spoon of capers

## Directions

1. Cook the fish as indicated
2. Keep it hot in its broth until serving time
3. Beat ten egg yolks with half bottle of water, one teacup of vinegar, salt, grated nutmeg, two spoons of butter and one spoon of capers
4. Stir over fire until it reaches consistency
5. Serve with the fish and boiled peeled potatoes

title: Roballo, Garopa etc., com Vinho Branco (Sea Bass with White Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- A dozen onions
- Two spoons of butter
- Salt
- Peppers
- Nutmeg
- Small sea bass
- Mustard powder
- Lemon juice
- Melted butter
- One glass of white wine
- Coals

## Directions

1. Put a dozen sliced onions in a frying pan with two spoons of butter
2. Add salt, peppers and nutmeg
3. Place a small cleaned sea bass on top, dusted with salt and mustard powder and moistened with lemon juice
4. Add some melted butter and one glass of white wine
5. Cover the pan with a lid full of coals
6. Roast over coals and serve when cooked

title: Roballo, Garopa etc., Estufados (Braised Sea Bass)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sea bass
- White wine
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Celery
- Melted butter
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Clean the fish
2. Boil it in white wine with salt, pepper, parsley and celery
3. Remove and let cool
4. Pour melted butter on it and wrap with a layer of breadcrumbs
5. Place on a plate and put in the oven until it takes good color
6. Serve

title: Roballo, Garopa, Cherne, Corvina e Pargo Cozido (Boiled Sea Bass, Grouper, Wreckfish, Corvina and Red Seabream)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sea water or well salted water
- Half bottle of milk
- Fish

## Directions

1. Put a large amount of sea water or well salted water and half bottle of milk in a large pan
2. Put the cleaned fish in this water and boil over moderate heat
3. When cooked, remove and dry
4. Serve hot with melted butter, or cold with sweet oil and vinegar

title: Rolos de Vitella Assados (Roasted Veal Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal meat, cut into thin slices
- salt
- pepper
- onion, chopped
- garlic
- parsley
- lemon juice
- paper
- butter
- mushroom sauce
- tubara sauce

## Directions

1. Cut veal meat into thin slices like for steaks
2. Beat them lightly and wrap in cloth, then marinate in salt, pepper, chopped onion, garlic, parsley and a little lemon juice for two hours
3. Then roll them and cover with strips of paper buttered, and thus prepared, place on a spit to roast and serve with mushroom and tubara sauce

title: Rosbif à Allemã (Roast Beef German Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef brisket
- salt
- butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take a good piece of beef brisket and rub it with salt.
2. Place it in a covered pan with only the lid.
3. Roast it in the oven without any other seasoning.
4. Use the juice that has dripped out to make a sauce, adding melted butter and powdered pepper.

title: Rosbif à Franceza (Roast Beef French Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef loin with ribs
- bacon
- butter
- potatoes
- beef broth
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- fresh butter
- meat substance

## Directions

1. Take a good piece of beef loin with ribs, covered with fat.
2. Remove the meat from the kidney side and carefully separate the loin.
3. Saw the rib bones.
4. Insert bacon through the edge where the ribs were cut.
5. Remove the membrane from the loin and cover it with the fat from the kidney side.
6. Tie it with a cord so it does not fall.
7. Four hours before serving, spit the meat, tie it with cords at both ends, and cover with buttered paper.
8. Roast it with a pan underneath to catch the dripping fat, which is used to baste the meat with a brush from time to time.
9. Half an hour before serving, remove the paper and cords and return to the fire to brown.
10. Baste with a bit of meat substance.
11. Place on a plate and surround with potatoes boiled in beef broth with salt, pepper, nutmeg, and fresh butter.
12. Let the butter drain and return the potatoes to the fire to toast, or arrange potatoes braised in butter.
13. When serving, place some meat substance on top and pour the sauce from the roasting pan into a tureen, being careful to remove excess fat.

title: Rosbif à Ingleza (Roast Beef English Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight beef ribs
- potatoes
- butter

## Directions

1. Scrape the intermediate bones so that no meat remains on them for the width of one inch.
2. Put on the spit and roast for three and a half hours, being careful not to let it dry out.
3. Remove from the spit and place on a platter.
4. Surround with potatoes toasted in butter.

title: Rã Frita (Fried Frog)
body:
## Ingredients

- frog legs with skin removed
- salt
- sour orange juice
- garlic
- peppers
- nutmeg
- bread crumbs
- fat or butter

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the frog legs and rub with salt
2. Soak in sour orange juice, garlic, peppers, and nutmeg for three hours
3. Remove and coat in bread crumbs
4. Fry in plenty of fat or butter and serve with salads or compotes

title: Rã Guisada (Stewed Frog)
body:
## Ingredients

- frog legs
- water
- salt
- fat
- wheat flour
- one onion, minced
- one bottle of water
- one glass of lemon juice
- two egg yolks
- one spoonful of sugar

## Directions

1. Take the frog legs and boil for half an hour in water and salt
2. Separate the meat from the bones
3. Braise the meat in one spoonful of fat, half a spoonful of wheat flour, and a minced onion
4. Add one bottle of water, one glass of lemon juice, two egg yolks, and one spoonful of sugar
5. Let boil briefly and serve

title: Rã Refogada (Stewed Frog)
body:
## Ingredients

- frog legs with skin removed
- fat
- one glass of wine
- water
- salt
- peppers
- nutmeg
- one spoonful of arrowroot
- one spoonful of sugar
- one lemon, sliced

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the frog legs and cut the toes
2. Braise with one spoonful of fat
3. Add one glass of wine, water, salt, peppers, nutmeg, one spoonful of arrowroot, one spoonful of sugar, and a lemon cut into slices
4. Let boil for half an hour and serve

title: Salad of Roasted Partridge, Quail, etc. (Salada de perdizes, codornizes, eto. assadas)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bird roasted on a spit
- dozen Nantes sardines
- two onions
- two apples
- dozen small potatoes
- vinegar
- sweet oil
- salt
- pepper
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Take the bird roasted on a spit
2. remove all the meat and cut into small pieces
3. do the same with a dozen Nantes sardines, two onions, two apples and a dozen small potatoes
4. mix everything without crushing
5. add vinegar, sweet oil, salt, pepper, chopped parsley and serve

title: Salada de Alface à Allemã (German-Style Lettuce Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lettuce
- Salt
- Sugar
- Vinegar
- Bacon
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Chop the leaves of four heads of lettuce after washing them
2. Place them in a terrine with salt two hours before serving
3. Squeeze them, then place them on a plate with sugar, six spoonfuls of vinegar and a quarter of bacon cut into small pieces and fried
4. Mix everything and garnish with two hard-boiled eggs cut into slices, placing them on top of the salad
5. Serve

title: Salada de Alface à Francesa (French-Style Lettuce Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lettuce
- Salt
- Sweet oil
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Take four heads of lettuce, remove the outer leaves, and wash the others two or three times
2. Let them drain in a sieve, pressing a little but without bruising them, and place them on a plate at the time of serving
3. Add a little fine salt, a spoonful of sweet oil and four to five spoonfuls of good vinegar
4. Mix everything
5. Serve
6. The same way is made watercress salad

title: Salada de Alface à Ingleza (English-Style Lettuce Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lettuce
- Salt
- Eggs
- Sweet oil
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Take the leaves of four heads of lettuce, wash them and place them on a plate
2. Sprinkle with salt and place on top two hard-boiled egg yolks, dissolved in two spoonfuls of sweet oil and six spoonfuls of good vinegar
3. Serve

title: Salada de Alface à Mineira (Minas Gerais-Style Lettuce Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lettuce
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Melted fat
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Take the leaves of four heads of lettuce, chop them fine, wash them and let them drain on a sieve
2. Place them on a plate with salt, vinegar, hot melted fat and pepper
3. Mix everything well
4. Serve

title: Salada de Anchovas de Conserva (Preserved Anchovy Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- anchovies
- four hard-boiled eggs
- parsley
- chives
- garlic
- pimento da India
- one tablespoon vinegar
- one tablespoon butter
- three to four tablespoons milk

## Directions

1. Take a portion of anchovies, wash them and drain well
2. Chop four hard-boiled eggs with parsley, chives and crushed garlic
3. Season with pimento da India and one tablespoon of vinegar
4. Melt one tablespoon of butter in a pan and add this mixture
5. Let it boil and add three to four tablespoons of milk if the sauce is too thick
6. Pour over the anchovies and serve

title: Salada de Batatas (Potato Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Onions
- Salt
- Ground pepper
- Sweet oil
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook a portion of small potatoes, peel them and cut them into thin slices
2. Mix with two onions cut into slices, salt, ground pepper, sweet oil and vinegar
3. Mix everything well
4. Serve with roasts

title: Salada de Beldroega (Purslane Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Purslane
- Salt
- Peppers
- Vinegar
- Hot fat

## Directions

1. Wash a portion of purslane
2. Remove it, mix with salt, peppers, vinegar and hot fat
3. Mix everything
4. Serve

title: Salada de Beldroegas (Purslane Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- purslane
- salt
- pimentos comaris
- vinegar
- hot fat

## Directions

1. Wash a portion of purslane
2. Remove it and mix with salt, pimentos comaris, vinegar and hot fat
3. Mix everything together and serve

title: Salada de Beterraba Cozida (Cooked Beet Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beets
- Salt
- Pepper
- Sweet oil
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil one or two beet roots, peel them and cut them into slices
2. Season with salt, pepper, sweet oil and vinegar
3. Serve
