1. Place in a casserole a little fat with beef cut into small pieces, onion, carrot, clove, and fry over coals for half an hour; then add a little wheat flour or corn meal, add water, a little parsley, and let it boil for four hours; then strain through a fine sieve and serve with meat.

title: Stewed Eggs with Cream (Ovos Guisados com Creme)
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve hard-boiled eggs
- one spoon of butter
- one spoon of wheat flour
- one chopped onion
- one egg yolk
- one cup of milk
- salt
- one spoon of sugar
- cinnamon powder

## Directions

1. Cut a dozen hard-boiled eggs into slices, place on a plate
2. Make a sauce of one spoon of butter, one spoon of wheat flour, and one chopped onion, and fry a little; add one egg yolk beaten in one cup of milk, a little salt, one spoon of sugar and cinnamon powder
3. Pour over the eggs and serve

title: Stewed Eggs with Mustard Sauce (Ovos Guisados com Molho de Mostarda)
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve eggs
- half a dozen onions
- quarter pound of fat
- salt
- pepper
- one cup of water
- one small spoon of cornstarch
- one glass of vinegar
- one spoon of English mustard

## Directions

1. Boil a dozen eggs until hard, shell and cut into four pieces
2. In another pan, fry half a dozen onions in a quarter pound of fat; add salt, pepper and one cup of water with one small spoon of cornstarch dissolved in one glass of vinegar
3. Let boil, and when of good consistency, add one spoon of English mustard; place the eggs in the sauce and serve, heating a little

title: Stewed Eggs with Toast (Ovos Guisados com Torradinhas)
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter pound of butter
- twelve eggs
- cow butter
- bread crumb
- one glass of white wine
- two egg yolks
- nutmeg
- one spoon of lemon juice
- cornstarch
- sugar

## Directions

1. Melt a quarter pound of butter in a pan that can withstand the heat of the fire; when melted, break a dozen eggs into it and place the pan over coals
2. In another pan, fry in cow butter a portion of bread crumbs cut into pieces; when browned, place them over the eggs
3. Make a sauce of one glass of boiled white wine, two egg yolks, a little nutmeg, one spoon of lemon juice and a pinch of cornstarch; pour over the bread, place a little sugar on top and cover with a lid full of coals; after a quarter of an hour, serve

title: Stewed Mutton Feet (Pés de carneiro ensopados)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton feet, cleaned
- water
- salt
- cornmeal
- fat
- white wine
- three egg yolks
- bay leaf
- parsley
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cook the cleaned feet in clean water with only salt
2. Cut into small pieces, removing the bones
3. Fry one spoonful of cornmeal in one spoonful of fat until brass-colored
4. Add one glass of white wine with three beaten egg yolks, one bay leaf, a little parsley and lemon juice
5. Let boil a little, then add the feet
6. After boiling for one hour over coals, serve with potato or cassava flour porridge

title: Stewed Mutton Feet (Pés de carneiro guisados)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton feet, cleaned and scalded
- water
- salt
- bacon skins
- dried cracker powder
- carapicu
- vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Clean and scald the feet well, then boil in water, salt and bacon skins until well cooked
2. Remove the bones and cut the bacon skin and feet into small pieces
3. Reduce the broth to a few cups and thicken with two spoonfuls of dried cracker powder
4. Add a handful of carapicu, one spoonful of vinegar and another of sugar
5. Return the feet to this sauce and after boiling a little, serve

title: Stewed Oysters (Ostras Guisadas)
body:
## Ingredients

- two spoons of butter
- one spoon of wheat flour
- little salt
- pepper
- half a bottle of white wine
- carapicús (pickled), chopped and cooked
- two dozen large oysters

## Directions

1. Melt two spoons of butter
2. Mix in one spoon of wheat flour, little salt, and pepper
3. Add half a bottle of white wine
4. Once boiling, add a portion of chopped cooked carapicús and two dozen large oysters
5. Heat through
6. Serve, placing the oysters on top of the stew

title: Stewed Turtle Eggs (Ovos de Cágado Ensopados)
body:
## Ingredients

- turtle eggs
- one spoon of wheat flour
- two spoons of butter
- one chopped onion
- one glass of vinegar
- one glass of water

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of turtle eggs; when hard, shell and cut in half
2. In another pan, brown one spoon of wheat flour with two spoons of butter, one chopped onion, one glass of vinegar and another of water; let boil a little, place the eggs in the sauce and serve hot

title: Stewed Turtle Eggs (Ovos de Cágado Guisados)
body:
## Ingredients

- turtle eggs
- salt
- fat
- half a bottle of milk
- two spoons of sugar
- one spoon of cornstarch

## Directions

1. Take a portion of turtle eggs, beat with salt and as much fat as needed; fry, and when ready, add half a bottle of boiled milk, two spoons of sugar and one spoon of cornstarch
2. Let boil, stirring continuously, and serve

title: Stewed Turtle or Terrapin (Cágado ou Tartaruga Guisados)
body:
## Ingredients

- turtle or terrapin
- fat
- one cup of water
- salt
- onion, chopped
- peppers

## Directions

1. Scald the turtle
2. Remove the meat from the shells
3. Braise in hot fat
4. Add one cup of water, salt, chopped onion, and peppers
5. Let boil for a quarter of an hour
6. Serve

title: Stimulating Sauce (Molho Estimulante)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of vinegar
- serpol
- bay leaves
- one garlic clove
- onion
- pepper
- beef broth
- substance of roasted meat
- wheat flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Place in a casserole one cup of vinegar, serpol, bay leaves, one garlic clove, onion, pepper; let everything boil until it reduces to two-thirds; add beef broth, a little substance of roasted meat, a little wheat flour fried with butter, and finally whatever is available, strain.

title: Stuffed Eggs (Ovos Recheados)
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve eggs
- cow butter
- bread crumb
- milk
- salt
- ground pepper
- half an onion
- parsley
- egg yolks
- vinegar
- hot sauce
- vegetables

## Directions

1. Boil a dozen eggs, shell and cut in half lengthwise; remove the yolks and mash them with an equal volume of cow butter and bread crumb moistened in milk
2. Mix salt, ground pepper, half an onion and a little chopped parsley, binding the mixture with two egg yolks; fill the whites with this mixture, spreading the rest on a plate that can withstand the heat of the fire
3. Place the stuffed whites on this mixture, brush with one egg yolk by means of a feather; place over coals, covering with a lid full of coals; when cooked, serve with a vinegar sauce, hot sauce, or with vegetables

title: Stuffed Mutton Feet (Pés de carneiro cheios)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton feet
- salt
- half a pound of pork
- bacon
- onion
- garlic clove
- three cloves
- pepper
- nitro
- bread crumb soaked in milk
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook the mutton feet in water and salt
2. When cooked, remove the bones
3. Stuff with the following filling: mince half a pound of pork with a little bacon, one onion, one garlic clove, three cloves, salt, pepper, a little nitro, bread crumbs softened in milk and two egg yolks
4. Fry the feet in fat until well browned, then bring to table

title: Substance for Sauces (Substancia para Môlhos)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of lean beef
- ham or pig's leg
- chicken
- butter or fat
- water
- onion
- parsley
- two cloves

## Directions

1. Take a piece of lean beef, ham (or pig's leg), and chicken; fry all this chopped over a gentle fire in butter or fat for half an hour; then add a little water, onion, parsley, and two cloves, and let it cook four hours, skimming well; pass through a fine sieve and keep to make sauces.

title: Suspiros (Sighs)
body:
## Ingredients

- six egg whites
- half a pound of sugar
- one quarter of grated coconut from Bahia
- one spoon of liqueur

## Directions

1. Beat six egg whites with half a pound of sugar, one quarter of grated coconut from Bahia and one spoon of liqueur
2. When a firm mixture forms, drop spoonfuls onto thick paper
3. Bake in a very gentle oven

title: Suspiros (Sighs)
body:
## Ingredients

- six egg whites
- half pound sugar
- half quarter grated coconut from Bahia
- one spoon of liquor

## Directions

1. Beat the six egg whites with half pound of sugar, half quarter of grated coconut from Bahia, and one spoon of liquor
2. Once a hard mass is formed, drop it in small pieces onto a sheet of thick paper
3. Bake in a very gentle oven

title: Suspiros à Mineira (Sighs Minas-Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- four fresh egg whites
- one pound of refined and strained sugar
- lemon zest or ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Beat four fresh egg whites with one pound of refined and strained sugar
2. Beat until firm
3. Add lemon zest or ground cinnamon
4. Drop spoonfuls of this mixture on paper
5. Place in a very gentle oven

title: Suspiros à Mineira (Sighs Minas-Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- four fresh egg whites
- one pound refined and strained sugar
- juice of lemon rinds or a pinch of ground anise
- a small amount of spirits

## Directions

1. Beat four fresh egg whites with one pound of refined and strained sugar
2. Beat until stiff, adding the juice of lemon rinds or a pinch of ground anise
3. Drop two hundred small portions of this mass onto a sheet of paper
4. Place in a very gentle oven to bake

title: Talhadas de Carne de Anta (Sliced Tapir Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- tapir meat
- salt
- comaris pepper juice
- fat
- cangica

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into fine slices
2. Beat them with the flat of a knife
3. Trim them and sprinkle with a little salt and a sauce made with plenty of comaris pepper juice
4. Pass them in fat and roast them on a grill on both sides
5. Serve with cangica

title: Talhadas de Carne de Vacca Cozida (Slices of Cooked Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter or fat
- seven or eight large onions
- wheat flour
- beef broth
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- cooked beef slices

## Directions

1. Place in a casserole a little butter or fat and seven or eight large onions cut into rings
2. Place this casserole on high heat and stir
3. Over the onions, when golden, pour a spoon of wheat flour and a cup of beef broth, stirring until it boils
4. Add salt, pepper, and a little vinegar
5. After the sauce has boiled for twenty minutes, pour it over the plate on which the slices of cooked beef have been placed
6. Put the plate back on the fire and let it heat for half an hour and serve

title: Talhadas de Lombo com Môlho de Pimenta (Sliced Loin with Pepper Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted pork loin
- Pepper sauce, reduced
- Bread
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cut the pork loin into even slices
2. Place them in a casserole in which pepper sauce, well reduced, has been previously prepared
3. Allow to warm in a water bath
4. Serve, placing buttered bread slices around them

title: Talhadas e Fritadas de Carne de Vacca Cozida (Sliced and Fried Cooked Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- aromatic herbs
- wheat flour
- beef broth
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- cooked beef
- beef fat
- wheat flour for coating
- pork fat or sweet oil or melted butter for frying

## Directions

1. Sauté a portion of aromatic herbs in two spoons of butter
2. Add a spoon of wheat flour moistened with a cup of beef broth
3. Season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg
4. Leave to boil until the sauce thickens
5. Chop the cooked beef to which beef fat has been added
6. Pour the sauce over it and mix well
7. Form meatballs from the chopped beef
8. Roll in wheat flour and fry in pork fat, sweet oil, or melted butter

title: Talhas de Porco Guisadas com Cebolas (Sliced Pork Braised with Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted pork loin
- Fifteen large onions
- Spanish sauce, reduced

## Directions

1. Cut the roasted pork loin into small slices, being careful to remove the skin and nerves
2. Cut the large onions in half and remove their centers
3. Cook them in two spoonfuls of reduced Spanish sauce
4. Allow the loin slices to warm in a water bath until ready to serve
5. At table service, arrange buttered bread slices around them

title: Tatu Frito (Fried Armadillo)
body:
## Ingredients

- one armadillo, shell removed and cleaned
- lemon juice
- tomatoes
- parsley
- onion, sliced
- salt
- peppers
- fat

## Directions

1. After removing the shell and cleaning the armadillo, cut it into pieces and soak in lemon juice, tomatoes, parsley, sliced onion, salt, and peppers for six hours
2. Fry in plenty of fat and serve

title: Tatú (Armadillo)
body:
## Ingredients

- Armadillo
- soap
- sour orange or lemon
- one whole onion
- one head of garlic
- peppercorns from India
- half a bottle of white wine
- one quarter pound of wheat flour
- one chalice of white wine

## Directions

1. Remove the shell and odor from the armadillo, wash it with soap and sour orange or lemon
2. Wash it again in cold water and cut into pieces
3. Brown the pieces in fat
4. Add one whole onion, one head of garlic, peppercorns from India, and half a bottle of white wine
5. Braise once it begins to boil
6. When cooked, dissolve one quarter pound of wheat flour in one chalice of white wine and pour it over the armadillo, stirring a little to thicken
7. Serve with its gravy

title: Tatú Ensopado com Guandús ou Mangalós (Armadillo Stewed with Pigeon Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- one armadillo, cleaned
- fat
- one bowl of water
- salt
- parsley leaves
- onion leaves
- peppers with cumin
- a portion of beans or mangalós or guandús (pigeon peas)
- one spoonful of cornmeal mixed with one glass of lemon juice
- two spoonfuls of sugar

## Directions

1. With the armadillo cleaned and shell removed, fry it whole in fat, turning equally to fry both sides
2. Add one bowl of water, salt, parsley leaves, onion leaves, peppers with cumin, and a portion of beans or mangalós or guandús
3. Let boil and when cooked, add one spoonful of cornmeal mixed with one glass of lemon juice and two spoonfuls of sugar
4. Boil further until the broth is reduced and serve

title: Tatú Guisado com Mogango (Armadillo Stewed with Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- one armadillo
- water
- salt
- peppers
- parsley
- onion leaves
- one squash, cut and cleaned
- fat
- one minced onion
- cornmeal
- one cup of broth
- half a dozen tomatoes

## Directions

1. Boil an armadillo in water with salt, peppers, parsley, onion leaves, and a cut and cleaned squash
2. When the armadillo is cooked, remove and cut into pieces
3. Braise in fat with a minced onion and dust with cornmeal
4. Add one cup of the broth in which the armadillo was boiled and half a dozen tomatoes
5. Serve over the pieces of squash

title: Tatú Guisado com Polpa de Batatas (Armadillo Stewed with Potato Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- one armadillo, cut into pieces
- water
- salt
- parsley
- onion
- peppers with cumin
- cloves
- wheat flour
- three to four spoonfuls of fat
- potatoes, grated
- two egg yolks
- sour orange juice

## Directions

1. Cut an armadillo into pieces and boil in water with salt, parsley, onion, peppers with cumin, and cloves
2. When cooked, remove the meat, pass it through wheat flour, and braise in three to four spoonfuls of fat
3. When fried, remove it and braise a portion of grated potatoes in the same fat
4. Add the broth reduced to half, boil, stirring until nearly dry
5. Add two egg yolks dissolved in one glass of sour orange juice, place the meat in this paste, heat, and serve together

title: Tatú Refogado (Braised Armadillo)
body:
## Ingredients

- one armadillo
- fat
- tomatoes
- salt
- peppers
- marjoram
- parsley
- onion leaves
- one cup of water
- one spoonful of vinegar
- one small spoonful of arrowroot

## Directions

1. Take an armadillo, singe it, and then place over coals to remove the shell easily
2. Remove the shell and clean
3. Cut off the head and feet and cut the rest into pieces
4. Braise in fat with tomatoes, salt, peppers, marjoram, parsley, and onion leaves
5. Add one cup of water, one spoonful of vinegar, and one small spoonful of arrowroot
6. Boil for half an hour and serve

title: Tatú Refogado à Moda com Farinha de Trigo (Braised Armadillo with Wheat Flour)
body:
## Ingredients

- one armadillo
- soap and sour orange or sour lemon for washing
- fat
- one whole onion
- one head of garlic
- peppers from India
- half a bottle of white wine
- a quarter pound of wheat flour
- one glass of white wine

## Directions

1. After removing the shell and odor from the armadillo, wash it with soap and sour orange or sour lemon
2. Wash again in cold water and cut into pieces
3. Braise in fat
4. Add one whole onion, one head of garlic, peppers from India, and half a bottle of white wine
5. Cover as soon as it begins to boil
6. When cooked, dissolve a quarter pound of wheat flour in one glass of white wine and pour over the armadillo, stirring a little to bind, and serve with its broth

title: Tayoba Cozida (Cooked Tayoba)
body:
## Ingredients

- tayoba
- water
- salt
- butter
- grated cheese
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take the roots of two tayobas
2. Boil in water and salt
3. When cooked, peel and cut into slices
4. Fry in butter and place on a plate sprinkled with grated cheese and plenty of sugar, and serve

title: Tayoba Cozida (Cooked Tayoba)
body:
## Ingredients

- roots of two tayobas
- water
- salt
- butter
- grated cheese
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take the roots of two tayobas; boil them in water and salt
2. When cooked, peel them
3. Cut them into slices
4. Fry them in butter
5. Place them on a plate, sprinkled with grated cheese and plenty of sugar
6. Serve

title: Tayoba Encapotada (Breaded Tayoba)
body:
## Ingredients

- tayoba
- water
- salt
- wheat flour
- salt
- pepper
- eggs
- fat

## Directions

1. Cut a tayoba root cooked in water and salt
2. Coat in wheat flour seasoned with salt and ground pepper
3. Pass through beaten eggs and fry in fat
4. When fried, pour the remaining egg on top and serve

title: Tayoba Encapotada (Tayoba in Batter)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tayoba root
- water
- salt
- wheat flour
- salt and ground pepper
- beaten eggs
- fat

## Directions

1. Cut a tayoba root, cooked in water and salt
2. Wrap it in wheat flour; salt it with salt and ground pepper
3. Pass it through beaten eggs
4. Fry it in fat
5. When fried, pour the remaining eggs over top
6. Serve

title: Tayoba Ensopada (Stewed Tayoba)
body:
## Ingredients

- tayoba
- wheat flour
- grated cheese
- fat
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the root of a tayoba cooked into small slices
2. Separately, sauté a spoon of flour and two of grated cheese in two spoons of fat, salt, and pepper

title: Tayoba Ensopada (Stewed Tayoba)
body:
## Ingredients

- tayoba root
- water
- salt
- one spoonful of flour
- two spoonfuls of grated cheese
- two spoonfuls of fat
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- one onion
- two cups of water
- a little vinegar
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut the cooked tayoba root into small slices
2. In another pan, sauté one spoonful of flour and two spoonfuls of grated cheese in two spoonfuls of fat, salt, pepper, parsley, and one chopped onion
3. Add two cups of water and a little vinegar
4. Boil for some time and add the tayoba slices
5. Boil for half an hour
6. Thicken the broth with two egg yolks
7. Serve

title: Tenca Ensopada com Pepinos (Tench Stewed with Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- two tablespoons of fat
- chopped onion
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- garlic clove
- cucumbers
- water
- cornstarch
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Clean and scale a portion of fish
2. Melt two tablespoons of fat, add a chopped onion, parsley, salt, pepper and a garlic clove
3. Place the fish in this hot fat, add a portion of cucumbers split without the seeds, and enough water
4. Once almost cooked, add one tablespoon of cornstarch dissolved in one chicara of vinegar, boil a little more, and serve

title: Tenca, etc. Assadas (Tench, etc. Roasted)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- manioc flour
- fresh butter
- parsley
- tomato sauce or piquant sauce

## Directions

1. After cleaning and scaling the fish, wrap them in manioc flour, filling them with fresh butter and chopped parsley, and roast them over a grill
2. Serve with a tomato sauce or piquant sauce

title: Tenca, etc. Fritas (Tench, etc. Fried)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bottle of water
- one bottle of vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chopped onions
- two tablespoons of butter
- fish
- manioc flour or breadcrumbs
- beaten eggs
- cow butter

## Directions

1. Place in a casserole one bottle of water, another of vinegar, salt, pepper, parsley, chopped onions and two tablespoons of butter
2. Heat until the butter melts, pour this liquid over a portion of cleaned fish, leaving them in the sauce for three to four hours
3. Remove them, wrap them in manioc flour or breadcrumbs, pass them in beaten eggs, wrapping them again
4. Fry in cow butter and serve

title: Tenca, Pocomon e Acarás Cozidos (Tench, Pocomon and Acarás Cooked)
body:
## Ingredients

- tench, pocomon and acarás fish
- water
- salt
- onion
- carapicus
- fat
- pepper
- parsley
- white wine
- three egg yolks

## Directions

1. To clean the scales of these fish easily, place them in hot water for a few minutes, then scale them, cut into pieces if large, and leave whole if small
2. Place in cold water, changing the water two more times
3. Remove and dry well with a towel and place in a casserole with a split onion, a portion of carapicus, and pour melted fat over it
4. Cover with salt, pepper, chopped parsley and place on the fire, let fry over a brisk heat for a few minutes
5. Moisten with a bottle of white wine, remove the casserole, place on the edge of the fire and let boil until the fish are cooked
6. Place the fish on a plate with onions and carapicus, and having broken three egg yolks in the sauce, pour over it, and serve

title: Tenca, Pyranga Refogados (Tench, Pyranga Sautéed)
body:
## Ingredients

- one head of water
- two chicaras of red wine
- salt
- pepper
- onion leaves
- parsley
- fish
- mustard
- sweet oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil one head of water, two chicaras of red wine, salt, pepper, onion leaves, parsley
2. Once boiling, add the cleaned and scaled fish
3. Once these are almost cooked, add one chicara of hot wine and serve cold with a mustard sauce, sweet oil, vinegar, finely chopped parsley

title: Terrina Caseira (Home Terrine)
body:
## Ingredients

- lean beef
- lean lard
- parsley
- chives
- cinnamon
- clove
- nutmeg
- thyme
- bay leaves
- brandy
- water

## Directions

1. Cut very lean beef into small slices along with lean lard, parsley, chopped chives, cinnamon, clove, nutmeg, thyme, and a few bay leaves
2. Take a terrine and place at its bottom a layer of beef, another of lard, and a little of the prepared seasonings
3. On top, pour a spoon of brandy and two of water
4. Cover the terrine well and place over hot coals or embers to cook slowly
5. When cooked, drain the fat and serve in the same terrine

title: Timbré and Curvina Braised (Braised Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish (timbré and curvina)
- water
- parsley
- onion
- celery root
- salt
- pepper
- cassava flour
- eggs
- melted fat
- one spoon of fat
- one chopped onion
- tomatoes
- mustard flour
- one cup of water
- one glass of boiled vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of water with parsley, onion, a celery root, salt and pepper; when hot, place the fish in this broth; allow to boil several times, then remove and skin the fish with the scales
2. Dust with cassava flour, pass through beaten eggs, toast with the flour and pour over the fish a portion of melted fat, and roast the fish over a grill on both sides
3. On the other hand, fry one spoon of fat, one chopped onion, a portion of tomatoes, salt, pepper and a little mustard flour; stir and add one cup of water and one glass of boiled vinegar over the fish, and serve

title: Timbré and Curvina Braised with Palmetto (Braised Fish with Palm Hearts)
body:
## Ingredients

- clean scaled fish
- water
- parsley
- celery
- onions
- peppers
- bay leaves
- hot fat
- palmetto
- fish broth
- one spoon of tapioca starch
- one cup of lime juice

## Directions

1. Boil clean scaled fish in enough water to cover them; add parsley, celery, onions, peppers, and bay leaves; when cooked, remove and pass through hot fat, removing them quickly
2. In the same fat, sauté finely chopped and washed palmetto, add the strained fish broth and one spoon of tapioca starch dissolved in one cup of lime juice; let boil until the palmetto is cooked, place the fish inside, heat and serve

title: Timbré Braised with Water Squash (Braised Fish with Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish (timbré)
- water
- salt
- parsley
- onion leaves
- bay leaves
- water squash
- fat
- fish broth

## Directions

1. Clean and scale a fish; boil in water, salt, parsley, onion leaves and bay leaves; when cooked, remove the fish
2. On the other hand, sauté a portion of peeled water squash (removing the seeds) finely chopped in two spoons of fat with necessary salt; stir, and when hot add the strained fish broth, let it boil until the broth reduces and serve

title: Timburé e Curvina Cozidos (Timburé and Curvina Cooked)
body:
## Ingredients

- two bottles of water
- two roots of parsley
- one root of celery
- parsley
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- fish
- manioc flour
- butter or fat

## Directions

1. Boil in two bottles of water two roots of parsley, one of celery, parsley, onion, salt and pepper
2. Once boiled a few times, remove these herbs with a skimmer, place the cleaned and scaled fish in this water, and continue to boil
3. Once cooked, serve with manioc flour pirão made with the fish broth, to which one tablespoon of butter or fat is added

title: Timburé e Curvina Cozidos e Dourados (Timburé and Curvina Cooked and Browned)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- water
- salt
- bay leaf
- onion
- manioc flour or breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Clean a fish, boil it in water, salt, a bay leaf, and a cut onion
2. Once cooked, remove the skin of the fish with the scales, place on a plate
3. Having fried a portion of manioc flour or breadcrumbs until copper colored, pour over the fish, which is served with manioc flour pirão

title: Timburé e Curvina Ensopados (Timburé and Curvina Stewed)
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- parsley
- onion
- root of celery
- salt
- pepper
- fish
- manioc flour
- beaten eggs
- melted fat
- tablespoon of fat
- chopped onion
- tomatoes
- mustard flour
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of water with parsley, onion, a root of celery, salt and pepper
2. Once hot, place the fish in this broth
3. Give it a few boils, then remove the skin of the fish with the scales, sprinkle with manioc flour, pass in beaten eggs, toast and sprinkle
4. Pour over the fish a portion of melted fat, and roast the fish over a grill on both sides
5. Fry on the other side one tablespoon of fat, a chopped onion, a portion of tomatoes, salt, pepper and a little mustard flour
6. Mix and add one chicara of water and one chalice of boiled vinegar

title: Timburé e Curvina Fritos (Timburé and Curvina Fried)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bottle of water
- one bottle of vinegar
- two tablespoons of fat
- salt
- parsley
- bay leaves
- onion
- pepper
- fish
- manioc flour
- butter or fat

## Directions

1. Beat one bottle of water, another of vinegar, two tablespoons of fat, salt, parsley, bay leaves, onion and pepper
2. Place the fish in this sauce, and after two hours, give it a boil
3. After cooling, remove the fish, remove the skin with the scales, and split down the middle, cover with manioc flour, and fry in butter or fat and serve with manioc flour pirão made with the fish broth

title: Tirar o Ranço à Manteiga (Removing Rancidity from Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- spirits of wine
- warm water
- crystallised salt

## Directions

1. Heat a portion of spirits of wine, wash the butter with it by kneading it well; pour out the liquid and wash it afterwards with warm water in which crystallised salt has been dissolved; afterwards wash the butter with plenty of water; and if salted again, it will be like fresh.

title: Tomates Guisados (Stewed Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- two dozen ripe tomatoes
- cow's butter
- wheat flour
- pepper
- one glass of wine

## Directions

1. Cut two dozen ripe tomatoes
2. Scald them and remove with a skimmer
3. Place in a casserole with cow's butter
4. Sprinkle with wheat flour and pepper
5. Fry a little
6. Add one glass of wine
7. Let boil a little and serve

title: Torresmo de Carne de Porco (Pork Cracklings)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of fresh and fatty pork
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Take a piece of fresh and fatty pork and cut it into small pieces
2. Put all these pieces in a vessel with a small quantity of water with salt, and increase the heat
3. When the water evaporates, continue to press the pieces with a skimming ladle so that they release all the water
4. When the cracklings are well cooked and have achieved a nice colour, remove them from the vessel and place them to drain before serving

title: Torresmo de Carne de Porco (Rendered Pork Fat)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh and fatty pork meat
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Take a piece of fresh, fatty pork meat and cut it into small pieces
2. Place all pieces in a vessel with a small quantity of water and salt
3. Activate the fire
4. As the water evaporates, continue pressing the pieces with a skimmer so they release all water
5. When the rendered pieces are well cooked and have achieved a nice color, remove from the vessel and drain
6. Serve

title: Torta de Beijo de Fada (Fairy's Kiss Tart)
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry dough
- twenty egg whites
- two pounds sugar
- lemon rinds
- vanilla
- one chalice of orange flower water
- one chalice of cognac
- fruits for decoration
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Make a form of puff pastry for pastries, three to four fingers high
2. Beat twenty egg whites with two pounds of sugar, lemon rinds, vanilla, one chalice of orange flower water and another of cognac
3. Continue beating until a very hard mass is formed
4. Place the form in a very gentle oven and bake
5. Decorate with fruits and melted sugar beaten with two egg yolks
6. Pour the sugar into a vessel with a hole below to form flowers or letters
7. Then cover the tart with a lid with hot coals to harden the sugar

title: Torta de Creme de Amendoim (Peanut Cream Tart)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound sugar
- four cups milk, added little by little
- one dozen egg yolks
- ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Pound half pound of sugar
2. Add little by little four cups of milk, and finally one dozen egg yolks and ground cinnamon
3. Boil a little, stirring well
4. Make a form of puff pastry for pastries, fill it with the mass
5. Having placed it in the oven, bake until a good color and serve

title: Torta de Palmito ou Espargos (Palm Heart or Asparagus Tart)
body:
## Ingredients

- portion of palm hearts or asparagus
- water and salt
- eight egg yolks
- half pound sugar
- lemon rinds
- juice of one lemon
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil a portion of palm hearts or asparagus in water and salt
2. When cooked, chop them very fine and mix with eight egg yolks beaten with half pound of sugar, lemon rinds and the juice of one lemon
3. Fill a portion of forms with this mass or fill just one form, with all being buttered
4. Take to the oven, bake well, place on a serving dish, cover with powdered sugar, and serve

title: Torta de Pombas Torquazes, etc. Recheadas (Pie of Stuffed Torcaz Pigeons, etc.)
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- giblets
- pork liver
- quarter pound of bacon
- salt
- peppers
- sugar
- cassava flour
- cup of wine
- eggs
- butter
- cheese
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Open the birds along the back and remove all bones
2. Chop the giblets with a pork liver, quarter pound of bacon, salt, peppers, sugar, cassava flour, a cup of wine, and six whole eggs
3. Stuff the pigeons with this mixture and place in a well-buttered baking dish or casserole with the remaining stuffing
4. Cover with a layer of cassava flour, grated cheese, and a bacon slice
5. Roast in the oven
6. When cooked, remove the bacon, turn the casserole over onto a plate so the pie comes out whole
7. Cover with sugar and ground cinnamon and serve

title: Torta de Requeijão (Cheese Tart)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds fresh cheese
- two cups cream
- six egg yolks
- one pound sugar
- juice of sour orange

## Directions

1. Mash two pounds of fresh cheese in two cups of cream
2. Add six egg yolks, one pound of sugar, and a little juice of sour orange
3. Fill puff pastry forms with this mass
4. Bake in the oven and serve

title: Torta Imperial (Imperial Tart)
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry dough
- butter
- pulp of peaches
- apples
- sugar syrup
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Place in a well-buttered form a layer of dough
2. Place on the bottom layer another of peach pulp
3. Place on this one of apples, and so on until filling the form, with the last layer being dough
4. Take to the oven, remove and turn over onto a serving dish
5. Cover with sugar syrup and cinnamon, and serve

title: Toucinho do Céu (Bacon from Heaven)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound shelled almonds
- half cup milk
- six egg yolks
- two egg whites
- one pound wheat flour

## Directions

1. Pound one quarter pound of shelled almonds until they form a homogeneous paste
2. Add little by little half cup of milk, six egg yolks, two egg whites, and one pound of wheat flour
3. Knead everything well, place in a form buttered with butter
4. Bake in the oven and serve, having dusted with sugar and ground cinnamon

title: Tripas de Vacca de Grelha (Grilled Beef Tripe)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tripe
- carrots
- onions
- small amount of bacon
- bouquet of aromatic herbs
- four cloves
- four cloves of garlic
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- small amount of ham
- white wine
- water

## Directions

1. Scrape the opened tripe and wash in many waters
2. Scald in hot water, then soak for twenty-four hours, changing the water many times
3. Place carrots, onions, a little bacon, a bouquet of aromatic herbs, cloves, four garlic cloves, and pepper in a casserole
4. Add the drained and dried tripe, sprinkled with salt and nutmeg, with a little ham, white wine and a small amount of water
5. Seal the casserole well, applying strips of glued paper, and let it boil for six or seven hours in the oven
6. Serve hot with vegetables

title: Tripas de Vacca Enroladas (Rolled Beef Tripe)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tripe
- fresh bacon, finely chopped
- parsley
- pepper
- onion
- salt
- nutmeg
- water or broth
- cloves
- aromatic herbs

## Directions

1. Cut the tripe into strips one palm in length
2. Cover with finely chopped fresh bacon, parsley, pepper, onion, salt and nutmeg
3. Roll these strips, each roll tied with string
4. Leave them tied and seasoned to cook for three or four hours in water or broth with salt, pepper, cloves and a bouquet of aromatic herbs
5. Serve these rolls after removing the string, with some sauce or vegetables

title: Tripas de Vacca à Mineira (Beef Tripe Miner Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tripe
- salt
- orange juice
- cold water
- hot water
- Indian pepper
- parsley
- onion leaves
- eggs
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Wash the tripe well in many waters
2. Place in a vessel with salt and orange juice and let sit until the next day
3. Remove and after washing once more in cold water, scald with hot water, leaving in this water until cooled
4. Place on the fire with cold water, salt, Indian pepper, parsley, and onion leaves
5. Let boil four to five hours
6. Add two or three eggs beaten in cold water
7. Let boil for another half hour
8. After adding slices of lemon, serve

title: Tripas de Vitella Afogadas (Smothered Veal Tripe)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal tripe
- veal sweetbread
- water
- salt
- wheat flour
- butter
- parsley
- onion
- wine or water
- orange juice

## Directions

1. Clean the tripe and sweetbread well, boil them in salted water, remove and let cool
2. Cut everything into small pieces
3. Make a sauce of wheat flour toasted in butter, parsley, an onion and a little wine or water
4. Place the tripe and sweetbread in the sauce, let boil a little longer and add a little orange juice

title: Tripas Ensacadas (Stuffed Tripe Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- tripe from a pig
- water
- salt
- pepper
- summer savory
- bay leaf
- marjoram
- sage
- garlic
- parsley
- chives
- cloves
- nutmeg
- meat broth
- aromatic herbs

## Directions

1. Having cleaned and washed the tripe well, soak it in cold water for twelve hours
2. After it has drained, dry it well, placing it in a vessel where it is seasoned with salt, pepper, summer savory, bay leaf, marjoram, sage, garlic, parsley, chives, cloves, nutmeg, all well pounded, and leave it in this seasoning for two hours
3. Choose the larger and thicker tripe, and in them introduce the smaller whole tripe, tying the ends of the thick tripe
4. Cook the tripe thus stuffed in meat broth and in a sauce of aromatic herbs; after it cools, roast it on the grill

title: Tripas Ensacadas (Stuffed Tripe Sausages)
body:
## Ingredients

- tripe
- beef sweetbread
- bacon slices
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- seasonings
- water
- milk
- carrots
- onions
- cloves
- aromatic herbs

## Directions

1. Having the tripe prepared as already explained, choose the thickest ones in which to stuff the others, which should be cut to the length of two fingers, as well as the sweetbread
2. Add to them bacon slices cut lengthwise, salt, pepper, pounded parsley and the other seasonings mentioned above; leave them in this seasoning for six hours
3. Thread through one end a piece of tripe, one of sweetbread and one of bacon, and so on until the tripe is filled, but without pressing, and tie at both ends
4. When ready, put them to cook for four hours over moderate heat in a vessel in which you have put half water and half milk with carrots, onions, cloves, salt and a bunch of aromatic herbs
5. Let them cool in the same vessel, then remove them and place them to drain; give them a few pricks and put them to roast over moderate heat

title: Tripas Ensacadas à Béchamel (Stuffed Tripe Sausages in Béchamel Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- a slice of ham
- three chives
- one clove of garlic
- summer savory
- marjoram
- bay leaf
- half a bottle of milk
- a handful of bread crumb or dried rusks
- a portion of fresh pork meat
- lard
- lean bacon
- beef sweetbread
- bread crumb
- six raw egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- seasonings
- milk
- meat broth
- aromatic herbs

## Directions

1. Put a little butter in a casserole with a slice of ham, three chives, one clove of garlic, summer savory, marjoram and bay leaf; place the casserole over a low heat and let it take heat for a quarter of an hour
2. Add to it half a bottle of milk and boil it all until reduced to half; pass it through a silk sieve, put it back in the casserole
3. Add to it a handful of bread crumb or dried rusks, let it boil until the crumb is well soaked
4. Cut into slices a portion of fresh pork meat, lard, lean bacon and beef sweetbread; mix all this with bread crumb, six raw egg yolks, salt, pepper and more seasonings
5. With this mixture fill the tripe, previously well washed and drained, and tie the other end
6. When you wish to eat them, cook them in an equal portion of milk and meat broth, add to them a bunch of aromatic herbs; proceed as already explained

title: Trout, Piabanha Etc. Fried and Roasted (Fried and Roasted Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- clean fish
- fresh butter
- parsley
- chopped onion
- salt
- one cup of white wine

## Directions

1. Clean some fish and fry them in fresh butter with a little parsley, chopped onion and salt; when fried, remove from heat, and add one cup of white wine, leaving them in this sauce for one hour; roast over grill and serve with the sauce

title: Trout, Piabanha, Etc. Roasted (Roasted Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- clean fish
- fat
- breadcrumbs
- salt
- pepper
- fat

## Directions

1. Clean some fish, pass through fat, wrap in breadcrumbs, place in a covered casserole, and sprinkle with salt and pepper, a little fat; place the casserole over coals, cover with coals and let roast; when roasted, serve

title: Trout, Piabanha, Etc. Sautéed (Sautéed Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- clean fish
- water
- salt
- white wine
- one clove of clove
- bay leaves
- one onion
- tarragon
- salt
- pepper
- one spoon of fat
- one spoon of corn meal
- lime juice

## Directions

1. After cleaning the fish, boil in water and salt; remove, dry and place to cook in white wine with one clove, bay leaves, one onion, a little tarragon, salt and pepper; when cooked, melt one spoon of fat, stir, add one spoon of fine corn meal until it takes color; add the fish and broth; and having boiled a little, serve with a little lime juice

title: Trout, Piabanha, Etc. Stewed (Stewed Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoon of fat
- parsley
- chopped onion leaves
- one cup of white wine
- fish
- salt
- nutmeg
- bay leaves
- one onion
- two spoons of fat
- two spoons of corn meal

## Directions

1. Fry one spoon of fat with a little parsley and chopped onion leaves, one cup of white wine and the fish; let over strong heat for half an hour, adding salt, nutmeg, bay leaves, one whole onion, two spoons of fat and two spoons of fine corn meal; boil several more times and serve

title: Trout, Piabanha, Prepetinga and Matrinchão Boiled (Boiled Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- clean fish
- water
- salt
- white wine
- one onion
- salt
- peppers
- bay leaves
- two spoons of butter
- two spoons of wheat flour
- fish broth

## Directions

1. After cleaning the fish, place in water and salt, then boil in white wine with a cut onion, salt, peppers, and bay leaves; when cooked, remove the fish and place them in a place to keep them warm
2. Melt two spoons of butter, mixing two spoons of wheat flour; add the fish broth, and having boiled, pour over the fish and serve

title: Trouxas (Bundles)
body:
## Ingredients

- one dozen eggs
- one spoon sugar
- salt
- one spoon wheat flour
- nutmeg
- one spoon milk
- lard
- marmalade or other sweets or chopped chicken liver
- sugar and cinnamon for dusting

## Directions

1. Beat one dozen eggs, and when well beaten, add one spoon of sugar, little salt, one spoon of wheat flour, a little nutmeg and one spoon of milk
2. On another part, have over the fire a frying pan greased with lard
3. Pour in gradually two to three spoons of this thin batter, which after frying on both sides is removed and placed on a plate
4. Proceed thus until the mass is finished; these fried ones, which should not be very browned, serve as crepes to roll in them pieces of marmalade, or any other kind of sweet, or chopped chicken liver
5. Dust these bundles with sugar and cinnamon, and serve

title: Tuna and Beijupirá (Tuna and Beijupirá)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish steaks
- red wine
- vinegar
- garlic clove
- bay leaves
- clove
- nutmeg
- pepper
- salt
- sweet olive oil

## Directions

1. Soak some steaks for three to four hours in red wine, vinegar, one clove of garlic, bay leaves, clove, nutmeg, pepper and little salt; place them afterwards in a pan with a little of this sauce, one spoon of sweet olive oil, and placing the pan over coals, cook for one hour, and serve with the hot sauce

title: Tutano Frito (Fried Marrow)
body:
## Ingredients

- marrow from backbone
- wheat flour
- butter
- onion slices
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Take the marrow that is in the backbone, roll it in wheat flour
2. Fry it in a little butter with some onion slices, salt, nutmeg, and serve

title: Ubre de Vacca (Beef Udder)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef udder
- water
- salt
- bouquet of aromatic herbs
- onions
- garlic
- carrots
- glass of aguardente

## Directions

1. Scrape and wash the udder
2. Keep soaked for two hours in cold water
3. After scalding and cooling, cook for five to eight hours with salt, a bouquet of aromatic herbs, onions, garlic, carrots and a glass of aguardente
4. Serve with a spicy sauce

title: Ubre de Vacca com Molho Encorporado com Ovos (Beef Udder with Egg Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef udder
- white sauce
- mushrooms
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Cook the udder as explained for the preparation of braised beef heart
2. Let it drain and then cut into pieces
3. Place in a casserole with white sauce, increasing the fire well
4. When adding the sauce, add mushrooms placed in an egg yolk

title: Vaca de Estalagem (Inn-style Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- sliced beef
- one calyx of brandy
- two of broth
- butter
- minced parsley
- one spoon of vinegar

## Directions

1. Place sliced beef on the fire moistened with one calyx of brandy and two of broth
2. When heated, remove them and put butter on the fire to heat
3. When the butter is melted and hot, add the meat with minced parsley and one spoon of vinegar
4. Leave to boil a little more and serve

title: Vacca de Fumaça à Hamburgueza (Smoked Beef Hamburg-style)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of beef sirloin
- nitre
- fine salt
- brown sugar, one quarter measure
- two large white onions
- two carrots
- one garlic clove
- two bay leaves
- two cloves
- Indian peppercorns
- blessed herb

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from the beef
2. Rub the meat with nitre and then with fine salt
3. Place in a terrine with a base covered with salt, brown sugar, two large white onions, two carrots, one garlic clove, two bay leaves, two cloves, Indian peppercorns, and blessed herb, all chopped and mixed
4. Cover the meat with this mixture, placing a board and weight on top
5. Cover the terrine with its lid, sealing it to prevent air entry, and keep for seven days in a dry room
6. Turn the meat and leave for another seven days
7. After this time, hang the meat in the chimney over the smoke for four to six weeks
8. When serving, soak this meat in hot water for one hour
9. Then cook it for four to five hours in a pot filled with water, onions, carrots, and a bundle of fragrant herbs
10. Serve with cooked cabbage, sauerkraut, carrots or porridge, potatoes cooked in water and salt, and mashed with sugar and butter

title: Vacca de Grelha à Brazileira (Grilled Beef Brazilian-style)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef cut into thin slices
- salt
- pounded pepper
- miner's sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the beef into thin slices
2. Rub with salt and pounded pepper
3. Grill on both sides
4. Serve with miner's sauce

title: Vagens Guisadas (Stewed Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- a portion of chopped beans
- water and salt
- one egg yolk, beaten
- one glass of wine
- one chopped onion
- one cup of water

## Directions

1. Cook a portion of chopped beans in water and salt
2. Add one beaten egg yolk mixed in one glass of wine
3. In the same time, fry one chopped onion, add one cup of water, and when boiled, add the beans with broth
4. Let them finish cooking over coals and serve

title: Vagens Guisadas com Ovos (Stewed Beans with Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- a portion of beans
- water
- salt
- pepper
- eggs

## Directions

1. Take a portion of beans, remove the ends and chop them
2. Put to cook in water, salt and pepper
3. Let them boil well, and when boiling, break eggs inside so they remain whole
4. Let boil a little more and serve

title: Vagens Guisadas Inteiras (Stewed Whole Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- a portion of beans
- water and salt
- vinegar
- hot lard
- Indian pepper or comaris

## Directions

1. Take a portion of beans, remove the ends, and cook whole with only water and salt
2. When well cooked, remove with a skimming spoon and put on a plate
3. Serve putting on top a sauce of vinegar, very hot lard, and plenty of Indian pepper or comaris

title: Vagens Refogadas (Sautéed Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- green beans
- lard
- about half a bottle of water
- salt
- a little vinegar
- ground Indian pepper

## Directions

1. Chop and remove the ends from green beans
2. Scald and sauté in lard
3. Add about half a bottle of water and salt, and boil over coals, putting on top a little vinegar and ground Indian pepper

title: Vatapá de Porco à Bahiana (Bahian-style Pork Vatapá)
body:
## Ingredients

- a cut of fatty pork
- water
- salt
- tomatoes
- onions
- chili peppers
- bay leaf
- ginger
- parsley
- garlic
- ground peanuts
- lemon juice
- fubá de cangica (corn flour)

## Directions

1. Take a cut of fatty pork, cut it into pieces the size of hazelnuts, and put it to boil in a little water with salt, tomatoes, cut onions, chili peppers, a bay leaf, ginger, parsley, garlic
2. When the meat is cooked, remove it and strain the broth
3. Add to it a spoonful of ground peanuts, the juice of a lemon and a little corn flour (fubá mimoso)
4. Boil the broth still a little with the meat, and serve

title: Vermelho, Pampro etc. Ensopados (Red Bream, Pomfret Braised)
body:
## Ingredients

- red bream, pomfret or similar fish
- water
- salt
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- tomatoes
- onion
- pepper
- one spoon of manioc flour
- one glass of wine
- fish liver

## Directions

1. Clean the fish well and boil in water and salt
2. Remove the skin and coat in breadcrumbs
3. Fry in butter on both sides
4. Place on a plate near the fire to keep warm
5. In the butter, add a portion of tomatoes, a cut onion, salt, pepper and one spoon of manioc flour
6. Stir and add one glass of wine in which the fish liver has been dissolved
7. Boil a little and serve the sauce and fish separately

title: Vermelho, Pampro, e Salmonete Assados na Grelha (Red Bream, Pomfret, and Red Mullet Grilled)
body:
## Ingredients

- red bream, pomfret or red mullet
- sweet oil
- salt
- pepper
- two spoons of butter
- fish liver
- capers

## Directions

1. Clean the fish and cut into transverse strips
2. Soak for one hour in sweet oil sprinkled with salt and pepper
3. Grill the fish
4. Melt two spoons of butter, add the fish liver and a portion of capers
5. When boiled, serve the fish with this sauce

title: Vermelho, Pampro, etc., Fervidos (Red Bream, Pomfret Boiled)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bottle of water
- one bottle of wine
- one onion
- bay leaves
- parsley
- salt
- peppers
- red bream, pomfret or similar fish
- sweet oil
- vinegar
- fish liver
- butter
- one egg yolk
- broth

## Directions

1. Place in a casserole a bottle of water, another of wine, a cut onion, bay leaves, parsley, salt and peppers
2. When boiling, add the well-cleaned fish
3. Let cook for a quarter hour and remove to serve
4. Serve with sweet oil and vinegar, or serve hot with a sauce made from the fish liver dissolved in vinegar and boiled with butter, one egg yolk and broth

title: Vitella Assada (Roasted Veal)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal meat
- bacon slices
- carrots
- salt
- pepper
- onions
- parsley
- chervil
- broth
- brazas or oven

## Directions

1. Place the veal meat on bacon slices, some carrots, salt, pepper, onions, parsley, chervil and a few cups of broth, and roast over hot coals or in the oven

title: Vitella Assada (Roasted Veal)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal meat
- bacon slices
- carrots
- salt
- pepper
- onions
- parsley
- chervil
- broth

## Directions

1. Place the veal meat over bacon slices with carrots, salt, pepper, onions, parsley, chervil and a few cups of broth
2. Roast over a bed of coals or in the oven

title: Vitella Assada com Môlho de Azeite Doce (Roasted Veal with Sweet Oil Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal meat
- sweet oil
- lemon juice
- anchovies
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- parsley, chopped
- bread, grated

## Directions

1. Place the veal on a spit, baste while roasting with a sauce made of sweet oil, lemon juice, anchovies, onion, salt, pepper and chopped parsley
2. Finally put grated bread over the roast
3. Remove from spit and serve with the sauce that dripped on the plate below

title: Vitella Assada com Môlho de Azeite Doce (Roasted Veal with Sweet Oil Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal piece
- sweet oil
- lemon juice
- anchovies
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Thread the veal onto a spit
2. While roasting, baste with a sauce made of sweet oil, lemon juice, anchovies, onion, salt, pepper, and chopped parsley
3. At the end, sprinkle grated breadcrumbs over the roast
4. Remove from the spit and serve with the drippings that fell onto the plate below the spit

title: Vitella Assada de Creme (Roasted Veal with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- three garafas of milk
- half pound of butter
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- nutmeg
- onion
- veal quarter
- lemon slices
- buttered paper

## Directions

1. Boil three garafas of milk with half pound of butter, salt, pepper, parsley, nutmeg, and a halved onion
2. Once it has boiled a little, add the moderately larded veal quarter
3. Bring to a boil again and let cool
4. Remove the quarter and place on a spit, cover with lemon slices and onions, and finally with buttered paper
5. Roast and serve with toasted potatoes, vegetables, and also with salad

title: Vitella Assada de Creme e Coberta (Roasted Veal with Cream and Covered)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal quarter
- dry rusks
- butter
- milk
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- nutmeg
- onion

## Directions

1. Prepare as vitella assada de creme, but instead of larding the quarter, cover it with dry rusks before roasting on the spit

title: Vitella Assada no Espeto (Veal Roasted on a Spit)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal meat
- salt
- garlic
- pepper
- parsley
- onion leaves
- buttered paper
- butter
- vinegar
- broth

## Directions

1. Lard the veal by rubbing it with salt, garlic, pepper, parsley, and onion leaves
2. Thread onto a spit, covering with strips of buttered paper
3. When almost cooked, remove the meat and place in a pan with butter, vinegar, and a little broth
4. Cook over high heat to finish cooking and brown well

title: Vitella Coberta e Frita na Grelha (Breaded and Fried Veal on the Grill)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal meat
- salt
- pepper
- egg
- dry rusks or crackers

## Directions

1. Cut the veal meat into slices
2. Season with salt, ground pepper, and a beaten egg
3. Sprinkle with ground dry rusks or crackers
4. Fry in butter on both sides and serve

title: Vitella Coberta e Frita na Grelha, Outro Modo (Breaded and Fried Veal on the Grill, Another Way)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal meat
- butter or fat
- breadcrumbs or dry rusks
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chives
- paper

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into slices
2. Cover both sides with a mixture made of butter or fat, breadcrumb powder or dry rusks, salt, pepper, finely chopped parsley and chives
3. Wrap the prepared slices in buttered paper
4. Roast over coals on the grill and serve with salad

title: Vitella com Limão (Veal with Lemon)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal piece
- broth
- white wine
- bay leaf
- pepper
- salt
- lemon juice
- breadcrumbs
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take a piece of veal and give it two boils
2. Remove the bones and skin
3. Place in a casserole with half broth and half white wine, a bay leaf, pepper and salt
4. When cooked, add plenty of lemon juice, breadcrumbs and a little sugar
5. Serve

title: Vitella com Mariscos (Veal with Shellfish)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal meat
- shellfish
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- white wine
- water
- breadcrumbs
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into finger-thick slices
2. Lard and let braise over moderate heat in butter, salt, a little pepper, nutmeg and two glasses of white wine
3. When cooked, add a dozen shellfish with water
4. Stir, and after adding breadcrumbs and lemon juice, serve with the broth

title: Vitella com Sardinhas (Veal with Sardines)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal meat
- water
- salt
- butter
- broth
- nutmeg
- sardines
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cut veal meat into finger-sized slices
2. Cook in salted water
3. Place in a casserole with a little butter, a spoonful of broth, a little nutmeg and let braise
4. Before serving, add whole washed sardines, heat and serve, arranging the meat and sardines symmetrically on the plate, sprinkling breadcrumbs on top and pouring the broth from the casserole over it

title: Vitella Frita com Carapicú (Fried Veal with Carapicú)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal meat
- bacon slices
- carrots
- carapicú
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- seasoning sauce
- white wine
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Cut veal meat into slices and place in a pan over bacon slices with some carrots, selected carapicú, sliced onion, salt, pepper, and a seasoning sauce
2. Let roast for an hour, then add a cup of white wine and lemon slices
3. Continue cooking over coals until done

title: Vitella Frita de Nantes (Fried Veal from Nantes)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal portion
- sweet oil
- pepper
- salt
- parsley
- onion
- garlic
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Place a veal portion in a pan with six spoonfuls of sweet oil, pepper, salt, parsley, onion, garlic and nutmeg
2. Fry over coals, turning occasionally until cooked, and serve with some spicy sauce

title: Vitella Frita sobre Grelha (Fried Veal on the Grill)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal meat
- sweet oil
- onion
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut veal meat into slices and marinate for three to four hours in sweet oil, sliced onion, parsley, salt, pepper, and a little vinegar
2. Roast the slices on both sides on the grill and serve with chef's sauce

title: Vitella Guisada (Stewed Veal)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal piece
- seasoning mixture
- carrots
- potatoes
- veal foot
- water
- wine

## Directions

1. Take a larded veal piece and place in a casserole with a seasoning mixture, some carrots, potatoes and veal foot cut into pieces
2. Cook over coals with equal parts water and wine, keeping the pan well covered
3. Remove the veal piece, strain the substance from the sauce that remains at the bottom of the pan and serve the meat with this sauce on top

title: Vitella Guisada com Língua (Stewed Veal with Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- veal portion
- butter
- onions
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- sweet oil
- white wine
- meat broth
- carapicú

## Directions

1. Take a cooked beef tongue, remove the skin, cut into strips and lard a veal portion with this tongue
2. Fry the portion in butter, turning occasionally
3. On the other hand, fry half a dozen onions with salt, pepper and parsley in three or four spoonfuls of fresh sweet oil, pour this mixture over the veal
4. Add two glasses of white wine and the same amount of meat broth and let finish cooking over moderate heat
5. Finally, add a handful of selected carapicú and serve

title: Vitella Guisada com Presunto (Stewed Veal with Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal sirloin
- lean ham
- butter
- parsley
- onion
- garlic
- carapicú
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- white wine
- paper

## Directions

1. Lard a veal sirloin with a piece of lean ham cut into strips
2. On the other hand, fry in a little butter some parsley, an onion, half a clove of garlic, a handful of carapicú, salt, pepper and nutmeg
3. Add a glass of white wine and let the sirloin boil for half an hour
4. When cold, wrap in four sheets of paper and let cook for two hours in a casserole with low heat below and above
5. Serve with the same paper

title: White Gemada (Gemada Branca)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bottle of milk
- salt
- three spoons of sugar
- four egg whites
- egg yolks
- sugar
- cinnamon powder

## Directions

1. Boil one bottle of milk with salt and three spoons of sugar until reduced to half
2. Add four well-beaten egg whites, continuing to beat
3. Add the egg yolks
4. Let boil for ten minutes
5. Pour onto plates and let cool
6. Cover with sugar and cinnamon powder
7. Serve

title: Whole Mushroom Sauce (Molho de Cogumelos Inteiros)
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- small potatoes
- corn meal or wheat flour
- butter
- olives
- capers
- lemon slices
- sardines, chopped
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cook mushrooms and small potatoes and peel them; then fry a little corn meal or wheat flour in butter, add the mushrooms and potatoes, olives, capers, lemon slices, some chopped sardines, and a little sugar, and let everything boil.
2. Serve with roasted pork or beef.
